symptom: Multiple PSI accounts on one Android device intermittently fail to receive notifications.
rootCause: Company Portal takes over as broker instead of Authenticator. Multi-account PSI needs updated broker library. Check logs: isAuthenticatorBroker: false.
solution: Uninstall Company Portal to ensure Authenticator is broker. Fixed at GA. Android 6.2404.2872+ supports multi-account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/MS Authenticator PSI Limitations and known issues

symptom: PSI notifications not appearing when sign-in from unfamiliar location. Failed MFA in sign-in logs. ESTS shows PSIBLK flag.
rootCause: Smart lockout blocks PSI notifications from unfamiliar locations. Location familiar after 14 days or 10 logins (ESTS RAM).
solution: Open Authenticator and swipe down for manual prompt. Check ESTS Kusto AllPerRequestTable for PSIBLK flag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Microsoft Authenticator (PSI) For Work Accounts/Whats New

symptom: Azure AD B2C 配置 LinkedIn 作为身份提供商时报错 AAD2C90273: An invalid response was received: Error: unauthorized_scope_error, Error Description: Scope "r_emailaddress" is not authorized for your application
rootCause: LinkedIn 已停止为新应用支持 OAuth2 协议作为 IdP。2023年8月1日后创建的新配置使用旧的 OAuth2 scope（如 r_emailaddress）会被 LinkedIn 拒绝
solution: 改用 OpenID Connect (OIDC) 协议配置 LinkedIn IdP：1) 在 LinkedIn Developer Portal 启用 'Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect' 产品 2) B2C 中添加 New OpenID Connect provider，Metadata URL 设为 https://www.linkedin.com/oauth/.well-known/openid-configuration 3) Scope 设为 'openid profile email'，Response type 设为 'code' 4) 自定义策略中使用 Protocol Name='OpenIdConnect' 的 TechnicalProfile
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C LinkedIn as OIDC IdP

symptom: Custom RBAC roles not listed under Enterprise Apps > <application> > Roles and administrators blade in Entra portal
rootCause: By design, only custom roles containing permission prefixes 'microsoft.aad.directory/applications', 'microsoft.aad.directory/applicationPolicies', or 'microsoft.aad.directory/servicePrincipals' appear under Enterprise Applications.
solution: Ensure the custom role definition includes at least one permission with the required prefixes: microsoft.aad.directory/applications, microsoft.aad.directory/applicationPolicies, or microsoft.aad.directory/servicePrincipals. Roles without these prefixes will not appear under the Enterprise Apps blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD RBAC Custom Roles for Enterprise Apps

symptom: Custom role members cannot access Microsoft Entra ID administration portal despite having valid role assignment
rootCause: Tenant setting 'Restrict access to Microsoft Entra ID administration portal' is set to 'Yes', which blocks custom role members from portal access.
solution: Change 'Restrict access to Microsoft Entra ID administration portal' to 'No'. Note: this is a by-design limitation — custom role members are subject to this tenant-wide restriction regardless of their assigned permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD RBAC Custom Roles for Enterprise Apps

symptom: Custom RBAC role permission denied errors not appearing in Microsoft Entra ID Audit logs
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID only records failure audit events when an actual directory backend update is attempted and fails. If the principal lacks permissions, denial occurs at a higher authorization layer (before backend) and is not logged in Audit logs.
solution: Use Kusto tracing in MSODS (msodsuswest.kusto.windows.net). Query Global('IfxBECAuthorizationManager') with internalCorrelationId, filter tagId='9xy6' for DENIED events, tagId='0azf' for GRANTED. Distinguish RBACv1 (message contains [Task:]) vs RBACv2 (message contains [Action:]). First use GlobalIfxUlsEvents with the resource objectId to find the internalCorrelationId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD RBAC Custom Roles for Enterprise Apps

symptom: Unable to create new Microsoft Entra ID tenant - option greyed out in portal with message 'Customers must own a paid license to create Microsoft Entra Workforce tenant'
rootCause: Starting Oct 2023, tenant creation from an existing tenant requires a non-trial paid M365 seat-based subscription. Azure subscriptions alone are not sufficient. A Graph query to /directory/subscriptions checks isTrial and commerceSubscriptionId to determine eligibility.
solution: 1) Verify tenant has paid M365 subscription via Graph: GET /beta/directory/subscriptions?$select=isTrial,status,skuId,commerceSubscriptionId. Need isTrial=False and commerceSubscriptionId!=skuId. 2) If no paid M365 subscription, customer can create tenant via https://azure.microsoft.com/free/ signup flow instead. 3) Any non-trial M365 seat-based subscription (e.g. Power BI paid) qualifies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Portal tenant properties showing data location as 'United States Datacenters' instead of expected 'Canadian Datacenters'
rootCause: By design - Microsoft currently does not offer in-country Entra ID data residency for Canadian customers. Entra ID is classified as a non-regional service.
solution: Inform customer this is by design. For M365 data, customer can request data move to Canada per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/moving-data-to-new-datacenter-geos. Entra ID data residency docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/data-residency. Reference: ICM 303609655.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Domain force deletion fails - DomainForceDeleteTask status shows 'Incomplete'. Error in logs: 'Property or link ProxyAddresses on this object may be updated only on behalf of Exchange' (DirectoryAuthorityException)
rootCause: Exchange mastered groups (_SingleAuthorityMetadata has ExchangeMastered=True) have proxy addresses referencing the domain being deleted. DomainForceDeleteTask cannot remove proxy addresses from Exchange-mastered groups.
solution: 1) Check DS Explorer PropagationTask for DomainForceDeleteTask. 2) Identify Exchange mastered groups referencing the domain and provide ObjectIds to customer. 3) Customer removes proxy addresses from those groups. 4) Submit ICM to EEE team to remove failed DomainForceDeleteTask. 5) Customer retries domain deletion. Kusto query for logs: GlobalIfxUlsEvents() | where message contains '<TaskId>'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Cannot verify custom domain in Microsoft Entra ID - domain is already verified in another managed tenant
rootCause: The custom domain is already registered and verified on a different Entra ID tenant. A domain can only be verified on one tenant at a time.
solution: 1) Identify which tenant owns the domain: use OIDC endpoint https://login.microsoftonline.com/{domain}/.well-known/openid-configuration to get tenant ID. 2) Or use ASC Tenant Explorer with domain name. 3) If customer owns the domain, contact Data Protection team to remove it from the other tenant (VKB: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2905700). 4) If domain was removed from another tenant, check if Source of Authority is still Microsoft - may need escalation to O365 team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Cannot verify custom domain - domain is verified on a viral/unmanaged tenant created by self-service signup (Power BI, RMS, PowerApps)
rootCause: Domain was auto-verified when a user signed up for a self-service cloud service, creating a viral tenant. CompanyTags contains 'EmailVerified'. The User Realm endpoint shows IsViral=True.
solution: 1) Check User Realm endpoint: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/userrealm/{domain}?api-version=2.1 - look for IsViral=True. 2) In ASC, check Company Profile > Profile tab for 'Email Verified'. 3) In DS Explorer, check CompanyTags for 'EmailVerified'. 4) Customer performs internal admin takeover or forced external takeover per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/domains-admin-takeover
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: 'Could not find the DNS record for this domain' error when trying to verify a custom domain in Microsoft Entra ID / Office 365
rootCause: DNS TXT or MX record not created correctly, or DNS propagation not complete (up to 72 hours for global propagation).
solution: 1) Check DNS record via PowerShell: Resolve-DnsName -Name domain.com -Type TXT (or MX). 2) Verify expected verification record in ASC Tenant Explorer > Domains > Registration tab. 3) Or use nslookup: Set type=txt, then enter domain name. 4) If record exists but verification still fails, wait up to 72 hours for DNS propagation. Reference: https://support.microsoft.com/help/2515404
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Error adding/verifying subdomain in Entra portal when parent domain is federated: 'One or more properties contains invalid values' / 'AddDomain. domain.LiveType must follow the root domain'. Subdomain inherits federated authentication type from parent.
rootCause: Portal bug - subdomains inherit parent domain's authentication type (LiveType). The portal cannot set a child domain of a federated parent as managed. Child domains have no own LiveNetId and use the parent's LiveType.
solution: Use PowerShell + Graph API instead of portal: 1) Connect-Entra -Scopes 'Domain.ReadWrite.All' then New-EntraDomain -Name child.mydomain.com. 2) Verify via Graph: GET /v1.0/domains/child.mydomain.com (confirm isRoot=false). 3) Promote to root via Graph: PATCH /v1.0/domains/child.mydomain.com with body to set authenticationType=Managed. 4) Once isRoot=true, the subdomain has its own authenticationType independent of parent. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/domains-verify-custom-subdomain
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Verifying a custom subdomain as managed domain

symptom: Error 'DirectoryQuotaExceededException: The directory object quota limit for the Tenant has been exceeded. Please ask your administrator to increase the quota limit or delete objects to reduce the used quota.' (ExceptionDetails: DirectoryQuotaExceededExceptionKind=Tenant)
rootCause: Default Entra ID tenant object quota is 50k objects. Quota protects service from accidental or intentional over-provisioning beyond tenant needs.
solution: 1) Customer checks current quota: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/organization?$select=directorySizeQuota. 2) Internally: ASC > Tenant Explorer > Tenant Config > Object Quota. 3) For quota increase: follow 'Creating ICM for tenant quota increase requests' process. 4) Customer can monitor with alerts using script from https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/core-infrastructure-and-security/the-case-of-azure-ad-quota-exhaustion/ba-p/2496133. Note: Azure AD B2C quotas are different, see separate B2C quota wiki.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra ID Object Quotas

symptom: Cannot verify custom domain - domain is not verified on any Entra ID tenant but verification still fails. Domain exists as orphaned record in OrgId.
rootCause: Orphaned domain record exists in OrgId but not in MSODS. Or domain is federated with Microsoft Exchange Online.
solution: 1) Check domain in Identity Consistency (ICC) tool: http://co1gr1sst101.grn001.msoprd.msft.net/IdentityConsistency/diagnosis.html - Select 'Namespace View', enter domain name. 2) If record in OrgId but not in MSODS, submit ICM to OrgId/Triage team to purge the orphaned domain. 3) If domain is federated with Exchange Online, see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/troubleshoot/administration/cant-verify-domain-error-when-verify-domain
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Outlook continually prompts for password when CAE (Continuous Access Evaluation) is enabled
rootCause: Tenant has not configured IPv6 trusted locations for Conditional Access. CAE-enabled clients connecting via IPv6 are not matched to any Named Location, causing repeated re-authentication.
solution: Verify and configure IPv6 trusted locations in Conditional Access Named Locations. Ensure the IPv6 address ranges used by the organization are added to the appropriate Named Location and marked as trusted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: User that was disabled and re-enabled is unable to access their mailbox after re-enabling
rootCause: CAE instant enforcement revoked the session when the account was disabled. After re-enabling, there is a propagation delay (35-45 minutes) before CAE allows the user to access resources again.
solution: Inform the user to wait 35-45 minutes after the account is re-enabled for mailbox access to restore. This is expected behavior due to CAE session revocation propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: CAE not enforcing Conditional Access policy when user's location changes; CountryLookupMethod:null appears in Named Location policy JSON
rootCause: A build deployed September 1-5, 2020 introduced a new CountryLookupMethod element with null value in Named Location policies created or edited during that window. The null value causes the CAE NuGet package used by EXO and SPO to fail when reading the policy, so CAE location enforcement is skipped.
solution: In ASC > Conditional Access > Named locations, find the ObjectID of any Named Location. Search for the object in Directory Objects. In the policyDetail JSON, find all instances of '"CountryLookupMethod":null'. For each impacted Named Location, rename it in the Azure Portal or via MS Graph API and save — this removes the null element. Then rename it back if desired.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: EAS (Exchange ActiveSync) clients cannot access email and receive quarantine message when CAE is enabled
rootCause: EAS clients use legacy authentication which does not support session controls. When CAE is enabled, the session controls translate to a block for EAS clients, causing a quarantine.
solution: Create a Conditional Access policy with the 'Customize continuous access evaluation' session control set to 'Disabled' and scope it to EAS users. This prevents CAE from being applied to EAS clients. Reference ICM 271404212.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: Sign-in logs show 'Success' status for non-CAE clients when CAE strict enforcement blocks access; actual access is denied but initial interactive sign-in shows success
rootCause: During the first leg to /Authorize endpoint, CAE strict enforcement doesn't block because the only token is an Authorization Code (not subject to strict enforcement). The block occurs at token endpoint redemption (non-interactive request), but the interactive sign-in event is recorded as Success.
solution: Check both interactive and non-interactive sign-in logs. Look for the 'SLEv2' value in the CaAdhoc field in PerRequestIfx (via Logsminer) to confirm strict location enforcement blocked the sign-in. The non-interactive request will show as Failed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: Clients with IPv6 addresses incorrectly have Conditional Access location-based policy applied; blocked despite being in excluded country
rootCause: Named Locations does not support IPv6 addresses for geolocation mapping. IPv6 addresses cannot be mapped to a country, so they fall into 'unknown areas'. If the Named Location has 'Include unknown areas' checked, the policy applies to IPv6 clients.
solution: Customer should uncheck the 'Include unknown areas' option in the Conditional Access Named Location. Alternatively, add IPv6 address ranges as IP-based Named Locations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Named Locations for Conditional Access

symptom: Government tenant users blocked by location-based Conditional Access policy when accessing 1st party apps from excluded locations; app sends auth request to public cloud URL (.com) first
rootCause: Some 1st party applications send the initial authorization request to the public cloud URL (.com), which then redirects through /common/GetCredentialType to the government cloud (.us). Due to these redirections, Azure AD sees a Microsoft IP address instead of the client's actual IP, causing the sign-in to not match the excluded location.
solution: For 'Manage your account' from Windows settings, see Epic 1404593. For Microsoft Visual Studio, follow the guide at 'Quickstart: Connect to Azure Government with Visual Studio' to configure direct government cloud connectivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Named Locations for Conditional Access

symptom: Workload identity (service principal) fails to acquire access token with AADSTS53003: Access has been blocked by Conditional Access policies
rootCause: A Conditional Access policy for workload identities is configured to block sign-in from untrusted locations. The service principal's sign-in attempt originates from an IP not in the excluded Named Location.
solution: 1. Identify the CA policy from the error claims (capolids). 2. Verify the service principal's sign-in IP. 3. Either add the IP to a Named Location excluded from the policy, or adjust the policy scope to exclude this service principal. Use Postman to test client_credentials grant with the SP's client_id and client_secret.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Conditional Access for Workload Identities

symptom: Error 1072: Policy cannot be assigned to both users and service principals when configuring Conditional Access for workload identities
rootCause: By design, Azure AD does not allow assigning both users and workload identities (service principals) to the same Conditional Access policy.
solution: Create separate Conditional Access policies: one for users and one for workload identities (service principals). The 'Users or workload identities' dropdown at the top of the policy must be set to only one type.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Conditional Access for Workload Identities

symptom: Error 1078: Service principal(s) with object ids are not supported when assigning Managed Identity or multi-tenant app to CA policy for workload identities
rootCause: By design, Managed Identities and multi-tenant applications cannot be assigned as workload identities to Conditional Access policies. Only single-tenant service principals (Enterprise apps) registered in the tenant are supported.
solution: Use only single-tenant service principals registered in the tenant. Managed identities and third-party SaaS/multi-tenant apps are not supported for CA workload identity targeting. For multi-tenant scenarios, the app must be registered as single-tenant in the customer's tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Conditional Access for Workload Identities

symptom: Error 1150: Cannot create or update policies with workload identity premium features when creating/editing CA policy for service principals via API
rootCause: Post-GA, Microsoft Entra Workload Identities premium licenses are required to create or edit Conditional Access policies targeting service principals. The Conditional Access APIs enforce licensing requirements.
solution: Customer must acquire Microsoft Entra Workload Identities premium licenses by purchasing them or activating a 90-day free trial. Trial activation URLs: Entra admin center (entra.microsoft.com) or Azure AD Portal (portal.azure.com) with feature flags. Note: 'Workload_Identities_for_EPM_customers' is NOT the same as Workload Identities premium.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Conditional Access for Workload Identities

symptom: Sending a governance invitation from the governed tenant fails with HTTP 403 forbidden: "Failed to send governance invitation"
rootCause: The governing tenant has not enabled the "Allow other tenants to send governance invitations to this tenant" setting in Tenant governance settings
solution: Navigate to Tenant governance settings blade under Settings → Toggle "Allow other tenants to send governance invitations to this tenant" to Enabled → Save. Graph API: PATCH /directory/tenantGovernance/settings with body {"canReceiveInvitations":true}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: GDAP users from governing tenant cannot sign in to governed tenant; Conditional Access policies block GDAP sign-in
rootCause: GDAP users do not have a user presence in the governed tenant. Conditional Access policies that require user resolution (e.g., Terms of Use) will block sign-in because there is no user object to resolve against
solution: Review and adjust Conditional Access policies in the governed tenant to accommodate GDAP users. Exclude policies requiring user presence (Terms of Use, user-based conditions) for GDAP/cross-tenant scenarios. GDAP users sign in via entra.microsoft.com/{governed tenant ID}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Governed tenant is unable to terminate a governance relationship; Terminate button is unavailable or does not work
rootCause: The governance relationship was created using a policy template from before November 2025 (private preview era). These older templates lack the governed-side termination capability
solution: The governing tenant must initiate termination: Go to Governed tenants blade → Governed tenants tab → select the relationship → click Terminate governance. Then the governed tenant confirms: Governing tenants blade → set Relationship status to Termination requested → select relationship → click Confirm termination
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Governance request fails with "License required" error when attempting to establish GDAP relationship or app injection
rootCause: Missing required licensing: Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 is required for GDAP relationships; Microsoft Entra ID Governance license is required for app injection (multitenant application provisioning) scenarios
solution: Ensure the governing tenant has Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 license for GDAP. For app injection scenarios, ensure Microsoft Entra ID Governance license is available. Verify licensing before sending governance requests
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: GDAP user from governing tenant cannot sign in to Microsoft 365 admin portal of governed tenant; target tenant endpoint not supported
rootCause: The Microsoft 365 admin portal does not support target tenant sign-in endpoints (e.g., admin.microsoft.com/{tenantId}). Only portals like entra.microsoft.com support this
solution: Use entra.microsoft.com/{governed tenant ID} to sign in as GDAP user. For M365 admin portal access, use the workaround documented at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/2275795/login-to-microsoft-365-admin-center-of-specific-ta
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Sending a governance invitation from the governed tenant fails with HTTP 403 Forbidden: Failed to send governance invitation
rootCause: The governing tenant has not enabled the Allow other tenants to send governance invitations setting in Tenant Governance Settings
solution: In the governing tenant: Entra admin center > Settings > Tenant governance settings > Toggle Allow other tenants to send governance invitations to Enabled > Save. Graph API: PATCH /directory/tenantGovernance/settings with body {canReceiveInvitations:true}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: GDAP users from governing tenant cannot sign in to the governed tenant; Conditional Access policy blocks authentication
rootCause: Conditional Access policies in the governed tenant require user resolution (e.g. Terms of Use) but GDAP users have no user presence in the governed tenant
solution: Adjust Conditional Access policies in the governed tenant to accommodate GDAP users who lack a local user object. Exclude GDAP sign-ins from policies requiring Terms of Use or similar user-presence-dependent controls
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Governed tenant is unable to terminate a governance relationship; Terminate button unavailable or action fails
rootCause: Governance relationship was established using a policy template created before November 2025 (private preview), which does not support direct termination by the governed tenant
solution: Two-step termination: 1) Governing tenant sends termination request via Governed tenants blade > Select relationship > Terminate governance. 2) Governed tenant confirms via Governing tenants blade > Termination requested > Confirm termination
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: GDAP user from governing tenant cannot sign in to Microsoft 365 admin portal of the governed tenant
rootCause: Microsoft 365 admin portal does not support target tenant endpoints (targettenant signin), unlike the Entra admin center
solution: Use the Entra admin center with target tenant URL: entra.microsoft.com/{governed-tenant-id}. Workaround doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/answers/questions/2275795
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Creating a governance policy template fails with HTTP 409: Governance Policy Template already exists
rootCause: A governance policy template with the same display name already exists in the tenant
solution: Use a unique display name. Check existing templates via Graph API: GET /directory/tenantGovernance/governancePolicyTemplates
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Creating a governance policy template fails with HTTP 400: Group is not role assignable
rootCause: The security group specified in the policy template is not configured as role-assignable (isAssignableToRole=false)
solution: Use a role-assignable security group: Entra admin center > Groups > New group > Toggle Microsoft Entra roles can be assigned to the group to Yes, or via Graph API with isAssignableToRole=true
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Sending a governance invitation fails with HTTP 403: Maximum limit of 10 reached
rootCause: The governed tenant has already sent the maximum of 10 outbound governance invitations
solution: Delete existing outbound invitations that are no longer needed, then retry. Invitations expire after 30 days automatically
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Sending a governance request fails with License required error for GDAP or app injection scenarios
rootCause: GDAP relationships require Microsoft Entra ID P1 or P2 license; app injection scenarios require Microsoft Entra ID Governance license
solution: Ensure governing tenant has required licenses: Entra ID P1/P2 for GDAP delegation, Entra ID Governance for multitenant app injection. Check in Entra admin center > Billing > Licenses
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Tenant Governance/Governance Relationships

symptom: Audit logs do not display specific application permissions granted during admin consent to Application type permissions; ConsentAction.Permissions field is empty
rootCause: By design: App roles (application permissions) can be granted outside the Consent to app experience. The consent audit event only captures delegated permissions in detail.
solution: Monitor individual "Add app role assignment to service principal" and "Add delegated permission grant" audit events. Group by correlation ID to associate with interactive consent events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: IDX14100 JWT is not well formed, there are no dots (.) error when calling /userinfo endpoint or from application logs
rootCause: Application sends a V1 access token to the V2 userinfo endpoint (https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo) which expects a V2 JWT token
solution: Either request a V2 access token for https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo, or send V1 access token to V1 userinfo endpoint at https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/openid/userinfo
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Error "No other properties may be modified when certification properties are being modified" when saving application manifest in Azure Portal
rootCause: Application has certification property set. Portal sends PATCH to update all properties including read-only certification property.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph API to update only the specific property needed instead of the manifest editor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Users prompted for admin consent despite User Consent Settings configured to "Let Microsoft manage your consent settings" + "Enable user consent for popular Mail clients"
rootCause: Known PG bug (Bug Task 3493813) in legacy consent settings handling
solution: Toggle User Consent Settings: set to "Do not allow user consent", Save, then change back to desired setting and Save again.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: SharePoint/OneDrive tile not visible in MyApps portal or collections
rootCause: SharePoint has a dedicated setting controlling tile visibility in MyApps, separate from Entra ID app visibility settings
solution: Revert SharePoint setting to Show via SharePoint admin. Note: visibility may take up to 24 hours to fully return.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: HTTP 404 (404.15 Request Filtering: Query String Too Long) during OpenID Connect authentication redirects in IIS-hosted apps
rootCause: Authorization code payload increased due to security fix MSRC 103168 adding VSMBindingKeyHash2 (~44 chars) for device-binding, pushing apps near 2048-char URL query string limit over the edge
solution: Increase IIS Request Filtering maxQueryStringLength in web.config; configure OIDC/OAuth clients to use response_mode=form_post to deliver auth response via HTTP POST body instead of URL query parameters.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: New App Registration experience does not display warning/error when adding redirect URI that already exists under a different platform type; URI silently not added
rootCause: UI bug in new App Registration experience (Bug 3497427). Old experience correctly shows validation message.
solution: Use the old App Registration experience which displays the expected validation error, or check existing redirect URIs across all platform types before adding.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Salesforce SSO fails with device activation requirement after Salesforce security hardening; SAML AuthnContextClassRef shows Password even when MFA was completed
rootCause: eSTS picks first auth type claim for AuthnContextClassRef. For Password+MFA, it emits Password. For passkeys, it emits Unspecified. Salesforce requires secure auth method indicators.
solution: As of 2026-01-30, Salesforce updated to accept multipleauthn and mfa values. If user completes MFA, SAML response includes multipleauthn claim value. For OAuth apps, use V1 endpoint to get amr claim. No action needed for customers using MFA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Custom Claims Provider attribute selected as NameID source in SAML SSO causes NameID format to unexpectedly change to Persistent with random identifier
rootCause: Custom claims provider attributes cannot be used as NameID source. Entra ID falls back to generating persistent identifier when NameID source is null, not in supported directory attributes, or from external source.
solution: Use a supported native Entra ID directory attribute (userPrincipalName, mail, onPremisesSAMAccountName, objectId) as NameID source. If application requires specific value, configure it as AttributeStatement claim instead of NameID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Connect-MgGraph fails with AADSTS50011 redirect URI mismatch for sovereign clouds (UsGov/China) after upgrading to Microsoft.Graph PowerShell 2.34.0+
rootCause: Microsoft.Graph PowerShell SDK 2.34.0 enables WAM (Web Account Manager) by default on Windows, but WAM redirect URIs are not registered for sovereign cloud applications
solution: Use -UseDeviceCode or -UseDeviceAuthentication switch with Connect-MgGraph, OR downgrade to Microsoft.Graph version 2.28.0
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Global Admin unable to add specific gallery applications in Enterprise Apps - Create button greyed out for Linked SSO or Password SSO only apps
rootCause: By design: apps supporting only Linked/Password SSO are not identity-integrated (no SAML/OIDC/SCIM) and cannot be added via Gallery template.
solution: Expected behavior. These apps cannot be added from the gallery. If SSO integration is needed, contact the ISV to integrate with Entra ID using SAML, OIDC, or SCIM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Enterprise Apps filter "Assignment Required = Not required" in Azure Portal shows fewer apps than Excel export with same filter
rootCause: Portal requires ExplicitAccessGrantRequired to be explicitly set to false. If property is missing, app is excluded. Excel treats missing property as false.
solution: Use MS Graph API: GET /servicePrincipals?$filter=appRoleAssignmentRequired -ne true (includes SPs with missing property). Or export to Excel and filter there.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: SAML claim sourced from onPremisesUserPrincipalName is empty in SAML Response; ASC shows "No values found for ID onpremisesuserprincipalname"
rootCause: onPremisesUserPrincipalName is only projected into token context when tenant is configured for Alternate Login ID.
solution: If enabling Alternate Login ID is not acceptable, populate a supported extension attribute (e.g. extensionAttribute1-15) with the value and map the SAML claim to that extension attribute.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: App management policy for password restriction not working; passwordAdditionExempted Custom Security Attribute created with Boolean type instead of String via Portal UI
rootCause: Bug in Portal UI creating CSA with wrong data type (fixed in Oct 2025 Bug 3410200). Tenants affected before fix still have wrong type.
solution: Create a new CSA set and attribute with String type and include it in Default App Management policy through Graph API. May need PG assistance to change data type of existing CSA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Soft deleted multi-tenant application objects not automatically hard deleted after 30 days
rootCause: By design: applications with signInAudience set to AzureADMultipleOrgs/AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount/PersonalMicrosoftAccount are not auto hard-deleted since Nov 2018.
solution: Manually hard delete via PowerShell: Get-MgDirectoryDeletedItemAsApplication | ForEach-Object { Remove-MgDirectoryDeletedItem -DirectoryObjectId $_.Id }. Or change AvailableToOtherTenants to false in manifest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: SAML SSO Claims Transformation window does not appear when editing a claim that has a known transformation configured
rootCause: UI bug in SAML SSO claims configuration page (ICM 693586805)
solution: Select either the Attribute or Directory Extensions radio button first, then select Transformation. This will cause the transformation window to appear.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Identifier URI Protection Policy cannot be disabled via PowerShell script from Microsoft documentation; script reports success but policy not updated
rootCause: PowerShell script includes unsupported properties in request payload (audiences: null, federatedIdentityCredentials: null, trustedSubjectNameAndIssuers: null)
solution: Use Graph API query directly instead of the PowerShell script to disable the policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Microsoft Intune Windows Agent first-party app automatically disabled by Microsoft Online Services and re-disabled each time customer enables it
rootCause: FPA is not linked to subscription-based lifecycle flow, causing subscription validity checks to fail. Intune team confirmed app is not being used.
solution: Delete the service principal: Remove-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId <id>. App ID: fc0f3af4-6835-4174-b806-f7db311fd2f3.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Unable to assign groups to Enterprise Application - error "Groups are not available for assignment due to your Active Directory Plan level" despite P1/P2 license
rootCause: Bug in license check for group assignment in Enterprise Apps (Bug 3333754). No ETA for fix.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph API as workaround for group assignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: SAML NameID IfEmpty() claim transformation produces persistent identifier instead of fallback value when source attribute is empty
rootCause: Known PG bug: Parameter 3 is calculated first, then Transform is evaluated. IfEmpty returns false because it uses pre-calculated value. Bugs 3032054 and 1709723.
solution: Use IfNotEmpty() transformation instead of IfEmpty(). IfNotEmpty() works correctly for this scenario.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Unable to configure User Consent Settings with Privileged Role Administrator role using Entra Portal
rootCause: Portal UX uses checkAccess API hosted by Ibiza backend which requires Global Administrator (Bug 3076284)
solution: Use Global Administrator role, or use PowerShell/API options for consent settings configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: User.usertype claim returns "UserTypeCloudManaged" instead of expected "Member" or "Guest" values
rootCause: Known backend bug (Work Item 2838247)
solution: Use conditional claim issuance: emit "Member" if User Type is "Members", emit "Guest" if User Type is "All Guests". Note: B2B users converted to Member still get "Guest"; use altSecId to distinguish external vs internal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Users and Groups menu missing for Enterprise Application in Azure Portal
rootCause: Service Principal missing tag WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryIntegratedApp, or multiple tags configured as single comma-separated entity
solution: Add tag: Update-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId <id> -Tags "WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryIntegratedApp". For multiple tags, each must be a separate array element.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: AADSTS7500525 XML error in SAML message when identifierUris is non-URI format (e.g. "test123") configured via Graph API; IdP-initiated SSO fails
rootCause: Non-URI identifiers not supported as issuer URL for SAML IdP-initiated SSO
solution: Use actual URIs (https:// or api://) for identifierUris, or update Identifier via Entra ID Portal UI.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Failed to delete SAML application: "Property appId is invalid" or "identifierUris is invalid" or trailing slash / unverified domain errors
rootCause: App ID URI has trailing slash, uses unverified domain, or SAML app misconfigured as OIDC public client (isFallbackPublicClient: true)
solution: Fix App ID URI format (remove trailing slash, use verified domain). For public client: set Allow public client flow to Disabled in App Registrations > Authentication > Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: AADSTS50012 error and authentication loop in Edge when accessing applications; tenant has AlternateID enabled
rootCause: Bug in login flow when AlternateIdLogin is enabled and multiple user credentials present in request (Bug 2914950)
solution: Try different browser or clear Edge cookies/sessions. PG tracking the fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Unable to add OAuth scope longer than 40 characters in App Registration UI
rootCause: UI bug: actual API limit is 120 characters but portal enforces 40 (ADO 2904326)
solution: Use Graph API to create scope, or create via UI with <=40 chars then edit in Manifest view to desired length.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Approving admin consent request automatically adds the approving administrator to Users and Groups of the Enterprise App
rootCause: Intended product behavior confirmed by PM: during admin consent flow, consenting user is automatically assigned. Not configurable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Unable to create new Entra ID tenant - option greyed out: Customers must own a paid license to create Microsoft Entra Workforce tenant
rootCause: Starting Oct 2023, tenant creation from existing tenant requires non-trial paid M365 seat-based subscription. Azure subscriptions alone are not sufficient.
solution: 1) Verify via Graph: GET /beta/directory/subscriptions?$select=isTrial,status,skuId,commerceSubscriptionId. Need isTrial=False and commerceSubscriptionId!=skuId. 2) Create via https://azure.microsoft.com/free/. 3) Any non-trial M365 seat-based subscription qualifies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Portal tenant properties showing United States Datacenters instead of expected Canadian Datacenters
rootCause: By design - Microsoft does not offer in-country Entra ID data residency for Canadian customers. Entra ID is a non-regional service.
solution: Inform customer by design. M365 data move: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/moving-data-to-new-datacenter-geos. Entra ID residency: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/data-residency. Ref: ICM 303609655.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Domain force deletion fails - DomainForceDeleteTask Incomplete. DirectoryAuthorityException: ProxyAddresses may be updated only on behalf of Exchange
rootCause: Exchange mastered groups (ExchangeMastered=True) have proxy addresses referencing the domain. DomainForceDeleteTask cannot remove these.
solution: 1) DS Explorer PropagationTask for DomainForceDeleteTask. 2) Identify Exchange mastered groups, provide ObjectIds. 3) Customer removes proxy addresses. 4) ICM to EEE team to remove failed task. 5) Retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Cannot verify custom domain - already verified in another managed tenant
rootCause: Custom domain registered on different Entra ID tenant. Domain can only be verified on one tenant at a time.
solution: 1) OIDC endpoint: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{domain}/.well-known/openid-configuration. 2) ASC Tenant Explorer. 3) Contact Data Protection team. VKB: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2905700.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Cannot verify domain - on viral/unmanaged tenant from self-service signup (Power BI, RMS, PowerApps). IsViral=True.
rootCause: Domain auto-verified when user signed up for self-service service creating viral tenant. CompanyTags=EmailVerified.
solution: 1) User Realm: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/userrealm/{domain}?api-version=2.1 for IsViral=True. 2) ASC Company Profile. 3) Admin takeover: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/domains-admin-takeover
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Could not find the DNS record for this domain - error when verifying custom domain
rootCause: DNS TXT or MX record not created correctly, or DNS propagation not complete (up to 72 hours).
solution: 1) Resolve-DnsName -Name domain.com -Type TXT (or MX). 2) ASC Tenant Explorer > Domains > Registration tab. 3) Wait up to 72h. Ref: https://support.microsoft.com/help/2515404
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Error verifying subdomain with federated parent: One or more properties contains invalid values / AddDomain.domain.LiveType must follow the root domain
rootCause: Portal bug - subdomains inherit parent authenticationType. Portal cannot set child of federated parent as managed.
solution: PowerShell+Graph: 1) New-EntraDomain -Name child.mydomain.com 2) GET /v1.0/domains/child.mydomain.com 3) PATCH to promote root, set authenticationType=Managed. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/domains-verify-custom-subdomain
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Verifying a custom subdomain as managed domain

symptom: DirectoryQuotaExceededException: The directory object quota limit for the Tenant has been exceeded
rootCause: Default tenant object quota is 50k. Protects service from over-provisioning.
solution: 1) GET /beta/organization?$select=directorySizeQuota. 2) ASC > Tenant Explorer > Tenant Config > Object Quota. 3) ICM for quota increase. 4) Monitor: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/core-infrastructure-and-security/the-case-of-azure-ad-quota-exhaustion/ba-p/2496133
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra ID Object Quotas

symptom: Cannot verify domain - not in any tenant but fails. Orphaned domain in OrgId.
rootCause: Orphaned domain record in OrgId but not in MSODS. Or domain federated with Exchange Online.
solution: 1) ICC tool Namespace View. 2) If OrgId not MSODS: ICM to OrgId/Triage to purge. 3) Exchange: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/troubleshoot/administration/cant-verify-domain-error-when-verify-domain
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting Tenant and Domain

symptom: Outlook continually prompts for password when CAE is enabled
rootCause: Tenant has not configured IPv6 trusted locations. CAE-enabled clients connecting via IPv6 not matched to Named Location.
solution: Configure IPv6 trusted locations in CA Named Locations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Conditional Access Policy/Azure AD Continuous Access Evaluation

symptom: Re-enabled user unable to access mailbox after disable/re-enable
rootCause: CAE instant enforcement revoked session on disable. 35-45 min propagation delay.
solution: Wait 35-45 minutes after re-enabling. Expected CAE behavior.

symptom: CAE not enforcing CA policy on location change; CountryLookupMethod:null in Named Location JSON
rootCause: Sep 2020 build bug: CountryLookupMethod null in Named Locations edited Sep 1-5. CAE NuGet in EXO/SPO fails.
solution: Rename impacted Named Locations in Portal/Graph to fix JSON, then rename back.

symptom: EAS clients quarantined when CAE enabled
rootCause: EAS uses legacy auth, no session control support. CAE translates to block.
solution: Create CA policy with CAE session control Disabled, scoped to EAS users. Ref ICM 271404212.

symptom: Sign-in logs show Success when CAE strict enforcement blocks access

solution: Check non-interactive sign-in logs. Look for SLEv2 in CaAdhoc field.

symptom: IPv6 clients incorrectly have CA location policy applied despite excluded country
rootCause: Named Locations has no IPv6 geolocation. IPv6 falls into unknown areas if checked.
solution: Uncheck Include unknown areas, or add IPv6 as IP-based Named Locations.
