rootCause: Token version mismatch: using a V2 token with the V1 UserInfo endpoint (login.microsoftonline.com/common/openid/userinfo) or vice versa. V1 and V2 endpoints issue tokens with different audiences and cannot be used interchangeably.
solution: Use the correct endpoint version: V2 tokens -> https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo; V1 tokens -> https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/openid/userinfo. Check the openid-configuration metadata (/.well-known/openid-configuration) to confirm the correct userinfo_endpoint value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Retrieving user information from Entra ID UserInfo Endpoints

symptom: UserInfo endpoint call returns AADSTS9002313: 'Invalid request. Request is malformed or invalid'
rootCause: Incorrect Authorization header format: double space between 'Bearer' and token, missing space, or incorrect header name
solution: Ensure Authorization header is exactly: 'Authorization: Bearer {token}' with a single space between 'Bearer' and the token value. Verify the header name is spelled correctly as 'Authorization'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Retrieving user information from Entra ID UserInfo Endpoints

symptom: Client Credential flow using certificate fails with authentication error - script cannot find or authenticate with the certificate
rootCause: Certificate thumbprint in the script does not match the actual thumbprint of the certificate in the certificate store
solution: Run 'Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\CurrentUser\My\' to list all certificates with thumbprints and verify exact match. Use: Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\CurrentUser\My\ | Where-Object -Property Thumbprint -EQ '{thumbprint}'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate fails - token request rejected with invalid_client or unauthorized_client error
rootCause: Client ID (Application ID) in the script does not match the application registration that has the certificate uploaded in Entra ID
solution: In Azure portal -> App registrations, locate the app associated with the certificate and copy the correct Application (client) ID. Verify the certificate thumbprint is uploaded under that app's Certificates & secrets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate fails - token endpoint returns error about tenant not found or invalid tenant
rootCause: Tenant ID in the script does not match the tenant where the client application is registered
solution: In Azure portal -> App registrations, find the app and verify the Directory (tenant) ID in the Overview blade. Use the correct tenant ID or tenant domain (yourtenant.onmicrosoft.com) in the token endpoint URL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate fails - POST to token endpoint returns 404 or endpoint not found
rootCause: Token endpoint URL is incorrect or uses wrong API version path
solution: Use the correct token endpoint format: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token (v2.0 endpoint). For v1.0: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/token
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate fails - Get-Item Cert:\CurrentUser\My\{thumbprint} returns nothing or certificate not found
rootCause: Certificate (with private key) is not imported to the CurrentUser\My certificate store on the machine executing the script
solution: Import the certificate .pfx file to the CurrentUser\My store via certmgr.msc or PowerShell. Alternatively, use Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK v2 which supports both CurrentUser and LocalMachine certificate stores: Connect-MgGraph -ClientId '{clientId}' -TenantId '{tenant}' -CertificateThumbprint '{thumbprint}'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate - client_assertion JWT is rejected by Entra ID token endpoint with invalid signature or invalid_client error
rootCause: JWT client_assertion is incorrectly constructed: not signed with the certificate private key, wrong x5t header value (should be base64url-encoded SHA1 cert hash), or wrong signing algorithm
solution: Ensure the JWT: (1) uses RS256 algorithm in header, (2) x5t header = base64url(SHA1(cert)) replacing +->- /->_ removing =, (3) signed using RSA private key with PKCS1 padding + SHA256. Decode the JWT at jwt.ms to verify header and payload. The aud claim in payload must be the token endpoint URL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client Credential flow with certificate fails with AADSTS70011 or certificate error - previously working flow suddenly fails
rootCause: Certificate used for client authentication has expired or been revoked
solution: Verify certificate validity: run 'certutil -verify -urlfetch {certificatename}' or 'certutil -URL {certificatename}'. If expired or revoked, create a new self-signed certificate (New-SelfSignedCertificate in PowerShell), upload the new public key (.cer) to Entra ID app registration under Certificates & secrets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: User unexpectedly prompted for password when accessing SAML application despite having an active pre-authenticated Entra ID session (SSO bypassed, no specific error shown)
rootCause: SAML AuthnRequest from the SP application contains ForceAuthn="true" parameter, instructing Entra ID to bypass any existing session and force re-authentication regardless of SSO state
solution: 1. Confirm ForceAuthn=true using ASC Advanced Troubleshooting on sign-in logs, or capture SAML request with Browser trace / SAML-tracer / Fiddler. 2. Advise customer to work with their SP application team or third-party vendor to disable ForceAuthn (set to false or remove the parameter from SAML AuthnRequest). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/single-sign-on-saml-protocol
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Understanding The ForceAuthn Parameter In A SAML Request

symptom: AADSTS error when using Insomnia to retrieve OAuth2.0 token - error indicates grant_type parameter is missing or not recognized in the request
rootCause: Insomnia request body is not set to 'Form Data' format; when body type is set to JSON or raw, parameters including grant_type are not sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded which is required by OAuth2 token endpoints
solution: In Insomnia, on the request body tab, click the format dropdown and change selection to 'Form Data'. This ensures the token request body is sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded as required by Entra ID token endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - how to use Insomnia application to test OAuth2.0 Authentication flows

symptom: Azure portal Test SAML SSO wizard shows misleading error for AWS application when customer has multiple AWS application instances configured per Microsoft documentation
rootCause: Known product issue with Azure portal SAML test button for multi-instance AWS app configurations; test wizard shows incorrect error despite SAML SSO working correctly for end users
solution: Known issue (logged 2021-08-20, PG aware, no ETA). Workaround: validate SSO via actual end-user sign-in rather than portal test button. The SAML SSO functions correctly - only the portal test wizard is affected by this bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - using Test SAML settings button in Azure Portal

symptom: Portal displays 'please use MS Graph to edit your claims' and claims are not shown when accessing an app whose claims were configured via Graph API; or vice versa, Graph API cannot read claims configured via Portal UX
rootCause: Two incompatible claim policy types exist: Claims Issuance Policy (Type 2, created via Portal UX) and Claims Mapping Policy (Type 14, created via Graph API). They use different schemas and do not interoperate.
solution: Use the same method (Portal or Graph API) that was originally used to create the policy. Alternatively, recreate claims using the new Claims Policy API (beta: GET/PUT/PATCH /servicePrincipals/{id}/claimsPolicy) which wraps Type 2 and works interchangeably with Portal. Previously created Type 14 policies must still be managed with the old API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Entra ID Claims API

symptom: Azure Storage fails to decrypt content with Customer Managed Key (CMK). Error code 403 ('This request is not authorized to perform this operation using this permission') or 500 ('Server encountered an internal error') when accessing cross-tenant CMK-encrypted storage blobs
rootCause: The multi-tenant application, its Federated Identity Credential (FIC), or the User Assigned Managed Identity assigned to the FIC was soft-deleted or hard-deleted, breaking the cross-tenant CMK trust chain. Access may continue working for ~2.5 days after deletion before failing.
solution: Check if the multi-tenant app, FIC, or UAMI was deleted. For soft-deleted apps, restore within 30 days via Entra ID. For hard-deleted apps or deleted FICs/UAMIs, re-create the multi-tenant app, configure a new FIC, and re-establish the trust relationship. Verify by checking Sign-in logs for Service Principal sign-in failures and checking if the Federated credential ID field is populated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Cross Tenant Access Using Managed Identity with Federated Identity Credentials

symptom: AADSTS7000226: No federated identity credential policy found on application ({appid}). The client_assertion used to authenticate the request does not match the subject or application being requested.
rootCause: The application ID in the cross-tenant CMK request does not have a federated credentials policy configured, or the client_assertion does not match the configured FIC subject/application.
solution: Ensure the application has a Federated Identity Credential (FIC) policy applied. Verify: 1) The application ID in the request is correct, 2) The FIC is properly configured with correct issuer/subject/audience, 3) The correct client_assertion is being provided. Check the multi-tenant app registration's Certificates & Secrets blade for FIC configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Cross Tenant Access Using Managed Identity with Federated Identity Credentials

symptom: AADSTS700213: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion subject '{subject}'. Please note that the matching is done using case-sensitive comparison.
rootCause: Breaking change deployed Aug 14, 2024: Entra ID now enforces case-sensitive matching for FIC subject claims (previously case-insensitive). Phase 1 blocks new FICs; Phase 2 (Sept 27, 2024) blocks inactive apps. Apps with active case-insensitive matching are allow-listed temporarily.
solution: 1) Filter Service principal/Managed identity sign-ins for Failure events with error code 700213. 2) Navigate to the app's Certificates & secrets > Federated credentials blade. 3) Check the external IdP activity logs for the actual claim value sent. 4) Update the FIC Subject Identifier to match the exact case sent by the external IdP. Use ASC Authentication Diagnostics (Expert view > Diagnostic logs > search 'AADSTS') to view the actual unmasked claim. If FIC cannot be found, use 'List All Applications and their FICs' script to dump all FICs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Federated Identity Credentials

symptom: AADSTS7002131: No matching federated identity record found for presented assertion subject '{subject}' or no federated identity credential expression matched. GitHub Actions or other CI/CD workflow fails authentication with Flexible FIC.
rootCause: The claimsMatchingExpression defined on the Flexible FIC does not match the claims presented by the external identity provider. Common causes: subject claim does not match the wildcard/pattern, job_workflow_ref claim does not satisfy the expression (e.g., workflow is not reusable), or expression syntax is incorrect.
solution: 1) Use ASC Authentication Diagnostics to view the actual subject claim presented (Expert view > Diagnostic logs > search AADSTS). 2) Compare with the claimsMatchingExpression on the FIC (use Graph API: GET /applications/{id}/federatedIdentityCredentials). 3) Verify job_workflow_ref matches if using reusable workflows. 4) Ensure the subject follows the expected format for the issuer (GitHub: repo:{org}/{repo}:{entity}, GitLab: project_path:{org}/{proj}:ref_type:{type}:ref:{name}).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS7002241: Issuer '{issuer}' may not use credentials matching via expression (FederatedIdentityFflBlockedIssuer).
rootCause: The identity provider issuer used in the FIC is not in the supported list for Flexible FIC. Only specific issuers for GitHub, GitLab, and Terraform Cloud are supported for expression-based matching.
solution: Verify the issuer is one of the supported issuers: GitHub (https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com), GitLab (https://gitlab.com, https://gitlab.{example}.com, https://gitlab.{example}.ca), Terraform Cloud (https://app.terraform.io, https://app.eu.terraform.io). For unsupported issuers, use standard FIC with explicit subject matching instead of Flexible FIC expressions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS700235: The federated identity credentials flow does not support credentials matching via expression at this time (FederatedIdentityFlexibleFicUnsupported).
rootCause: Flexible FIC is being used on a resource type that does not yet support it. Initially, Flexible FIC is only supported for FICs configured on Application objects (App Registrations), not on Managed Identities.
solution: Use Flexible FIC only on Application objects (App Registrations). For Managed Identities, use standard FIC with explicit subject matching. Flexible FIC support for Managed Identities is planned for CY25Q1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS700239: Claims matching expressions are not supported in this cloud at this time (FederatedIdentityFlexibleFicRestrictedCloud).
rootCause: Flexible FIC (expression-based matching) is not available in certain sovereign/national cloud environments such as Mooncake (China Cloud) or government clouds.
solution: Use standard FIC with explicit subject matching instead of Flexible FIC expressions in unsupported cloud environments. Wait for Flexible FIC support to be enabled in the target cloud.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS700237: Federated Identity Credential expression not allowed (FederatedIdentityCredentialExpressionNotAllowed). Detailed error provided in message.
rootCause: The expression used in the Flexible FIC contains unsupported operators or claims for the given issuer. Each issuer has specific claim/operator restrictions: GitHub supports eq/startswith/matches on 'sub' and eq/matches on 'job_workflow_ref'; GitLab supports startswith/matches on 'sub'; Terraform Cloud supports startswith/matches on 'sub'.
solution: Review the supported operators per issuer and adjust the expression. Ensure only supported claims and operators are used for the specific issuer. The 'and' operator is universally supported. Refer to the Flexible FIC documentation for the full operator/issuer compatibility matrix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS700233: Federated Identity Credential expression parsing failed (FederatedIdentityCredentialInvalidExpression). Detailed error provided in message.
rootCause: The claimsMatchingExpression syntax is invalid and cannot be parsed. Common issues: missing/incorrect single quotes, wrong claim lookup format, unsupported operator name, or malformed boolean expression.
solution: Verify expression follows the pattern: claims['claimName'] operator 'comparand'. Supported operators: eq, startswith, matches, in, has, and. Ensure single quotes around string values. For wildcards use '*' (multi-char) and '?' (single-char) with 'matches' operator. Use 'and' to combine multiple conditions. Set languageVersion to 1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: AADSTS700234: Federated Identity Credential expression evaluation failed (FederatedIdentityCredentialExpressionEvaluationException). Detailed error provided in message.
rootCause: The claimsMatchingExpression parsed successfully but failed during runtime evaluation, typically due to type mismatch between the claim value in the incoming token and the comparand in the expression.
solution: Verify that claim types match the expected types for the operator being used. Check that the external IdP is sending claims in the expected format. Supported types: Boolean, Integer, String, Arrays of string/integer. Type checking happens at runtime since claim value types are unknown until a token is presented.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Flexible FIC

symptom: Managed Identity from one cloud (e.g., Public) fails to authenticate as FIC for an Entra ID application in a different cloud (e.g., Mooncake/China Cloud). Token exchange fails silently or with authentication error.
rootCause: Managed Identities from one cloud cannot be federated into Entra ID applications in a different cloud. Cross-cloud federation is architecturally unsupported. This includes Public↔Mooncake, Public↔Fairfax, etc.
solution: Ensure the Managed Identity and the Entra ID application are in the same cloud environment. Use cloud-specific audience values: Public=api://AzureADTokenExchange, Mooncake=api://AzureADTokenExchangeChina, Fairfax=api://AzureADTokenExchangeUSGov, USNat=api://AzureADTokenExchangeUSNat, USSec=api://AzureADTokenExchangeUSSec. Only User-Assigned Managed Identities are supported as FICs (not System-Assigned).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Managed Identities as FICs

symptom: Creating multiple federated identity credentials concurrently on the same user-assigned managed identity returns success (200 OK) for each API call, but some credentials may not be created or may have unexpected configuration.
rootCause: Concurrent API calls to create FICs under the same UAMI are not properly serialized, leading to race conditions where some operations may overwrite or fail to persist despite returning success responses.
solution: Create federated identity credentials sequentially (one at a time) rather than concurrently. Wait for each creation request to complete before sending the next. Verify all expected FICs exist after batch creation by listing them (GET /identities/{id}/federatedIdentityCredentials). Contact Microsoft support if credentials are missing after sequential creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Federated Identity Credentials (FIC)/How to/Workload Identity Federation with User Assigned Managed Identities

symptom: AADSTS50146 error when using Claims Mapping Policy: 'This application is required to be configured with an application-specific signing key'
rootCause: The acceptMappedClaims attribute in the application Manifest is not set to true. When a Claims Mapping Policy is assigned to a Service Principal, the app must either have acceptMappedClaims=true or use a custom signing key.
solution: Set acceptMappedClaims to TRUE in the application Manifest. Alternatively, configure a custom signing key using PowerShell. Note: Claims Mapping Policy must be assigned to the Service Principal (not the Application object) using New-MgServicePrincipalClaimMappingPolicyByRef.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Add custom claims to JWT_SAML tokens using custom claims mapping

symptom: Group name claims (SAMAccountName/NetbiosDomain/DNSDomain format) not appearing in SAML/JWT tokens for on-premises synced groups after migrating from ADFS
rootCause: Group name claims require proper configuration and only work for groups synced from on-premises AD. Cloud-only groups show object IDs only.
solution: For SAML apps: Configure via Enterprise Apps > SSO > Add a group claim > select Source attribute format. For JWT apps: Configure via App Registration > Token Configuration > Add Groups claim. Ensure groups are synced from on-premises AD. Group membership evaluation is transitive.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Group Name Claims for Synced Groups

symptom: Groups assigned to an application via App Registration are not appearing in token group claims even with GroupMembershipClaims set to ApplicationGroup
rootCause: Apps created via App Registration with no custom roles assign groups with 'default access' role, which is NOT used for adding groups to the token. Apps created via Enterprise Apps have 'user' role that works correctly.
solution: Create a custom app role for the application and assign the group using that role instead of 'default access'. Set GroupMembershipClaims to 'ApplicationGroup' in the manifest. Requires Azure AD P1 license.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Limiting Group Claims for Enterprise Apps

symptom: Customer wants to configure IsManaged, IsKnown, or IsCompliant optional claims in SAML tokens for third-party applications
rootCause: IsManaged/IsKnown/IsCompliant are restricted claims not supported for 3rd party apps. They bypass Conditional Access premium licensing. PG will not block the unsupported method to avoid breaking changes.
solution: Microsoft does not support these claims for 3rd party apps. Do NOT escalate to PG if unsupported method fails. Key: isknown only issued when device is Hybrid AAD Joined; no conditions where isknown is false; AAD registered+MDM enrolled gets only ismanaged+iscompliant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Optional Claims

symptom: family_name and given_name optional claims not appearing in ID tokens when using the v2 endpoint
rootCause: For v2 endpoint, profile scope must be included in the request along with openid for these claims to be issued.
solution: Add profile to the scope parameter: scope=openid+profile. Known behavior specific to v2 endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Optional Claims

symptom: Optional claims configured on the application are not appearing in access tokens but appear correctly in ID tokens
rootCause: Access tokens are generated using the manifest of the resource application, not the client. Developers configure claims on client app manifest expecting them in access tokens for other resources.
solution: Configure optional claims on the resource application manifest (the API the token is for), not the client. To validate, request an access token for your own app as the resource. MS Graph tokens cannot have customized optional claims.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Optional Claims

symptom: Application breaks after July 2024 parsing ipaddr claim from tokens. IPv6+IPv4 format changed from mixed notation (YYY.YYY.YYY.YYY) to pure hex (xxxx:xxxx)
rootCause: Breaking change MC798672: IPv6 addresses with embedded IPv4 now serialized in pure hexadecimal format. Apps comparing ipaddr as string fail.
solution: Parse ipaddr using IPAddress.Parse() instead of string comparison. Do not depend on string format. Change does not impact Conditional Access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Breaking Change The 'ipaddr' claim format in Access and ID tokens will change for IPv6 addresses with embedded IPv4 addresse

symptom: AADSTS1100001 or AADSTS1100000 error during sign-in to an application with custom claims provider (custom authentication extension)
rootCause: Custom claims provider API endpoint error or misconfiguration. Error codes 1003001/1003000 in sign-in logs indicate internal Azure error (requires CRI).
solution: Check sign-in logs > Authentication events tab. If 1003001/1003000: file CRI under CPIM > CRI-Triage. Otherwise follow custom-extension-troubleshoot docs. Customer should check API hosting logs and correlate via correlationId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Custom claims provider for Entra ID tokens

symptom: AADSTS50146 error 'This application is required to be configured with an application-specific signing key' when using Claims Mapping Policy to add custom claims to JWT/SAML tokens
rootCause: The 'acceptMappedClaims' attribute in the application manifest is not set to true. When a Claims Mapping Policy is assigned to a service principal, the app must either accept mapped claims or use a custom signing key.
solution: Set 'acceptMappedClaims' to TRUE in the application manifest (App Registrations > Manifest). Alternatively, configure a custom signing key using PowerShell per docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/jwt-claims-customization#configure-a-custom-signing-key-using-powershell
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Add custom claims to JWT_SAML tokens using custom claims mapping

symptom: Group claims missing or incomplete in tokens obtained via OAuth2 implicit flow - only 5 groups appear instead of all user group memberships
rootCause: Implicit flow has a 5-group limit for group claims. If user is member of more than 5 groups, a groups overage claim (_claim_sources/_claim_names) is emitted instead of the full group list.
solution: Switch from implicit flow to authorization code flow, or use the overage indicator (_claim_sources) to call Microsoft Graph API to retrieve the user's full group membership list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Group Name Claims for Synced Groups

symptom: Group name claims (sAMAccountName format) not working for cloud-only groups or cloud-only users - only ObjectID format is emitted
rootCause: Group name claims in sAMAccountName/NetbiosDomain/DNSDomain format only work for groups synced from on-premises Active Directory. Cloud-only groups do not have these attributes and cannot emit group names in these formats.
solution: For cloud-only groups, use group ObjectID format in claims, or add 'cloud_displayname' to additional properties (works only when groupMembershipClaims is set to ApplicationGroup). For on-premises groups, ensure they are synced via AD Connect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Group Name Claims for Synced Groups

symptom: Groups assigned to application not appearing in token claims when using Application Registration experience with groupMembershipClaims set to 'ApplicationGroup' - groups assigned with 'default access' role are excluded
rootCause: When an application is created via App Registration (not Enterprise Applications/Gallery), it has no custom app roles. Groups assigned get the 'default access' role, which is not used for adding groups to the token. Apps created through Enterprise Applications or Gallery get a 'user' role by default which works correctly.
solution: Create a custom app role for the application (App Registrations > App roles), then re-assign the groups using that custom role. The groups will then appear in the token claims.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Azure AD Limiting Group Claims for Enterprise Apps

symptom: Application fails after July 8, 2024 breaking change - ipaddr claim in access/ID tokens changed format for IPv6 addresses with embedded IPv4 (e.g. '2001:558:1416:0:200:5efe:169.152.178.93' became '2001:558:1416:0:200:5efe:a998:b25d'). Apps using string comparison on ipaddr claim break.
rootCause: .NET upgrade in ESTS changed serialization of IPv6 addresses containing embedded IPv4 addresses (format xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:xxxx:200:5efe:xxxx:xxxx). The last two groups changed from decimal IPv4 notation (YYY.YYY.YYY.YYY) to hexadecimal IPv6 notation. No tenant allowlisting available.
solution: Parse the ipaddr claim into an IPAddress object instead of using string comparison: 'IPAddress address = IPAddress.Parse(ipAddress)'. See MC798672 message center announcement. Applications should never take dependency on string format of IP addresses.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Breaking Change The 'ipaddr' claim format in Access and ID tokens will change for IPv6 addresses with embedded IPv4 addresse

symptom: AADSTS1100001 or AADSTS1100000 error during sign-in when custom claims provider (custom authentication extension) is configured - indicates issue with the external API callout for token enrichment
rootCause: The custom claims provider API endpoint is returning errors, timing out, or returning unexpected response format. Error codes 1003001/1003000 indicate internal ESTS errors (requires CRI). The customer's external API must return proper response within timeout.
solution: 1) Check Sign-in logs > 'Authentication events' tab for the client application to see detailed error codes. 2) Review customer's API hosting service logs (e.g. App Insights in Azure Functions) correlated by data.authenticationContext.correlationId. 3) Follow troubleshooting guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/custom-extension-troubleshoot. 4) For error codes 1003001/1003000, file CRI to CPIM > CRI-Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Claims_and_Token_Customization/How to/Custom claims provider for Entra ID tokens

symptom: Microsoft Graph permission GUIDs differ between commercial and government cloud tenants, causing permission mismatch errors when configuring applications across clouds (e.g., TeamworkTag.ReadWrite.All has different GUIDs in Commercial vs USGov)
rootCause: The MS Graph GUIDs were assigned at random during onboarding for each cloud, and there were no requirements that they be the same between clouds. Most MS Graph GUIDs won't match between clouds as a result.
solution: Use Connect-MgGraph -Environment USGov (or -Environment China for Mooncake) to connect to the target government cloud, then run Find-MgGraphPermission to list the correct permission GUIDs for that environment. Replace any hardcoded commercial-cloud GUIDs with the government-cloud equivalents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Microsoft Graph permissions reference for Government tenants

symptom: New app registrations created after March 1, 2021 do not appear in My Apps portal for end users, even though the app is properly configured
rootCause: Starting March 1, 2021, new App Registrations are hidden by default in My Apps. The 'Visible to users' property on the enterprise application is set to No by default.
solution: Navigate to Microsoft Entra admin center > Enterprise applications, find the application, go to Properties, and set 'Visible to users?' to Yes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: Applications in a My Apps Collection differ between team members - some users see all apps in the collection while others see only a subset
rootCause: Individual users were assigned directly to applications instead of through groups, or users are assigned via nested group membership which Collections do not support for visibility. Collections only organize apps visually; they do not grant permissions.
solution: Assign users to a group and grant that group direct access to the applications. Verify the user has been assigned any necessary licenses. Note: nested group membership is not supported for application visibility in Collections.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: Collection Owners don't see the Collections blade under Enterprise Apps when they sign into the Azure portal
rootCause: The Collection owner account has Guest membership in another tenant and they signed into the Azure portal of that guest tenant instead of their home tenant.
solution: Perform a 'Switch user' or 'Switch directory' in the Azure portal to change back to the owner's home tenant where the Collections are configured.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: End users can no longer add Password SSO Apps in My Apps portal; the 'Add apps' option for password SSO is missing
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated the ability for end users to add Password SSO Apps in My Apps effective November 14, 2023. The admin control under Enterprise applications > User settings has also been removed.
solution: Administrators must add password SSO apps for end users through the Microsoft Entra admin center (Enterprise applications > configure password-based single sign-on). Previously user-added apps continue to work and are not removed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: User encounters failures accessing myapplications.microsoft.com. AppManagement API calls fail with AADSTS50076: 'Due to a configuration change made by your administrator, you must use multi-factor authentication to access' the target application
rootCause: A Conditional Access policy targeting the Office 365 app group enforces MFA, but the token issued during initial sign-in was not evaluated against that CA policy. The AppManagement API call inherits the original authentication context which does not satisfy the MFA requirement.
solution: Review Conditional Access policies targeting the Office 365 app group. Ensure the user satisfies MFA requirements when signing into My Apps. Use Jarvis MyApps namespace tracing to diagnose: filter by user UPN, look for Error entries, trace Partner-request-id through AppManagement namespace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: Certificate-based authentication (CBA) fails with error code 500189 when CA scoping policy is configured. End users see 'Your account or session could not be verified' error.
rootCause: The user's certificate issuing CA is scoped to a security group via CA Scoping policy, but the authenticating user is not a member of that group. Entra walks up the entire CA chain and applies all scope rules; if the user is not found in any of the scoped groups, authentication is denied (error 500189) even if the certificate is otherwise valid.
solution: 1) Check sign-in logs for error code 500189 in Basic info tab. 2) Verify the user is a member of the group assigned to the CA scoping rule. 3) Review CA scoping rules in Microsoft Entra admin center > Authentication methods > Certificate-based Authentication > Certificate issuer scoping policy. 4) SKI of the blocking CA is shown in the Additional Details tab of sign-in logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA CA Scoping Scoping groups to certificate authorities

symptom: CBA CA Scoping policy configuration fails or produces unexpected results due to design limitations: only one group per CA, maximum 30 scoping rules, scoping enforced at intermediate CA level only
rootCause: Design limitation of the CA Scoping feature in Microsoft Entra CBA: (1) Only one security group can be assigned per Certificate Authority, (2) Maximum of 30 scoping rules is supported, (3) Scoping is enforced at intermediate CA level, not at root or leaf CA level. Improper configuration may result in user lockouts.
solution: Consolidate users into a single group per CA. Plan CA scoping rules within the 30-rule limit. For large organizations with multiple user populations per CA, use nested groups to consolidate into one group. Test thoroughly in a non-production environment before applying to avoid user lockouts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA CA Scoping Scoping groups to certificate authorities

symptom: Intermittent HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) or timeouts when calling Microsoft Entra endpoints (e.g., /discovery/v2.0/keys), especially during bursts like deployments or nightly jobs
rootCause: Tenant or application exceeds Entra request rate limits, triggering throttling. Common causes: not caching JWKS keys, no exponential backoff, synchronized bursts across instances
solution: 1) Cache JWKS (OpenID Connect signing keys) for 5-10 minutes, do not fetch per request. 2) Implement exponential backoff on 429 responses, honoring Retry-After header. 3) Stagger app restarts, key refresh, and batch jobs. 4) Reduce concurrency to confirm rate-related failures. If 429s persist >15 min across regions despite mitigations, escalate via IcM to AAD Gateway or ESTS team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Microsoft Entra Throttling � Support Engineer Playbook

symptom: TCP RST (connection reset) after ~120 seconds idle on connections to Entra endpoints, causing intermittent failures confused with throttling
rootCause: Azure/Entra platform closes HTTP connections idle for approximately 2 minutes (expected server behavior). Not throttling but idle connection cleanup.
solution: Avoid leaving HTTP connections idle for ~2 minutes. Reuse HTTP client connection pools correctly. If no HTTP 429 evidence, investigate caching/retry and network path (proxy/firewall/TLS interception) before assuming throttling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Microsoft Entra Throttling � Support Engineer Playbook

symptom: Microsoft Graph permission GUIDs (e.g., TeamworkTag.ReadWrite.All) differ between commercial and government (USGov/GCC) clouds, causing permission configuration failures when using hardcoded GUIDs
rootCause: MS Graph GUIDs were assigned randomly during onboarding for each cloud. No requirement for consistency between clouds, so most GUIDs do not match.
solution: Use PowerShell to query correct GUIDs from target government tenant: Connect-MgGraph -Environment USGov, then Get-MgServicePrincipal to list Graph permissions with correct GUIDs. Do not use commercial cloud documentation GUIDs for government tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Microsoft Graph permissions reference for Government tenants

symptom: Applications in a My Apps Collection differ between team members: some users see all apps while others see an incomplete list
rootCause: Users assigned individually to applications may see different subsets. Nested group membership is not expanded for Collections, so apps assigned via nested groups do not appear.
solution: 1) Assign users to a group and grant that group access to applications (instead of individual assignment). 2) Verify user has required licenses. 3) Note: Collections do not expand nested groups - use direct group membership.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: Collection Owners cannot see the Collections blade under Enterprise Apps in Azure portal
rootCause: The Collection owner account has Guest membership in another tenant and signed into the Azure portal of that (guest) tenant instead of their home tenant.
solution: Perform Switch user / switch directory to change back to the home tenant where the owner account has full access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: End users can no longer add Password SSO apps in My Apps after November 14, 2023 deprecation
rootCause: Microsoft deprecated end user self-service addition of Password SSO Apps in My Apps. Admin setting for user-added password SSO is removed from Entra portal.
solution: Admins must add password SSO apps for end users via Microsoft Entra admin center. Previously user-added password SSO apps continue to work but no new ones can be added by end users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/MyApps/How to/Azure AD My Apps Refresh and Collections

symptom: CBA (Certificate-Based Authentication) fails with error code 500189 when user authenticates with X.509 certificate
rootCause: CA (Certificate Authority) scoping rule blocks authentication: the certificate issuing CA is scoped to a specific Entra group but the authenticating user is not a member of that group.
solution: 1) Check CA scoping rules in Entra admin center > Authentication methods > Certificate-Based Authentication > Certificate issuer scoping policy. 2) Verify the user is member of the group associated with the CA scoping rule. 3) Add user to the correct scoped group. Known limits: only 1 group per CA, max 30 scoping rules.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA CA Scoping Scoping groups to certificate authorities

symptom: CBA CA Scoping misconfiguration causes user lockouts: improper scoping rules block all certificate-based authentication for affected users
rootCause: CA Scoping enforces at intermediate CA level with strict rules: only one group per CA, max 30 scoping rules. If no valid scoping rules exist for a user certificate chain, authentication fails.
solution: Review all CA scoping rules before enabling. Ensure each affected user belongs to at least one scoped group in the certificate chain. Test with a pilot group first. Max 1 group per CA, max 30 rules, scoping at intermediate CA level only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Cert Based Auth/CBA CA Scoping Scoping groups to certificate authorities

symptom: User is prompted for password instead of Temporary Access Pass (TAP) passcode after admin configured TAP
rootCause: TAP policy not enabled for user, TAP not yet active (up to 10 min activation delay), TAP expired or already used (one-time), or policy changed from multiuse to one-time after TAP was issued
solution: Verify: 1) TAP policy is enabled and user is assigned, 2) TAP has not expired, 3) For one-time TAP check audit logs for AccessPassUsage=3 (Used), 4) If policy changed, delete old TAP and issue new one
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: Administrator cannot modify One-time use toggle when creating TAP credential for a user
rootCause: TAP policy-level One-time use setting is set to Yes, which overrides per-user configuration
solution: Change the One-time use setting on the TAP Policy to No to allow per-user configuration. Note: this affects all TAP credential creation tenant-wide
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP activation duration cannot be raised or lowered when creating credential in Azure portal
rootCause: TAP policy minimumLifetimeInMinutes/maximumLifetimeInMinutes constraints prevent the desired value
solution: To raise: increase maximumLifetimeInMinutes in TAP policy. To lower: decrease minimumLifetimeInMinutes below defaultLifetimeInMinutes in TAP policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP not immediately available as sign-in option, up to 10 minute delay after creation
rootCause: Activation delay during Private Preview; replication latency for TAP credential propagation
solution: Wait up to 10 minutes after TAP creation. This delay was addressed in Public Preview and should be minimal in production
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: Error 'Temporary Access Pass cannot be added to an external guest user' when trying to assign TAP
rootCause: TAP is not supported for external guest users (users federated or invited from other Entra ID tenants). Only members and internal guests are supported
solution: TAP can only be issued to members and internally managed guests. For external guest users, alternative authentication methods must be used
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP sign-in fails for SSPR-enabled users who are redirected to SSPR registration interrupt flow
rootCause: SSPR registration is required before accessing Security Info. FIDO2 and Phone sign-in are not supported SSPR authentication methods
solution: User must first register SSPR-required authentication methods (e.g., email) before navigating to My Security Info to register passwordless credentials
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: MFA required message appears when registering FIDO key using TAP, but registration resumes after page refresh
rootCause: Session timeout occurs if FIDO registration takes more than 10 minutes after TAP sign-in
solution: Refresh the page and resume FIDO key registration. Complete registration within 10 minutes of sign-in
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP sign-in blocked with error 'Temporary Access Pass sign in was blocked due to User Credential Policy'
rootCause: Admin changed TAP policy from multiuse to one-time use after the user's multiuse TAP was already issued
solution: Delete the existing TAP and issue a new one-time use TAP to the user that complies with the current policy

symptom: Windows Web Sign-in (lock/unlock screen) only works with TAP, cannot use other credentials
rootCause: Web Sign-in is a private preview feature restricted to TAP for bootstrap and recovery scenarios (Autopilot)
solution: For federated customers using Autopilot: if federation provider supports ws-trust/ws-fed (ADFS, Ping, Okta), regular credential sign-in works. For other IdPs, TAP is required
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: Error 'Temporary Access Pass cannot be added to an external guest user' when trying to add TAP to a guest account; admin expects TAP to work for all guest types
rootCause: TAP is supported for internal guest accounts but NOT for external guest accounts. Admin may confuse internal vs external guest types
solution: Verify account type in ASC: Internal Guest has Sources=MOERA domain only, CreationType=N/A, UserType=Guest, ExternalUserState=N/A. External Guest has Sources from other tenants, CreationType=Invitation. TAP only works for Internal Guests

rootCause: No Windows Hello container (PIN) exists on the device. WebAuthn call fails to find a Windows Hello container and falls back to enumerating USB security keys

rootCause: Windows Hello AAGUIDs not added to the Passkey (FIDO2) authentication method policy, or user not in assigned group
solution: Add Windows Hello AAGUIDs to FIDO2 policy: 08987058-cadc-4b81-b6e1-30de50dcbe96 (hardware TPM), 9ddd1817-af5a-4672-a2b9-3e3dd95000a9 (VBS), 6028b017-b1d4-4c02-b4b3-afcdafc96bb2 (software TPM). Ensure user is in assigned group

rootCause: Older Windows Hyper-V guest VM or host does not meet minimum build requirements for passkey support
