symptom: Passkey in Microsoft Authenticator not listed as registration option in Security Info dropdown
rootCause: Microsoft Authenticator AAGUIDs not added to Passkey (FIDO2) authentication method policy
solution: Add Authenticator AAGUIDs: 90a3ccdf-635c-4729-a248-9b709135078f (iOS), de1e552d-db1d-4423-a619-566b625cdc84 (Android)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey authentication fails with 'Your company policy requires a different method'; sign-in error code 135016 'FIDO sign-in is disabled via policy'
rootCause: AAGUID for user's version of Microsoft Authenticator no longer listed in Passkey (FIDO2) authentication method policy
solution: Add correct AAGUID for user's Authenticator to FIDO2 policy: iOS=90a3ccdf, Android=de1e552d
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: 'There are no matching passkeys saved in Authenticator for login.microsoft.com' during passkey auth; authenticationMethod=null in sign-in logs
rootCause: Passkey deleted from user Entra ID account but remains in Authenticator app on device
solution: Verify passkey not in registered auth methods via Graph (users/{id}/authentication/methods). If missing, delete passkey from Authenticator app and re-register
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: 'Unknown error occurred during passkey registration' on Android when registering passkey in Microsoft Authenticator
rootCause: All providers for Passwords and accounts disabled on Android device
solution: On Android: Settings > General management > Passwords, passkeys, and autofill > toggle Authenticator to Enabled
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: 'We were unable to register the passkey you attempted to add' error; CMUX logs show 'Attestation format none not supported for aaguid'
rootCause: User used a provider other than Microsoft Authenticator or Windows Hello; AAGUID mismatch
solution: Check CMUX logs (IAMUXPROD db, IfxDiagnosticsEventPII table) for AAGUID. Verify against known AAGUIDs. Guide customer to use only MS Authenticator or Windows Hello
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Username cross-device passkey sign-in from iOS to Android fails with 'Something went wrong'; usernameless sign-in works fine
rootCause: Bug: userHandle not passed through WebAuthn on iOS side during username-based flow (ICM 547376843)
solution: Known bug (ICM 547376843). Workaround: use usernameless cross-device passkey sign-in instead
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey registration stuck in loop when CA policy requires phishing-resistant MFA for all cloud apps including Authenticator
rootCause: Circular dependency: CA policy requires passkey to authenticate to Authenticator app, but user needs Authenticator to register passkey
solution: Enable TAP for registration flow. Configure CA to accept TAP for 'Register security information' action, limit to mobile platforms during registration window
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Cross-device passkey registration fails with 'Devices couldn't connect'; same-device registration works. WebAuthN event log shows 0x80072EE2 TIMEOUT
rootCause: Corporate firewall/proxy blocks FIDO2 tunnel service domains: Cable.auth.com (Apple), Cable.ua5v.com (Google)
solution: Unblock FIDO2 tunnel URLs in firewall: Cable.auth.com (Apple), Cable.ua5v.com (Google). For web proxy, configure machine-level proxy for cryptsvc (NetworkService) via bitsadmin
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Cross-device passkey registration fails: user sees 'Allow this site to see your security key?' prompt and registration does not complete
rootCause: User taps 'Skip' instead of 'Allow' on the security key visibility prompt
solution: Instruct user to tap 'Allow' (not 'Skip') when prompted at login.microsoft.com
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Windows users see stale/old passkey device entries in second factor auth prompt even after deleting passkeys from Security Info
rootCause: Passkey device links in Windows registry persist after Entra ID passkey deletion
solution: Clean stale passkeys: Settings > Account > Passkeys > delete stale entries. Or manually remove from HKEY_USERS/S-1-5-20/Software/Microsoft/Cryptography/FIDO/(SID)/LinkedDevices
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Users prompted to register MS Authenticator passkey despite Authenticator passkeys not enabled in tenant; cannot choose different method
rootCause: Known bug (ICM 566930730) in proof-up page when CA requires phishing-resistant MFA
solution: Known bug. Workaround: register phishing-resistant method directly at https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Windows Web Sign-in with passkey fails with 'Something went wrong' after scanning QR code
rootCause: Corporate firewall/proxy blocks FIDO2 tunnel service domains for cross-device communication
solution: Unblock FIDO2 tunnel URLs: Cable.auth.com (Apple), Cable.ua5v.com (Google)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: iOS device registers and authenticates with passkey even when Bluetooth appears disabled from control center
rootCause: Apple control center toggle does not fully disable Bluetooth; BT remains active for Apple proprietary features
solution: Expected Apple behavior. To truly disable BT, use iOS System Settings (not control center). Not an Entra issue
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: 'Unknown Error' registering passkey in Authenticator on Android; AADSTS135011 'Device used during authentication is disabled'
rootCause: Device disabled/deleted in Entra, or backup restored on new device causing stale device registration
solution: 1) Authenticator Settings > Device Registration > Unregister device. 2) Remove stale accounts with Phone sign-in enabled from Authenticator
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: User is prompted for password instead of TAP passcode after entering UPN
rootCause: TAP policy not enabled for user, TAP not yet activated (up to 10 min delay), TAP expired, or TAP one-time already used
solution: 1) Verify TAP policy is Enabled and user is assigned. 2) Wait up to 10 min after creation. 3) Check TAP expiry. 4) Check Audit log for AccessPassUsage=3 (Used). 5) If valid TAP exists but blocked, delete and recreate (Issue 7).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: Administrator cannot modify One-time use setting when creating TAP credential for a user
rootCause: One-time use is set to Yes at the tenant TAP policy level, which overrides per-user configuration
solution: Change the One-time use setting on the TAP Policy to No to make it configurable per-user. Note: this impacts all TAP credential creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP Activation duration cannot be raised above default or lowered below default in Azure portal
rootCause: Activation duration is bounded by minimumLifetimeInMinutes and maximumLifetimeInMinutes in TAP policy. Default lifetime populates the UI field.
solution: Adjust maximumLifetimeInMinutes (to allow higher values) or minimumLifetimeInMinutes (to allow lower values) in the TAP Policy before setting per-user Activation duration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP does not work for external guest users - error: Temporary Access Pass cannot be added to an external guest user
rootCause: External guest users (federated signInType) are not supported for TAP. Only internal member and internal guest accounts can use TAP.
solution: Verify account type via Graph API: GET /users/{id}?$select=identities. If signInType=federated and issuer=ExternalAzureAD, TAP is not supported. TAP only works for accounts with signInType=userPrincipalName.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP sign-in fails for SSPR-enabled users - redirected to SSPR interrupt flow but cannot register FIDO2/phone sign-in for SSPR
rootCause: SSPR requires users to register SSPR-compatible methods first. FIDO2 and Phone sign-in are not SSPR authentication methods.
solution: User must first register SSPR required methods (e.g., email) before going to My Security Info to register passwordless methods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: MFA required error appears during FIDO key registration with TAP
rootCause: FIDO registration took more than 10 minutes after sign-in, causing the session to time out
solution: User should refresh the page and resume registration. Ensure FIDO registration is completed within 10 minutes of signing in with TAP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP sign-in blocked by User Credential Policy (error 130502) despite user having valid multiuse TAP
rootCause: Admin changed TAP policy from Multiuse to One-time use after the TAP was already issued to the user
solution: Delete the existing TAP and issue a new One-Time use TAP to the user.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP sign-in error codes: 130503 InvalidAccessPass, 130504 AccessPassExpired, 130505 AccessPassAlreadyUsed, 130506/130507 AccessPassNotPresent
rootCause: Various TAP validation failures: wrong passcode, expired TAP, one-time TAP already used, or user not assigned TAP method
solution: 130503: Verify correct passcode. 130504: Create new TAP. 130505: Issue new one-time TAP. 130506/130507: Assign TAP method to user and ensure TAP policy is enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass

symptom: TAP provisioning fails for guest account - need to determine if account is internal guest (TAP supported) vs external guest (not supported)
rootCause: TAP only applies to Internal Guest Accounts (signInType=userPrincipalName, issuer=tenant domain). External Guest Accounts (signInType=federated, issuer=ExternalAzureAD) are blocked.
solution: Check user identities via Graph: GET /users/{id}?$select=identities. Internal Guest: signInType=userPrincipalName. In ASC: Sources should not show Microsoft Account; Creation Type=N/A; External User State=N/A.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Entra ID Temporary Access Pass For Internal Guest Accounts

symptom: Security key or passkey method not available in Add sign-in method dropdown
rootCause: Windows Hello AAGUIDs not added to Passkey (FIDO2) authentication method policy. Required AAGUIDs: 08987058 (HW TPM), 9ddd1817 (VBS), 6028b017 (SW TPM).
solution: Admin must add Windows Hello AAGUIDs to the Passkey (FIDO2) policy Configure tab, and ensure user is in a group assigned to the passkey profile containing those AAGUIDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Entra Passkeys on Windows

symptom: Entra Passkey registration from Hyper-V VM creates passkey but fails at final redirect to Security info page
rootCause: Older Windows Hyper-V guest VM build does not support passkey finalization
solution: Use Windows 11 Build 26100+ guest VM on Windows Server 2025 Build 26100+ host with Basic session (Enhanced/RDP does not support Windows Hello).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Entra Passkeys on Windows

symptom: Passkey auth fails on iOS17/iOS18 devices restored from iCloud backup - passkey appears in Authenticator but authentication fails
rootCause: Passkeys in Authenticator are device-bound and not syncable. iCloud backup restores the passkey entry but not the key material.
solution: User should delete and re-register passkey on the new device. After Authenticator update (Oct 2024), opening the app auto-removes incorrectly restored passkeys.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey in Microsoft Authenticator not listed for registration in Security Info dropdown
rootCause: Microsoft Authenticator AAGUIDs not added to Passkey (FIDO2) policy. iOS: 90a3ccdf-635c-4729-a248-9b709135078f, Android: de1e552d-db1d-4423-a619-566b625cdc84.
solution: Admin must add one or both Authenticator AAGUIDs to the Passkey (FIDO2) policy Configure tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey auth fails with 'Your company policy requires a different method' - error code 135016 FIDO sign-in disabled via policy
rootCause: The AAGUID for the users registered passkey is no longer listed in the Passkey (FIDO2) policy
solution: Admin must re-add the relevant AAGUID to the Passkey (FIDO2) policy Configure tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey sign-in shows 'There are no matching passkeys saved in Authenticator for login.microsoft.com'
rootCause: Passkey method was deleted from user account in Entra ID but passkey entry remains in the Authenticator app
solution: Verify passkey not present via ASC Graph Explorer: GET /users/{id}/authentication/methods (beta). If missing, user should delete passkey from Authenticator and re-register.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Unknown error during passkey registration on Android - all credential providers disabled
rootCause: All providers for Passwords and accounts disabled on Android device
solution: On Android: Settings > General management > Passwords, passkeys, and autofill > toggle Authenticator to Enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey registration fails with attestation format not supported for third-party AAGUID
rootCause: User selected a third-party passkey provider instead of MS Authenticator or Windows Hello
solution: Check CMUX log (Kusto: idsharedneueudb/idsharedscus, DB: IAMUXPROD) for attestation error. Guide user to select MS Authenticator when saving passkey. Device-bound passkeys only support MS Authenticator and Windows Hello.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey registration stuck in infinite loop - CA policy requires passkey for All cloud apps including Authenticator
rootCause: CA policy targets All cloud apps with passkey Authentication Strength, creating circular dependency for in-app registration
solution: Enable TAP for registration. Have users satisfy CA policy with TAP for Register security information action. Issue TAP only during registration with limited lifetime.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Cross-device passkey registration fails with 'Devices could not connect' - FIDO2 tunnel domains blocked
rootCause: Corporate firewall/proxy blocks Cable.auth.com (Apple) or Cable.ua5v.com (Google). WebAuthn log shows 0x80072EE2 WININET_E_TIMEOUT.
solution: Unblock Cable.auth.com and Cable.ua5v.com. For machine-level proxy: bitsadmin /util /setieproxy networkservice AUTOSCRIPT <pac-url>, deploy via GPO. Alternative: use same-device in-app registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Stale passkeys in Windows second factor prompt after device reset - old devices listed even after Security Info deletion
rootCause: Passkey device links persist in Windows registry: HKEY_USERS\S-1-5-20\Software\Microsoft\Cryptography\FIDO\{SID}\LinkedDevices
solution: Remove via Settings > Account > Passkeys. Or manually: find SID via PowerShell, export/backup registry, delete stale deviceID entries from LinkedDevices.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Windows Web Sign-in with Passkey fails 'Something went wrong' when scanning QR code
rootCause: Corporate firewall/proxy blocks FIDO2 tunnel service domains (Cable.auth.com, Cable.ua5v.com)
solution: Unblock Cable.auth.com and Cable.ua5v.com in corporate firewall/proxy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Passkey registration fails with Unknown Error on Android - AADSTS135011 Device disabled
rootCause: Multiple accounts in Authenticator with disabled/deleted devices, or Authenticator restored from backup on new device
solution: 1) Authenticator Settings > Device Registration > Unregister device. 2) Remove old accounts with Phone sign-in. 3) Re-register passkey.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator Passkeys do not work in China (Mooncake/21Vianet)
rootCause: Authenticator Passkeys not supported in China. Only limited auth methods available.
solution: Use alternative passwordless methods: WHfB (GA), Certificate-based Auth (preview). Authenticator does NOT support passwordless in Mooncake.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Microsoft Authenticator Device Bound Passkeys

symptom: Saving Passkey (FIDO2) policy fails with 'Include conditions are missing passkey profile targets'
rootCause: Target group added without linking to a Passkey Profile after opting in to Passkey Profile schema
solution: Before clicking Save, link each targeted group to a passkey profile in the policy configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: Saving Passkey (FIDO2) policy fails with error: Include conditions are missing passkey profile targets, after opting in to Passkey Profiles preview and adding a Target group not linked to any Passkey Profile.
rootCause: Once Passkey Profile schema is enabled, every Target in includeTargets must be associated with at least one Passkey Profile. Adding a Target without a profile link triggers 400 Bad Request.
solution: Before clicking Save, link the targeted group to a Passkey Profile by selecting one or more profiles in the Passkey Profiles dropdown for that Target row in the Enable and Target tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: Saving Passkey (FIDO2) authentication methods policy fails with error: Policy size is larger than allowed by N bytes. 400 Bad Request in browser trace during PATCH to Graph API.
rootCause: Default User Credential Policy in Entra ID has a 20KB size limit covering all nested authentication method configurations. Too many targeted groups, AAGUIDs, or Passkey Profiles exceeds this limit.
solution: Reduce targeted groups or simplify AAGUID lists. Check current size: GET /beta/policies/authenticationMethodsPolicy, save JSON as .txt, check file size. Upcoming: Passkey policy will be decoupled from default container with its own 20KB limit.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: Hybrid users with WHfB Kerberos Cloud Trust trigger bad password attempts accessing on-prem resources (file shares), exceeding bad password threshold and locking AD account. Device is cloud-joined only, not hybrid joined.
rootCause: Occurs when Credential Guard is enabled on cloud-joined device with hybrid user using WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust. The combination causes incorrect authentication attempts against on-prem AD.
solution: Disable Credential Guard as workaround. See Evergreen article: ADDS: WHfB: Kerberos Cloud Trust users experience lockout issues with credential guard enabled (https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/486bdf30-5b19-d543-d7cc-658a14a5e1fd).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O7 WHfB cloud kerberos users locked credential guard

symptom: WHfB Key Trust enrollment does not happen on hybrid Azure AD joined devices. Event 362 shows Enterprise user logon certificate enrollment endpoint is ready: No and Enterprise user logon certificate template is: Not Tested.
rootCause: GPO 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' (Administrative Templates > Windows Component > Windows Hello for Business) is enabled. This is for Certificate Trust, not Key Trust. Blocks provisioning by requiring certificate enrollment infrastructure not configured.
solution: Set GPO 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' to Not Configured. Located under User/Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Component > Windows Hello for Business. Verify via GPRESULT /h > gp.html.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P1 WHfB Key Trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: Hybrid users provisioned with WHfB Kerberos Cloud Trust trigger bad password attempts when accessing on-premises resources (e.g., file shares), eventually exceeding bad password threshold and locking the AD account. Device is cloud-joined only. Issue only occurs with Credential Guard enabled.
rootCause: Credential Guard interferes with WHfB Kerberos Cloud Trust authentication flow on cloud-joined devices accessing on-premises resources, generating bad password attempts against AD.
solution: Disable Credential Guard as a workaround. See internal KB: https://internal.evergreen.microsoft.com/en-us/topic/486bdf30-5b19-d543-d7cc-658a14a5e1fd for detailed reproduction steps and additional workarounds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O7 WHfB cloud kerberos users locked credential guard

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Azure AD joined Key Trust enrollment does not trigger. Event 362 shows 'Enterprise user logon certificate enrollment endpoint is ready: No' and 'Enterprise user logon certificate template is: Not Tested'.
rootCause: GPO 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' (under Administrative Templates > Windows Component > Windows Hello for Business) is enabled. This GPO is for Certificate Trust Deployment, not Key Trust Deployment, causing Key Trust enrollment to fail.
solution: Set the GPO 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' to 'Not Configured'. This setting is located under Administrative Templates > Windows Component > Windows Hello for Business (can be in user or computer configuration).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P1 WHfB Key Trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: WHfB provisioning fails with error 0x801C03ED (DSREG_E_NGC_INVALID_REQUEST). Event 301 shows 'NGC key registration failed' with 'New NGC key could not be added to store'. Error occurs in On-Premises Key Trust Deployment.
rootCause: User is a member of AD Protected Groups. AdminSDHolder overwrites security descriptors and disables inheritance, causing the ADFS service account to lose permissions to write/update the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute on the user object.
solution: Grant Key Admins group Read and Write msDS-KeyCredentialLink permissions on AdminSDHolder: AD Users & Computers > Advanced Features > System > AdminSDHolder > Security > Advanced > Add 'KeyCredentials Admins' and 'Key Admins' > Apply to 'Descendant User Objects' > Select 'Read msDS-KeyCredentialLink' and 'Write msDS-KeyCredentialLink'. Then run 'Repadmin /Syncall' to force replication.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P10 WHfB prov fails error 0x801C03ED

symptom: Passkey (FIDO2) policy save fails with 400 'Include conditions are missing passkey profile targets' after opting in to Passkey Profiles preview, when a Target group is added without being linked to any Passkey Profile
rootCause: Once Passkey Profile schema is enabled via preview opt-in, every Target group must be linked to at least one Passkey Profile. Adding a Target without a profile link causes the PATCH call to fail with badRequest.
solution: Before clicking Save, link the targeted group to a passkey profile by selecting a profile from the Passkey Profiles drop-down for that target.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: Passkey (FIDO2) policy save fails with 400 'Policy size is larger than allowed by N bytes' when configuring many Passkey Profiles, targets or AAGUIDs
rootCause: Default User Credential Policy has 20KB limit including all nested auth method configs. Passkey policy will be decoupled to own 20KB limit in future.
solution: Reduce targeted groups or simplify settings. Check size: GET graph.microsoft.com/beta/policies/authenticationMethodsPolicy, save as .txt, check size. Ref: base ~1.44KB, 1 target+profile ~0.23KB.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: After passkey profiles auto-enablement (April-May 2026), users who already registered Microsoft Authenticator unexpectedly prompted to register a passkey during sign-in
rootCause: Auto-enablement with synced passkeys + Microsoft-managed campaign changes target from Authenticator to passkeys, expands to all MFA-capable users, snooze becomes non-configurable.
solution: Set registration campaign to Enabled keeping Authenticator selected, or Disabled. Opt in before April 2026 to configure preferred passkeyType values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/FIDO2 passkeys/FIDO2: Passkey Profiles in Entra ID

symptom: WHfB Cloud Kerberos Trust hybrid users on cloud-joined devices trigger bad password attempts accessing on-prem resources (file shares), locking AD account. Only with Credential Guard enabled.
rootCause: Credential Guard on cloud-joined devices causes WHfB Kerberos Cloud Trust auth against on-prem resources to register as bad password attempts in AD.
solution: Disable Credential Guard on affected devices. See internal article on internal.evergreen.microsoft.com for details and additional workarounds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/O7 WHfB cloud kerberos users locked credential guard

symptom: WHfB Key Trust enrollment does not happen on Hybrid AADJ device. DSREGCMD: WillNotProvision. Event 362: 'Enterprise user logon certificate enrollment endpoint is ready: No', 'Enterprise user logon certificate template is: Not Tested'.
rootCause: GPO 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' enabled but only for Certificate Trust. In Key Trust deployment it blocks provisioning.
solution: Set 'Use certificate for on-premises authentication' GPO to 'Not Configured'. Only enable for Certificate Trust deployments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P1 WHfB Key Trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: Windows Hello for Business provisioning fails with error 0x801C03ED (DSREG_E_NGC_INVALID_REQUEST). Event 301 shows 'NGC key registration failed' with 'New NGC key could not be added to store'. On Premises Key Trust Deployment.
rootCause: ADFS Service Account cannot update the msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute on user accounts that are members of Protected Groups. AdminSDHolder overwrites ACL and removes ADFS service account permissions; inheritance is disabled for protected users.
solution: Grant Key Admins group Read/Write msDS-KeyCredentialLink permissions on AdminSDHolder: AD Users & Computers → System → AdminSDHolder → Properties → Security → Advanced → Add → Select 'KeyCredentials Admins and Key Admins' → Applied to 'Descendant User Objects' → Check 'Read msDS-KeyCredentialLink' and 'Write msDS-KeyCredentialLink'. Force replication: repadmin /Syncall.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P10 WHfB prov fails error 0x801C03ED

symptom: Windows Hello for Business PIN prompt does not appear during WHfB setup. The PIN setup window is hidden behind the main window. UAC is disabled on client devices (EnableLUA=0).
rootCause: UAC is disabled on the client device. Registry key HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\system\EnableLUA is set to 0, which causes the CloudExperienceHost PIN setup window to be hidden.
solution: Set EnableLUA registry key to 1: Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\system -Name EnableLUA -Value 1. Reboot the device.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P11 WHfB prompt does not trigger UAC

symptom: Cannot acquire PRT on Hybrid Azure AD Joined machine. AzureAdPrt=NO in dsregcmd /status. WHfB not working, Conditional Access failing, SSO not working. Event 344 shows 'Failed to access the device key. TPM might be locked out.' Event 1098 shows error 0x80090030 'cryptographic provider is not ready'. System event 1026 shows 'TPM defending against dictionary attacks, time-out period'.
rootCause: TPM is locked out (dictionary attack protection) or in an unknown state, preventing access to TPM keys used to protect device keys for PRT acquisition.
solution: Clear/reset the TPM: Windows Defender Security Center → Device security → Security processor details → Security processor troubleshooting → Clear TPM → Restart. Warning: clearing TPM may break BitLocker and other TPM-dependent services. Check BIOS settings for TPM lockout reset first. Engage hardware vendor if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P12 Not able to get PRT TPM issue

symptom: ADFS certificate-based MFA fails or hangs when AD FS token-binding is enabled. Smart card MFA authentication during WHfB provisioning fails or times out. Bug 14866615.
rootCause: Token binding conflict in AD FS when using smart card as MFA factor. The MFA page remains loading and eventually times out, causing WHfB provisioning to fail.
solution: Workaround: Reduce security by disabling token binding in ADFS: Set-AdfsProperties -IgnoreTokenBinding $true. Note: This is a known bug being addressed in RS5 timeframe.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P12 Not able to get PRT TPM issue

symptom: hmkd.dll missing from Windows Server Core when installing AD FS role. WHfB deployments utilizing TPM 2.0 on clients fail. Bug 19853477.
rootCause: The DLL hmkd.dll is not included in Server Core installation when installing the AD FS role. This DLL is required for WHfB deployments with TPM 2.0 clients.
solution: Workaround: Manually copy hmkd.dll from an existing full server installation and register it on the Server Core machine. Fixed in 19H1 build of Windows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P12 Not able to get PRT TPM issue

symptom: Newly created WHfB PIN credential is not cached and not available for use immediately. Up to 30 minute delay between PIN creation and availability.
rootCause: Azure AD Connect sync cycle interval causes delay between PIN credential creation and key synchronization to Azure AD. The PIN credential must sync before it is available for authentication.
solution: After creating a PIN, log off and log back on using the PIN after 30 minutes to cache the credential. Manual one-time sync is acceptable for troubleshooting but not scalable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P12 Not able to get PRT TPM issue

symptom: Device registration failing for Windows Hello for Business deployment. Devices cannot authenticate to identity providers (Azure AD for cloud/hybrid, AD FS for on-premises).
rootCause: Device registration is invalid or incomplete. Device may not be properly joined to Azure AD or the on-premises AD FS.
solution: Verify device registration using dsregcmd /status. Check event logs under Application and Service Logs > Microsoft > Windows > User Device Registration. For Hybrid deployment, verify both AzureAdJoined and certificate trust chain. See Device registration/Troubleshooting Windows 10 Automatic Device Registration wiki for detailed steps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P3 Device registration failing

symptom: Microsoft.Windows.CloudExperienceHost (CXH) application fails to launch on workstations. WHfB provisioning UI doesn't load even after prerequisites are met. Triggering via ms-cxh://nthaad also fails. ShellCore_Oper.evtx shows 'ClearTemporaryWebDataAsyncFailed'.
rootCause: CXH app cache not cleared properly. Known bug in OS versions older than Nickel (Windows 10/11 22H2). A backport request exists for older OS versions.
solution: Workaround: 1) Rename %localappdata%\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy to .backup; 2) Run: Add-AppxPackage -Register "$env:windir\SystemApps\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy\Appxmanifest.xml" -DisableDevelopmentMode -ForceApplicationShutdown; 3) Rename registry key HKCU\Software\Microsoft\IdentityCRL\TokenBroker\DefaultAccount to DefaultAccount_backup; 4) Reboot device.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P4 CloudExperienceHost fails to run

symptom: WHfB cert trust provisioning popup shows only 'Cancel' option. Event 303 shows 'NGC key registration initialization operation failed - action cancelled by user'. Event 311 shows 'NGC create container operation failed'. Event 1098 shows error 0xCAA50021 'retry attempts exceeds expectation' and AADSTS50155 'Device is not authenticated'.
rootCause: Device authentication has failed. Device object may be missing in Azure AD, or device certificate/registration is invalid. PRT update flow fails because device cannot authenticate.
solution: 1) Verify device registered: dsregcmd /status; 2) Verify device exists in Azure AD: Connect-MgGraph → Get-MgDevice -DeviceId <GUID>; 3) If device missing: re-register with dsregcmd /Leave then reboot; 4) Check Azure AD logs using correlationID from event 1098 for detailed failure reason.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P5 WHfB cert trust fails cancel option

symptom: GPO 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' blocks WHfB PIN sign-in. PIN sign-in unavailable for initial logon but available from unlock screen. Windows 1709.
rootCause: Group Policy 'Computer Configuration > Policies > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows Logon Option > Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' conflicts with WHfB. Bug 15643318.
solution: Do not use this GPO until the bug is resolved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P7 GPO blocks WHfB UI provisioning

symptom: GPOs 'Block launching Universal Windows apps with Windows Runtime API access from hosted content' or 'AppLocker Policies' block WHfB provisioning. PIN configuration dialog opens and closes or does not complete during WHfB provisioning. Windows 1709.
rootCause: Group Policies under 'App runtime' or 'AppLocker Policies' prevent Universal Windows apps (including CloudExperienceHost used by WHfB provisioning) from running properly.
solution: These GPOs are not compatible with Windows Hello for Business. Remove or disable: 1) Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > App runtime > Block launching Universal Windows apps with Windows Runtime API access from hosted content; 2) Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Application Control Policies > AppLocker Policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P7 GPO blocks WHfB UI provisioning

symptom: GPO 'Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' blocks WHfB login. Error: 'We can't sign you in with this credential because your domain isn't available.' Windows 1709.
rootCause: Group Policy 'Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 0, preventing cached logon with WHfB credentials when domain is not reachable.
solution: Login as Local Administrator and set GPO 'Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' to minimum value of 1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P7 GPO blocks WHfB UI provisioning

symptom: WHfB provisioning does not trigger with ADFS and Domain Controllers on Server 2019. Hybrid Certificate Trust. Event 362 shows 'Enterprise user logon certificate template is: No (1: StateNoPolicy)' and 'User has successfully authenticated to the enterprise STS: No'. ADFS event 1021 shows 'MSIS9368: client forbidden to access resource with scope ugs'.
rootCause: ADFS on Server 2019 does not have the 'ugs' scope configured by default. The OAuth request from the WHfB client requires the 'ugs' scope, and ADFS rejects it with MSIS9368.
solution: On ADFS server: 1) ADFS management → Services → Scope Descriptions → Add Scope Description → Name: 'ugs' → Apply → OK; 2) PowerShell: Get-AdfsApplicationPermission → find entry with ScopeNames {openid, aza} and ClientRoleIdentifier 38aa3b87-a06d-4817-b275-7a316988d93b → note ObjectIdentifier; 3) Set-AdfsApplicationPermission -TargetIdentifier <ObjectIdentifier> -AddScope 'ugs'; 4) Restart ADFS service; 5) Restart client.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P8 WHfB provisioning fails ADFS DC 2019

symptom: MFA is not prompted during Windows Hello for Business provisioning. User provisions WHfB PIN and facial recognition without MFA challenge. Scenario: Azure AD Join during OOBE with federated domain, MFA performed on on-prem IDP.
rootCause: By design. MFA claims are cached in the PRT for 10 minutes to enable SSO. During OOBE, if MFA was already performed during Azure AD Join and user provisions WHfB within 10 minutes, cached MFA is accepted. This is intentional to prevent malware from leveraging cached MFA for silent WHfB provisioning.
solution: This is expected behavior (by design). WHfB requires fresh MFA (within last 10 minutes). If user waits >10 minutes at provisioning screen or provisions later, they will be prompted for MFA. This is not configurable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P9 MFA skipped WHfB provisoning

rootCause: EnableLUA registry key at HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\system is set to 0 (UAC disabled)
solution: Set EnableLUA registry key to 1 to re-enable UAC, then retry WHfB provisioning

symptom: Windows Hello for Business PIN setup prompt does not appear during WHfB provisioning - PIN window is hidden behind the main window when UAC is disabled (EnableLUA=0)
rootCause: UAC is disabled on the client device. Registry key HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\system\EnableLUA is set to 0, causing the PIN setup dialog to be hidden behind other windows.
solution: Set EnableLUA registry key to 1 to re-enable UAC. Run: Set-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\policies\system -Name EnableLUA -Value 1. Reboot the device.

solution: Clear/reset TPM: Windows Defender Security Center > Device security > Security processor details > Security processor troubleshooting > Clear TPM. Check BIOS for TPM lockout reset. WARNING: Clearing TPM may break BitLocker.

rootCause: AD FS token-binding feature conflicts with smart card certificate-based MFA during WHfB provisioning. Bug ID 14866615.

rootCause: Azure AD Connect sync cycle interval causes delay between PIN creation and key synchronization to on-premises AD.
solution: Wait 30 minutes after PIN creation. For troubleshooting only: one-time manual AAD Connect sync is acceptable but not scalable. Log off and back on using PIN after 30 minutes to cache the credential.

symptom: WHfB cert trust provisioning does not trigger - DSREGCMD shows AdfsRaIsReady: NO, Event 362 shows 'Enterprise user logon certificate enrollment endpoint is ready: NO'. OpenID config missing winhello_cert capability.
rootCause: ADFS server is not advertising itself as a Registration Authority. The .well-known/openid-configuration endpoint does not include winhello_cert in capabilities.
solution: Workaround: ADFS Management > Services > Authentication Methods > Multi-factor Authentication Methods > Edit > Select any MFA provider > Save. This triggers ADFS to advertise as RA. Do NOT enable MFA on Office 365 Identity Platform relying party.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P2 WHfB cert trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: WHfB cert trust provisioning fails with ADFS Event 1021 MSIS9400 Unable to create certificate - error 0x80094800 CERTSRV_E_UNSUPPORTED_CERT_TYPE. Template not found by CA.
rootCause: Certificate template for WHfB is not published on the CA, or the template name in certutil command does not match the published template.
solution: Verify template: certutil -catemplates -Config Machine\CAName. If missing, publish via CA console. If name mismatch: certutil -dsTemplate <CorrectName> msPKI-Private-Key-Flag +CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P2 WHfB cert trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: WHfB provisioning fails with AAD Event 1081 MSIS9682 'certificate used to sign JWT Bearer request is not from a registered device with a Transport key'. Device not found in on-prem AD.
rootCause: Device object not written back to on-premises AD. AAD Connect device writeback not enabled or device deleted from on-prem AD.
solution: Verify device in on-prem AD: Get-ADObject -LDAPFilter '(cn=$deviceid)' -SearchBase 'CN=RegisteredDevices,DC=contoso,DC=com'. Enable device writeback in AAD Connect. If deleted, restore from backup or re-register: DSREGCMD /Leave then reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P2 WHfB cert trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: WHfB cert trust provisioning does not trigger - Event 362 shows 'Enterprise user logon certificate template is: No (6: StateMultipleTemplates)'. Multiple templates have HELLO_LOGON_KEY flag.
rootCause: certutil -dsTemplate command with +CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY was executed for 2+ certificate templates, causing conflict.
solution: Remove flag from unused templates: certutil -dsTemplate <UnusedName> msPKI-Private-Key-Flag -CTPRIVATEKEY_FLAG_HELLO_LOGON_KEY. Force AD replication. Restart client. If unresolved, delete unused template from AD.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P2 WHfB cert trust enrollment does not happen

symptom: WHfB cert trust fails with Event 1081 MSIS9699 'GlobalAuthenticationPolicy does not allow this Auth JWT Bearer request' and Event 1118 status 0xC000250.
rootCause: Multiple ADFS Claims Provider Trusts configured (including LDAP directory). WHfB requires KB4338822 when multiple claims providers exist.
solution: Install KB4338822. Verify claims providers: Get-AdfsClaimsProviderTrust (GUI may not show LDAP-based providers).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/P2 WHfB cert trust enrollment does not happen

solution: Ensure client has network access to a writable Domain Controller. Adjust network topology or AD site configuration.

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust: User unable to access on-premises resources from Azure AD Joined PC with federated account. Login with PIN succeeds but Kerberos SSO to on-prem resources fails.
rootCause: One or more prerequisites not met: (1) DC missing certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, (2) AADJ PC cannot reach a domain controller, (3) public key not written back from AAD to AD (msDS-KeyCredentialLink), (4) CRL from issuing CA not accessible, (5) root CA cert not in PC trusted root store.
solution: 1. Verify DC has certificate per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/hello-hybrid-key-whfb-settings-pki#domain-controller-certificate-template. 2. Check public key writeback with: repadmin /showobjmeta DCSERVER "CN=USER,OU=...". 3. Verify DC reachable: nltest /dsgetdc:FQDN. 4. Ensure CRL accessible. 5. Ensure root CA cert in Trusted Root store. 6. Wait 30 min after PIN provisioning for AAD Connect sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S2 Unable to access onprem resources AADJ

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust: Login with PIN fails with error 'The request is not supported'. KDC Event ID 19 (PKINIT missing suitable certificate) or Event ID 29 (KDC cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logon) on Domain Controller.
rootCause: Domain Controller does not have a certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, or has multiple certificates and is using an older one without the correct EKU. Alternatively, the issuing CA certificate is not in the NTAuth store.
solution: 1. Verify 2016 DC has cert with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2. If multiple certs exist, import new cert to 'Active Directory Domain Service' store via MMC and restart DC to clear cache. 3. Verify NTAuth store: certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth. 4. Export DC cert (.cer) and validate: certutil -verify -urlfetch <cert.cer>. 5. Fix any revocation or root trust failures. 6. Enable CAPI2 Operational logs if still failing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust: Sign-in with PIN fails with error 0x00000bb. User provisioned PIN successfully but cannot authenticate.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not populated on user object in on-premises AD. Possible causes: (1) AAD Connect delta sync not completed (need 30 min minimum), (2) AAD Connect schema not updated to include msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute, (3) machine cannot locate/communicate with Server 2016+ DC.
solution: 1. Wait 30 min and trigger delta sync on AAD Connect server. 2. Check msDS-KeyCredentialLink on user: if empty after sync, verify AAD Connect schema includes the attribute ($ADConnector = Get-ADSyncConnector | ? {$_.name -notmatch '.onmicrosoft.com'} | Select -expand AttributeInclusionList). 3. Refresh schema in AAD Connect if needed. 4. Verify client can reach 2016+ DC. 5. Ensure DC cert in Trusted Root store on client. 6. If still failing, collect NGC trace via Auth trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S4 Error message 0x00000bb sign in with PIN

symptom: WHfB: Login with PIN fails with 'Sign-in failed. The KDC certificate could not be validated.' Kerberos Event ID 9 on client: 'The client has failed to validate the domain controller certificate... The revocation function was unable to check revocation because the revocation server was offline.'
rootCause: The CRL (Certificate Revocation List) distribution point for the DC certificate issuing CA is unreachable from the client, or the CRL has expired and not been republished.
solution: 1. Verify the CRL distribution point URL is accessible from the client. 2. Check CRL validity dates. 3. If connectivity valid, republish the CRL from the issuing CA. 4. Verify with: certutil -verify -urlfetch <exported-dc-cert.cer>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out. Users cannot change or reset PIN, set up fingerprint, etc.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions has Value set to 0, which disables all sign-in options. This may be deployed via Group Policy or baked into the base/golden image.
solution: Set the registry key Value to 1: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions\Value = 1. Check if this is being set by Group Policy and update the GPO accordingly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust cross-forest: Login fails with 'Credential cannot be verified' when user logs in with UPN (e.g. BOB@MYDOMAIN.COM) but succeeds with DOMAIN\USERNAME format. Environment has devices in one forest and users in another forest with trust.
rootCause: When credentials are serialized in UPN format, the Kerberos client attempts to locate a Server 2016 DC in the device's domain (which may only have 2012 R2 DCs). When using DOMAIN\USERNAME, Kerberos client correctly locates a 2016 DC in the user's domain. NGC cache behavior affects credential serialization format.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016 or above DCs in ALL realms in the trust referral path between devices and users (not just the user's domain). This is the supported configuration per Microsoft WHfB requirements. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/hello-identity-verification
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: WHfB PIN registration fails with error 0x800706d9 after completing MFA successfully. Process Monitor shows 'Access Denied' when writing to registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Authentication\LogonUI\NgcPin. No WHFB sign-in options appear for manual setup.
rootCause: The NgcCtnrSvc (Microsoft Passport Container) service was disabled on the affected machine. This service is required for WHfB PIN operations as it manages the Hello container where the private key associated with the PIN is stored.
solution: 1. Set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NgcCtnrSvc\Start to 3 (Manual). 2. Restart the machine. 3. The Microsoft Passport Container service will start successfully. 4. WHfB sign-in options will appear and PIN provisioning will succeed. Tip: Always verify NgcCtnrSvc and NgcSvc service status early in WHfB troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S8 WHFB PIN Registration Failure (Error 0x800706d9)

symptom: Windows 24H2/25H2 behavior change: FIDO2 security keys without a PIN set are no longer functional for authentication after October patch.
rootCause: Windows 24H2 introduced a behavior change requiring PIN on FIDO2 security keys. However, Entra ID is NOT impacted because it requires User Verification (UV) for passkey/FIDO2 registration, meaning keys are always registered with a PIN.
solution: If the case involves FIDO2 keys without PIN in non-Entra scenarios (home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra web auth), route to Directory Services team: Windows/Windows 11/{version}/Windows Security Technologies/Biometric, Passwordless Authentication, SSO, and Windows Hello. Entra ID cases are not affected by this change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset: User clicks 'I Forgot My PIN' on login screen and gets error 'This feature is not supported in your organization. Please contact your administrator'.
rootCause: Either (1) PIN Reset Service is not properly configured, (2) client has no internet access to reach PIN Reset Service, or (3) user set their PIN before the PIN reset policy was applied (recovery key was not encrypted with PIN Reset Service public key).
solution: 1. Verify PIN Reset Service configuration per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/identity-protection/hello-for-business/pin-reset. 2. Ensure client has internet access. 3. If PIN was set before policy: user must first reset PIN from Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options > PIN > Change > I forgot My PIN. After this reset, PIN Reset Service public key encrypts the recovery key, and login-screen PIN reset will work next time. Note: PIN reset from login screen requires Windows 10 1709+ Enterprise (not Professional).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: WHfB login fails with The request is not supported error on hybrid key trust deployment
rootCause: Domain controller does not have a certificate with the Kerberos Authentication EKU, or older certificates are being used instead of the correct one. Can also occur if CRL is not current or reachable.
solution: 1) Ensure 2016 DCs have cert with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2) If multiple certs exist, import new cert to AD DS certificate store via MMC and restart DC to clear lsass cache. 3) Ensure CRL is current and reachable. 4) Verify issuing cert is in NTAuth store: certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: KDC Event ID 19/29 on DC: KDC is unable to use PKINIT protocol or cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logons
rootCause: DC cannot validate its own certificate due to CRL revocation check failure, root trust failure, or issuing CA certificate not in NTAuth store.
solution: 1) Export DC cert as .cer, run certutil -verify -urlfetch. 2) Fix revocation or root trust issues. 3) Verify issuing cert in NTAuth store. 4) Enable CAPI2 logs for further analysis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB PIN login fails with KDC certificate could not be validated - client Event ID 9: revocation server was offline
rootCause: CRL Distribution Point for the DC certificate is not reachable from client, or CRL is expired/not published.
solution: Verify CRL is reachable and up to date from the client machine. If connectivity is valid, republish CRL from the issuing CA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out - cannot change/reset WHfB PIN
rootCause: Registry AllowSignInOptions Value set to 0, disabling all sign-in options. May be deployed via Group Policy or golden image.
solution: Set registry AllowSignInOptions Value to 1. Check if Group Policy or base image is enforcing Value=0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB hybrid key trust cross-forest: Login fails with Credential cannot be verified when using UPN format, but works with DOMAIN\username
rootCause: NGC credential provider serializes UPN-format credentials differently - Kerberos client locates DC in device domain (no 2016+ DCs) instead of user domain.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016+ DCs in ALL realms in the trust referral path. Workaround: login with DOMAIN\USERNAME format.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: WHFB PIN registration fails with error 0x800706d9 after completing MFA - NgcCtnrSvc service disabled
rootCause: NgcCtnrSvc (Microsoft Passport Container) service is disabled. Handles private key storage in Hello container. When disabled, Access Denied occurs writing to NgcPin registry.
solution: Set registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NgcCtnrSvc Start=3 (Manual). Restart machine. Always check NgcCtnrSvc and NgcSvc service status first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S8 WHFB PIN Registration Failure (Error 0x800706d9)

symptom: Windows 24H2 FIDO2 security key behavior change - keys without PIN no longer work after October patch
rootCause: Windows 24H2/25H2 October patch introduced mandatory PIN for FIDO2 keys. Entra ID NOT impacted (requires UV for registration).
solution: Entra ID NOT impacted. Route to Directory Services team. Affects home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset: I Forgot My PIN on login screen returns This feature is not supported in your organization
rootCause: PIN Reset Service not configured, or client has no internet access to reach the endpoint.
solution: Verify PIN Reset Service config. Ensure client has internet connectivity.

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset from login screen does not work on Windows 10 Professional edition
rootCause: PIN Reset from login screen requires Windows 10/11 Enterprise edition (build 1709+). Professional SKU not supported.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10/11 Enterprise edition.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: WHfB PIN Reset from login screen fails after policy configured - works from Settings but not lock screen
rootCause: User provisioned PIN before PIN Reset policy was applied. Recovery key not encrypted with PIN Reset Service public key.
solution: Reset PIN from Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options > PIN > Change > I forgot My PIN first. This re-encrypts recovery key. Subsequent lock-screen resets will work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Built in features

symptom: Hybrid user unable to access on-premises resources from Azure AD Joined PC using Windows Hello for Business (Key Trust). Login works but local/AD resources are inaccessible.
rootCause: Missing or misconfigured domain controller certificate (Kerberos Authentication EKU), user public key not synced back from AAD to on-prem AD via AAD Connect (msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute), CRL not accessible from client, or root CA certificate not in client trusted store.
solution: 1) Ensure DC has certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU per docs. 2) Verify public key writeback via repadmin /showobjmeta. 3) Verify DC is locatable via nltest /dsgetdc:FQDN. 4) Ensure root CA cert is in client trusted root store. 5) Verify CRL accessibility. 6) Wait 30 min after provisioning for AAD Connect sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S2 Unable to access onprem resources AADJ

symptom: WHfB sign-in fails with error 'The request is not supported'. System event log shows KDC Event ID 19 or 29 indicating KDC cannot find suitable certificate for smart card logon.
rootCause: Domain controller lacks certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU, or DC is using an older certificate instead of the correct one. DC certificate may fail validation (revocation check failure, root trust failure, or issuing CA not in NTAuth store).
solution: 1) Verify 2016 DC has certificate with Kerberos Authentication EKU. 2) If multiple certs exist, import correct cert to AD DS store and restart DC. 3) Verify CRL is accessible via certutil -verify -urlfetch. 4) Verify issuing CA is in NTAuth store via certutil -viewstore -enterprise NTAuth. 5) Enable CAPI2 logs for further diagnostics.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S3 The request is not supported error

symptom: WHfB PIN provisioning succeeds but sign-in with PIN fails with error 'Something went wrong, 0x00000bb' in Hybrid Azure AD Key Trust deployment.
rootCause: msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute not synced from AAD to on-prem AD (AAD Connect delta sync not completed or schema not updated), client unable to reach Server 2016 DC, or DC certificate validation failure.
solution: 1) Wait 30 min for AAD Connect sync. 2) Trigger delta sync on AAD Connect server. 3) Verify msDS-KeyCredentialLink is populated on user object. 4) If not, refresh AAD Connect schema. 5) Verify client can reach 2016 DC. 6) Verify DC certificate trust chain and CRL. 7) If still failing, collect NGC tracing within Auth trace.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S4 Error message 0x00000bb sign in with PIN

symptom: WHfB PIN login fails with 'the KDC certificate could not be validated'. System event log shows Kerberos Event ID 9: 'The revocation function was unable to check revocation because the revocation server was offline.'
rootCause: CRL Distribution Point (CDP) for the domain controller certificate is unreachable or the published CRL is expired/stale.
solution: Verify the CRL is reachable from the client. If connectivity is valid, republish the CRL from the issuing CA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: All options under Settings > Accounts > Sign-in Options are grayed out. Users cannot change/reset WHfB PIN from settings.
rootCause: Registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions has Value set to 0, disabling all sign-in options. May be deployed via Group Policy or baked into the golden image.
solution: Set the registry key HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PolicyManager\default\Settings\AllowSignInOptions Value to 1. Check if a Group Policy is enforcing this setting or if the base image has it set to 0.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S5 WHfB fail login KDC certificate

symptom: WHfB Hybrid Key Trust login fails with 'Credential cannot be verified' in cross-forest trust deployment when user logs in using UPN format (user@domain.com). Same user logging in with DOMAIN\USERNAME format succeeds.
rootCause: NGC credential provider serializes credentials in UPN format, causing Kerberos client to look for a Server 2016 DC in the device domain (which may only have 2012 R2 DCs). When using DOMAIN\USERNAME, Kerberos correctly locates a 2016 DC in the user domain.
solution: Deploy Windows Server 2016 or above domain controllers in ALL realms in the trust referral path between the device domain and the user domain. This is a PG-confirmed requirement for WHfB Hybrid Key Trust in cross-forest scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S6 WHfB sign in cross tenant

symptom: FIDO2 security keys without PIN stop working after Windows 24H2 update (October 2025 patch). Users prompted for PIN on security keys that previously worked without one.
rootCause: Windows 24H2 behavior change requiring PIN on FIDO2 security keys. Entra ID is NOT impacted because it always requires User Verification (UV) for passkey/FIDO2 registration. Affected scenarios: home-grown apps, custom IDPs, non-Entra web auth where UV was optional.
solution: If using Entra ID: not impacted (UV always required). For non-Entra scenarios: route case to Directory Services team with SAP: Windows/.../Windows Security Technologies/Biometric, Passwordless Authentication, SSO, and Windows Hello.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/Common scenarios/S9 Windows 24H2 Security Key PIN Requirement Issue

symptom: Customer asks if WHfB key-trust self-signed certificate 30-year validity period can be changed
rootCause: The WHfB key-trust certificate validity period is non-configurable by design. It is a self-signed certificate not trusted by any entity.
solution: Inform customer the validity is non-configurable. The self-signed cert is not trusted externally, so the long validity is not a security concern.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB policies configured via both Group Policy and Intune produce unexpected results (e.g., wrong minimum PIN length)
rootCause: When GPO and Intune both configure WHfB policies, GPO takes precedence over Intune for conflicting settings.
solution: Use only one management method (GPO or Intune) for WHfB policies to avoid conflicts. If both must be used, ensure GPO settings match desired outcome since GPO wins.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User cannot find REMOVE button for PIN in Windows Settings and needs to remove WHfB PIN
rootCause: The REMOVE button may not be visible depending on policy configuration.
solution: Ensure user can logon with username/password first. Run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer' under user context to delete Hello container. Before deleting, run 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc' to check what keys exist (other keys may need reprovisioning).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User forgot PIN and wants to reset from the Windows logon screen
rootCause: PIN reset from logon screen requires Windows 10 Enterprise edition and the PIN reset service configured in Azure AD.
solution: For Enterprise: configure PIN reset service per MS docs. For non-Enterprise: user must logon with password, go to Settings > Account > Sign-in options > I forgot my PIN (destructive reset, ~30 min before new PIN usable).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User does not know password and asks for destructive PIN reset from Windows logon screen
rootCause: Destructive PIN reset from logon screen is not supported by design. It would destroy the credential and prevent logon.
solution: User must recover password first, then logon with password to perform PIN reset via Settings. Destructive reset cannot be done from logon screen.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Devices are both AAD Registered and Hybrid Azure AD Joined, causing nondeterministic Conditional Access evaluation
rootCause: AAD Registered + Hybrid Joined is an unsupported dual state. Hybrid join usually takes precedence but dual state can cause nondeterministic device evaluation.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 v1803+ which auto-cleans the AAD registered state. Do not AAD Register devices that will be Hybrid Joined. If already in dual state, remove the AAD registration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Need to know biometric and PIN failed attempt limits for WHfB lockout behavior
rootCause: Non-configurable limits: Biometric locks after 3 failed matches. PIN allows 4 attempts, then shows captcha (A1B23C), then 1 more attempt, then Windows reboots.
solution: These limits are hardcoded and non-configurable. After biometric lockout, user must sign in with another method. After PIN lockout sequence, device reboots.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB authentication fails on AADJ or Workplace Joined devices; DC certificate does not have KDC EKU
rootCause: AADJ and standalone/workplace-joined devices require KDC EKU on domain controller certificate for strict KDC validation. Hybrid AADJ trusts KDC via domain join so it works without EKU, but AADJ/WPJ does not.
solution: Deploy DC certificates with KDC EKU using autoenrollment. This ensures WHfB works for all join types. Enable strict KDC validation policy on hybrid devices for additional security.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Customer asks if WHfB is supported with Azure AD Domain Services (AAD DS)
rootCause: Azure AD Domain Services does not have the feature to support WHfB.
solution: WHfB is not supported with Azure AD Domain Services. Customer must use traditional AD DS or consider alternative authentication methods.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: WHfB credential queried via Graph API shows null displayName for windowsHelloForBusinessMethods
rootCause: Null displayName indicates either the user is not the registered owner of that device, or the device has been removed from Azure AD.
solution: Use ?$expand=device parameter to get definitive device ID and state. Check device object status in Azure AD. PowerShell: Get-MgUserAuthenticationWindowHelloForBusinessMethod -UserId {upn} -Expand device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: WHfB credential queried via Graph API shows device ID as all-zeros (00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000)
rootCause: Device ID of all-zeros indicates the device on which the credential was provisioned has been deleted from Azure AD. The credential is orphaned and unusable.
solution: The orphaned credential can be safely removed. Query with ?$expand=device to confirm. Use Graph API or PowerShell to manage credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: Microsoft Authenticator method queried via Graph API returns device: null for microsoftAuthenticatorMethods
rootCause: Device null for microsoftAuthenticatorMethods indicates the device has been removed from Azure AD.
solution: Confirm device removal in Azure AD. User may need to re-register Authenticator on a current device. Query: GET /users/{id}/authentication/microsoftAuthenticatorMethods?$expand=device. PowerShell: Get-MgUserAuthenticationMicrosoftAuthenticatorMethod -UserId {upn} -Expand device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Querying for passwordless credentials via Graph

symptom: WHfB policies deployed via both GPO and Intune produce unexpected configuration on Windows client devices (e.g. PIN length differs from Intune setting)
rootCause: When Group Policy and Intune are both used to configure Windows Hello for Business, GPO always takes precedence over Intune settings
solution: Use only one management tool for WHfB configuration. If both are required, be aware GPO settings override Intune (e.g. GPO minimum PIN length 6 overrides Intune minimum PIN length 4). Review and align policies across both management tools.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: PIN REMOVE button no longer appears in Windows Settings menu; user cannot remove Windows Hello PIN
rootCause: UI change or policy removed the REMOVE option from Settings
solution: Confirm user can logon with username/password first (local admin rights required). Run 'certutil -user -key -csp ngc' to inspect Hello container. Then run 'certutil -deleteHelloContainer' under user context to delete all keys. Note: Hello container may contain other keys (WHfB, FIDO2) that will need reprovisioning.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: User forgot Windows Hello for Business PIN and cannot sign in; no Enterprise edition for self-service PIN reset from logon screen
rootCause: PIN reset from logon screen requires Windows 10 Enterprise and Azure AD PIN reset service. Without Enterprise, only destructive PIN reset via Settings is available.
solution: With Win10 Enterprise: use PIN reset from logon screen (requires PIN reset service in Azure AD). Without Enterprise: logon with username/password, Settings > Account > Sign-in options > 'I forgot my PIN' (destructive reset, ~30 min wait). Without knowing password: no recovery from logon screen (by design).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Windows Hello for Business does not work with Azure AD Domain Services (AADDS)
rootCause: Azure AD Domain Services does not support Windows Hello for Business. This is a known platform limitation.
solution: WHfB is not supported with AADDS. Use alternative authentication methods or consider on-premises AD with Azure AD Connect for WHfB deployment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ

symptom: Devices AAD Registered via 'Add a Work Account' show nondeterministic auth and CA evaluation after attempting Hybrid Azure AD Join (dual state)
rootCause: AAD Registered + Hybrid AADJ is not a supported device state. Dual registration causes nondeterministic evaluation of device compliance and identity.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 1803+ which automatically cleans up AAD Registered state when Hybrid Joined. Do not manually attempt both join types. Hybrid AADJ should take precedence but dual state can cause access issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/StrongAuth Passwordless(WHfB FIDO phone based)/Hello for Business/WHfB: Internal FAQ
