sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS50177/50178: CSP admin blocked by Terms of Use or Session Control CA policy - actual error is ExternalChallengeNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers or SessionControlNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers
rootCause: Passthrough User authentication (CSP/GDAP) does not support certain CA policies including Terms of Use and Session Controls. Customer-facing error misleadingly says user does not exist.
solution: Customer must exclude the application or External User Type = Service Provider from the Terms of Use / Session Control CA policy. Use ASC Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to find true error in expert diagnostic logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS90072 PassThroughUserMfaError: Guest user signed in at home tenant cannot complete MFA requirement of resource tenant
rootCause: Guest user authenticated at home tenant but could not satisfy MFA requirement imposed by resource tenant conditional access policy during cross-tenant access.
solution: Investigate with ASC Auth Troubleshooter CA Diagnostic. Check resource tenant CA policies for MFA requirements. Consider configuring Cross-Tenant Access Policy trust settings to accept MFA claims from home tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS500212/500213: Cross-tenant access policy blocks B2B user access - outbound or inbound XTAP policy denies access
rootCause: AADSTS500212: Home tenant outbound XTAP policy does not allow access to resource tenant. AADSTS500213: Resource tenant inbound XTAP policy does not allow external user access.
solution: For 500212: Home tenant admin must update cross-tenant access settings to allow outbound access. For 500213: Resource tenant admin must update inbound cross-tenant access policy. Check XTAP Tab in ASC Auth Troubleshooter for policy details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS50058 with B2B: We could not find an account with that username or This username may be incorrect during guest sign-in
rootCause: Two variants: 1) UPN domain suffix not found as verified on any managed tenant and no MSA account found. 2) Domain suffix found on a managed tenant but no matching user with that UPN exists in that tenant.
solution: Verify user account exists in home tenant. Check UPN and domain suffix are correct. If user should be B2B guest, ensure they are invited and have redeemed invitation. Try using invite email Accept Invite link instead of direct/JIT redemption.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: B2B Invite Internal Users fails: Cannot convert internal user to B2B guest when user has Exchange license, direct federation domain, or is cloud-only not in Entra ID
rootCause: Preview limitation: Inviting internal users to B2B is blocked if (1) internal user has Exchange license, (2) user domain is configured for direct federation, or (3) internal user is cloud-only and main account not in Entra ID.
solution: Exchange license: unassign license first then retry. Direct federation / cloud-only: delete internal account and send new B2B invitation. Invitation is one-way - cannot remove B2B credentials once added, must delete user object and recreate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B Invite Internal Users

symptom: B2B guest user cannot leave external organization via My Account page - account disabled or blocked by conditional access, admin does not respond
rootCause: Guest user must sign into the org to leave it. If account disabled, blocked by CA, or external org admin/privacy contact does not respond, user cannot complete leave operation. Microsoft cannot remove guest users from tenants (by design).
solution: Advise customer to contact admin or privacy contact from target tenant to have guest account deleted. Microsoft support cannot remove guest users (by design). Reference: leave-the-organization doc. Related ICMs: 460572256, 460576296, 584421846.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : Guest user can't leave an organization

symptom: USGov application users get AADSTS900439 (USGClientNotSupportedOnPublicEndpoint) when signing in via public cloud endpoints (login.microsoftonline.com, login.windows.net)
rootCause: Azure AD enforces that US Government users must sign in using the .us endpoint (login.microsoftonline.us) instead of public cloud endpoints. Apps still using old endpoints are blocked.
solution: 1) Customer-owned app: update to use login.microsoftonline.us authority per national cloud docs. 2) Microsoft 1st party app: file ICM against that app team. 3) Need temporary allowance: file ICM to Azure AD ESTS/Incident Triage for allow-list. Use root cause 'National Cloud issues' in Service Desk.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/USGov applications will stop working with public cloud endpoints

symptom: Public cloud users get AADSTS900440 (PublicTenantNotSupportedOnUSGovEndpoint) when signing into apps hosted in US Government tenants via login.microsoftonline.us
rootCause: Azure AD blocks public cloud users from signing into apps hosted in US Government tenants. The app must host two versions pointing to the correct login endpoint for each cloud.
solution: Application must send users to the correct login endpoint. Host two versions (e.g., portal.azure.us and portal.azure.com). Update app to support public cloud users per national cloud onboarding guidance: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/develop/authentication-national-cloud
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/USGov applications will stop working with public cloud endpoints

symptom: Error inviting internal user to B2B collaboration: 'user has Exchange license assigned' or 'email matches direct federation domain' or 'email does not exist as Microsoft work or school account' or DirSync user cannot leave org
rootCause: B2B invite-internal-users has preview limitations: cannot invite users with Exchange license, from direct federation domains, or cloud-only accounts not in Entra ID. DirSync users with OnPremisesUserPrincipalName set are also blocked.
solution: 1) Unassign Exchange license before inviting. 2) Direct federation domains: not supported in preview. 3) Cloud-only: ensure account exists in Entra ID. 4) DirSync: modify AAD Connect sync rule to clear OnPremisesUserPrincipalName. Warning: invitation is one-way, B2B credentials cannot be removed without deleting user object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B Invite Internal Users

symptom: AADSTS50020/16003/50034/51004: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant. Common B2B sign-in failure when guest object missing or proxyAddress mismatch.
rootCause: External user signed in at home tenant but no matching guest account found in resource tenant. Causes: user not invited, proxyAddress conflict with Contact object, or redemption state stale after home PUID change.
solution: 1) Verify guest object in resource tenant via ASC. 2) Check ProxyAddress (smtp:user@domain) on guest. 3) If proxyAddress conflict: find duplicate via Get-MgUser/Get-MgContact filter on proxyAddresses, delete conflicting object, reinvite. 4) If PUID changed: reset redemption state. 5) Use direct redemption link (Resend invitation) to bypass proxyAddress lookup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS50177/50178: CSP/GDAP admin blocked by customer tenant Conditional Access (Terms of Use or Session Controls). Error misleadingly says 'user does not exist in tenant'.
rootCause: Passthrough User (CSP/GDAP) authentication does not support Terms of Use or Session Control CA policies. True error is ExternalChallengeNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers or SessionControlNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers.
solution: Customer tenant admin must exclude the application or External User Type = Service Provider from Terms of Use / Session Control CA policy. Use ASC Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to see true error name in Expert Diagnostic logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS90072 (PassThroughUserMfaError): Guest user authenticated at home tenant but fails MFA requirement at resource tenant. Error says 'user does not exist'.
rootCause: Guest user signed in at home tenant but could not complete MFA requirement of resource tenant CA policy. The error message is misleading.
solution: Investigate correlation ID with ASC Auth Troubleshooter CA Diagnostic. Resource tenant admin may need to adjust MFA CA policy for external users or configure cross-tenant trust for MFA claims via XTAP settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: AADSTS500212 (NotAllowedByOutboundPolicyTenant) or AADSTS500213 (NotAllowedByInboundPolicyTenant): Cross-tenant access blocked by XTAP policy.
rootCause: User home tenant outbound XTAP policy (500212) or resource tenant inbound XTAP policy (500213) blocks cross-tenant B2B access per configured Cross-Tenant Access Policy.
solution: 500212: Home tenant admin updates outbound cross-tenant access policy to allow resource tenant. 500213: Resource tenant admin updates inbound policy to allow home tenant. Configure at Entra portal > External Identities > Cross-tenant access settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: B2B guest sign-in fails with 'user does not exist' despite guest object existing in resource tenant - missing proxyAddress due to duplicate conflict at invite time
rootCause: Guest invite created duplicate proxyAddress conflict with existing Contact or user object, causing proxyAddress not to be stamped on guest. Without matching proxyAddress, eSTS cannot locate guest during sign-in.
solution: 1) Check guest proxyAddresses. 2) Find duplicate: Get-MgUser/Get-MgContact -Filter proxyAddresses/any(p:startswith(p,'smtp:user@domain')). 3) Delete conflicting object. 4) Reinvite or Resend invitation for direct redemption link. 5) Bulk fix: query all invited users missing proxyAddress, patch mail attribute to trigger recalculation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: B2B guest fails sign-in after home account recreated or PUID changed. Guest exists with accepted invitation but AltSecID no longer matches current home identity.
rootCause: Guest home account ObjectID/PUID changed (deleted and recreated). Resource tenant guest AltSecID references old PUID, so eSTS cannot match current user to existing guest object.
solution: Resource tenant admin resets redemption state on guest via Entra portal or Graph API, then user re-accepts invite. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/external-id/reset-redemption-status
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : AADSTS Error Codes

symptom: B2B guest user cannot leave external organization via My Account portal because account is disabled or blocked by Conditional Access in the external tenant, or admin is unresponsive.
rootCause: To leave an org, guest must sign into that org first. If account disabled/blocked by CA/admin unresponsive, self-service leave fails. Microsoft cannot remove guests from other tenants (data owned by tenant admin).
solution: Contact admin or privacy contact of the external tenant to delete the guest account. Microsoft support cannot delete guest users from other tenants (By Design, PG resolves IcMs as By Design). Doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/external-id/leave-the-organization
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : Guest user can't leave an organization

symptom: B2B cross-cloud invitation fails: This invitation is blocked by cross-tenant access policies
rootCause: Both home and resource tenant must configure cross-tenant access settings for cross-cloud. If either side not configured, invitations blocked.
solution: Both tenant admins configure cross-cloud settings in External ID > Cross-tenant access. Add partner by tenant ID. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/external-id/cross-cloud-settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration cross-cloud settings

symptom: B2B cross-cloud invitation fails: Microsoft B2B Cross Cloud Worker application has been disabled in the invited user tenant
rootCause: The Microsoft B2B Cross Cloud Worker service principal was disabled in the invited user tenant.
solution: Ask the invited user admin to re-enable the Microsoft B2B Cross Cloud Worker application, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration cross-cloud settings

symptom: Cross-cloud B2B guest user cannot access resources; sign-in fails at portal.azure.com
rootCause: Cross-cloud scenarios require tenanted endpoints. Generic portal.azure.com will not work.
solution: Use tenanted endpoint: portal.azure.com/<tenant>.onmicrosoft.com or portal.azure.com/<tenantId>.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration cross-cloud settings

symptom: Unsupported cross-tenant access policy error when viewing admin UX or calling MS Graph XTAP APIs
rootCause: Early private preview participants have outdated policy schema no longer supported.
solution: Manually update XTAP policy to new schema. Contact xtenantex@microsoft.com or raise ICM to IAM Services > Cross Tenant Experience Team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B collaboration cross-cloud settings

symptom: Guest users in Yammer cannot leave groups when guest permissions set to Restricted
rootCause: Known limitation of Restricted guest access permission level (guestUserRoleId: 2af84b1e).
solution: No workaround. Use Limited permission level if group leave is required. ICM: AAD Distributed Directory Services > RBAC Graph API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Entra ID User Management Restrict Guest Access

symptom: Guest users cannot access plans or tasks in Planner mobile app when guest permissions set to Restricted
rootCause: Known limitation of Restricted guest access permissions on Planner mobile.
solution: No workaround. Use Limited (default) permission level if Planner mobile needed for guests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Entra ID User Management Restrict Guest Access

symptom: Joining MTO fails: Joining a multitenant was unsuccessful. Tenant has GDAP relationship.
rootCause: MTO does not support tenants with Granular Delegated Admin Privileges (GDAP) relationship.
solution: Remove GDAP relationship before joining MTO, or wait for limitation to be lifted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Multi-Tenant Organization (MTO)

symptom: Tenant Restrictions v2 (TRv2) stops working after enabling Multi-Tenant Organization (MTO)
rootCause: Enabling MTO irreversibly blocks TRv2 functionality. Known compatibility issue during preview.
solution: Warn customers before enabling MTO. TRv2 will be permanently blocked until resolved. No rollback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Multi-Tenant Organization (MTO)

symptom: Customer asks about B2C private preview features: Captcha in custom policy, OnPageRenderStart, or OBO flow
rootCause: Captcha and OnPageRenderStart are private preview (IcM required). OBO flow is no longer supported.
solution: For Captcha/OnPageRenderStart: submit IcM. OBO flow: no longer supported. Public preview features: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/custom-policy-developer-notes
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C Private Preview and Public Preview Features

symptom: B2C tenant quota error creating users but Graph API shows total quota below 100%
rootCause: B2C has 1 company segment + 4 data segments with individual 250K limits. Individual segments can hit 100% while aggregate appears below.
solution: Check per-segment quotas via DS Explorer. Add/verify custom domain to raise limit from 1.25M to 5.25M. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/service-limits
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C Quota check and alert

symptom: B2C custom policy RestfulProvider API calls timing out after April 2025; previously working APIs now fail
rootCause: RestfulProvider timeout reduced from 30s to 10s as of April 2025. PG no longer accepting extension requests.
solution: Optimize REST API to respond within 10s. Use Kusto (AllIfxRequestEvent in CPIM) to check failure rates. Retries auto-happen with fresh 10s window. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/api-connectors-overview?pivots=b2c-custom-policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C RestfulProvider Timeout Update

symptom: B2C countryList block for SMS/Voice MFA not working; attackers bypass region restrictions despite UX changes
rootCause: countryList changes applied to parent policy XML instead of child relying party XML. B2C does not support parent policy inheritance for this feature.
solution: Apply all countryList changes to child relying party XML only (e.g. SignUpOrSignin.xml). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/phone-based-mfa#mitigate-fraudulent-sign-ups-for-custom-policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C SMS or Voice MFA Region Block

symptom: B2C tenant DDoS via default b2clogin.com domain causing throttling; need to block default domain
rootCause: b2clogin.com default domain publicly accessible, targetable by DDoS without WAF protection.
solution: 1) Configure custom domain via AFD + WAF. 2) Verify no legit traffic on b2clogin.com via Kusto AllIfxRequestEvent. 3) Get written customer consent. 4) Submit IcM. Block is permanent, rollout takes days via AAD Gateway.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C tenant throttling, service limit increase and default domain block handbook/Block B2C default domain (b2clogin) internal process

symptom: Error AADB2C90080: The provided grant has expired when using a refresh token to obtain a new access token in Azure AD B2C
rootCause: The refresh token being used has expired. B2C refresh tokens have a sliding window expiration and the token lifetime has been exceeded.
solution: Add error handling in the application to detect expired grant errors and prompt the user to interactively sign in again to obtain a new set of tokens.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90080

symptom: Error AADB2C90088: The provided grant has not been issued for this endpoint. Actual Value: B2C_1A_yyyy and Expected Value: B2C_1A_xxxx in Azure AD B2C custom policy
rootCause: The B2C policy used to acquire the authorization code differs from the policy used when exchanging it for an access token.
solution: Ensure the same B2C custom policy is used for both acquiring the authorization code and exchanging it for an access token. In ASP.NET Core, extract the tfp claim from the id_token and dynamically build the B2C authority URL then pass it to MSAL via WithB2CAuthority().
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90088

symptom: Error AADB2C90167: The signing certificate does not contain a private key and cannot be used for signing, when configuring SAML in Azure AD B2C
rootCause: A certificate uploaded to B2C policy keys (e.g. B2C_1A_SamlIdpCert) contains only the public key. The SAML2AssertionIssuer technical profile requires a private key to sign SAML responses.
solution: Upload a .PFX certificate file containing both the public and private keys to the B2C policy key. A .CER or .PEM with only the public key will cause this error.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90167

symptom: Error AADB2C90240: The provided token is malformed - external OpenID Connect identity provider issues JWE (JSON Web Encrypted) tokens to Azure AD B2C
rootCause: The external identity provider issues JWE tokens. Azure AD B2C does not support JWE tokens. The JWT header contains an enc parameter indicating encryption.
solution: Azure AD B2C does not support JWE tokens. Verify by base64-decoding the JWT header - if it contains an enc parameter (e.g. A256CBC-HS512), the token is JWE. The external IdP must issue standard JWT (JWS) tokens instead. Feature request is tracked.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90240

symptom: Error AADB2C90240: Token malformed - B2C cannot verify the signature of the token from an external identity provider
rootCause: The external IDP signed the token but B2C could not verify the signature because the client_secret is not configured or the JWK metadata endpoint is not accessible.
solution: Verify the client_secret is correctly configured and referenced in the B2C policy. For symmetric keys see docs. For asymmetric keys, ensure JWK metadata endpoints are publicly available from the token issuer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90240

symptom: Error AADB2C90289: We encountered an error connecting to the identity provider - caused by AADSTS7000222 expired client secret on external Entra ID app registration
rootCause: The client_secret in the B2C policy key for the external Entra ID identity provider has expired (AADSTS7000222).
solution: Generate a new client secret on the external tenant app registration (https://aka.ms/NewClientSecret). Update the B2C policy key: Azure Portal > Azure AD B2C > Identity Experience Framework > Policy Keys > update the key. Verify via ASC Tenant Explorer > Audit Logs > Category=KeyManagement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90289

symptom: Error AADB2C90289: We encountered an error connecting to the identity provider - caused by AADSTS50146 MissingCustomSigningKey when SSO/claims-mapping is configured
rootCause: The external app has custom signing keys due to claims-mapping configuration (AADSTS50146). B2C uses standard OIDC metadata URL which does not include app-specific signing key info.
solution: Update the metadata URL in B2C identity provider configuration to include appid query parameter: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/.well-known/openid-configuration?appid={applicationId}. This directs B2C to the app-specific signing key endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90289

symptom: Azure AD B2C is not available in Microsoft Azure Government - end of sale May 1, 2025, customers need to migrate
rootCause: Azure AD B2C Government (GCC preview) has been discontinued. After May 1, 2025, the B2C-G SKU is removed and new tenant creation is blocked.
solution: Advise customers to migrate from B2C Government to Entra External ID (commercial) for CIAM. MEEID for US Government has no timeline yet. After May 1 existing customers cannot create new B2C Government tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Azure Government

symptom: Azure AD B2C policy is being throttled with category Cpim.TPEngine.RequestPerPolicyTenantWithDevMode
rootCause: The B2C custom policy has DeploymentMode set to Development, which imposes a lower throttling limit
solution: Remove or change the DeploymentMode from Development in the TrustFrameworkPolicy element. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/trustframeworkpolicy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AAD B2C tenant throttling, service limit increase and default domain block handbook/Identifying the cause of B2C throttling

symptom: AADB2C90006: The redirect URI provided in the request is not registered for the client ID. Occurs during B2C sign-in.
rootCause: The redirect URI in the sign-in request does not match any registered redirect URI on the B2C application registration
solution: Add the exact redirect URI to the application registration in Azure AD B2C portal (Web App / SPA / Native). B2C tenants have long propagation times - wait up to 2 hours after adding.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90006

symptom: AADB2C90019: The key container with id in tenant does not have a valid key. Custom policy fails with expired or future IEF policy key.
rootCause: The IEF Policy Key container referenced by StorageReferenceId in custom policy XML contains only expired keys (exp <= now) or only future keys (nbf > now)
solution: Use Kusto query "Find Everything With Correlation ID" to locate the invalid key container name. In Azure AD B2C Portal > Identity Experience Framework > Policy Keys, verify nbf/exp timestamps. If expired, create or replace the key per docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/policy-keys-overview?pivots=b2c-custom-policy#replace-a-key
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90019

symptom: AADB2C90031: Policy does not specify a default user journey. Error during B2C custom policy execution.
rootCause: The relying party policy XML is missing a DefaultUserJourney element or the referenced user journey definition does not exist in parent policies
solution: Add <DefaultUserJourney ReferenceId="X"> to the relying party policy and ensure user journey X is defined in a parent policy. See https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/relyingparty#defaultuserjourney
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90031

symptom: AADB2C90031 during SAML federation: B2C returns "Policy does not specify a default user journey" when SAML IDP responds without RelayState parameter
rootCause: The SAML identity provider does not include the original RelayState parameter in its response to B2C SAML SP AuthRequest, causing B2C to lose context about which policy/journey to use
solution: Configure the external SAML IDP or SAML RP to include RelayState in its replies to the B2C SAML Technical Profile. Use Kusto logs and HAR trace to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90031

symptom: AADB2C90052 Invalid username or password in Azure AD B2C China (Mooncake). Login-NonInteractive technical profile fails.
rootCause: In B2C China (Mooncake), the Login-NonInteractive technical profile ProviderName must include the tenant ID in the URL. Without tenant ID, authentication fails.
solution: Set ProviderName to include tenant ID: <Item Key="ProviderName">https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}/</Item>. Also set METADATA to https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/{tenant}/.well-known/openid-configuration and authorization_endpoint to https://login.partner.microsoftonline.cn/{tenant}/oauth2/token
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90052

symptom: AADB2C90057: The provided application is not configured to allow the OAuth Implicit flow. B2C sign-in with response_type=id_token or token fails.
rootCause: The B2C application registration does not have Implicit grant flow enabled. B2C requires BOTH ID tokens and Access tokens to be enabled for implicit flow, even if only one is needed.
solution: In Entra Admin Center > App registrations > Authentication > Implicit grant and hybrid flows: enable both "ID tokens" and "Access tokens" checkboxes. Alternatively, set enableIdTokenIssuance and enableAccessTokenIssuance to true in app manifest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90057

symptom: AADB2C90063: Unable to create new IEF Policy key in B2C portal. Error: The key has failed to be created. There is a problem with the service. IDX10659 UnwrapKey failed.
rootCause: The B2C_1A_AdminClientEncryptionKeyContainer has been accidentally modified to contain more than 1 key. The service expects exactly 1 key for encryption/decryption and throws an error when multiple keys are found.
solution: Delete the B2C_1A_AdminClientEncryptionKeyContainer (which has multiple keys). Then create a new policy key using the manual option. The AdminClientEncryptionKeyContainer will be automatically regenerated with a single key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90063

symptom: AADB2C90063: Unable to create custom authentication extension in B2C. Error: There is a problem with the service.
rootCause: The Enterprise App "Azure Active Directory Authentication Extensions" (AppId 99045fe1-7639-4a75-9d4a-577b6ca3810f) does not exist in the tenant, which is required for custom authentication extensions.
solution: Create the service principal: Connect-MgGraph then New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId 99045fe1-7639-4a75-9d4a-577b6ca3810f. Wait 30 minutes for propagation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90063

symptom: AADB2C90068: An application of version V1 was found when searching for version V2, or the provided application is not valid against this service.
rootCause: The B2C application was not registered following the correct B2C app registration steps. The Supported account types is not set to "Accounts in any identity provider or organizational directory (for authenticating users with user flows)".
solution: Re-register the application following https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/tutorial-register-applications. Ensure Supported account types is set to "Accounts in any identity provider or organizational directory". Verify in app registration > Authentication settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90068

symptom: B2B guest user cannot remove themselves from microsoft.onmicrosoft.com (MSIT) tenant via self-service option due to error or conditional access policy
rootCause: Self-service account removal blocked by error or conditional access policy in MSIT corporate tenant
solution: Open ICM to Identity SRE / Corp Tenant (Azure AD) via ASC with guest user UPN and Object ID. MSIT IAM Support team will delete the guest object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/Azure AD B2B TSG : Request MSIT delete guest account in microsoft.onmicrosoft.com

symptom: B2B guest user with existing SSO session gets error that account does not exist in resource tenant when authenticating via Email OTP with configurable redemption
rootCause: Existing SSO session interferes with the Email OTP flow; user needs to explicitly switch account to trigger OTP
solution: User should choose "Use another account" and re-enter their username to trigger the OTP flow
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Custom Redemption Flow

symptom: B2B user prompted to choose between Entra ID and Microsoft account even after admin disables MSA as a redemption method in configurable redemption settings
rootCause: Known limitation: when user has the same email in both Entra ID and Microsoft accounts, the prompt always appears regardless of admin MSA redemption config
solution: Known limitation - no fix available. User will still see the prompt. Ensure correct redemption order is configured via cross-tenant access settings default policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Custom Redemption Flow

symptom: B2B guest users using email one-time passcode (OTP) get errors when signing in to non-tenanted endpoints (e.g., myapps.microsoft.com, portal.azure.com, Teams common endpoint)
rootCause: Email OTP guest users must sign in at endpoints that include tenant context; non-tenanted URLs are not supported for OTP authentication
solution: Use tenanted endpoint URLs with tenantid parameter, e.g., https://myapps.microsoft.com/?tenantid=<tenant-id>. Direct links to applications with tenant context also work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Email One-Time Password

symptom: B2B Google federation user gets "header too long" error during sign-in
rootCause: Accumulated browser cookies causing oversized HTTP headers in Google federation authentication flow
solution: Clear browser cookies or sign in using a private/incognito browser tab
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Google Federation

symptom: B2B Google federation guest users cannot sign in at endpoints without tenant context (e.g., myapps.microsoft.com, portal.azure.com)
rootCause: Google federated B2B users are not supported at non-tenanted sign-in endpoints; tenant context is required
solution: Use tenanted endpoint URLs, e.g., https://myapps.microsoft.com/?tenantid=<tenant-id>. Direct links to applications and resources with tenant context work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Google Federation

symptom: In Azure AD UI, "Manage invitations" option to reset B2B redemption status is grayed out
rootCause: User performing the operation lacks required permissions (Global Administrator or User Administrator role)
solution: Confirm the user has Global Administrator or User Administrator role. These permissions are required because the operation changes an existing user, not inviting a new guest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Reset Invitation Status

symptom: Reset B2B redemption status option is missing, grayed out, or returns error "The invited user already exists in the directory as object ID"
rootCause: External user was added via non-B2B API (SharePoint, OneDrive, PowerBI) and lacks required B2B properties on the user object
solution: 1) Enable SharePoint integration with AAD B2B. 2) Re-invite the user via PowerShell (New-MgInvitation) to stamp B2B properties. 3) Then reset redemption status via PowerShell or MS Graph API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Reset Invitation Status

symptom: Reset B2B redemption status via MS Graph API with App-Only mode fails with insufficient permissions error
rootCause: Known limitation: first-party app does not have required permission for directory layer change in App-only mode
solution: Use delegated (user context) authentication instead of App-only mode to call the reset redemption API
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Reset Invitation Status

symptom: Reset B2B redemption status fails with "Error Unknown"; audit log shows result="clientError" with empty resultReason
rootCause: Target user has multiple identities (e.g., SMS-based phone issuer + ExternalAzureAD). Backend error: "UserHasMultipleExternalCredentials"
solution: Remove the additional identity (e.g., SMS authentication method) from the user before resetting redemption status. PG tracking fix in Task 3462235.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Collaboration Reset Invitation Status

symptom: Admin with administrative unit (AU) scoped role cannot reset B2B invitation status
rootCause: B2B reset redemption API does not support indirect role assignments via administrative unit or group-based role scope
solution: Use tenant-scoped role assignment (direct Global Admin or User Admin) instead of AU-scoped assignment for reset redemption operations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Inviter profile picture is not showing to guest users in B2B invitation email
rootCause: B2B invitation service calls AAD Graph endpoint (graph.windows.net) for profilePhoto instead of MS Graph endpoint. By design.
solution: Workaround: manually set the inviter profile photo via the AAD Graph API endpoint (thumbnailPhoto). No timeline for fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: B2B allow/block domain list reached 25 KB (25,000 characters) limit; customer needs more space for domain entries
rootCause: The allow/deny list has a fixed 25 KB limit that cannot be increased
solution: Remove Entra ID domains from the allow/deny list and add them to XTAP (Cross-Tenant Access Policies) as allowed. This frees up space for non-AAD domains.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Error "Detected tenant domain name change from X to Y, however, domain name change is not currently supported" in B2B cross-tenant policy
rootCause: Current policy implementation does not support tenant domain name changes (by design)
solution: Change the domain back to the previous domain. Feature request tracked in Engineering work item 2693073.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Unknown EntraGDAP/AssistAPI audit log entries for "Add a partner to cross-tenant access setting" with Office365ConciergeSupport tenant (b4c546a4-7dac-46a6-a7dd-ed822a11efd3)
rootCause: Audit logs created when someone creates a support ticket in M365 Admin Center, which onboarded to Entra GDAP in Nov 2025
solution: Expected behavior. Check open/closed service requests in M365 Admin Portal to identify case creator. Additional questions to ICM team Office365 Admin Portal and Support / Assist 365.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: B2B invite recipients no longer receiving invitation emails from invites@microsoft.com (since Jan 2026)
rootCause: Starting January 2026, B2B invitations sent from tenant primary/default domain (e.g., invites@tenant.onmicrosoft.com) instead of invites@microsoft.com
solution: Check mail/spam/trash for emails from invites@{tenant}.onmicrosoft.com. Use "Resend invitation" option or copy direct invite redemption link and send manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: B2B guest users see unexpected branding change on password prompt (resource tenant branding replaced by home tenant branding)
rootCause: B2B auth flow changed to AAD Federated Authentication: guests redirected to home tenant for auth, showing home tenant custom branding instead of resource tenant
solution: Expected new behavior (by design). Users will see home tenant branding on password prompt. No action needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B Sign In Flow Update

symptom: Error "Invitations are blocked for this directory due to suspicious activity" when inviting B2B guests. Portal may show misleading "Insufficient privileges" error.
rootCause: Microsoft anomaly detection blocks suspicious bulk invites. Limits: Paid >30d=quota; Paid <=30d=200/day; Unpaid >30d=100/day; Unpaid <=30d=10/day
solution: See guide: guides/drafts/ado-wiki-a-b2b-invitations-blocked-tsg.md. Steps: 1) Check Jarvis logs. 2) Get business justification. 3) Raise ICM [ID][IDM][B2B] Unblock template. 4) TA unblocks via TM-SCIMID-TA-B2BOps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2B/B2B TSG : Invitations are blocked for this directory due to suspicious activity

symptom: Azure AD B2C MFA: Custom Greetings for MFA phone call setting at tenant level is not supported. There are no near-future plans to enable this feature.
rootCause: Product team explored this feature but encountered challenges; it was not enabled. No near-future plans exist.
solution: No workaround available. Custom greetings for MFA phone calls are not supported in Azure AD B2C. Advise customer this is a known limitation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Multi-Factor Authentication

symptom: Azure AD B2C Sign in with Apple ID stops working after ~6 months. Users cannot authenticate via Apple identity provider.
rootCause: Apple client secrets expire every 6 months. If not renewed, the federation breaks silently.
solution: Renew the Apple client secret before expiration. Admin receives a warning 1 month before expiry. Requires Apple developer account membership. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/identity-provider-apple-id
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Sign in with AppleID

symptom: Azure AD B2C OpenIdConnect ClaimsProvider does not retrieve claims from external IDP user info endpoint. Claims like email, name are missing after OIDC federation.
rootCause: By design, OpenIdConnect technical profiles do NOT call the user info endpoint. OIDC validates tokens using certificate public key via well-known configuration endpoint, unlike OAuth2 which calls userinfo.
solution: Use OAuth2 technical profile instead of OpenIdConnect if userinfo endpoint claims are needed. Alternatively, use RESTful technical profile to call the userinfo endpoint with the access token. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/secure-rest-api and https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/idp-pass-through-user-flow
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/AAD B2C OpenIdConnect ClaimsProvider does not retrieve claims from external IDP's User Info endpoint

symptom: Azure AD B2C OIDC federation with Amazon as Identity Provider fails. Amazon IDP provides incorrect modulus during key exchange, causing invalid decryption.
rootCause: Amazon has an identified issue where the third-party IDP fails to provide a correct modulus, resulting in invalid decryption during the key exchange process.
solution: Temporary workaround: Set Item Key Metadata Response Types to Id_Token (less secure, requires customer approval) to circumvent the key exchange. Permanent solution: Customer must work with Amazon to obtain the correct modulus. Ref ICM: 564151847, 407748942, 550082381.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Amazon Federation as Identity Provider (OIDC-FED)

symptom: Azure AD B2C with Apple IDP: Apple does not return email, firstName, lastName claims on sign-up. User created in B2C tenant is missing these values. Logs show claim not found errors for user.name.firstName, user.name.lastName, user.email.
rootCause: Apple only sends user profile information (first name, last name, email) on the very first sign-in with a new relying party. Subsequent sign-ins do not include these claims.
solution: Use Custom Policies instead of User Flows. Configure email as an output claim in the Apple technical profile. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory-b2c/identity-provider-apple-id?pivots=b2c-custom-policy#configure-apple-as-an-identity-provider-1
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Apple Idp doesn't send email Claim or firstname lastname

symptom: Azure AD B2C Custom OIDC IDP returns 404 error when doing POST to /authresp endpoint. RouteException: The request contained an invalid or missing policy.
rootCause: The redirect URL from the external IDP back to B2C does not contain the correct policy parameter, causing B2C to throw a RouteException with 404 status.
solution: Collect HAR trace from customer. Check AllIfxRequestEvent logs with correlation ID for RouteException. Verify the external IDP redirect URL includes the correct B2C policy name in the state/redirect parameters. Ensure the IDP is configured to pass back the full redirect URI including policy reference.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Azure AD B2C - Custom OIDC IDP errors with 404 not found at authresp

symptom: Azure AD B2C Mooncake (21Vianet): Custom Policies with OpenID Connect authentication suddenly stops working or fails during initial setup. Users cannot authenticate via local account sign-in with OIDC technical profile.
rootCause: The ProviderName key behavior changed in Mooncake. The value https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/ alone is no longer sufficient; it now requires the tenant ID appended.
solution: Change the ProviderName metadata in the OIDC technical profile from <Item Key="ProviderName">https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/</Item> to <Item Key="ProviderName">https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}</Item>. Ref ICM: 402958181.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Azure AD B2C - Mooncake Custom Policies OpenID Connect configuration -  Authentication Issues

symptom: Custom Greetings for MFA phone call setting at tenant level is not available in Azure AD B2C
rootCause: Product team explored this feature but had challenges due to which it was not enabled. No near-future plans to enable this feature.
solution: Not supported. No workaround available. MFA can be enabled at user flow/custom policy level or tenant level, but custom greetings for phone call are not supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Multi-Factor Authentication

symptom: Sign in with Apple integration in Azure AD B2C stops working after approximately 6 months
rootCause: Apple client secrets expire every 6 months automatically.
solution: Renew the Apple secret before expiration. Admin receives a warning when 1 month remains. Apple developer account membership is required to configure Sign in with Apple.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Sign in with AppleID

symptom: Azure AD B2C OIDC federation with Amazon IDP fails during authentication. Key exchange process fails with invalid decryption error.
rootCause: Amazon IDP provides an incorrect modulus value, causing invalid decryption during the key exchange process.
solution: Temporary workaround: set Item Key Metadata Response Types to Id_Token (less secure, needs customer approval) to bypass the key exchange. Permanent fix: customer must work directly with Amazon to obtain the correct modulus.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Amazon Federation as Identity Provider (OIDC-FED)

symptom: Sign-up with Apple IDP in Azure AD B2C does not return email, firstname, or lastname. User created in B2C is missing these values. Log shows: A claim could not be found for lookup claim with id user.name.firstName/lastName/user.email.
rootCause: Apple only provides user profile information (first name, last name, email) on the very first sign-in with a new relying party. Subsequent sign-ins do not include this data.
solution: Use Custom Policies where email can be added as an output claim. Configure Apple as identity provider with custom policy per Microsoft docs: identity-provider-apple-id?pivots=b2c-custom-policy#configure-apple-as-an-identity-provider-1
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Apple Idp doesn't send email Claim or firstname lastname

symptom: Azure AD B2C returns 404 error on POST to /authresp endpoint when using custom OIDC IDP. RouteException: The request contained an invalid or missing policy.
rootCause: The STATE parameter is being passed in the body of the POST request instead of as a query string parameter. B2C backend cannot parse the state parameter when sent in the request body.
solution: Pass the state parameter as query string when sending the POST request to the authresp endpoint, not in the body of the request.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Azure AD B2C - Custom OIDC IDP errors with 404 not found at authresp

symptom: Azure AD B2C custom policies with OpenID Connect in Mooncake (Azure China) fail authentication or suddenly stop working. ProviderName set to https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/ does not work.
rootCause: The ProviderName key behavior changed. It now requires the tenant ID to be appended to the URL instead of just the base STS endpoint.
solution: Change ProviderName in custom policy technical profile metadata from https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/ to https://sts.chinacloudapi.cn/{tenantId}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Azure AD B2C - Mooncake Custom Policies OpenID Connect configuration -  Authentication Issues

symptom: Azure Support Center (ASC) Tenant Explorer shows a pinned B2C tenant that cannot be expanded or browsed.
rootCause: Known bug with ASC Tenant Explorer when a B2C tenant is pinned.
solution: Re-pin the tenant via 3 dots menu -> Pin tenant, immediately un-pin it via 3 dots menu -> Un-pin tenant, refresh the page, then add the target tenant again without pinning.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Azure AD B2C Customer Data Collection

symptom: Azure AD B2C does not clear SSO cookies on logout. After logout, user can access app without re-auth. HAR shows logout response does not clear x-ms-cpim-sso cookie.
rootCause: Custom policy has SingleSignOn Scope='Application' in UserJourneyBehaviors. B2C may not properly clear SSO session cookies during logout with this scope.
solution: Review RelyingParty SingleSignOn Scope. Change from 'Application' to 'Tenant' or 'Policy'. Compare working vs non-working HAR traces for cookie handling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/B2C Logout Scenarios

symptom: B2C custom policies: Continue/Create button not grayed during email verification (Signup/Forgot Password). Users can click before verification completes. Works in user flows.
rootCause: Missing Display Controls (VerificationControl) with SSPR-based email verification. Without them, B2C does not enforce verification completion before enabling submit.
solution: Add: 1) verificationCode ClaimType, 2) emailVerificationControl/emailVerificationSSPRControl DisplayControls, 3) AadSspr-SendCode/VerifyCode TPs, 4) Update ForgotPassword/LocalAccountDiscovery to use DisplayControlReferenceId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Continue or Create Button not Grayed while doing Signup or forgot Password Flow using Custom Policies

symptom: B2C portal error: cannot find b2c-extensions-app. Application missing from App registrations.
rootCause: b2c-extensions-app soft/hard-deleted, or exists with wrong Redirect URI (should be https://extensions.cpim.windows.net).
solution: 1) Check App registrations for b2c-extensions-app. 2) Fix Redirect URI to https://extensions.cpim.windows.net. 3) If deleted, restore from Deleted apps (30-day window). 4) If hard-deleted, submit IcM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/How to fix when Azure AD B2C cannot find b2c-extensions-app

symptom: B2C GenerateJson produces escaped double quotes in JSON array values. dest shows as escaped string instead of proper value.
rootCause: CreateJsonArray intermediate step then GenerateJson causes double-encoding. First transformation outputs JSON string, second re-escapes it.
solution: Remove CreateJsonArray. Pass original claim directly to GenerateJson. Test with minimal policy using SelfAssertedAttributeProvider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/How to test GenerateJson Claim Transformation Method

symptom: B2C + SAML IDP: popup appears/vanishes when SPA acquires access token. MSAL.js silent iframe fails, falls back to popup.
rootCause: SAML IDP metadata has HTTP-POST before HTTP-Redirect in SingleSignOnService. iframe POST blocked by browser.
solution: Reorder SAML IDP metadata: HTTP-Redirect before HTTP-POST in SingleSignOnService. Verify via Kusto IfxSamlProviderRequestEvent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/How to troubleshoot Authentication issues With a SAML IDP in Azure AD B2C

symptom: Azure AD B2C SSO does not work after password reset when using custom policies - user is not recognized in subsequent authentication requests because the SSO session cookie (x-ms-cpim-sso) lacks required claims
rootCause: The password reset UserJourney does not enlist into SM-AAD session management, so objectId/signInName/authenticationSource claims are not written to the SSO cookie after password reset
solution: Add an OrchestrationStep in the password reset UserJourney that calls a ClaimsTransformation TechnicalProfile (CT-EnlistIntoSM-AAD) with UseTechnicalProfileForSessionManagement referencing SM-AAD. This writes objectId into the x-ms-cpim-sso cookie enabling SSO post password reset
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/Azure AD B2C SSO post password rest using custom policies

symptom: Password incorrect error message does not change in Azure AD B2C custom policy even after modifying the metadata in login-NonInteractive technical profile
rootCause: Known B2C bug: localization breaks when login-NonInteractive TechnicalProfile metadata contains <Item Key='grant_type'>password</Item>
solution: Remove the line <Item Key='grant_type'>password</Item> from the login-NonInteractive TechnicalProfile metadata and re-upload the custom policy. Also update LocalizedResources/LocalizedString as they take precedence over metadata
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/Customize or localize error messages in B2C

symptom: Azure AD B2C custom policy months dropdown is broken when using Thai language (th) localization
rootCause: Default localization file has incorrect format for Thai language months string - commas missing between month values
solution: Override months string in custom policy LocalizedResources for Thai: <LocalizedString ElementType='UxElement' StringId='months'>,,,,,,,,,,,</LocalizedString>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/Customize or localize error messages in B2C

symptom: MFA SMS localization not working for Estonian language (et) in Azure AD B2C despite it being listed as a supported MFA language
rootCause: Estonian language is incorrectly treated as unsupported when MFA request is initiated from B2C tenant (ADO Bug#1290638)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/Customize or localize error messages in B2C

symptom: Error uploading B2C custom policy: The policy being uploaded is not XML or is not correctly formatted: Data at the root level is invalid. Line 1, position 1
rootCause: Hidden BOM (Byte Order Mark) or invisible space character at position 1:2 in the XML policy file that is not visible in VSCode
solution: Use a hex editor or Beyond Compare to identify the hidden character at line 1 position 1, remove it (or copy content to a new file), and re-upload. The extra space/BOM causes XML parsing to fail
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/The policy being uploaded is not XML or is not correctly formatted

symptom: Sign-in logs in customer Log Analytics workspace show timestamps with >2 hour delay from original event time, but IdxIngestion shows normal latency (<10 seconds processing)
rootCause: The ingestion pipeline (UDI) processed logs normally; the delay occurs at the Azure Monitor consumption side when routing to Log Analytics/Sentinel
solution: Query IdxIngestion table (idsharedwus cluster) with correlation ID. If Latency is <2h and LogReceived is within minutes of original event, ingestion is healthy. Cut collab/IcM to Azure Monitor team for consumption-side investigation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Activity Logs in Azure Monitor/TSG - Reporting Latency for Sign In Logs sent to Azure Monitor

symptom: Sign-in/audit logs show >2 hour processing latency in IdxIngestion table (Latency column shows thousands of seconds)
rootCause: UDI log processing delay at the Reporting and Audit Insights pipeline level; data took abnormally long between arrival and availability for consumption
solution: Query IdxIngestion (idsharedwus cluster) to confirm ProcessingDuration/Latency is excessive. Raise ICM to IDX/Reporting and Audit Insights team if customer needs RCA. Check for re-ingestion events (large env_time gaps between entries for same UniqueId)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Activity Logs in Azure Monitor/TSG - Reporting Latency for Sign In Logs sent to Azure Monitor

symptom: Sign-in or audit log event cannot be found in IdxIngestion table when queried by correlation ID and tenant ID
rootCause: The log event was never received or processed by the UDI pipeline; possible upstream partner service issue (ESTS, MFA, MSODS)
solution: Confirm correlation ID, tenant ID, and timestamps are correct and in UTC. If still no results, escalate via ICM to IDX/Reporting and Audit Insights team per the CRI escalation process
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Activity Logs in Azure Monitor/TSG - Reporting Latency for Sign In Logs sent to Azure Monitor

symptom: Entra log event (sign-in, audit, managed identity, service principal, or Graph activity) is missing from Log Analytics/Sentinel but present in Entra ID portal sign-in logs
rootCause: Data flow issue between Entra reporting pipeline and Azure Monitor diagnostic settings routing; could be at ingestion (UDI), streaming, or consumption side
solution: Verify diagnostic settings are configured. Query IdxIngestion (idsharedwus, database IdxIngestion) filtering by Type=="ShoeboxEventAudit", Message matching log type, TenantId and UniqueId. No results: IcM to IDX/Reporting and Audit Insights. Results found: request BLOB path from IDX team, then transfer case to Azure Monitor with correlationID + timestamp + BLOB path
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Activity Logs in Azure Monitor/TSG - Troubleshoot Activity Logs sent to Azure Monitor

symptom: Audit records show app@sharepoint as the user performing file activities (search queries, file access, list operations) instead of a real user account
rootCause: The application has SharePoint App-Only access, performing organization-wide actions on behalf of a user/admin/service. Common triggers: M365 Groups creation, Teams creation, compliance features (retention policies, eDiscovery holds, auto-labeling)
solution: This is expected behavior. Check ApplicationDisplayName and EventData fields in the audit record to identify the triggering scenario. Multiple rapid audit records from app@sharepoint within seconds indicate automated application-triggered actions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Azure AD Auditing: Sharepoint App Account

symptom: Admin cannot see sign-ins for Password-based SSO application in Entra ID sign-in logs
rootCause: Password-based SSO apps use IAMUX (Microsoft App Access Panel) as the client application for ESTS CA policy evaluation; the 3rd party app appears as the resource, not the client
solution: Check sign-ins for "Microsoft App Access Panel" application, then add filter by resource (Password-based SSO app display name or app ID). Check both interactive and non-interactive user sign-ins tabs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: User type for the same user is inconsistent in sign-in logs - sometimes showing as member and sometimes as guest
rootCause: Sign-in logs include cross-tenant sign-ins. User is "member" in home tenant (resourceTenantId == homeTenantId) and "guest" in resource tenant (resourceTenantId != homeTenantId)
solution: Compare resourceTenantId and homeTenantId fields in the sign-in log entry. If they differ, the user is a guest in the resource tenant. This is expected behavior for B2B/cross-tenant scenarios
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Part of the IP address shows as .XXX (redacted) in Entra ID sign-in logs
rootCause: Azure AD redacts part of IP addresses for privacy in cross-tenant sign-ins or when user identity confidence is low
solution: This is expected behavior. IP redaction occurs in cross-tenant scenarios (e.g., CSP technician signing into managed tenant). No workaround available
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Identity Protection Risky Detections shows a different IP address than what appears in the sign-in log for the same event
rootCause: MFA sign-ins aggregate multiple sub-events (browser + phone MFA challenge) with different IPs. Risk detected on one IP, sign-in log shows another (most recent) IP
solution: Expected behavior. MFA spans multiple clients (PC browser + phone) which may use different IPs. Risk detection IP and sign-in log IP may legitimately differ. No config change needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Sign-ins from a user who does not belong to the tenant appear in the tenant sign-in logs
rootCause: The user is a pass-through user (UserIsPassthru=1) who signed into a resource in the tenant without being a direct member
solution: Check sign-in log in ASC > click Troubleshoot this sign-in > Expert View PerRequestLogs > check if UserIsPassthru=1. Refer to MS docs on auditing troubleshooting scenarios for investigating successful logins by external users
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Location information is missing for some entries in Entra ID sign-in logs (both failed and successful events)
rootCause: For failed events: location is populated late in the sign-in workflow; early failures skip location processing. For successful events: IP-to-country resolution is best-effort and sometimes data is unavailable
solution: For failed sign-ins: by design when failure occurs before location processing (check if UPN shows as GUID = very early failure). For successful sign-ins: occasional missing location is expected. Systematic drops to 0% should be escalated to Protection Signals and Intelligence team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Customer needs last sign-in date for users but sign-in log retention period has expired (7 days unlicensed, 30 days licensed)
rootCause: Sign-in logs have limited retention; signInActivity metadata on user objects persists beyond log retention but requires P1/P2 license
solution: Use Graph API: GET /beta/users?$select=displayName,userPrincipalName,signInActivity&$top=999. Requires app registration with AuditLog.Read.All + User.Read.All permissions (application type). Export results via PowerShell client credentials flow script
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Get Azure AD users Last Sign On metadata values

symptom: Customer needs to retrieve non-interactive user sign-ins, service principal sign-ins, or managed identity sign-ins via MS Graph API but only sees interactive sign-ins
rootCause: Default signIns endpoint returns interactive sign-ins; non-interactive/SP/managed identity require explicit signInEventTypes filter on beta endpoint
solution: Use beta endpoint with signInEventTypes filter: /beta/auditLogs/signIns?$filter=signInEventTypes/any(t: t eq 'nonInteractiveUser'). Also available via Graph PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes "AuditLog.Read.All"; Get-MgAuditLogSignIn -Filter "signInEventTypes/any(t: t eq 'nonInteractiveUser')"
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/How To Retrieve Non-Interactive Sign-ins Using MS Graph API

symptom: Need to verify if tenant has Entra ID Premium subscription for reporting features; sign-in logs to Azure Monitor require Premium
rootCause: AAD_PREMIUM or AAD_PREMIUM_P2 required, standalone or bundled in EMS, EMSPREMIUM, SPE_E3, SPE_E5, M365EDU_A3/A5, IDENTITY_THREAT_PROTECTION, SPE_F1, or GOV SKUs
solution: Check ASC > Customer > Subscriptions tab for AAD_PREMIUM/AAD_PREMIUM_P2 or bundles. Expand row for Start/Expiry dates. If SKU unknown, look up SkuId in MS docs "Product names and service plan identifiers for licensing". No premium = no sign-in logs to Azure Monitor
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/How to Verify if an AAD Tenant has a Premium subscription

symptom: Sign-in logs in Azure Monitor (Log Analytics) show timestamps >2 hours delayed from original event timestamp in Entra ID portal
rootCause: UDI (central log processor) adds processing latency between ingestion partner (ESTS/MFA/Risk) and consumer partner (Azure Monitor). Delays from: re-ingestion events (PG backfill with large date gaps), large log splitting, or partner-side delays.
solution: Query idsharedwus/Idxingestion table with correlationId to check env_time, LogReceived, Latency, ProcessingDuration. Compare original event timestamp (from ASC) with env_time to identify delay source. Normal processing is seconds; large gaps in env_time indicate re-ingestion events.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Activity Logs in Azure Monitor/TSG - Reporting Latency for Sign In Logs sent to Azure Monitor

symptom: Audit records show app@sharepoint as the user who performed file activities, search queries, or list operations instead of the actual user
rootCause: SharePoint App-Only access mechanism: applications with granted permissions perform organization-wide actions (search, retention policies, eDiscovery) on behalf of users/admins/services.
solution: Check ApplicationDisplayName and EventData fields in the audit record to identify the triggering scenario (M365 Groups creation, Teams creation, compliance features like retention policies, eDiscovery holds, auto-applying sensitivity labels).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Azure AD Auditing: Sharepoint App Account

symptom: User type for the same user shows inconsistently in sign-in logs - sometimes member, sometimes guest
rootCause: Sign-in logs also show cross-tenant sign-ins. When resourceTenantId differs from homeTenantId, user type shows as guest. When both match (home tenant), user type shows as member.
solution: Check resourceTenantId and homeTenantId fields. Different values = cross-tenant sign-in (user type=guest). Same values = home tenant sign-in (user type=member). This is expected behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: IP address shows .XXX partial redaction in Entra ID sign-in logs
rootCause: Azure AD redacts part of IP for privacy in two scenarios: (1) cross-tenant sign-ins (e.g., CSP technician), (2) service could not determine user identity with sufficient confidence to confirm user belongs to viewing tenant.
solution: Expected privacy protection behavior. No action needed. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/reports-monitoring/reports-faq
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Identity Protection risky detection shows a different IP address than what appears in the corresponding sign-in log entry
rootCause: MFA sign-ins compose multiple sub-events (browser + phone) aggregated into one row. Risk detected on a different sub-event IP than the final aggregated MFA event. Sign-in log defaults to most recent IP.
solution: Expected behavior. MFA spans multiple IPs (PC browser + phone). Risky detection IP and final sign-in IP may legitimately differ. Verify both IPs belong to the user context. Ref ICM: 264383498.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Need to retrieve non-interactive user sign-ins, service principal sign-ins, or managed identity sign-ins via MS Graph API but default endpoint only returns interactive sign-ins
rootCause: The v1.0 Graph API /auditLogs/signIns returns interactive sign-ins by default. Non-interactive, service principal, and managed identity sign-ins require filtering by signInEventTypes.
solution: Use beta endpoint with signInEventTypes filter: /beta/auditLogs/signIns?$filter=signInEventTypes/any(t: t eq 'nonInteractiveUser'). Values: interactiveUser, nonInteractiveUser, servicePrincipal, managedIdentity. PowerShell: Get-MgAuditLogSignIn -Filter "signInEventTypes/any(t: t eq 'nonInteractiveUser')".
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/How To Retrieve Non-Interactive Sign-ins Using MS Graph API

symptom: User field shows Object ID or GUID instead of UPN in Entra sign-in logs for unauthenticated users (error 50058), during aggregation delay, or when user cancels MFA prompt
rootCause: Multiple transient causes: (1) Unauthenticated user with interrupt 50058, (2) Sign-in data not yet aggregated with MFA data (takes up to 2 hours), (3) User chose No Its not me on mobile causing interrupted sign-in
solution: For 50058: expected behavior. For aggregation delay: wait up to 2 hours. For interrupted sign-in: expected when UPN cannot be resolved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot User field is showing as Object ID or GUID

symptom: User field shows as 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 (all-zeros GUID) in sign-in logs
rootCause: Sign-in is caused by Tenant Restrictions, which uses a special all-zeros user ID
solution: Use TSG - Troubleshoot Tenant Restrictions Sign-ins to investigate tenant restriction configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot User field is showing as Object ID or GUID

symptom: User field shows Object ID in sign-in logs when passwordless authentication is used
rootCause: Passwordless feature sign-ins may show GUID; authenticationDetail field will indicate passwordless
solution: Check authenticationDetail field for passwordless indicator. Refer to Reporting Change History February 2022.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot User field is showing as Object ID or GUID

symptom: No data displayed in Entra Authentication Methods Activity report after license upgrade or app deletion
rootCause: Two causes: (1) Recently upgraded to Premium and data needs days to populate, (2) IAMTenantCrawler first-party app was deleted or had permissions removed
solution: For new Premium tenants: wait a few days. For IAMTenantCrawler: verify app exists with correct permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Azure AD Authentication Methods Activity Blade

symptom: Bulk operation failed error when downloading Authentication Methods reports on large tenants
rootCause: Bulk operations in Entra admin portal timeout on very large tenants due to scaling limitations
solution: Split records into smaller batches by filtering on group type or user name. Or use MS Graph PowerShell for MFA reporting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Azure AD Authentication Methods Activity Blade

symptom: User missing from User Registration Details report
rootCause: By design: blocked users excluded; soft-deleted and disabled users not listed; newly created users may have delay of days
solution: Check if user is blocked/soft-deleted/disabled. For new users, wait a few days.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: User shows as SSPRCapable in User Registration Details but has no methods registered
rootCause: Administrators are always SSPR capable regardless of registered methods, per administrator reset policy differences
solution: Check the users role assignments. Admins are always SSPR capable by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: User incorrectly marked as Multifactor Authentication Capable in User Registration Details
rootCause: User must be included as target in tenant MFA policy to appear as MFA capable
solution: Verify user is targeted in tenant MFA policy under Authentication methods policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: Deleted user still appearing in User Registration Details report
rootCause: Backend service did not receive the delete event to remove user from reporting database
solution: Open IcM to IDX/Reporting and Auditing Insights with ObjectIDs or UPNs of deleted users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: Bulk download fails for User Registration Details report with timeout or 429 throttling error
rootCause: Azure Portal downloader access token expires mid-download or Graph API throttling limit reached
solution: Run smaller downloads or use MS Graph API: GET /reports/authenticationMethods/userRegistrationDetails.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: defaultMfaMethod in User Registration Details does not match portal Authentication Methods blade
rootCause: Hardware token (hardwareOneTimePasscode) not shown as default MFA method by design
solution: Use userPreferredMethodForSecondaryAuthentication property via MS Graph API for both hardware and software tokens.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/Common Issues with User Registration Details report

symptom: User sign-in summary dashboard shows no data or is empty
rootCause: signinEventsSummary report requires AAD P2 license; data only populated after P2 license assigned
solution: Assign Azure AD P2 license to the tenant. Report owned by IDX/Reports and Auditing team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/SigninEventsSummary - "User sign in summary" dashboard

symptom: Authentication methods mismatch between User Authentication Methods blade and User Registration Details report
