sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/TSGs/Workflow scheduling not supported for disabled workflows

symptom: PIM Activate button is greyed out when trying to activate eligible Azure RBAC role assignment in Azure portal
rootCause: User already has an active/direct role assignment for the same role, or has a pending scheduled activation request that blocks new activation
solution: Check for existing active assignments (banner shows 'role assignment exists'). For pending scheduled activations: My Roles > click pending requests notification > Cancel the pending request > then activate immediately. If no existing assignments found, check ApiRole WebRole logs for service-side API returns.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Resources Troubleshooting Workflow

symptom: MFA is configured as required for PIM role activation at Subscription scope, but user is not prompted for MFA when activating the same role at Resource Group or Resource scope
rootCause: PIM role settings (including MFA requirement) do not inherit between Subscription, Resource Group, and Resource scopes. Each scope has independent role settings configuration.
solution: Configure 'Require Multi-Factor Authentication on activation' independently at each scope level (Subscription, Resource Group, Resource) where MFA enforcement is needed. Alert subscription/resource group owner about the role setting inconsistency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Resources Troubleshooting Workflow

symptom: After PIM activation of Azure AD Joined Device Local Administrator role, user does not have Local Administrator (BUILTIN\Administrators) privileges on the Azure AD Joined device
rootCause: Cached Primary Refresh Token (PRT) was issued before PIM role activation. PRT only auto-refreshes every 4 hours, so new role claims (including Device Local Admin) are not reflected until PRT is renewed.
solution: 1) Run 'dsregcmd /refreshprt' in Command Prompt to schedule PRT refresh. 2) Wait 1-2 minutes. 3) Sign out of Windows session. 4) Sign back in. 5) Verify with 'whoami /all' that BUILTIN\Administrators group is listed. If still failing, collect Auth.zip traces (start-auth.ps1) and check Aad_Analytic.evtx Event IDs 1018,1144,1145,1028,1032,1246.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/TSG: Elevating to the Azure AD Joined Device Local Administrator Role with Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

symptom: After activating Entra role via PIM, user cannot use elevated permissions in M365 Admin Center, Intune Portal, SharePoint Online, or Exchange Online; receives insufficient permission or access denied errors
rootCause: Role assignments activated in PIM are stored in AAD/MSODS and replicated to application-specific directory stores via ForwardSync with ~15min delay. Applications also cache role assignment info locally for 15+ minutes from previous sign-ins.
solution: 1) Wait 10-15 minutes after PIM activation before signing into target applications. 2) Completely sign out of ALL sessions before attempting access. 3) Use incognito/private browser. 4) Do NOT retry within 15 minutes as it refreshes local app cache without new role data. For persistent issues: collect HAR/Fiddler trace from incognito browser, check id_token claims for role assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/TSG: PIM Role Activation Delays

symptom: Access Review Agent activation or administration fails with authentication errors when the admin account uses PIM-activated (just-in-time) Identity Governance Administrator role
rootCause: The Access Review Agent runs using the identity of the activating administrator. PIM-activated (non-standing) role assignments cause authentication failures because the agent requires permanent role presence for continuous operations.
solution: Assign Identity Governance Administrator + Lifecycle Workflows Administrator roles as standing (permanent) assignments directly or through a group. Do NOT use PIM activation. Note: This feature public preview is being removed on March 27, 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Security Copilot Access Review Agent

symptom: Access Reviews historical data older than 12 months becomes inaccessible via Graph API, Entra portal UI, and review history reports after August 1, 2025 enforcement
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID Governance enforces new data retention policy: Access Reviews data limited to 12 months via API/portal. Data older than 1 year permanently inaccessible. MC posts: MC1101895 (WW/GCC), MC1102763 (Gallatin).
solution: Export all required historical data BEFORE August 15, 2025 using custom reports script: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/id-governance/custom-entitlement-report-with-adx-and-entra-id. Import into ADX or other storage. No extensions granted. ICM path: AAD Access Reviews / Access Reviews team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Upcoming Changes to Identity Governance Data Retention

symptom: Linux/Unix MEDS domain join: kinit fails with 'Preauthentication failed' (error -1765328360) when two Azure AD accounts share the same mailnickname, causing SAMAccountName collision in MEDS
rootCause: When two accounts in Azure AD have the same mailnickname, MEDS syncs them with different SAMAccountNames (one with random GUID appended). kinit resolves UPN to pre-Windows 2000 format (REALM\SAMACCOUNTNAME), authenticating the wrong account whose password does not match.
solution: Use the full SAMAccountName with GUID suffix for kinit (e.g., kinit 'test (1234)@DOMAIN.COM'), or fix the SAMAccountName conflict in Azure AD by ensuring unique mailnickname values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services - Unix Domain Join Troubleshooting

symptom: RDP from non-MEDS-joined client to MEDS-joined server fails when NTLM password hash sync is disabled in MEDS security settings. NLA requires Kerberos but non-domain-joined client falls back to NTLM which is also disabled.
rootCause: Customer hardened MEDS security by disabling NTLM hash sync. Non-domain-joined clients cannot use Kerberos (no domain membership) and NTLM fallback is blocked, making RDP with NLA impossible.
solution: Workarounds: (1) Domain-join the client machine, (2) Re-enable NTLM password hash sync and have user change password to trigger re-sync, (3) Disable NLA on target server (less secure).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services - Windows Domain Join Troubleshooting

symptom: MEDS user account locked out after 5 bad password attempts within 2 minutes. Account remains locked for 30 minutes. Common causes: forgotten password, cached old credentials, password sync delay (~30s from Entra to MEDS), or potential spray attack.
rootCause: MEDS lockout policy: 5 bad password attempts in 2 minutes triggers 30-minute lockout. Error codes: 0xC000006A (bad password), 0xC0000071 (expired password), 0xC0000234 (account locked), 0xC0000064 (nonexistent username - potential spray attack).
solution: Use Azure Support Center > Resource Explorer > User Object Comparison to check BadPwdCount and BadPasswordTime. Have user change password via aka.ms/aadprofile, wait 30 min for unlock. Enable MEDS Security Audits with KQL: AADDomainServicesAccountManagement | where OperationName has '4740'. Check Jarvis logs for lockout source.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services Account Lockout Troubleshooting

symptom: Applications authenticating against MEDS fail after TLS 1.0/1.1 enforcement (deprecated Aug 31, 2025). ~667 tenants had active legacy TLS applications at announcement.
rootCause: Azure-wide TLS 1.0/1.1 deprecation. Apps using legacy TLS protocols cannot authenticate to MEDS DCs after TLS 1.2 Only mode enforced.
solution: Identify legacy TLS clients via DGrep query (portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/9450C455) filtered by tenantID. Data2 column shows app names. Enable TLS 1.2 Only mode to test. Extensions: S500=90d via CSAM, active-app=90d, no-legacy=30d.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/TLS 1.0 1.1 Deprecation For Entra Domain Services

symptom: OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID (CIAM): verification_uri points to microsoft.com/devicelogin which redirects to login.microsoftonline.com. This endpoint is not accepted by CIAM tenants, auth fails.
rootCause: Preview limitation: Device Code Flow response returns verification_uri pointing to microsoft.com/devicelogin instead of CIAM-specific endpoint (DomainName.ciamlogin.com).
solution: Manually change verification_uri from login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/deviceauth to DomainName.ciamlogin.com/common/oauth2/deviceauth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Labs/OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenant

symptom: OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in CIAM: signing in with external IDP fails with ADSTS50132 'The session is not valid due the following reasons: password expiration or recent password change, SSO Artifact is invalid or expired...'
rootCause: Preview limitation: External IDP federation + Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenants causes session validation failure.
solution: Known preview limitation (Oct 2025). No workaround. Use local account auth (email+password/OTP) instead of external IDP for Device Code Flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Labs/OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenant

symptom: After PIM elevation to Azure AD Joined Device Local Administrator role, user does not see Local Administrator privileges on AAD joined device. whoami /all does not show BUILTIN\Administrators group.
rootCause: Cached Primary Refresh Token (PRT) issued before PIM elevation. PRT only refreshes every 4 hours by default, so new role claims are not reflected until a new PRT is obtained.
solution: Run dsregcmd /refreshprt to schedule PRT refresh, wait 1-2 minutes, then log out and log back in. Verify with whoami /all that BUILTIN\Administrators group appears. Check AzureAdPrtUpdateTime in dsregcmd /status to confirm PRT was issued after PIM activation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/TSG: Elevating to the Azure AD Joined Device Local Administrator Role with Privileged Identity Management (PIM)

symptom: Azure Lighthouse deployment with eligible authorizations fails with Governance API error: The tenant has not onboarded to PIM.
rootCause: The managing tenant has not performed initial PIM resource discovery for the target subscription prior to deploying Lighthouse with eligible authorizations.
solution: Go to Privileged Identity Management > Azure resources > Discover resources, then select Manage resources for the unmanaged subscription. If deployment already failed, delete the failed assignments and deployment, then redeploy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Lighthouse

symptom: Azure Lighthouse deployment fails with InvalidRegistrationDefinitionCreateRequest: eligible authorizations with same roleDefinitionId must use same justInTimeAccessPolicy settings.
rootCause: Multiple eligibleAuthorizations entries with the same roleDefinitionId have different multiFactorAuthProvider, maximumActivationDuration, or managedByTenantApprovers settings.
solution: Ensure all eligibleAuthorizations entries sharing the same roleDefinitionId use identical justInTimeAccessPolicy settings (multiFactorAuthProvider, maximumActivationDuration, managedByTenantApprovers). Different settings are only allowed across different roleDefinitionIds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Lighthouse

symptom: MSP tenant users activate Azure Lighthouse eligible role but do not see activated role in PIM My Roles blade.
rootCause: Known bug (Bug 7570951) affecting eligible role assignments from resource group-level Lighthouse deployments.
solution: For resource group deployments, navigate to Azure resources > select resource > Active assignments tab. Subscription-level deployments work correctly in My Roles blade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Lighthouse

symptom: MFA required for PIM role activation at subscription level but user not prompted for MFA when activating the same role at resource group or resource level.
rootCause: PIM Role Settings do NOT inherit across scopes. Each scope (Subscription, Resource Group, Resource) has independent Role Settings configuration.
solution: Configure Require Multi-Factor Authentication on activation for the specific role at each scope level independently via Resource filter > Role Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Resources Troubleshooting Workflow

symptom: Unable to programmatically change directly assigned role members from Permanently assigned to time-bound or convert to Just In Time members using AzureRM PowerShell.
rootCause: AzureRM PowerShell cmdlets are not PIM for Azure Resources aware.
solution: Use the PIM for Azure Resources blade in portal to manually convert role memberships. No PowerShell cmdlet support for this operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/Azure RBAC PIM for Azure Resources Troubleshooting Workflow

symptom: After PIM Entra role activation, user cannot utilize permissions in M365 apps (Intune, M365 Admin Center, SPO, Exchange) and receives insufficient permission errors.
rootCause: PIM activation reflected in AAD/MSODS within 1-2 min, but apps sync via ForwardSync (up to 15 min) and cache role info locally (additional 15 min). M365 Admin Center: ~30 min. SPO: ~2 min (15 max). Purview: up to 2 hours.
solution: Wait 10-15 min after activation. Sign out completely. Sign in from incognito browser. Do not retry within 10-15 min (refreshes stale cache). For persistent issues: collect HAR trace + audit log timestamps and compare id_token wids claim with activated role.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Privilege Identity Management (PIM)/TSG: PIM Role Activation Delays

symptom: ServiceNow App for Entra Permissions Management: Validate Credentials fails during configuration. OAuth token generation failing.
rootCause: Incorrect Client Secret value or Tenant ID in ServiceNow OAuth Application Registry.
solution: Verify Client ID and Client Secret match App Registration in Entra. Verify Authorization URL and Token URL. Regenerate OAuth token via Get OAuth Token link.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/ServiceNow App for Entra Permissions Management

symptom: ServiceNow App for Entra Permissions Management: No data populated in dropdowns when creating PoD requests.
rootCause: Missing API permissions on App Registration, Service Principal not configured in MEPM, or expired OAuth token.
solution: Check: 1) App registration has Graph User.Read delegated permission. 2) Service Principal has Approver and Viewer for All in MEPM. 3) OAuth token is valid.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/ServiceNow App for Entra Permissions Management

symptom: Access Reviews historical data older than 12 months inaccessible via Graph API, Entra portal, or review history reports after August 1, 2025.
rootCause: New data retention policy limits AR data to 12 months. Archived data (1-2 years) available via support ticket only.
solution: Export historical data before Aug 15, 2025 using custom report script (learn.microsoft.com). Use ADX for storage. No extension requests granted. MC1101895 (WW/GCC), MC1102763 (Gallatin).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Upcoming Changes to Identity Governance Data Retention

symptom: Access Review Agent shows Try again error and does not recover.
rootCause: Insufficient Security Compute Units (SCUs), missing Security Copilot license, or incorrect role assignments. Feature removed from public preview Mar 27, 2026.
solution: Check SCU usage. Verify roles (Security Copilot Contributor + Identity Governance Admin + Lifecycle Workflows Admin). Use ASC Kusto medeinaapiprodwestus3g.westus3 to check agent status. Avoid PIM-activated roles. ICM: Zero Trust AI Agent > Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Security Copilot Access Review Agent

symptom: Access Review Agent setup fails with authentication errors when admin uses PIM-activated roles.
rootCause: Agent runs using admin identity who activated it. PIM just-in-time roles cause auth failures because agent requires standing permissions.
solution: Assign roles directly (standing assignment), not via PIM activation. Required: Security Copilot Contributor + Identity Governance Admin + Lifecycle Workflows Admin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Security Copilot Access Review Agent

symptom: MEDS Linux/Unix domain join kinit fails with Preauthentication failed when two Azure AD accounts share the same mailnickname, causing SAMAccountName conflict in AAD DS
rootCause: When two Azure AD accounts share the same mailnickname, they sync to AAD DS with different SAMAccountNames (one gets random GUID appended). kinit maps UPN to pre-Windows 2000 format REALM\SAMAccountName, authenticating against the wrong account
solution: Use the full SAMAccountName with appended GUID in kinit, e.g. kinit 'test (1234)@DOMAIN.COM'. To fix permanently, follow AAD DS SAMAccountName Resolution procedure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services - Unix Domain Join Troubleshooting

symptom: MEDS account locked out after 5 bad password attempts within 2 minutes, user cannot authenticate for 30 minutes. BadPwdCount=5 in ASC User Object Comparison
rootCause: MEDS default password policy: 5 bad attempts in 2 min triggers 30-min lockout. Common causes: forgotten password, cached old credentials, new password not yet synced from Entra ID (~30 sec delay), or spray attack
solution: Change password via https://mysignins.microsoft.com/security-info/password/change or reset via https://passwordreset.microsoftonline.com, wait 30 min for unlock. Verify in ASC User Object Comparison that BadPwdCount resets to 0. Enable MEDS Security Audits to monitor lockout events (Event 4740/4771). KQL: AADDomainServicesAccountManagement | where OperationName has '4740'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services Account Lockout Troubleshooting

symptom: User changes password in Entra ID and immediately tries to login to MEDS-joined resource, gets locked out because new password hash has not synced yet
rootCause: Password hash sync from Entra ID to MEDS takes approximately 30 seconds. If user tries new password before sync completes, MEDS still has old hash, counts as bad attempts and locks account
solution: After changing password in Entra ID, wait at least 30 seconds before logging in to MEDS resources. If already locked out, wait 30 minutes for automatic unlock
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services Account Lockout Troubleshooting

symptom: Applications fail authentication after TLS 1.0/1.1 is deprecated/disabled for Microsoft Entra Domain Services (deadline August 31, 2025)
rootCause: TLS 1.0/1.1 deprecation enforced for MEDS. Apps using legacy TLS cannot authenticate after TLS12Only mode enabled. Approx 667 tenants had active apps on legacy TLS at announcement
solution: Identify affected apps via DGrep query (portal.microsoftgeneva.com/s/9450C455, filter by tenantID+timestamp). Migrate to TLS 1.2 per docs. S500 customers: 90-day extension via CSAM/support ticket. Non-S500 with active apps: 90-day extension on request. Rollback: disable TLS12Only mode temporarily while remediating
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/TLS 1.0 1.1 Deprecation For Entra Domain Services

symptom: OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID (CIAM) tenant fails - verification_uri points to login.microsoftonline.com which is rejected by External ID
rootCause: Preview limitation: initial verification_uri returned by /devicecode endpoint still points to https://microsoft.com/devicelogin redirecting to login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/deviceauth instead of ciamlogin.com
solution: Manually change verification_uri from https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/deviceauth to https://{DomainName}.ciamlogin.com/common/oauth2/deviceauth when presenting to user
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Labs/OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenant

symptom: ADSTS50132 error (session not valid due to password expiration/recent change/SSO artifact invalid) when signing in with external IDP during Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenant
rootCause: Known preview limitation of Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenants - external identity provider authentication triggers session validation failure
solution: No current workaround for external IDP + Device Code Flow in CIAM. Use local account authentication (email + password or OTP) as alternative until feature exits preview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Labs/OAuth 2.0 Device Code Flow in Entra External ID tenant

symptom: MEDS health alerts show networking errors, DC connectivity issues. Default route 0.0.0.0/0 on MEDS subnet not pointing to InternetGateway in ASC Test Traffic
rootCause: Customer custom routes (ExpressRoute, Azure Firewall, peered VNet gateway) advertise non-default routes to MEDS subnet, diverting required 0.0.0.0/0 to Internet traffic. MEDS DCs require direct internet access for AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices service tag communication
solution: Use ASC Test Traffic tool (InternetIn test with AzureActiveDirectoryDomainServices IP, port 5986) to verify NSG/routing. If routing issue: create UDR with 0.0.0.0/0 to Internet, associate to MEDS subnet, disable VNet gateway route propagation. Also verify service tag IPs match documented ranges
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/Microsoft Entra Domain Services Test Traffic

symptom: User has username identity on their Entra External ID user object but cannot sign in using username - only email works
rootCause: Username sign-in method is not enabled in tenant Sign-in Identifiers policy. Information on the user object is separate from what the user can utilize for sign-in.
solution: Enable Username in Sign-in Identifiers under Entra ID > External Identities. The sign-in identifier policy must explicitly allow username as a sign-in method.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Entra External ID Sign In With An Alias Or Username

symptom: Custom regex validation configured for username in Entra External ID but users can still sign in with usernames that do not match the regex pattern
rootCause: Both Username and Custom Username are selected in Sign-in Identifiers. Username overrides Custom Username when both are enabled.
solution: Configure Sign-in identifiers to only include Custom Username, remove the Username option. If both are selected, Username will override Custom Username.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Entra External ID Sign In With An Alias Or Username

symptom: Custom regex for username validation in Entra External ID does not enforce validation - authentication succeeds with non-matching usernames
rootCause: The regex expression itself is invalid. No built-in validation exists for custom regular expressions beyond ensuring they do not match email address format.
solution: Verify the regex is actually valid. Test regex patterns independently before configuring. Authentication will fail at runtime if the username does not match a valid regex.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Entra External ID Sign In With An Alias Or Username

symptom: Email OTP verification code stated to work for 30 minutes expires before 30 minutes during MFA in Entra External ID (CIAM)
rootCause: OTP expiration depends on scenario: 30 minutes for first-factor auth, but only 10 minutes when used as second-factor MFA or SSPR.
solution: By design. First-factor OTP valid 30 min. MFA second-factor or SSPR must complete within 10 minutes. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/external-id/customers/concept-multifactor-authentication-customers#email-one-time-passcode
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Customer wants to unlink Entra External ID tenant from one Azure subscription and relink to another subscription
rootCause: Unlike Azure AD B2C, Entra External ID does not support unlinking and re-linking tenants to subscriptions. Deleting the Azure resource will attempt to delete the entire tenant.
solution: Unlinking/re-linking is NOT supported. WARNING: Deleting the Azure resource will attempt to delete the entire tenant (unlike B2C which just orphans billing). Do not delete the resource if intent is only to move subscriptions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Invited guest users cannot sign in through External ID Tenant User Flows in Entra External ID (CIAM)
rootCause: Invited guest users are only supported in External ID Tenants for managing the tenant via administrator roles, not for user flow sign-in.
solution: Instruct users to create new consumer accounts. Guest users in CIAM tenants are for admin management only, not end-user sign-in via user flows.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID for Customers (CIAM) - Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: AADSTS160021: Application requested a user session which does not exist. Sign-in fails with AppSessionSelectionInvalidSessionNotExist when app enforces a specific session ID (sid parameter) that is no longer valid or never existed.
rootCause: The caller application includes a sid (Session ID) parameter in the authorize request that references a non-existent or expired session in eSTS. This is not an eSTS issue but a client-side misconfiguration.
solution: The caller app should either: A) stop enforcing a specific SID by removing the sid parameter from the authorize call, or B) validate where the SID originates and ensure it uses a valid, active session ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS160021

symptom: AADSTS399274: The application with App ID is configured for SAML SSO and could not be used with non-SAML protocol. New SAML applications fail to acquire OAuth2/OIDC JWT tokens after October 2024 rollout.
rootCause: Security change rolled out by October 11, 2024: apps marked as SAML (via preferredSingleSignOnMode on service principal) can no longer receive JWT tokens through OAuth2/OIDC protocols. Only affects new dependencies; existing apps with OAuth/OIDC traffic are allowlisted.
solution: Customer must create a separate App Registration specifically for OAuth authentication (via App registration portal). Two apps needed: one Enterprise App for SAML SSO and one App Registration for OAuth/client credentials flow. If not working, escalate to ICM: AAD First Party Apps / Application Model.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS399274

symptom: AADSTS50000: There was an error issuing a token or an issue with our sign-in service. Occurs in On-Behalf-Of (OBO) flow when calling Microsoft Graph.
rootCause: The API app uses an incorrect client_id in the OBO flow token request. The client_id must match the API app own Application ID, not some other identifier.
solution: Ensure the client_id field in the OBO token request specifies the API App own Application ID (not Object ID or any other identifier). When using the correct Application ID, the OBO flow works correctly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50000

symptom: AADSTS50001: The application named X was not found in the tenant named contoso.onmicrosoft.com. Application has not been installed by admin or consented to by any user.
rootCause: The application (identified by client_id) does not exist as a service principal in the target tenant. Causes: wrong Application ID, wrong tenant in auth request, or app is single-tenant but accessed from a different tenant.
solution: 1) Verify client_id (Application ID) is correct. 2) Verify the tenant in the auth URL is correct (if common, uses user home tenant). 3) If app is from another tenant, configure as multi-tenant. 4) Have admin navigate to consent URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/adminconsent?client_id={application-id}. Note: Application ID != Object ID.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50001

symptom: AADSTS500011: The resource principal named resource-id was not found in the tenant. The requested resource (service principal) does not exist in the tenant.
rootCause: The service principal for the requested resource does not exist in the target tenant. The resource identifier (via resource or scope parameter) cannot be resolved to a service principal.
solution: 1) Look up app in Enterprise Apps (Application Type=All). 2) Go to Permissions, click Grant admin consent. 3) If still failing, use consent URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/adminconsent&client_id={resource-id}. 4) If SP does not exist, create via PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId. First-party apps: owning team provides correct identifiers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500011

symptom: AADSTS5000225: This tenant has been blocked due to inactivity. Authentication to Entra tenant fails because commerce lifecycle policy has deauthenticated the tenant.
rootCause: The tenant was deauthenticated by commerce lifecycle policy due to prolonged inactivity. See https://aka.ms/tenantlifecycle for tenant lifecycle policies.
solution: Reference the Lifecycle based authentication blocks TSG for steps to identify, respond to, and re-activate the tenant. TSG: AzureAD wiki /1345264/Lifecycle-Based-Authentication-Blocks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS5000225

symptom: AADSTS5000819: SAML Assertion is invalid. Email address claim is missing or does not match domain from an external realm. Internal user fails to access internal resources over external SAML/WS-Fed trust.
rootCause: Customer configured a Direct Federation (SAML/WS-Fed) trust pointing to their own Entra tenant. This is unsupported when a federated trust between Entra ID and on-prem IdP already exists. Entra ID does not support multiple federations with identical issuers.
solution: Delete the SAML/WS-Fed external trust that points to the same Entra tenant. Direct Federation is meant for guest users (external to tenant), not internal users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS5000819

symptom: AADSTS500083: Unable to verify token signature. No trusted realm found with identifier. Federated user sign-in fails with SAML/WS-Fed authentication from IdP due to ImmutableId mismatch.
rootCause: Mismatch between ImmutableId stored in Azure AD and NameID/ImmutableID in SAML token from federated IdP. Match is case-sensitive. Common: on-prem sAMAccountName case changed after initial sync but AAD Connect did not re-sync (only casing differs).
solution: 1) Check AADConnect sync AnchorAttribute in ASC. 2) Retrieve ImmutableId from Directory object in ASC. 3) Compare against NameID/ImmutableID in SAML token - must be exact case-sensitive match. 4) If casing differs, change on-prem sAMAccountName to match ImmutableId exactly (same case).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500083

symptom: AADSTS50011: The reply URL specified in the request does not match the reply URLs configured for the application. Also appears as invalid_request for redirect_uri mismatch.
rootCause: The redirect_uri (OpenID Connect) or AssertionConsumerServiceUrl (SAML) in the auth request does not exist in the application registration in Entra ID.
solution: Add the redirect URI or ACS URL to the application registration. Use Fiddler to capture the auth request and identify the exact redirect_uri/ACS URL. For first-party (Microsoft-owned) apps, the owning product team must update URLs - Entra ID Auth team cannot modify first-party app URLs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50011

symptom: AADSTS50013: Assertion failed signature validation (wrong audience). In OBO flow, the access token aud claim is for a different resource (e.g. Microsoft Graph) and the app tries to validate a token not meant for it.
rootCause: The application attempts to validate/consume an access token whose audience (aud claim) is for a different API (e.g. graph.microsoft.com). Access tokens are meant for their target audience only.
solution: Solution 1: First get an access token for your own app, then acquire another token for the target API via OBO. Solution 2: If you only need user info (display name, groups), use the id_token instead of the access token.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50013

symptom: AADSTS50013: Assertion failed signature validation (certificate chain order). Client assertion fails when chained certificate file has wrong certificate at top, causing public key mismatch.
rootCause: When using a chained certificate (PEM/CER) for client assertion, the certificate with the matching public key is not at the top of the chain file. The wrong certificate is used for signature verification.
solution: 1) openssl rsa -noout -modulus -in private.key | openssl md5 to get private key modulus. 2) Separate each cert in chain to own file. 3) Run openssl x509 -noout -modulus -in server.pem | openssl md5 on each until finding matching modulus. 4) Move matching cert to top of chain file. If no modulus matches, wrong public cert uploaded or wrong private key used.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50013

symptom: Bulk download fails if the CSV file was initiated for a long time (~20 days ago). User sees download failure for old bulk operation results.
rootCause: By design limitation. Bulk operation result files are expected to be valid for ~30 days but may fail earlier. Tracked in bug 1555597.
solution: Re-initiate the bulk download operation to generate a fresh file. The old file may have expired.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: Bulk import does not work for restricted Administrative Units (AUs). Error: Insufficient privileges to complete the operation. Target object is a member of a restricted management administrative unit.
rootCause: Bug - the bulk processor lacks permission to write to restricted AUs. Tracked in bug 2698816.
solution: Workaround: Use MgGraph PowerShell module with directory.write.restricted permission to perform bulk import.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: Bulk operations in the Entra portal timeout and fail on very large tenants (e.g., Microsoft Corporate tenant).
rootCause: Known scaling limitation of the bulk operations service. New service is being developed. Related CRI: 503517148.
solution: Refine filters to limit data returned, or use PowerShell with direct MS Graph API calls. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/bulk-operations-service-limitations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: Bulk Download fails for non-admins when Restrict access to Microsoft Entra admin center toggle is enabled.
rootCause: By design - the toggle prevents non-admin users from accessing the bulk download blade. Users without admin roles are blocked when this switch is on.
solution: Disable the Restrict access toggle, or assign admin roles to users who need bulk download. New bulk ops experience with RBACv2 and custom role support is planned. Related CRI: 658144444.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: After toggling Restrict access to Microsoft Entra admin center, non-admins can still access some blades like Groupslist.ReactView, Devices, Enterprise Apps.
rootCause: Feature gap - Devices, Enterprise Apps and Groups blades do not honor the client-side setting. Enforcement is limited to AAD_IAM and UsersAndTenants extension blades only. Multiple partner teams needed. Related CRI: 649723689.
solution: This switch is NOT a security measure. Create Conditional Access policy targeting Windows Azure Service Management API to block non-admin access. Even if the switch worked, users could still access data via Graph API/PowerShell. Work item: 3047372.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: Duplicated users returned when checking user license assignment in Entra portal. Clicking Load More shows duplicate entries in license product blades.
rootCause: BEC API limitation in ListUsers. Affected blades: ProductUsersBladeV2, ProductGroupsBladeV2, GroupsProductsBladeV2, UserAssignmentErrorsBlade. Work item: 1066723.
solution: Known issue being addressed. No current workaround in portal. Use PowerShell/Graph API for accurate license assignment data.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: Entra portal search box does not return privileged identity management or PIM-related results when searching.
rootCause: PIM has an asset type declared in their extension but it is not declared in the Entra search curation. Work item: 2581420.
solution: Navigate directly to PIM blade via URL or menu. Known gap being tracked for fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Known Issues

symptom: New Bulk Operations job submission fails with error External security challenge not satisfied (ADSTS50158/InsufficientClaims). Seen in ExportGroupMembers and possibly other job types.
rootCause: Authentication issue with bulk ops 1st-party app. Fix being deployed (target ~April 2026). Bug: 3548683.
solution: Workaround: 1) Sign out of all Azure/Entra sessions. 2) Revoke refresh tokens (optional). 3) Clear browser cache or open InPrivate. 4) Sign in + MFA. 5) Retry immediately. Do NOT raise CRI - ping astobart@microsoft.com with SR number.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/New Bulk Operations

symptom: Guest users unable to create bulk operations jobs in the new Bulk Operations experience. Permission error when initiating a bulk job.
rootCause: By design - Guest users are not allowed to submit bulk jobs. Bulk Ops requires UserType=member. Some old internal users may lack UserType property.
solution: Guest users cannot use bulk ops by design. Use Graph API or PowerShell as workaround. For users missing UserType property, check if user is a legacy internal member.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/New Bulk Operations

symptom: Bulk Ops export produces empty CSV when exporting members of a group with IsMembershipHidden (HiddenMembership) set to true, despite Entra Portal showing users to admin.
rootCause: The 1st-party Bulk Ops application is missing the Member.Read.Hidden permission, so ListGroupHiddenMembership task is denied. Bug: 3487970.
solution: Use Graph API or PowerShell to export members of hidden-membership groups. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/bulk-operations-service-limitations#bulk-download-members-of-a-group
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/New Bulk Operations

symptom: Bulk Ops fails in GDAP/partner scenarios. Partner opens Partner Center, gets redirected to customer tenant, attempts bulk operation and gets Error authenticating with resource in HAR.
rootCause: GDAP scenarios are not currently supported by new Bulk Ops (as of Feb 2026). Work item: 3530684.
solution: No workaround for GDAP bulk ops. Partners should use Graph API or PowerShell directly in the customer tenant context.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/New Bulk Operations

symptom: TenantOverview blade in Entra portal displays incorrect license type (shows P1 when tenant has P2, or vice versa).
rootCause: TenantOverview calls graph.microsoft.com/beta/subscribedSkus to detect P1/P2. If response contains AAD_PREMIUM it shows P1, AAD_PREMIUM_P2 shows P2. License may be bundled in SKUs like M365 E5 Security.
solution: 1) Check ASC Graph Explorer for subscribedSkus response. 2) Cross-reference SKU IDs with https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/licensing-service-plan-reference
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/TSG: TenantOverview blade doesn't show the right license

symptom: Tenant creation fails with error: Tenant name is empty or contains invalid characters when entering certain tenant display names.
rootCause: Tenant displayName validation: no special characters, not numbers only, no emoji, no nonPrintable characters, max 256 chars. After creation, cannot change name to include test pattern. Work item: 3976815.
solution: Use alphanumeric characters only. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/quickstart-create-new-tenant
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Tenant display Name requirement when creating a new tenant

symptom: Microsoft account (MSA/Live ID) user logs into Azure portal and is redirected to Microsoft Service tenant (f8cdef31-a31e-4b4a-93e4-5f571e91255a, microsoftservices.onmicrosoft.com) instead of expected AAD tenant.
rootCause: By design - happens when MSA was previously invited to an AAD tenant as guest but later removed. With no tenant invitations remaining, user is redirected to Microsoft Service tenant.
solution: Expected behavior. To log into a specific tenant, user needs to be re-invited as guest. Logging into Microsoft Service tenant itself is not an issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra portal (Microsoft_AAD_IAM)/Why I was logged into a Microsoft Service tenant?

symptom: AADSTS500131: Assertion audience does not match the Client app presenting the assertion. HTTP 400 error during On-Behalf-Of (OBO) flow when the access token audience GUID does not match the calling app client ID.
rootCause: The access token used in the OBO assertion was issued for a different application/resource (e.g., Azure AD Graph 00000002-0000-0000-c000-000000000000) instead of the calling Web API. The audience claim in the token does not match the client ID making the assertion request.
solution: Ensure the client ID in the sign-in request matches the audience claim in the access token used as the assertion. Verify the resource/scope in the initial token acquisition targets the correct Web API application ID, not a different resource.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500131

symptom: AADSTS500173: Unable to download a Certificate Revocation List (CRL). Invalid status code NotFound from CRL distribution point. Users authenticating with Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) to M365 services fail.
rootCause: Entra ID cannot download CRL from the configured CRL distribution point (HTTP 404). Even with CRL validation disabled or CA in exemption list, Entra ID still attempts CRL download if a CRL URL exists. CRL URL may come from cached CA metadata or Delta CRL distribution point in CA certificate.
solution: Option 1: Publish CRL file at the expected distribution point. Option 2: Remove CRL reference from CA certificate if CRLs are not used. Option 3: Allow Entra ID CA metadata cache to refresh automatically. Troubleshoot: check CrlBaseUrl/CrlDeltaUrl in logs, test CRL endpoint manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500173

symptom: AADSTS50020: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant and cannot access the application. Occurs in CSP/Partner scenarios where partner user lacks AdminAgent group membership.
rootCause: User account from partner/external tenant is not present as a guest or does not have required partner admin group membership. In B2B direct link redemption, no corresponding guest user in PendingAcceptance state with matching proxyAddress found in resource tenant.
solution: Ensure user is added to Partner Admin Groups (e.g., AdminAgents) in the Partner tenant. For B2B scenarios, create guest user in resource tenant. For CSP, follow MS Graph CSP auth docs and GDAP admin permissions guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50020

symptom: AADSTS500207: The account type cannot be used for the resource you are trying to access. Occurs when accessing a CIAM (Entra External ID for Customers) application.
rootCause: CIAM apps only support applications configured for single-tenant (Accounts in this directory only). The application registration is configured as multi-tenant, which is not supported for CIAM.
solution: Configure the application registration as single-tenant (Accounts in this directory only) in Entra ID portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500207

symptom: AADSTS500208: The domain is not a valid login domain for the account type. Consumer user from Azure AD B2C or Entra External ID (CIAM) tenant fails to login at login.microsoftonline.com.
rootCause: Consumer user from B2C or CIAM tenant is directed to login.microsoftonline.com instead of the correct endpoint (b2clogin.com for B2C, ciamlogin.com for CIAM). For guest admin/invitation scenarios hitting login.microsoftonline.com, the user has no valid role assigned in the resource tenant.
solution: For B2C users: redirect to b2clogin.com. For CIAM users: redirect to ciamlogin.com. For guest admin scenarios accessing B2C/CIAM tenant via Entra/Azure portals: assign a valid role to the user in the resource tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS500208

symptom: AADSTS50059: No tenant-identifying information found in either the request or implied by any provided credentials. Silent sign-in fails without Azure AD redirecting the error back to the application.
rootCause: Silent request (prompt=none) uses a multi-tenant endpoint (common or organizations) without tenant-identifying info. The estsauth cookie is missing or expired. Azure AD cannot determine the tenant and does not redirect the error back to the app.
solution: Option 1: Use tenant-specific endpoint URL (login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant-id}/oauth2/authorize). Option 2: Configure app as multi-tenant. Then implement detection logic to catch the error and prompt for interactive sign-in (see MSAL error handling docs).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50059

symptom: AADSTS50070: Signout failed. The request specified session indexes which did not match the existing session(s). SAML logout fails when session index in LogoutRequest does not match the active SAML session.
rootCause: User has multiple SAML sessions active (each with different SessionIndex values). The logout request sends a SessionIndex from an earlier session that no longer matches the currently active session. This is a browser/app issue, not an ESTS issue.
solution: Ensure the SAML logout request uses the SessionIndex from the most recent SAML Response AuthnStatement. The application must track which SessionIndex is currently active and use that value in the LogoutRequest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50070

symptom: AADSTS50196: The server terminated an operation because it encountered a client request loop. User stuck in MFA loop between mysignins.microsoft.com and login.microsoftonline.com after initial password login.
rootCause: Mismatch between authentication methods policy (UCP policy) configured by admin and the strong auth methods registered on user account. User default MFA method (e.g., NotificationOverAuthenticatorApp) is not in PolicyEnabledCredentials list, causing infinite MFA prompt loop.
solution: Align tenant authentication methods policy (UCP) with user registered/default MFA method. Either add user default method to PolicyEnabledCredentials, or change user default MFA to an allowed method. Diagnose via ASC auth troubleshooter: compare AllRegisteredMethods vs PolicyAllowedOrderedSAMs vs PolicyEnabledCredentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50072

symptom: AADSTS50076: Due to a configuration change made by your administrator, or because you moved to a new location, you must use multi-factor authentication to access the resource.
rootCause: Conditional Access policy requires MFA but the application is using a non-interactive flow (Windows Integrated Authentication or Resource Owner Password Credential) that cannot satisfy MFA interactively.
solution: 1) Use an interactive flow instead. 2) Ensure openid is in scopes during interactive sign-in (needed for auth code exchange). 3) Add client app to CA policy exception list. 4) Add user to CA policy exception. 5) Last resort: disable per-user MFA if not using CA policies.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50076

symptom: User stuck during MFA/SSPR combined registration - no methods available when Security Defaults enabled and admin disabled app notification/code in MFA settings
rootCause: Conflict between Security Defaults (only allows Authenticator app) and MFA/authentication methods policy (disabled app notification/code). No registration methods remain.
solution: Re-adjust policy to allow mobile app notification/code, OR disable Security Defaults. Notify ISPDefaultProtection@service.microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/SSPR - Self Service Password Reset/SSPR - Windows Logon Screen

symptom: Reset Password link not showing on Windows 10/11 logon screen for SSPR
rootCause: Multiple possible causes including registry/policy/connectivity issues
solution: Run SSPR Win10 Logon Diagnostics script to identify specific cause.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/SSPR - Self Service Password Reset/SSPR - Windows Logon Screen

symptom: 3P app-only calls to Entra Graph APIs return stale/inconsistent data after mesh routing rollout disables app-only session consistency
rootCause: PG enabled mesh routing disabling app-only session consistency for 3P apps. Apps relying on session stickiness now see eventual consistency. Never guaranteed for app-only flows.
solution: (1) Point to docs: no session consistency for app-only, (2) Switch to delegated flows, (3) Design for eventual consistency, (4) S500: request allow-listing via Sev3 CRI to Programmability Infra.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/[Breaking Change] Enabling Mesh Routing (Disabling App Only Session Consistency) for 3P Apps calling Entra Graph APIs

symptom: AADSTS100033: Regional Cache Auth Service token requests for flows needing CA policy evaluation are forbidden
rootCause: App uses ESTSR regional endpoint instead of global ESTS. CA blocked on regional endpoints since late 2022. App Service REGION_NAME may override MSAL settings.
solution: (1) Switch to login.microsoftonline.com, (2) Check App Service REGION_NAME and set empty, (3) Update MSAL to latest, (4) Set ATTEMPT_REGION_DISCOVERY=False.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS100033

symptom: AADSTS1000901: Certificate cannot be used for requesting tokens - token_not_after expired. Managed Identity sign-in flow.
rootCause: Managed Identity certificate used by first-party resource has expired. Issuer is always managedidentitysnissuer.login.microsoft.com.
solution: Engage first-party resource support team and PG. Managed Identity must be recreated. NOT fixable by AAD AUTH team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1000901

symptom: AADSTS1003031: Misconfigured required resource access when using tenant-wide admin consent URL for multi-tenant app
rootCause: App registration has no API permissions. Tenant-wide admin consent URL requires at least one API permission.
solution: (1) Add Microsoft Graph User.Read permission to app registration, OR (2) Use New-MgServicePrincipal or Graph Explorer POST /serviceprincipals to add SP manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1003031

symptom: AADSTS1400001: Request nonce not provided. Common during WHFB sign-in. OAuth invalid_request.
rootCause: Device missing OS updates/patches, or nonce not generated correctly. Outdated OS builds commonly trigger this.
solution: (1) Update device OS, (2) Check app nonce generation, (3) If patched+correct, ICM to ESTS EEE. Check Event IDs 1085/1081 + Webauth.ETL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS1400001

symptom: AADSTS501051: Service principal gets error "Application isn't assigned to a role for the application" when requesting token via client_credentials grant for a resource API.
rootCause: The client service principal has not been granted an application permission (app role assignment) on the target resource service principal. Application permissions are implemented as app role assignments in Entra ID.
solution: Grant the calling service principal an app role assignment: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/{client-sp-id}/appRoleAssignments with principalId, resourceId, appRoleId. Or use New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment in Graph PowerShell. Requires AppRoleAssignment.ReadWrite.All permission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS501051

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" during On-Behalf-Of (OBO) flow with SAML assertion.
rootCause: The SAML assertion used in OBO flow is issued by Azure AD (http://sts.windows.net/{TenantID}) instead of the federated IdP (e.g., AD FS). Azure AD blocks its own issuer in this context.
solution: Ensure the SAML assertion is issued by the federated IdP (e.g., AD FS), not by Azure AD. Regenerate the assertion using the correct identity provider.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" for federated domain with third-party IdP returning incorrect IssuerURI.
rootCause: Third-party IdP returns an incorrect IssuerURI in the SAML response. Entra ID cannot match it to an existing federation realm object.
solution: Option 1: Contact third-party IdP to fix the IssuerURI. Option 2: Update IssuerURI in Entra ID using Update-MgDomainFederationConfiguration -DomainId "domain.com" -IssuerUri "{correct-value}". For SAML protocol add -PreferredAuthenticationProtocol SAMLP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS50107: "The requested federation realm object does not exist" with multiple federated domains or child domains on same AD FS.
rootCause: Issuance Transform Rule is missing or outdated, or the AD FS issuer does not match what Entra ID expects when multiple domains/child domains are federated to the same AD FS instance.
solution: Review and fix the issuerid claim issuance transform rules in AD FS. See ADO Wiki: ADFS understanding the issuerid claim issuance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50107

symptom: AADSTS501209: "JWT signature is invalid" when accessing Office/Teams via browser. Error references expired MS-Organization-Access certificate (ThresholdJwtDeviceCredential.ValidateSignature failure).
rootCause: The device's MS-Organization-Access certificate (10-year Workplace Join cert) has expired. The ThresholdJwtDeviceCredential signature validation fails because the signing key is expired.
solution: 1) Check certificates via certlm.msc (Personal Store) for MS-Organization-Access certs. 2) Run dsregcmd /status and check WorkPlaceThumbprint and DeviceCertificateValidity. 3) Go to Settings > Accounts > Access work or school, select the expired account and Disconnect to remove the outdated certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS501209

symptom: AADSTS50126: "Invalid username or password" during interactive sign-in at Entra ID sign-in page.
rootCause: Username or password entered incorrectly, or if using Password Hash Sync, the password from on-premises AD has not yet synced to Entra ID.
solution: Verify credentials for typos. If using Password Hash Sync, check sync status and troubleshoot per MS docs on tshoot-connect-password-hash-synchronization.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50126

symptom: AADSTS50126: "Invalid username or password" during non-interactive sign-in using Resource Owner Password Credential (ROPC) grant_type=password flow.
rootCause: Federated user accounts are not supported for ROPC grant by default. Also may occur if Password Hash Sync has not completed, or if Public Client Flows is not enabled in the app registration.
solution: Use a managed (non-federated) account for ROPC, or switch to a supported auth flow. Enable Public Client Flows under app registration Authentication settings. Check PHS sync status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50126

symptom: AADSTS50140: Users must re-enter credentials and MFA each time they reopen Edge browser for third-party SAML apps, but Chrome works fine. Device credential ignored due to missing nonce.
rootCause: Edge Chromium sends POST request for BSSO but device credential nonce is missing. ESTS will not initiate nonce challenge (302 redirect) for POST requests because POST parameters would be lost. Chrome BSSO uses GET which supports nonce refresh.
solution: Check if customer can change HTTP POST request to GET for authentication flow. If POST is an absolute requirement, customer can raise a feature request with Microsoft.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50140

symptom: AADSTS50146: "This application is required to be configured with an application-specific signing key. It is either not configured with one, or the key has expired or is not yet valid."
rootCause: Application uses claims mapping policy but lacks a custom signing key, or the key has expired. Also triggered when AcceptMappedClaims is true but Identifier URI uses a non-verified/non-supported domain format.
solution: Option 1: Configure a custom signing key per MS docs. Option 2: Update app manifest with AcceptMappedClaims: true AND ensure Identifier URI uses a verified domain format. See jwt-claims-customization docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50146

symptom: AADSTS50158: "External security challenge not satisfied" during non-interactive sign-in to application with Terms of Use or third-party MFA conditional access policy.
rootCause: Terms of Use (and similar CA extensibility features) require interactive sign-in. Non-interactive sign-in (token endpoint) cannot verify ToU agreement. The client app receives interaction_required but does not handle it correctly.
solution: Client application must handle interaction_required error by prompting user for interactive sign-in. Refer to MSAL.js error handling docs. For CRI/ICM: confirm error relates to interactive sign-in requirement, then transfer to partner team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50158

symptom: AADSTS50177: "User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant and cannot access the application." External user trying to access resource tenant app.
rootCause: External user does not have a B2B Guest account with matching AlternativeSecurityID (PUID) or ProxyAddress in the resource tenant.
solution: Invite the external user to the resource tenant as a B2B guest first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50177

symptom: AADSTS50177 for passthrough user (B2B/CSP) accessing resource tenant. True error: ExternalChallengeNotSupportedForPassthroughusers.
rootCause: Resource tenant has conditional access policy with external challenge controls (e.g., Terms of Use) that are not supported for CSP/B2B passthrough users.
solution: Customer must exclude CSP service provider from CA policies enforcing external challenge controls. Use Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to identify blocking policy. See GDAP FAQ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50177

symptom: AADSTS50178 for passthrough user (B2B/CSP) accessing resource tenant. True error: SessionControlNotSupportedForPassthroughUsers.
rootCause: Resource tenant has conditional access policy with session controls that are not supported for CSP/B2B passthrough users.
solution: Customer must exclude CSP service provider from CA policies enforcing session controls. Use Auth Troubleshooter with correlation ID to identify blocking policy. See GDAP FAQ.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50178

symptom: AADSTS50196: "The server terminated an operation because it encountered a loop while processing a request." Client app issues 15+ duplicate login requests in 5 minutes.
rootCause: Client application is misconfigured: missing token caching or exhibiting prompt loops. ESTS detects the loop (tracked per-instance via cookie on user hint, scopes, client ID, redirect URI, response type) and returns invalid_grant.
solution: Implement proper token caching in the client app. Handle invalid_grant by showing an interactive prompt instead of silently retrying. Ensure tokens with sufficient lifetime (10-60 min) are cached and not re-requested.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50196

symptom: AADSTS65005: The application asked for scope that does not exist. Occurs when app was created as SAML SSO (has applicationTemplateId for non-gallery SAML app) and tries to use OAuth2 scope request.
rootCause: Application was created as a SAML SSO application via applicationTemplateId. SAML apps cannot expose or use OAuth2 scopes.
solution: Recreate the application as an OAuth2 application using the App Registration portal or Microsoft Graph create Application API. Do not use applicationTemplate instantiate for OAuth2 apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65005

symptom: AADSTS650051: The application needs access to a service the organization has not subscribed to. Resource API service principal missing in user tenant for multi-tenant app.
rootCause: Multi-tenant app references a resource API registered in another tenant. The resource API service principal has not been consented or created in the user tenant.
solution: Admin of user tenant must consent to resource API: 1) Admin Consent URL with resource API App ID, or 2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId {Resource-API-AppId}. Then create app role assignment if needed via New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650052: The app is trying to access a service that the organization lacks a service principal for. Tenant missing the required service principal.
rootCause: The application requires a service principal that does not exist in the user tenant. The app or its dependencies have not been provisioned.
solution: Create the missing service principal: Graph Explorer POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals with {appId: ClientID}, or PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -BodyParameter @{appId=ClientID}. Then grant admin consent in Entra portal Enterprise Applications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650052

symptom: AADSTS650053 Scenario 1: Scope does not exist on the resource. Auth request sends scope parameter with incorrect format (commas instead of spaces separating scope values).
rootCause: The scope parameter in the OAuth2 authorization request uses incorrect separator characters (commas instead of spaces/%20) or contains special characters not recognized by Entra ID.
solution: Fix the scope parameter format: use spaces (%20 in URL encoding) to separate multiple scope values, not commas or other delimiters. Reference: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650053 Scenario 2: Scope does not exist on the resource. Admin consent URL used to consent application-type permissions (app roles), which is not supported.
rootCause: Application permissions (app roles) cannot be consented through the tenant-wide admin consent URL. Only delegated permissions support dynamic consent via admin consent URL.
solution: Add application permissions in the app registration API permissions blade and grant admin consent there. Do not use admin consent URL for application-type permissions. Alternative: use admin consent v2 endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650056 (OAuth): Misconfigured application. Client has not listed any permissions for the resource in app registration, or admin has not consented.
rootCause: The client app registration lacks API permissions for the requested resource, or admin consent has not been granted for the configured permissions.
solution: Add required API permissions (at minimum Microsoft Graph User.Read delegated) to the app registration for both client and resource apps. Then have admin grant consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650056 (SAML): Misconfigured application. Issuer in SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier (Entity ID) configured on the Enterprise application.
rootCause: The Issuer element in the SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier URI / App ID URI configured on the Enterprise application in Entra ID.
solution: Either update the Enterprise application Identifier (Entity ID) to match the Issuer in the SAMLRequest, or update the SaaS application configuration to send the correct Issuer value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650057: Invalid resource. Client requested access to a resource not listed in its app registration required permissions.
rootCause: The resource API requested in the authentication flow is not added to the client app registration API permissions.
solution: Add the requested resource to the client app registration API permissions and grant admin consent. If resource and client are the same app, ensure the app has been consented to.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650057

symptom: AADSTS65006: Resource had no entitlements matching required permissions. A permission ID in the client app registration no longer exists on the resource.
rootCause: A scope or app role was deleted from the resource application, but the stale reference with the now-invalid permission ID remains in the client app registration API permissions.
solution: Remove the invalid permission from the client app registration API permissions. Check resource Application Manifest oauth2Permissions and appRoles for the missing permission ID. If resource has a replacement permission with same name, re-add it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65006

symptom: AADSTS70000: Provided grant is invalid or malformed. Occurs when redeeming a refresh token for a new access token.
rootCause: The ClientID or ClientSecret used in the token refresh request differs from the one that originally acquired the refresh token. Refresh tokens are bound to the client credentials used during initial acquisition.
solution: Ensure the same ClientID and ClientSecret that originally acquired the refresh token are used in the token refresh request. Verify no credential rotation occurred between token acquisition and refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS70000

symptom: AADSTS700005: InvalidGrantRedeemAgainstWrongTenant. Authorization code redeemed in a different tenant than where it was issued. HTTP 400 invalid_grant.
rootCause: Tenant mismatch between /authorize and /token endpoints. Authorization code is tenant-bound. Common causes: mismatched authorities between frontend/backend, hard-coded tenant ID in SDK/middleware, mixed identity models (AD FS vs Entra).
solution: Align the tenant in /authorize and /token endpoints. For multi-tenant apps use /common or /organizations consistently. Capture browser trace to compare tenant IDs at both endpoints. Review MSAL/OIDC middleware configuration for hard-coded tenant IDs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS700005

symptom: AADSTS7000112: Application is disabled. Users cannot sign in to the application.
rootCause: The application 'Enabled for users to sign in' setting is set to No in the Enterprise application configuration in Entra ID.
solution: Go to Entra portal > Enterprise applications > find the app > Properties > set 'Enabled for users to sign in' to Yes. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/disable-user-sign-in-portal
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS7000112

symptom: AADSTS65005: The application asked for scope that does not exist on the resource. OAuth2 sign-in request specifying a scope fails even though the scope is valid for the app.
rootCause: The application was created as a SAML SSO app (has applicationTemplateId for non-gallery SAML SSO template 8adf8e6e-67b2-4cf2-a259-e3dc5476c621). SAML-created apps cannot use OAuth2 scopes.
solution: Recreate the application as an OAuth2 application using the App Registration portal or Microsoft Graph POST /applications API. Do not use applicationTemplate instantiate for OAuth2 apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65005

symptom: AADSTS650051: The application needs access to a service that your organization has not subscribed to. Occurs when signing into a multi-tenant app and the resource API service principal does not exist in the user tenant.
rootCause: The resource API registered in tenant A has not been consented or provisioned in tenant B where the user is signing in. The service principal for the resource API is missing in the target tenant.
solution: Create the service principal for the resource API in the target tenant using: (1) Admin Consent URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?prompt=admin_consent&response_type=code&client_id={Resource-API-AppId}, or (2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId {Resource-API-AppId}. Then create app role assignments with New-MgServicePrincipalAppRoleAssignment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650051: Application is requesting permissions that are either invalid or out of date. Misleading error during consent grant, actual cause is role assignment limit exceeded.
rootCause: User or service principal exceeded the limit of 1500 role assignments (Azure AD service limit). MSODS returns EntitlementValidationException but the error message is masked as AADSTS650051.
solution: Query MSODS tables in Kusto using the Correlation ID + Timestamp from the Audit Log to find the actual EntitlementValidationException. Reduce role assignments below 1500 limit per Azure AD Service Limits and Restrictions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650051

symptom: AADSTS650052: The app is trying to access a service that your organization lacks a service principal for. Tenant does not have the required application instance.
rootCause: The service principal for the target resource API does not exist in the tenant. The tenant has not subscribed to or enabled the required service.
solution: Create the service principal: (1) Graph Explorer: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals with body {appId: ClientID}, or (2) PowerShell: New-MgServicePrincipal -BodyParameter @{appId=ClientID}. Then admin consent via URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/authorize?prompt=admin_consent&response_type=code&client_id={ClientID}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650052

symptom: AADSTS650053: The application asked for scope that does not exist on the resource. Scope parameter uses commas instead of spaces to separate values.
rootCause: Invalid scope parameter format in the OAuth2 authentication request. The app developer used commas to separate scope values instead of spaces (space = %20 in URL encoding).
solution: Update the authentication request to use spaces (not commas) to separate scope values in the scope parameter. Reference: Microsoft identity platform OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650053: Scope does not exist on the resource. Occurs when trying admin consent via admin consent URL for Application-type API permissions.
rootCause: Application permissions (app roles) cannot be consented through the admin consent URL. The admin consent URL only works for delegated permissions.
solution: Add application permissions in the App Registration portal under API Permissions and consent from portal. Alternatively use the admin consent v2 endpoint for granting application permissions with dynamic scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650053

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application in OAuth flow. Client has not listed any permissions for the resource in the requested permissions.
rootCause: The app registration for the client did not add API permissions for the requested resource, or admin has not consented to the permissions in the tenant.
solution: Add the required API permissions to the app registration (at minimum Microsoft Graph User.Read delegated permission for both client and resource). Have a tenant admin consent to the application.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS650056

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application in SAML authentication flow. The Issuer provided in the SAMLRequest does not match the Identifier (Entity ID).
