sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: OWIN or OpenIDConnect middleware error in ASP.NET application using Azure AD authentication, case routing needed
rootCause: OWIN/OpenIDConnect middleware development is owned by IIS Server team, not AAD Developer. AAD Developer only supports pipeline setup for token acquisition.
solution: Route OWIN/OpenIDConnect specific errors to IIS/ASP.NET server-side component support team. AAD Developer can set up the OWIN/OpenIDConnect pipeline for AAD Graph and MS Graph API token acquisition only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: Customer wants MSAL.js to not request refresh token or needs to remove offline_access from default OIDC scopes
rootCause: MSAL.js includes offline_access in OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES array by default, which always requests a refresh token
solution: Remove offline_access from OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES: import { OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES } from '@azure/msal-common'; OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.splice(OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.indexOf('offline_access'), 1);
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL js How to Scenarios

symptom: MSAL.NET application fails to authenticate when behind a corporate proxy; requests to login.microsoftonline.com time out or get blocked because default MSAL HttpClient has no proxy configured
rootCause: By default, MSAL.NET uses its own internal HttpClient that does not have proxy settings. Applications running behind a corporate proxy cannot reach Azure AD endpoints without explicit proxy configuration.
solution: Implement IMsalHttpClientFactory with a custom HttpClient configured with WebProxy (including credentials if needed), then pass it to PublicClientApplicationBuilder via .WithHttpClientFactory(new CustomMsalHttpClientFactory(httpClient)). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/msal/dotnet/advanced/httpclient
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/Add custom HttpClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK sends requests to the default v1.0 commercial endpoint but the application needs to target the beta API, US Government cloud (graph.microsoft.us), or a specific partner tenant
rootCause: GraphServiceClient defaults to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0. Developers must explicitly override the BaseUrl property to target different endpoints (beta, government, or partner tenant).
solution: Set graphClient.BaseUrl to the desired endpoint: https://graph.microsoft.com/beta for beta API, https://graph.microsoft.us/beta for US Government, or https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/{tenant-id} for partner scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK requests fail or time out when the application is behind a corporate proxy because GraphServiceClient uses default HttpClient without proxy settings
rootCause: The default GraphServiceClient does not configure proxy settings. Applications behind a corporate proxy need to provide a custom HttpClient with HttpClientHandler.Proxy configured.
solution: Create an HttpClient with HttpClientHandler configured with WebProxy (Proxy = new WebProxy(proxyUrl), UseProxy = true), then pass it to new GraphServiceClient(httpClient, authProvider).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient

symptom: Power BI workspace management collab - guest users or regular users cannot read other users in the tenant, sign-in or user query fails with insufficient privileges
rootCause: User or guest user lacks directory read permissions (microsoft.directory/users/standard/read, microsoft.directory/users/identities/read), or custom admin role is missing required user read permissions
solution: Check user permissions via ASC or Graph. For guest users verify they have read access. For custom admin roles, ensure microsoft.directory/users/identities/read and microsoft.directory/users/standard/read are assigned. Refer to Entra ID custom role creation docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Power BI Scenarios with Microsoft Graph

symptom: Power BI Desktop or Power Platform apps fail with 403 Forbidden when calling Microsoft Graph API directly through Power Query connector
rootCause: Power Platform (including Power BI Desktop) does not natively support Microsoft Graph API - the Power Query connector does not consent to the correct Graph permissions. Some queries may work but others fail with 403.
solution: This is expected behavior - Power Platform does not support direct MS Graph calls. Customer should set up custom Power Platform connectors to call Graph with proper app registration and permissions. See https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/connecting-to-graph
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Power BI Scenarios with Microsoft Graph

symptom: Microsoft Graph API returns HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type error with message: A supported MIME type could not be found that matches the content type of the response, content type is application/x-www-form-urlencoded
rootCause: HTTP request Content-Type header is set to an unsupported MIME type (e.g., application/x-www-form-urlencoded) instead of a supported type like application/json
solution: Set the HTTP request Content-Type header to application/json (or another supported type: application/atom+xml, application/json;odata=minimalmetadata, application/json;odata=fullmetadata, application/json;odata=nometadata, etc.)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/ms graph 415 unsupported media type

symptom: Microsoft Graph API returns InvalidTokenEncryptionKeyId error: No KeyCredential found with the configured TokenEncryptionKeyId when accessing application
rootCause: Token Encryption is enabled for the application but the referenced TokenEncryptionKeyId does not match any existing KeyCredential (certificate expired, removed, or ID mismatch)
solution: Option 1: Temporarily disable token encryption in Azure Portal (Entra ID > App registrations > Token encryption > Deactivate), add new certificate, then re-enable. Option 2: PATCH the application via Graph beta API to set keyCredentials with matching keyId and tokenEncryptionKeyId. Ensure the keyId in keyCredentials matches the tokenEncryptionKeyId value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/ms graph InvalidTokenEncryptionKeyId
Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph RBAC APIs/Unified RBAC API Trouble Shooting Guide
symptom: Using resourceScopes attribute in Unified RBAC API role assignment requests returns deprecation warning or unexpected behavior
rootCause: The resourceScopes attribute is deprecated in the Unified RBAC API and will be removed in a future version.
solution: Use directoryScopeId or appScopeId instead of resourceScopes. directoryScopeId references Azure AD objects (tenant, admin units, groups), e.g. "/" for tenant-wide. appScopeId references application-specific scopes native to non-AAD RBAC providers like Intune.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph RBAC APIs/Unified RBAC API Trouble Shooting Guide
symptom: Error creating role assignment via Unified RBAC API - request fails when neither directoryScopeId nor appScopeId is provided
rootCause: Both directoryScopeId and appScopeId are missing in the role assignment creation request. Providing one of them is mandatory for POST /roleManagement/{provider}/roleAssignments.
solution: Provide either directoryScopeId or appScopeId when creating a role assignment. Use directoryScopeId="/" for tenant-wide scope in Azure AD. For Intune and other app-specific scopes, use appScopeId. Example: POST /roleManagement/directory/roleAssignments with {"directoryScopeId": "/"}.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph RBAC APIs/Unified RBAC API Trouble Shooting Guide
symptom: Permission error (403 Forbidden or Insufficient privileges) when managing Azure AD roles via application using Unified RBAC API in delegated flow
rootCause: In delegated flow, effective permission is the intersection of user permissions and app permissions. The user must have Privileged Role Admin (or Global Admin) AND the app must have RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory or Directory.ReadWrite.All scope granted.
solution: Ensure the signed-in user has Privileged Role Admin or Global Admin role. Grant the application either RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory or Directory.ReadWrite.All OAuth scope. For Intune/Exchange workloads, use the corresponding workload-specific permissions.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: Subscription validation request failed. Error: Notification endpoint must respond with 200 OK to validation request when creating Microsoft Graph webhook subscription.
rootCause: The notificationUrl endpoint does not correctly respond to the validation POST request from Microsoft Graph. The endpoint must return HTTP 200 with the validationToken value in the response body as text/plain within 10 seconds.
solution: 1) Verify notificationUrl is accessible from public internet (private networks not supported). 2) Test: POST to {notificationUrl}?validationToken=1234, should return 1234 as text/plain with 200 OK. 3) Ensure response within 10 seconds. 4) Allow inbound traffic from Microsoft Graph Change Notifications IP addresses.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: 403 Forbidden error when creating Microsoft Graph webhook subscription for Azure AD resources (users, groups) due to subscription quota limits exceeded
rootCause: Azure AD subscription quota limits exceeded. Limits: per app = 50,000 subscriptions; per tenant = 1,000 subscriptions across all apps; per app+tenant combination = 100 subscriptions.
solution: Review and clean up existing subscriptions using GET /subscriptions. Delete unused subscriptions to free up quota. Consider consolidating subscriptions where possible.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: Microsoft Graph subscription request fails when using Outlook resource path with a UPN containing an apostrophe (e.g., /users/sh.o'neal@contoso.com/messages)
rootCause: UPN containing apostrophe in the resource path causes subscription request failure for Outlook resources (messages, events, contacts). This is a known limitation.
solution: Use GUID user ID instead of UPN in the resource path. Example: use /users/{guid-user-id}/messages instead of /users/sh.o'neal@contoso.com/messages.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: Microsoft Graph change notifications stop being delivered after TLS 1.0/1.1 deprecation. Subscription latestSupportedTlsVersion shows TLS 1.0 or 1.1.
rootCause: The notification endpoint uses TLS 1.0 or 1.1, which are deprecated. Microsoft Graph requires TLS 1.2 or later for notificationUrl endpoints.
solution: Update the notification endpoint to support TLS 1.2 or later. Check the latestSupportedTlsVersion property on the subscription object to verify TLS version compatibility.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: Delta query returns HTTP 501 Not Implemented when using $select with properties stored outside the main data store (e.g., user skills property)
rootCause: Some resource properties are stored outside the main data store (e.g., user skills is in SharePoint Online, not Azure AD). Delta query only tracks changes for properties in the main data store.
solution: Test property support by performing a regular GET on the resource collection with $select for that property. If 501 is returned, the property is not supported for delta query. Use alternative sync mechanisms for such properties.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/Microsoft Graph WebHooks Change Notifications/Microsoft Graph Notifications and Change Tracking using webhooks
symptom: Delta query stops returning changes or deltaLink/deltaToken becomes invalid after approximately 30 days for Azure AD identity objects (users, groups, directoryRoles)
rootCause: Delta tokens for identity objects (directoryObject, directoryRole, group, user) are valid for only 30 days. After expiration, the token is no longer usable and the client must perform a full resync.
solution: Implement token expiration handling: when a delta token expires, perform a full synchronization by calling the delta function without a deltaToken. Schedule periodic delta query calls within the 30-day window to maintain a valid token chain.

symptom: Conditional Access policy shows Result as Failure in interactive sign-in log, but the user sign-in actually succeeded. CA policy requiring compliant application appears failed even though access was granted.
rootCause: By design: compliant application requirement can only be evaluated at the Token endpoint, not at the Authorize/ProcessAuth endpoint. The interactive sign-in log captures the Authorize endpoint result (deferred/failure), while the actual CA satisfaction happens at the Token endpoint and is recorded in a separate non-interactive sign-in log entry.
solution: Check the non-interactive sign-in log with the same Correlation ID - the CA policy status should show as Success there. In ASC sign-in diagnostic, look for the message: Interactive request for AuthzCode only: deferring MicrosoftEnrollmentId challenge to token endpoint call. The Token endpoint entry will show the CA policy satisfied with MicrosoftEnrollmentId passed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Conditional Access information in sign in log

symptom: Cannot assign users or add members to an Administrative Unit - operation fails due to insufficient permissions
rootCause: Only Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator can create or add members to an Administrative Unit. For Restricted AUs, only specific built-in or custom roles scoped to the Restricted AU can manage its resources.
solution: Ensure the administrator has Global Administrator or Privileged Role Administrator role. For Restricted AUs, assign the appropriate built-in or custom role scoped to the specific Restricted AU. Review the role-scope matrix in the AU documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Administrative Units

symptom: Adding user to Administrative Unit via PowerShell or Graph API fails with error indicating the resource is already a member of the administrative unit
rootCause: The user/resource is already a member of the targeted Administrative Unit. In the Azure Portal UI, existing members appear greyed out and unavailable for selection.
solution: Verify the user is not already a member of the Administrative Unit before attempting to add. Use Get-MgDirectoryAdministrativeUnitMember or Graph API GET /administrativeUnits/{id}/members to check existing membership.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Administrative Units

symptom: Bulk add of users to Administrative Unit fails because CSV file is not in the correct format
rootCause: The downloaded CSV template first two rows (headers/metadata) were deleted or modified by the user. The template requires those rows to remain intact.
solution: Use the downloaded CSV template without modifying the first two rows. Only add member UPNs starting from row 3, one UPN per line. Re-download a fresh template if the original was corrupted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Administrative Units

symptom: HAR trace exported from Chrome 130+ or Edge browser is sanitized by default - missing sensitive data such as cookies (e.g. AppProxyAccessCookie) and authorization headers, making authentication flow troubleshooting impossible or leading to false conclusions
rootCause: Chrome 130 introduced sanitized HAR export by default as a security measure (response to security incidents). Edge adopted the same behavior. All sensitive headers and cookies are stripped from exported HAR files.
solution: 1) In DevTools Settings > Network preferences, enable the Export HAR with sensitive data option - this adds a second export option with full data. 2) Alternatively use NET EXPORT (edge://net-export/ or chrome://net-export/) with include cookies and credentials option. 3) Or use Fiddler/Charles Proxy for data collection instead of browser DevTools HAR export. 4) For cases where sensitive HAR is not possible, use backend logs in ASC (Sign-in logs, Kusto) or do live troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Training/Tools/Using Developer Tools

symptom: Removing Service Administrator fails with error: Cannot delete user <principalId> since they are not the service administrator - when the Service Admin user is not in the directory
rootCause: The Service Administrator user account no longer exists in the directory, so the system cannot match the PUID to remove the role assignment
solution: Try changing the Service Administrator to an existing user first, then remove. If the option to update is greyed out: (1) Collect Subscription Id, PUID from error, and user performing action, (2) Contact TA/PTA to confirm via Jarvis, (3) File IcM to Owning Service: Azure Commercial Experiences, Signup and Provisioning / Owning Team: Tier2 - Subscription Lifecycle
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Classic Administrator Role Retirement

symptom: Customer lost access to Azure subscription after Azure classic administrator roles (Co-Administrator/Service Administrator) were retired or removed
rootCause: Classic administrator roles (Co-Admin, Service Admin) were retired as of August 31, 2024. Users relying solely on these roles lost subscription access without equivalent Azure RBAC assignments
solution: Global Administrator can use the elevation process (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/elevate-access-global-admin) to regain access to subscriptions and management groups. Then assign appropriate Azure RBAC roles (Owner at subscription scope is equivalent)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Classic Administrator Role Retirement

symptom: Starting December 2025 Azure automatically assigned Owner role to users still assigned Co-Administrator or Service Administrator but the classic admin role was not removed
rootCause: Azure backfill process converts classic admin to Owner RBAC role but does not auto-remove the classic administrator assignment. Conversion creates role assignments with createdBy: 0469d4cd-df37-4d93-8a61-f8c75b809164
solution: Manually remove the classic administrator role assignment after confirming the Owner RBAC role is in place. Follow steps at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/role-based-access-control/classic-administrators to remove Co-Administrator
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Classic Administrator Role Retirement

symptom: Creating a custom role in Microsoft Entra ID fails when the role name contains non-alphanumeric characters such as hyphens, equals signs, or unicode characters
rootCause: The custom role creation API does not properly handle non-alphanumeric characters in role names
solution: Remove non-alphanumeric characters from the Role Name when creating the custom role. The role can be renamed to include special characters after the fix is deployed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD RBAC Custom Roles for App Registrations

symptom: Need to determine if a custom domain in Microsoft Entra is configured as Federated or Managed, or identify which tenant a domain belongs to
rootCause: Domain configuration details (federation status, tenant association) are not always clearly visible in the portal
solution: Use OpenID Connect metadata: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId-or-domain}/.well-known/openid-configuration to verify tenant existence and region (tenant_region_scope: NA=Public, EU=Europe, USG=Government). Use User Realm API: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/userrealm/domain.com?api-version=2.1 to check NameSpaceType (Managed vs Federated)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Name Management/Troubleshooting domain verification configuration
Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services/ForestTrustIssueBetweenAADDSandOnPremise
symptom: Cannot create forest trust between Microsoft Entra Domain Services (AADDS) and on-premise Active Directory. Error: "The Trust relationship cannot be created because the following error occurred: The operation failed: This operation cannot be performed on the current domain."
rootCause: Both domains (AADDS and on-premise AD) have the same NetBIOS name. Forest trust does not work when two forests share duplicate NetBIOS names, duplicate DNS domain names, or duplicate domain SIDs. This also applies to transitive trust partners with duplicate legacy names.
solution: Ensure NetBIOS, legacy domain, and DNS domain names are not duplicated between the two forests. Option 1 (Recommended): Recreate Microsoft Entra Domain Services with a different domain name to avoid naming conflict. Option 2: Migrate on-premise domain to a new domain/forest with a unique NetBIOS name using ADMT tool. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/domain-services/tutorial-create-instance

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting/How to Fix "invalid_request" Error When Using login_hint With Email Providers in Entra External ID (CIAM)
symptom: In Entra External ID (CIAM), using login_hint parameter with email addresses from providers like hotmail.com, outlook.com, gmail.com, or yahoo.com returns error: "invalid_request: The provided value for the input parameter redirect_uri is not valid. The expected value is a URI which matches a redirect URI registered for this client application." User is then redirected to login.live.com.
rootCause: When login_hint contains an email from a well-known provider domain, the authorization endpoint interprets it as a federated identity provider login without proper domain disambiguation, causing a redirect to login.live.com instead of the CIAM tenant.
solution: Add the domain_hint parameter alongside login_hint in the authorization request URL. For example, for Gmail users add "&domain_hint=google.com". Full URL format: https://{tenant}.ciamlogin.com/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id=<id>&response_type=code&redirect_uri=<uri>&login_hint=<email>&domain_hint=<provider_domain>. Ensure redirect_uri matches the registered URI for the client application.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting/How to update custom CSS file to include the new selector
symptom: After Fluent migration UX rollout in Entra External ID, internal navigation links ("No account? Create one", "Sign in instead", Resend code) lose their CSS customization. Previously applied styles via "a, a:link" selector no longer take effect on these links.
rootCause: As part of modern accessibility enhancements (Fluent migration), internal navigation links changed from using the generic "a, a:link" CSS selector to a new ".ext-link" class selector. Existing custom CSS targeting "a, a:link" no longer matches these elements. Note: Custom CSS is being removed for Entra ID (workforce) but remains available for Entra External ID.
solution: Update the custom CSS file to include the new ".ext-link" selector. Copy the styles previously applied to "a, a:link" and also apply them to ".ext-link". This can be done proactively before the UX rollout to ensure a seamless transition. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/reference-company-branding-css-template

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Proxy Addresses & Mail
symptom: Guest user mail property and primary proxy address (SMTP:) are out of sync. The mail shows the invited email but proxyAddresses does not contain it as primary SMTP.
rootCause: When a guest is invited to a tenant where a contact already exists with the same proxy address, the mail property is set for the invited user but the address is not added to the proxyAddresses collection. This is by-design behavior for guest users.
solution: This is expected behavior for guest users. The mail property for guests reflects the invited email regardless of proxy address state. If the customer needs them in sync, they may need to remove the conflicting contact object first, then re-invite the guest.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Proxy Addresses & Mail
symptom: Adding a non-verified domain to proxyAddresses for a user with Exchange license silently fails (no error returned) in certain scenarios: guest user re-invitation, or user update where mail property is updated to existing value.
rootCause: The code has special-case handling for guest re-invitation and mail-update-to-same-value scenarios that bypasses the normal verified-domain check, causing the operation to silently succeed without actually applying the change.
solution: Be aware that these two scenarios are known silent failure cases. Verify the domain in the tenant or remove the Exchange license before attempting to add non-verified domain proxy addresses.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Proxy Addresses & Mail
symptom: Cannot update mail property to a non-verified domain (e.g. hotmail.com, gmail.com) for users with Exchange Online license. The update may return success but the value does not change.
rootCause: By design, EXO-licensed users can only have mail on a verified domain of the tenant because Exchange creates a mailbox for such users. Many licenses (e.g. Teams) implicitly include EXO. The proxy address computation discards the non-verified domain value.
solution: 1) Remove the Exchange license (including implicit EXO licenses like Teams) before updating mail. 2) Add and verify the target domain in the tenant. 3) Use an email address on an already-verified domain. Note: many licenses that are not directly Exchange still require Exchange mailbox creation.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Proxy Addresses & Mail
symptom: PATCH user call to update mail property via MS Graph returns 200 success, but the mail value remains unchanged after the call.
rootCause: The mail update flow adds the new value as primary proxy address, then runs proxy address computation (CalculateUserProxyAddressesInternal). If computation discards the new value (e.g. non-verified domain + Exchange license), the mail is set back to the old primary proxy address. The API does not return an error in this case.
solution: Verify the domain is verified in the tenant, or remove Exchange license before updating. Check proxyAddresses after update to confirm the change took effect. A repair item is in progress to return an error when this silent failure occurs.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 during assign user license for self-service purchased SKU
symptom: Assign user license via MS Graph API fails with 403 Access Denied, even though the calling principal has sufficient permissions as documented. MSODS logs show CheckAccessForPrivilegedAppLicensingSkuOperations AccessGranted=False for a self-service purchase SKU.
rootCause: The target SKU is a Self-service purchase SKU (e.g. Microsoft Teams Exploratory Dept, SKU ID e0dfc8b9-9531-4ec8-94b4-9fec23b05fc8). These SKUs can only be allocated by authorized partners or the users who purchased them. Tenant admins cannot assign or remove self-service purchase SKUs via standard admin APIs.
solution: 1) Ask the customer to sign in with the self-service purchaser account to manage the license. 2) Global Admin can perform admin takeover: Billing > Your products > click the product > Take control of the subscription. 3) Global Admin can disable self-service purchase option in the tenant to prevent future occurrences. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/commerce/subscriptions/manage-self-service-purchases-admins

symptom: Cannot create a dynamic group - option not visible in Azure portal or PowerShell returns error
rootCause: Tenant does not have Azure AD P1/P2 Premium license, or the user creating the group lacks the required admin permissions (Global Administrator or User Administrator role)
solution: 1. Verify tenant has Azure AD P1/P2 Premium license. 2. Ensure the user has Global Administrator or User Administrator role. 3. Check group creation permissions under All groups > General (Settings).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Dynamic group Creation and Deletion

symptom: Cannot find the attribute to create a dynamic group membership rule - attribute not available in rule builder dropdown
rootCause: The attribute is not in the list of supported user/device properties for dynamic group rules, or the customer is using device attribute in user context
solution: 1. Check supported user properties list. 2. Check device attributes list separately. 3. If not in Simple Rule dropdown, use Advanced Rule with correct object prefix (user.country, device.deviceOSType). 4. For custom attributes, use Extension Attributes queryable via PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Dynamic group Creation and Deletion

symptom: Dynamic group membership processing is very slow, impacting other dynamic groups in the tenant
rootCause: Dynamic group rule uses the contains or match operator, which is not indexed by Mezzo (internal service for dynamic group processing)
solution: Replace contains/match operator with startsWith in the dynamic group rule. The contains operator has been removed from the Entra Admin Center rule builder UX. Advise customer to migrate existing rules to startsWith for better performance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Dynamic group Creation and Deletion

symptom: Error 'Get max groups allowed' when creating a Dynamic group in PowerShell
rootCause: Tenant has reached the maximum limit of 15,000 dynamic groups
solution: Delete existing unused dynamic groups. The 15,000 dynamic group limit per tenant cannot be increased. Check current count and remove unnecessary groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Dynamic group Creation and Deletion

symptom: Restored a deleted dynamic group but the group shows no members / no membership update
rootCause: When a dynamic group is deleted and restored, it is treated as a new group and must be re-populated. Re-population can take up to 24 hours.
solution: Wait up to 24 hours for the dynamic group membership to be re-populated after restoration. The group will be re-evaluated against its membership rule as if newly created.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Dynamic group Creation and Deletion
Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra
symptom: Security Copilot returns different/inconsistent results for the same prompt in Entra Admin Center.
rootCause: Security Copilot uses LLM which is non-deterministic by design. The same prompt may return different results each time.
solution: This is expected behavior. Use thumbs up/down feedback icons to help tune responses over time. Microsoft will continuously tune responses based on user feedback.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra
symptom: User sees a permissions warning or cannot use Copilot button in Entra Admin Center despite having an Entra admin role.
rootCause: Security Copilot in Entra requires an active Microsoft Security Copilot subscription with Security Consumption Units (SCUs) and appropriate Entra admin role. Without SCUs, the Copilot button opens but shows permissions errors.
solution: Ensure the tenant has an active Security Copilot subscription with provisioned SCUs. Verify the user has the required Entra admin role for the specific scenario (e.g., Security Reader for sign-in logs, Identity Governance Administrator for access reviews).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra
symptom: Customer uses Azure Copilot in portal.azure.com for Entra-related queries and gets no results or incorrect guidance.
rootCause: Azure Copilot (in portal.azure.com) is a separate free offering that does not integrate with Entra. Entra-related prompts must be used in the Entra Admin Center or Security Copilot standalone portal.
solution: Direct the user to use Security Copilot embedded in Entra Admin Center (entra.microsoft.com) or the standalone Security Copilot portal (securitycopilot.microsoft.com). Azure Copilot at portal.azure.com is a different product.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra/Security Copilot Embedded in Entra
symptom: Enhanced Data Exploration view does not appear for multi-step or complex queries in Security Copilot; only shows text summary or markdown table instead of Open list button.
rootCause: Multi-step queries are not supported during the initial public preview of Enhanced Data Exploration. The feature only triggers for simple single-step prompts returning 10+ data points.
solution: This is a known limitation in public preview. Use simple single-step prompts to trigger the Enhanced Data Exploration view. Multi-step query support is planned for a future release.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Bulk Actions for Users and Groups
symptom: Bulk Delete or Bulk Create buttons appear grayed out/inactive in the Entra Admin Center Users blade.
rootCause: The user does not have the required Azure AD role (Global Administrator or User Administrator), or the target groups are unmanageable types (directory synced, dynamic, or exchange mastered groups).
solution: Ensure the user has Global Administrator or User Administrator role. For group membership operations, only Azure AD Security Groups and Microsoft 365 Groups support bulk actions. Directory synced, dynamic, and exchange mastered groups cannot be managed via bulk operations.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Bulk Actions for Users and Groups
symptom: Cannot upload CSV file for Bulk invite users with additional custom columns like Job Title, Phone Number, or other custom fields.
rootCause: The Bulk invite CSV template does not support additional custom columns beyond the standard fields. Only the predefined CSV format is accepted.
solution: Known limitation. Use Microsoft Graph API or PowerShell (New-MgInvitation) to perform bulk invitations with custom properties. The portal bulk invite feature only supports the standard CSV template fields.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Custom Security Attributes
symptom: Global Administrator cannot view or manage Custom Security Attributes in the Entra Admin Center. Operations fail with insufficient permissions.
rootCause: By design, Global Administrators do not have permissions for Custom Security Attributes. Similarly, Global Reader role does not allow reading custom security attributes. This is an intentional security boundary.
solution: Assign the Attribute Definition Administrator role to manage attribute definitions and sets. Assign the Attribute Assignment Administrator role to assign attributes to users and applications. Assign Attribute Log Reader or Attribute Log Administrator for audit log access.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Custom Security Attributes
symptom: Custom Security Attributes cannot be added to users synced from on-premises Active Directory. The option is unavailable or returns an error.
rootCause: Custom security attributes are only supported for cloud-only users (members and B2B guests). On-premises synced users are excluded by design.
solution: Known limitation. Custom security attributes can only be assigned to cloud-only users, B2B guests, and service principals/managed identities. For on-prem synced users, consider using directory extension attributes via Azure AD Connect instead.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Custom Security Attributes
symptom: Error when creating a custom security attribute set or attribute name that contains spaces.
rootCause: Attribute set names and custom security attribute names cannot contain spaces. This is a platform validation constraint.
solution: Remove spaces from attribute set names and attribute names. Use camelCase or underscores instead (e.g., HRAttributes or HR_Attributes instead of HR Attributes).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Enhanced search sort filter in user blade
symptom: Domain name filter in Entra Admin Center Users blade does not return users whose alternate email addresses match the searched domain.
rootCause: The domain name filter only searches User principal name (UPN) and Mail properties. It does not search Alternate emails or other identity properties.
solution: Known limitation. Use Microsoft Graph API with $filter to search otherMails. Example: GET /users?$filter=otherMails/any(m:endsWith(m,'@contoso.com'))

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD Custom Security Attributes
symptom: Custom Security Attributes are not available or not supported in Azure AD B2C tenant.
rootCause: Custom security attributes are not supported in B2C tenants by design. The feature is only available in Azure AD (Entra ID) tenants.
solution: Known limitation. For B2C tenants, use standard extension attributes or custom user attributes via user flows/custom policies instead of custom security attributes.

symptom: Access package request in Entitlement Management is automatically denied without reaching the standard approval workflow when ID Protection-based risk approval is enabled
rootCause: ID Protection-based risk approval was triggered because the user was flagged as risky by Microsoft Entra ID Protection. The Security Administrator approver did not take action within the 14-day timeout window, causing the request to be auto-denied
solution: Ensure Security Administrators actively monitor and respond to risk-based approval requests within the 14-day window. Check configuration at ID Governance > Entitlement management > Control configurations > Risk-based approval > ID Protection risk settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/ID Protection based approvals for access package requests in Entitlement Management

symptom: Access package request in Entitlement Management is automatically denied without reaching the standard approval workflow when Insider Risk Management-based approval is enabled
rootCause: Insider Risk Management-based approval was triggered because the user risk level matched the configured threshold (Moderate or Elevated). The Compliance Administrator approver did not take action within the 14-day timeout window, causing the request to be auto-denied
solution: Ensure Compliance Administrators actively monitor and respond to insider risk-based approval requests within the 14-day window. Check configuration at ID Governance > Entitlement management > Control configurations > Risk-based approval > Insider Risk settings. Prerequisites: Must have an Insider Risk Management policy created in Microsoft Purview
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Insider risk management based approvals for access package requests in Entitlement Management

symptom: Access package request takes longer than expected to reach the standard approval workflow or appears stuck in multiple pending approval stages
rootCause: Both ID Protection and Insider Risk Management risk-based approvals are enabled in Entitlement Management Control configurations. When both are active, the request routes through IDP approver first, then IRM approver, then standard access package policy approvers, creating two extra approval stages
solution: Review Control configurations > Risk-based approval settings to check if both ID Protection and Insider Risk Management approvals are enabled. If only one risk signal is needed, disable the other. If both are required, ensure both Security Administrators (for IDP) and Compliance Administrators (for IRM) are aware of the multi-stage flow and act promptly
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/ID Protection based approvals for access package requests in Entitlement Management

symptom: Identity Governance Inactive Guest Report shows 0 inactive guest accounts or the Inactive guest accounts bar is missing from the dashboard
rootCause: All guest accounts were added to the tenant more recently than the configured inactivity threshold (default 90 days). The report only considers guests as inactive if their inactive days exceed the threshold
solution: Adjust the inactivity threshold using Edit inactivity threshold on the ID Governance dashboard. The threshold can be set from 0 to 360 days in 30-day increments. Review the percentile data in the Guest inactivity overview section to determine an appropriate threshold for the tenant
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Identity Governance Inactive Guest Report

symptom: Catalog Access Review for custom data provided resource is stuck in Initializing state and never transitions to Active
rootCause: Custom access data CSV was not uploaded within the 2-hour window from when the review entered the Initializing state. The upload must happen within 2 hours of review creation
solution: Create a new catalog access review and upload the CSV data within 2 hours of creation. Navigate to ID Governance > Entitlement management > Catalogs > [catalog] > Resources > select the custom data resource > Upload custom access data. Provide both the access review object ID and instance object ID during upload
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Include custom data provided resource for catalog user Access Reviews

symptom: Catalog Access Review for custom data provided resource is stuck in Applying state and never transitions to Applied
rootCause: Not all review decisions have been PATCH-ed with applyResult via the Graph API. The review only transitions to Applied state once ALL custom data provided decisions have been marked with an apply result
solution: For each denied decision, call PATCH /identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/{reviewId}/instances/{instanceId}/decisions/{decisionId} with body {applyResult: Success/Failure/PartialSuccess/NotSupported, applyDescription: description}. The review has a 30-day window during Applying state to complete all PATCH operations
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Include custom data provided resource for catalog user Access Reviews

symptom: Administrator performs PATCH on /beta/admin/people/photoUpdateSettings and receives error 400 PeopleAdminValidationFailure with message 'Unsupported role for cloud photo update settings' when using unsupported role TemplateIDs in the allowedRoles section.
rootCause: Only Global Administrator (62e90394-69f5-4237-9190-012177145e10) and User Administrator (fe930be7-5e62-47db-91af-98c3a49a38b1) roles are supported in the allowedRoles section of photoUpdateSettings. Other role TemplateIDs are not accepted.
solution: Ensure only Global Administrator and User Administrator role TemplateIDs are specified in the allowedRoles section when configuring cloud photo update settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Account

symptom: User revoked LinkedIn permissions under Privacy > LinkedIn in My Account portal, but cannot re-allow or re-connect LinkedIn permissions from the same portal.
rootCause: My Account portal only supports revoking LinkedIn permissions, not re-establishing them. Once revoked, LinkedIn no longer appears under connected services in My Account.
solution: User must re-connect their LinkedIn account with their work account by following the instructions at https://support.office.com/article/connect-your-linkedin-and-work-or-school-accounts-c7c245f2-fa56-4c9b-ba20-3fceb23c5772. The permission cannot be restored from within My Account itself.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Account

symptom: PATCH on photoUpdateSettings returns error 400 PeopleAdminValidationFailure with message 'Roles should be passed only for cloud photo update settings' when attempting to set allowedRoles with source set to onPremises.
rootCause: When the photo update settings source is set to 'onPremises', the allowedRoles section must be null. Roles are only applicable when source is 'cloud'.
solution: When setting source to 'onPremises', set the allowedRoles section to null. Only pass role TemplateIDs in allowedRoles when the source is configured for cloud photo update settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Account

symptom: User navigates to My Staff portal and sees error: 'Oops, seems you are not authorized to see My Staff at this time. Please contact your admin for more information.'
rootCause: The user has not been assigned any admin role, or the My Staff feature has not been enabled under Azure AD > User Settings > Manage user feature preview settings.
solution: 1) Assign an admin role (any scoped or tenant-wide role) to the user. 2) Enable My Staff under Azure portal > Azure Active Directory > User Settings > Manage user feature preview settings. Both conditions must be met for My Staff access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Staff

symptom: Scoped Admin Unit administrator in My Staff sees all AUs in the tenant list, but when selecting an AU they do not have control over, gets error 'The list is empty'. Navigating away continues to show the error, requiring browser close and re-sign-in.
rootCause: My Staff displays the complete list of all AUs in the tenant, including those the scoped admin does not have delegated control over. Selecting an unauthorized AU triggers the persistent error.
solution: Close the browser and sign in to My Staff again. Avoid selecting AUs that the admin does not have delegated control over. This is a known UX limitation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Staff

symptom: In My Staff portal, user sees 'You must be an admin to view this site' error even though the user is a member of an Administrative Unit and has role assignments visible.
rootCause: Being a member of an AU does not grant admin access to My Staff. The role assignment Scope must be 'This resource' (scoped at AU level). Role assignments with Scope = 'Directory (Inherited)' do not grant access over the AU in My Staff.
solution: Verify the user has a scoped admin role assigned at the AU level, not inherited from the directory level. Check the role assignment Scope column - it must show 'This resource', not 'Directory (Inherited)'. Grant a scoped role at the AU level if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Staff

symptom: In My Staff, rapidly adding, changing, or deleting a user phone number causes the phone number to become corrupted in the backend. Even with correct settings, the phone number no longer works for MFA/SSPR/SMS sign-in.
rootCause: Rapid sequential edits (add/change/delete) to phone numbers in My Staff cause backend data corruption due to race conditions in the credential management system.
solution: Contact Microsoft support directly if phone number corruption is suspected. Avoid rapidly performing multiple sequential actions on a user phone number in My Staff.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Azure AD My Staff

symptom: convertExternalToInternalMemberUser API returns error 'Non-external user is not eligible for conversion' even though user was originally invited as external.
rootCause: The API considers a user external only if: (1) creationType=Invitation + externalUserState=Accepted + identities contains non-UPN signInType, OR (2) creationType=Invitation + userType=Guest. If user was invited but never accepted invite and userType was set to Member, the API rejects the conversion.
solution: Check user attributes via Graph: GET /users/{id}?$select=creationType,externalUserState,userType,identities. If userType is Member but user was created via Invitation and never accepted, change userType to Guest first, then call convertExternalToInternalMemberUser. The API will automatically change userType back to Member during conversion.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Convert External Users to Internal Users

symptom: convertExternalToInternalMemberUser API returns error 'Password is required for users synchronized from on-premises and with managed domain' for a hybrid user with Password Hash Sync enabled.
rootCause: The API identifies the user as a hybrid user with PHS. Although PHS users do not need a password for conversion, the API currently requires at least a passwordProfile in the request body. This is a known bug (Bug 3428519).
solution: Include a blank passwordProfile in the API request as a workaround: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/users/{objectId}/convertExternalToInternalMemberUser with body: {"passwordProfile": {"forceChangePasswordNextSignIn": "false"}}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Convert External Users to Internal Users

symptom: Soft-deleted user is not permanently (hard) deleted after 30 days as expected. User remains in deleted users list beyond the 30-day retention window.
rootCause: The permanent deletion process starts after 30 days but is asynchronous. There is no guarantee data will be deleted at exactly the 30-day mark. The garbage collection process can take additional days to complete.
solution: Wait for the asynchronous deletion process to complete (may take a few additional days after the 30-day window). If less than 30 days have passed, admins can manually hard-delete the user from the UX. After 30 days, manual hard-delete is not available and you must wait for the automatic process.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQ - User soft delete and hard delete

symptom: Hard-deleted user still appears in Entra portal All Users list when searching, even though the user has been permanently deleted.
rootCause: The Entra portal User Blade uses $count=true and consistencyLevel=eventual in MS Graph API calls, which routes requests to the Mid-Tier query service instead of RestDirectoryService. The Mid-Tier query service may have stale cached data for deleted users.
solution: Verify the user is not returned without $count=true and without consistencyLevel=eventual header. If confirmed stale, raise an ICM to 'AAD Mid-tier Query Service / Mid-tier Query Service team' to clear the stale information. Reference ICM: 471093230.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQ - User soft delete and hard delete

symptom: Cannot create Lifecycle Workflow without a template in Azure portal
rootCause: Azure portal requires workflows to be based on a template; creating workflows from scratch without a template is only supported via Microsoft Graph API
solution: Use Microsoft Graph API to create a workflow without using a template. Portal-created workflows must be based on an existing template.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Self-help/CRUD

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow execution condition scope rule not working with schema extensions, open extensions, or custom security attributes
rootCause: Only extension attributes and directory (Azure AD) extensions are supported for configuring workflow scope rules. Schema extensions, open extensions, and custom security attributes are NOT supported.
solution: Use extension attributes or directory (Azure AD) extensions for workflow scope rules. See https://learn.microsoft.com/graph/extensibility-overview for supported extension types.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Self-help/CRUD

symptom: Changes to Lifecycle Workflow tasks or execution conditions not taking effect after update
rootCause: Modifying tasks or execution conditions requires creating a new version of the workflow. Direct in-place updates to existing version tasks/conditions are not supported.
solution: In Azure portal, changes to tasks/execution conditions auto-create a new version. In Microsoft Graph, call the createNewVersion method explicitly. Only basic info (display name, description) can be updated without versioning.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Self-help/CRUD

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow not running on schedule / workflow not executing automatically
rootCause: Either the workflow itself is disabled, or the workflow schedule is disabled. Both must be enabled for scheduled execution.
solution: Check in ASC under Tenant Explorer > Identity Governance > Lifecycle Workflows: (1) Verify workflow is enabled, (2) Verify workflow schedule is enabled. See Workflow scheduling not supported for disabled workflows TSG if workflows are unexpectedly disabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/TSGs/Workflows not starting as expected

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow not triggering for a specific user even though workflow is enabled and scheduled
rootCause: User does not meet execution conditions: (1) User lacks the required time-based attribute (employeeHireDate or employeeLeaveDateTime), (2) Current date is outside the trigger offset window, or (3) User properties do not match scope rules. Workflow checks execution condition in a 2-day window only.
solution: Verify: (1) User has the time-based attribute set in AAD, (2) Current date falls within the trigger offset range (note: 2-day window, e.g. offset 7 days checks only days 6-7), (3) User properties match scope rules exactly, (4) Expected run time is within the past 3 days. Use ASC to check all conditions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/TSGs/Workflows not starting as expected

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow scope rule not matching expected users despite correct property values
rootCause: Scope rule values are case-sensitive. Mismatched casing between workflow scope rule and user property values in Azure AD causes the user to not match the scope.
solution: Ensure exact case match between workflow scope rule values and user property values. For example, if scope rule is department eq Marketing, the user department must be exactly Marketing (not marketing or MARKETING).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/TSGs/Workflows not starting as expected

symptom: Blank tables in Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytics Report (PAR) PDF download
rootCause: The authorization system (AWS account, Azure subscription, or GCP project) has no findings for that particular category.
solution: This is expected behavior. The table will display a message like 'You do not have any inactive users.' No action needed - the blank table means zero findings in that category.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytic Report

symptom: Error 'Failed to generate & download report' in Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytics Report
rootCause: Data collection may not have started or completed for the selected authorization systems.
solution: Wait for the next data collection cycle (~4 hours) and try again. If issue persists, file an ICM to Owning Service: Cloud Infrastructure Entitlement Management Service (MCIEM), Owning Team: Incident Manager.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytic Report

symptom: Cannot open Permissions Analytics Report PDF from email link
rootCause: Authentication or browser session issue preventing access to the report download link.
solution: Open the link in an incognito/private browser window and log in with Permissions Management credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytic Report

symptom: Permissions Analytics Report email not received after requesting PDF export
rootCause: Email address configured in Permissions Management Account Settings is incorrect.
solution: In Permissions Management, go to Account Settings and verify the email address. If incorrect, ask the Global Administrator (for the AAD tenant where PM is enabled) to update the email credentials. If issue persists, file an ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra Permissions Management Permissions Analytic Report

symptom: Customer asking about Microsoft Entra Permissions Management (MEPM) retirement, end-of-life, or inability to purchase new licenses
rootCause: Microsoft announced MEPM retirement effective October 1, 2025. End-of-sale for new customers: April 1, 2025. End-of-sale CSP: May 1, 2025. Decision driven by realignment of security investments toward core identity categories and AI.
solution: Existing customers retain access until Sept 30, 2025 with full support. Migration options: (1) Delinea partner CIEM solution (https://delinea.com/microsoft-ciem), (2) CIEM capabilities in Microsoft Defender for Cloud CSPM plan continue and are being enhanced. Refunds available for remaining contract value after April 1, 2025. Offboarding guide: https://aka.ms/MEPMmigration. Refund questions to commerce team. Non-technical questions to customer account specialist. ICM template: https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v3/incidents/create?tmpl=42d1O1
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra Permissions Management Retirement

symptom: createdDateTime property returns null for a user in Microsoft Graph API
rootCause: The createdDateTime property was introduced in June 2018. For users created before this date, the property will display as null. The value is permanently lost and cannot be recovered.
solution: This is a known limitation for older user records created before June 2018. Inform customer that the value cannot be recovered. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/user
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQ - Why UserType and createdDateTime properties are null or empty for a user

symptom: userType property is null/empty for a user in Microsoft Graph or Azure AD
rootCause: The userType attribute was introduced in August 2014. Users created prior to this date may have userType as null. Users with null userType are treated as Members by default.
solution: No functional impact - null userType is treated as Member. If needed, update via Microsoft Graph API (PATCH user) or PowerShell. For DirSync-enabled users: use Entra Cloud Sync (how-to-map-usertype) or Entra Connect Sync configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQ - Why UserType and createdDateTime properties are null or empty for a user

symptom: Microsoft Graph filter on identities property ignores issuerName value - query returns correct B2C record regardless of issuer value supplied
rootCause: By design: when signInType is 'username', the issuer value is ignored in the filter (issuerAssignedId is still required). Documented at graph/api/resources/objectidentity#filtering.
solution: Inform customer this is by design per Microsoft Graph documentation. The issuer value is ignored for username signInType but must still be provided in the filter syntax. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/objectidentity
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: Unable to update HideFromAddressList attribute via Microsoft Graph PATCH call - error: Unable to update the specified properties for objects that have originated within an external service
rootCause: HideFromAddressList is managed by Exchange, not Azure AD. Direct modification via Graph API is not supported.
solution: For on-premises synced users: modify MsExchHideFromAddressList attribute on-prem. For cloud users: modify via Microsoft 365 Admin Center (admin.microsoft.com) > Active Users > user > Mail > Show in global address list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: After disabling Directory Synchronization, cannot modify some object attributes through Azure AD - changes fail or are silently ignored
rootCause: When DirSync authority changes, SingleAuthorityMetadata is stamped on the object with ExchangeMastered=True, restricting certain attributes to Exchange-only modification.
solution: Use Exchange to modify the affected attributes (workaround). Engineering work item 1908437 tracks permanent fix to stop stamping SingleAuthorityMetadata.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: UPN of an invited guest user appears to be truncated - only partial email address shown before #EXT#
rootCause: The UPN part before @ is limited to 54 characters (59 including #EXT#). If the invited guest has a very long email address, only the first 54 characters are used.
solution: This is a known platform limitation. The 54-character limit on UPN prefix for guest users is enforced by design. Inform customer of this limitation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: Graph API call to /users/{id}/manager returns 404 Request_ResourceNotFound error even though the user exists
rootCause: When a user does not have a manager assigned, the manager navigation property does not exist on the user object, so Graph returns 404.
solution: This is expected behavior. The 404 means no manager is set, not that the API is broken. Handle 404 in application code as 'no manager assigned'. Microsoft is investigating a more verbose error message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: Deleted users are not permanently removed after exactly 30 days - user still appears in deleted users list past 30-day mark
rootCause: The permanent deletion process starts after the 30-day soft-delete window but is asynchronous and can take additional days to complete.
solution: Inform customer this is expected behavior. Before 30 days: can permanently delete from UX. After 30 days: wait for the async permanent deletion process. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/fundamentals/users-restore
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: Helpdesk Administrator cannot reset password for a non-admin user who is a member/owner of a role-assignable group. Similarly, Authentication Admin cannot revoke refresh tokens for such users.
rootCause: Members of role-assignable groups are treated as protected/sensitive principals. Helpdesk Admin and Authentication Admin lack permissions to perform sensitive actions on these users.
solution: Use Global Admin or Privileged Authentication Admin to reset the password or revoke tokens. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/role-based-access-control/privileged-roles-permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/FAQs

symptom: CSP/partner technician with GDAP relationship cannot sign in to customer tenant - blocked by Conditional Access policy targeting external users
rootCause: Conditional Access policies that target external users may interfere with service provider access (GDAP). The CA policy blocks the partner user as an external identity.
solution: Use the updated fine-grained CA policies for guests and external users (released Nov 2022) to exclude service providers. Configure inbound cross-tenant access policies with GDAP. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-users-groups#external-partner-access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/GDAP (Granular Delegated Admin Privileges) launch for partners

symptom: Unable to bulk download user list from Entra portal when Custom Security Attributes filter is applied
rootCause: Known bug - Bulk Processor service does not have permissions to GetCustomSecurityAttributeValues. Bug 2934174.
solution: Remove the Custom Security Attributes filter before performing bulk download. Bug fix tracked at https://dev.azure.com/IdentityDivision/Engineering/_workitems/edit/2934174
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: On Users Blade the user count is incorrect when using a Last sign-in time filter
rootCause: Expected behavior: when Last sign-in time filter is applied, count, other filters and sorting are not supported (as noted by the warning icon in the UX).
solution: Inform customer this is expected when using Last sign-in time filter. The count displayed is approximate. Use other methods (e.g., Graph API with signInActivity) for accurate counts.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: Insufficient Privileges error when Global Admin tries to list all users from Azure/Entra Portal
rootCause: The Last sign-in time column is selected for display, which requires Entra ID P1/P2 premium license. If the tenant lacks premium license, the signInActivity data request returns insufficient privileges.
solution: Remove the Last sign-in time column from the Users blade display columns. Alternatively, obtain Entra ID P1/P2 license for the tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: The interactive sign-in count graph on user Overview Monitoring tab shows data shifted by one day (e.g., Dec 18 sign-ins shown on Dec 17)
rootCause: Known bug: the Monitoring tab graph displays sign-in event dates shifted by a single day. Note the graph uses UTC time, not local time. Bug 3125923.
solution: Workaround: use Sign-in Logs directly for accurate counts of interactive sign-in events per day (ensure UTC time is used). Bug tracked at https://dev.azure.com/IdentityDivision/Engineering/_workitems/edit/3125923
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: Changing Email property of an Exchange Online licensed user to a non-validated domain via Entra portal or Graph API returns success, but the email address is not actually updated
rootCause: Exchange Online ProxyCalc enforces that email domains must be validated (accepted domains) in the tenant. It silently blocks the change (ShouldSanitizeSmtp=True) even though Graph API returns 200 success and audit logs show the change succeeded.
solution: Ensure the email domain is a validated/accepted domain in the tenant before updating. Use Exchange Online admin center which restricts selection to validated domains only. Bug 1292458 tracks returning proper error instead of false success.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: Error when filtering/sorting by CreatedDateTime column in Users blade for B2C tenants
rootCause: Sort/filter on createdDateTime is not supported for B2C tenants. Engineering confirmed this is a known limitation.
solution: Workaround: click the link at the top of the Users page in the B2C tenant to switch back to the legacy users list experience. Engineering will disable the sort option for B2C tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: B2B Collaboration card on User Overview blade incorrectly shows a Member user as external - conflicting with the correct Member userType shown on the same blade
rootCause: Known bug triggered when user has Identities: phone set. The B2B Collaboration card misinterprets this identity type as external. Bug 3372098.
solution: Workaround: change the user Identities from phone to another type. Bug tracked at https://dev.azure.com/IdentityDivision/Engineering/_workitems/edit/3372098. CRI 681944445.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: User photos are inconsistent between Azure AD/Entra portal and other Microsoft 365 services (Teams, Outlook, SharePoint)
rootCause: For hybrid customers, photos uploaded to on-premises AD sync to Azure AD thumbnailPhoto property. This property only syncs to the M365 photo repository if its size is under 10KB. Photos larger than 10KB remain in Azure AD but do not propagate to M365 services.
solution: Upload a photo under 10KB to on-premises AD, or update the user photo directly through Azure AD Portal / Microsoft Graph API to bypass the 10KB sync limitation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/Known issues

symptom: Local account guest users in Entra External ID are redirected to federated login page when signup is disabled in user flow and home tenant has Alternate Login ID enabled. User sees home tenant logo followed by an error message instead of being able to sign in.
rootCause: Bug in ESTS (Bug 3043172): When signup is disabled in user flow and the home tenant has Alternate Login ID enabled, the system incorrectly redirects local account guest users to the federated login flow instead of allowing local authentication.
solution: Workaround: Enable signup in user flow, then configure an OnAttributeCollectionStart custom authentication extension to send a block page to users attempting to sign up. This allows invited/created guest users to sign in while preventing unwanted signups. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/external-id/customers/concept-custom-extensions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting/Known Issue: Local account guest users are redirected to federated login

symptom: AADSTS50058 error during silent sign-in: 'A silent sign-in request was sent but no user is signed in. The cookies used to represent the user session were not sent in the request to Azure AD.'
rootCause: Silent sign-in request (prompt=none) is sent to Entra ID but the estsauth cookie is missing from the request headers. This happens when the cookie is blocked by browser security zones (IE/Edge zones mismatch between app and login.microsoftonline.com), the session has expired, or browser configuration prevents cookies from being passed.
solution: 1) Application must handle this error gracefully and prompt the user for interactive sign-in. 2) Ensure browser is properly configured: add both login.microsoftonline.com and the application endpoint to trusted sites in IE/Edge security zone settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50058

symptom: AADSTS50197 error: 'Sorry, we could not find the user, please sign-in again.' User encounters this error during authentication flow.
rootCause: Conflict due to the presence of different user sessions within the Flow Token (tracked under Bug 1989295). Multiple user sessions create a conflict that prevents proper user identification during the authentication flow.
solution: Temporary workarounds: 1) Use InPrivate/Incognito browsing window to avoid session conflicts. 2) Workplace Join the device using the conflicting account (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/devices/concept-device-registration). 3) Temporarily disable Phone/Passwordless sign-in (PSI) on the conflicting account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS50197

symptom: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application with ID. User sees consent error when accessing third-party or registered application.
rootCause: Admin consent has not been granted for the application. The app API permissions require admin consent but it has not been performed.
solution: For registered apps: Azure AD > App registrations > select app > API Permissions > Grant consent. For third-party apps: use admin consent URL v1 or v2 format with tenant-id, app-id, and redirect-uri.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65001

symptom: AADSTS65001 caused by malformed scope parameter in OAuth2 authentication request. Scope value uses incorrect URL encoding (e.g. double-encoded %2520 instead of %20).
rootCause: Third-party application sends scope parameter with double-encoded or improperly formatted values. Scope values must be separated by a space (%20), but app sends incorrect encoding.
solution: Check the scope parameter in the authentication request. Values must be space-separated (%20 in URL encoding). Contact third-party app developer to fix the scope parameter format.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65001

symptom: AADSTS65002: Consent between first party application and first party resource must be configured via preauthorization.
rootCause: Cannot modify permissions for first party (Microsoft) applications through normal consent flow. First-party-to-first-party consent requires a special internal preauthorization process.
solution: The product team for the first party application client must follow the internal preauthorization process. Use Get-MgServicePrincipalByAppId -AppId to identify which Microsoft application the client ID belongs to.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/AADSTS Errors/AADSTS65002

symptom: AADSTS65004: User Declined to consent to access the app. Error appears after user clicks Back to app button in the Admin Consent Workflow.
rootCause: Expected behavior by design. When user clicks Back to app during admin consent workflow, Entra ID sends AADSTS65004 to the redirect URI because user is not authorized to perform user consent.
solution: This error is expected. Set expectations with customer: after user submits admin consent request and administrator approves it, user must start a NEW authentication flow for the application. The Back to app button does NOT complete the original auth flow.
