solution: Rename the Private Network to remove all special characters and trigger a new forwarding profile download or wait until the profile is updated. Fix is included in GSA Client version 2.28.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Internal Docs/Microsoft Entra Private Access: Known Issues

symptom: When multiple connectors are in a connector group for GSA Private Access, connections from the same client are routed through different connectors, causing varying source IP addresses. Users experience unexpected logouts or application misbehavior.
rootCause: GSA Private Access does not support connector persistency (stickiness) for clients by default. Each connection may be routed to any available connector, resulting in different source IPs.
solution: Options: 1) NAT all connector IPs to a single IP. 2) Use cookie-based session persistence. 3) For RDWEB, configure RD Web and RD Gateway per docs. 4) Use single connector (not recommended). 5) Join the Connector Stickiness Private Preview via enrollment form.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Internal Docs/Microsoft Entra Private Access: Known Issues

symptom: UDP-dependent applications stop functioning on the connector server. System Event Log Event 4266: A request to allocate an ephemeral port number from the global UDP port space has failed due to all such ports being in use. Netstat shows connector service keeping many UDP ports open.
rootCause: High-volume UDP traffic from applications using Entra Private Access leads to exhaustion of UDP ephemeral ports on the connector server. The current design does not immediately release UDP ports after use.
solution: 1) Increase the UDP ephemeral port range per Microsoft documentation. 2) Deploy additional connectors to distribute load. 3) Restart connector service to temporarily release ports (may cause disruption).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Internal Docs/Microsoft Entra Private Access: Known Issues

symptom: GSA client displays Disabled by your organization after installation. EU users see Event 421 Warning: User token acquisition failed with AADSTS9002341: User is required to permit SSO.
rootCause: The Digital Markets Act (DMA) SSO Sign-In Prompt feature enforces user consent for SSO in EU regions. The GSA client uses silent (non-interactive) auth which cannot display the DMA consent prompt.
solution: Update GSA Windows client to version 2.22.90.0 or later which adds a Sign in button for DMA consent. User must click Sign in, provide UPN, and consent to SSO. After consent, SSO works normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Internal Docs/Microsoft Entra Private Access: Known Issues

symptom: Application published via Microsoft Entra App Proxy works with a single connector but fails intermittently or hangs after adding multiple connectors. Requests randomly succeed or fail depending on which connector handles them.
rootCause: Backend application requires session persistence (session affinity). With multiple connectors, each connector uses a different IP address, so requests within the same session may route to different backend servers, breaking session stickiness.
solution: Confirm session persistence is the issue by testing with a single connector. Then configure load balancer for session persistence: (1) Use session cookie-based affinity via Azure Application Gateway or L7 LB (recommended), (2) Use X-Forwarded-For header affinity with L7 LB, or (3) Redesign backend app to not require session persistence. Ref: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/manage-apps/application-proxy-high-availability-load-balancing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra App Proxy -  Application does not work correctly after adding Connectors

symptom: External client accessing an Application Proxy published application receives GatewayTimeout error (The Request Timed Out). Backend server does not respond within the default 85-second timeout.
rootCause: Backend server does not respond in time to the request forwarded by the App Proxy connector. Default connector timeout is 85 seconds. Could be caused by network latency between connector and backend server, or backend application performance issues.
solution: In Entra Portal App Proxy configuration, change Backend Application Timeout from Default (85s) to Long (180s). If timeout persists after changing to Long, investigate network path (capture netsh traces on both connector and backend server simultaneously) and backend application performance. If backend app inherently needs >180s response time, App Proxy is not a viable publishing solution.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra App Proxy - GatewayTimeout-The Request Timed out

symptom: Application Proxy or Web Application Proxy Kerberos Constrained Delegation (KCD) SSO fails after installing November 9, 2021 Windows Update on Domain Controllers. Users receive Kerberos authentication errors when accessing KCD-configured published apps. System event log on DC shows Kdcsvc warnings Event ID 35/36/37/38.
rootCause: November 9, 2021 Windows Update (KB5007206/KB5007192/KB5007247/KB5007260/KB5007236/KB5007263) introduced a Kerberos S4U regression (ICM 271547888) that breaks S4u2self evidence ticket authentication used by App Proxy/WAP for KCD.
solution: Install Out-of-Band hotfix released Nov 15, 2021 (see https://docs.microsoft.com/windows/release-health/windows-message-center#2750). Target DCs in AD Sites where App Proxy Connector/WAP servers are located (use nltest /dsgetsite to identify). For Azure AD DS, PG deployed backend fix automatically. Alternative (not recommended): uninstall the problematic KB from affected DCs. Kdcsvc Event 35-38 warnings on DC are normal after hotfix and can be ignored. Case closure RC: CID App Int and Dev > Microsoft Entra Application Proxy > Kerberos Patch Issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application App Proxy AP Plan for cases

symptom: Customer wants to publish a file server (SMB file share) via Microsoft Entra Application Proxy but it does not work - SMB protocol is not supported
rootCause: Application Proxy is an HTTP reverse proxy that only supports HTTP/HTTPS protocols. SMB (Server Message Block) is not a web protocol, so file servers cannot be directly published through Application Proxy.
solution: Use one of these alternatives: (1) WorkFolders published via App Proxy, (2) Remote Desktop Services via App Proxy, (3) SharePoint on-premises via App Proxy, (4) SharePoint Online with file migration, (5) Azure Files. Customer must evaluate which solution fits their requirements and contact the appropriate support team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/How to publish a file server (share) over Azure AD Application Proxy?

symptom: Users receive HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests) error when accessing application published through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy
rootCause: The application exceeds the Application Proxy throttling limits: maximum 500 TPS per application, or 750 TPS per tenant. All requests reaching the App Proxy cloud service count toward the limit, including unauthenticated redirects.
solution: Verify throttling via Kusto TransactionSummaries with ResponseStatusCode==429; check ExtraResultData for throttling type; if 1-2 apps affected, change external URL to distribute load; fix software bugs causing excessive traffic; for DDoS, deploy WAF/AFD; for limit increase, raise AVA/ICM to Product Team with tenant info and Kusto evidence. Check settings.ini in AD-AppProxy repo for existing high-volume entries.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Increasing the maximum transactions per second for Azure AD Application Proxy (HTTP 429)

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector (ApplicationProxyConnectorService.exe) exhibits high CPU usage or high memory consumption on the connector server
rootCause: Connector is overloaded due to exceeding the default concurrent connection limit of 200, or experiencing memory/handle leak. WebSocket-based applications can significantly increase connection count.
solution: Ensure latest connector version; check CurrentConnections vs ConnectionLimit via Kusto BootstrapRootOperationEvent; add more connectors; increase DefaultConnectionLimit in MicrosoftEntraPrivateNetworkConnectorService.exe.config (add DefaultConnectionLimit=800 in appSettings); isolate WebSocket apps to separate connector groups; schedule periodic restarts as workaround; consider Global Secure Access Private Access; for persistent issues collect PerfView dumps and escalate via AVA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Investigating performance issues/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector High CPU Usage, High Memory Consumption

symptom: Backend web server returns HTTP 404 Not Found when accessed through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy, even though the application works correctly when accessed directly
rootCause: The Translate URLs In Headers setting controls whether the connector sends the internal or external hostname in the HTTP Host header and SNI. When enabled (default), Host header is rewritten to internal URL hostname. If backend expects the external hostname, it returns 404.
solution: Toggle Translate URLs In Headers in Enterprise Application > Application Proxy config. Enabled: Host header uses internal hostname; Disabled: preserves external hostname. Apps like SharePoint and RD Gateway typically need this disabled. Verify by inspecting Host header in Fiddler traces from connector server.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Link Translation in the Request or Response Header and Azure AD Application Proxy

symptom: Cookies are not shared between multiple applications published through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy, or cookies are lost after HTTP redirection (302/307)
rootCause: When Translate URLs In Headers is enabled, Set-Cookie Domain is rewritten to app-specific external hostname (e.g., app1-contoso.msappproxy.com). Each published app gets a unique external hostname, so cookies cannot be shared across apps.
solution: To share cookies across published apps: (1) ensure all apps use the same external hostname (e.g., https://common-contoso.msappproxy.com/app1 and /app2), or (2) configure wildcard or complex app patterns so apps share a common domain, or (3) disable Translate URLs In Headers if backend apps handle cookies correctly with internal domain names.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Link Translation in the Request or Response Header and Azure AD Application Proxy

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy returns BadGateway with SSL certificate error: 'One or more errors were found in the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) certificate sent by the server' or 'The SSL server certificate presented to Microsoft Entra private network connector by the backend server is not valid; the certificate is not trusted.' Kusto shows HRESULT 80072F8F (ERROR_WINHTTP_SECURE_FAILURE).
rootCause: The App Proxy connector cannot negotiate the SSL/TLS session with the backend server. Common causes: (1) TLS protocol/cipher suite mismatch, (2) expired or untrusted SSL certificate on backend, (3) certificate subject/SAN doesn't match internal URL hostname, (4) backend certificate validation enabled (isBackendCertificateValidationEnabled=true, default since June 2021 for new apps) and cert fails validation, (5) backend requires TLS 1.3 but connector runs on Windows Server older than 2022.
solution: 1) Temporarily disable backend cert validation via MS Graph to isolate the issue. 2) Ensure cert subject/SAN covers the internal URL hostname (note: *.domain.com does NOT cover app.sub.domain.com). 3) Verify cert is not expired. 4) Install root/intermediate CA certs in connector's certificate store (certlm.msc). 5) For TLS 1.3, upgrade connector to Windows Server 2022+. 6) If connector and app are on same server, verify IIS HTTPS binding listens on all unassigned IPs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - BadGateway with Secure Sockets Layer error

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy returns BadGateway 'An error occurred' with HRESULT 80004005 (E_FAIL). Connector log shows 'Processing OnBEHeadersReadCompleted event failed with hr=0x80004005'. Backend response returns HTTP 500 with X-Error-HRESULT: 80004005.
rootCause: The backend server sends malformed Set-Cookie response headers that the App Proxy connector cannot parse. Common patterns: missing cookie value (e.g., 'Set-Cookie: ASPSESSIONID; path=/' instead of 'Set-Cookie: ASPSESSIONID=value; path=/'), or Apache-style malformed cookies ('Set-Cookie: HttpOnly;Secure;SameSite=Strict' without name=value pair).
solution: Fix the Set-Cookie header syntax on the backend web application to follow RFC 6265 format: Set-Cookie: <name>=<value>; <attributes>. Collect Fiddler on connector server comparing working (direct access) vs non-working (through proxy) scenarios to identify the specific malformed header. If error code is 0x80072F00, follow the HTTP/2.0 known issue article instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - BadGateway: An error occurred

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy returns BadGateway 'Failed to get the backend application's response' with HRESULT 80072F78 (ERROR_HTTP_INVALID_SERVER_RESPONSE). Backend response shows HTTP 500 with X-Error-HRESULT: 80072F78. Issue cannot be reproduced when bypassing App Proxy.
rootCause: The WinHTTP API used by the App Proxy connector rejects the backend server's HTTP response as invalid. Common causes: (1) response body length is 0, (2) missing response headers, (3) duplicate header field names, (4) invalid characters in header field names (spaces or underscores, e.g., 'content type' or 'content_type'), (5) Content-Length value less than 0. Browsers use more lenient APIs so the issue doesn't reproduce with direct access.
solution: Fix the backend web application's HTTP response headers. Collect Fiddler on connector server comparing working (direct) vs non-working (through proxy) scenarios. Check for: duplicate headers, spaces/underscores in header names, proper Content-Length values. For deeper investigation, analyze the AppProxyLog.bin with Network Monitor 3.4 using filter 'WEBIO_MicrosoftWindowsWebIO.HttpInvokeParserComplete.Error.WinErrorValue == 0x3a'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - BadGateway: Failed to get the backend application's response

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy returns BadGateway error 'The service detected a possible loop. Make sure that the internal URL does not point to the external URL of any application.' Kusto ExtraResultData shows 'GotIntoCircularRequest'. Issue may be constant or intermittent.
rootCause: DNS on the connector server resolves the internal hostname (from the App Proxy internal URL) to the external/public IP address, causing the HTTP request to loop back to the App Proxy cloud service instead of reaching the backend server. The cloud service detects the loop via the X-MS-Proxy header it adds to forwarded requests. Causes: (1) DNS server returns public IP for internal hostname, (2) Azure DNS 168.63.129.16 resolving incorrectly, (3) wrong entry in hosts file, (4) multiple DNS servers with inconsistent resolution, (5) web proxy between connector and backend not configured correctly.
solution: 1) Add correct internal IP + hostname to hosts file on all connector servers, flush DNS (ipconfig /flushdns), verify with ping. 2) Check all DNS servers (ipconfig /all) with nslookup (append dot to hostname). 3) If Azure DNS 168.63.129.16 is the culprit and not needed, block its ports 53/udp and 53/tcp in Windows Firewall. 4) If using web proxy, verify connector's proxy configuration per MS docs. 5) For intermittent issues: check all connectors in the connector group and collect network traces while issue reproduces.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - BadGateway: The service detected a possible loop

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy published app freezes or shows errors approximately 50 minutes after sign-in. Browser developer console shows CORS violation. The AzureAppProxyAccessCookie has expired, causing requests to be redirected to login.microsoftonline.com. Common with apps that use background AJAX scripts (e.g., Exchange OWA).
rootCause: The default access token lifetime (~1 hour) expires during the user's session. Once the AzureAppProxyAccessCookie expires, the App Proxy cloud service redirects unauthenticated requests to login.microsoftonline.com. For background/AJAX requests (scripts), this redirect triggers a CORS violation because the script cannot follow the cross-origin redirect to the IdP.
solution: Create and assign a Token Lifetime Policy to extend the access token lifetime. Use MS Graph PowerShell: 1) Connect-MgGraph with Policy.ReadWrite.ApplicationConfiguration scope. 2) New-MgPolicyTokenLifetimePolicy with longer AccessTokenLifetime (e.g., '2:00:00' for 2 hours, max 1 day). 3) Assign policy to the MEAP app registration via New-MgApplicationTokenLifetimePolicyByRef using the app's ObjectId. Verify in ASC under Application > Linked Policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS exception after the authentication cookie expires

symptom: User authentication fails with You cant get there from here error and AADSTS500021 when Tenant Restrictions V1 is enabled on corporate proxy
rootCause: The Restrict-Access-To-Tenants HTTP header injected by the proxy does not include the users home tenant ID. Entra ID denies token issuance for tenants not listed in the permitted tenant list.
solution: Verify the proxy Restrict-Access-To-Tenants header includes the home tenant of the admin configuring it AND all tenants users need to access. Use Fiddler to inspect the header on login.microsoftonline.com requests. Also ensure Restrict-Access-Context is set to the policy-setting tenant for reporting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Azure AD Tenant Restrictions V1

symptom: Opening OME (Office Message Encryption) protected email from another tenant fails in Outlook desktop client but succeeds in OWA when Tenant Restrictions V1 is enabled
rootCause: Outlook desktop client attempts to directly contact the senders tenant to verify OME key/policy, which is blocked by Tenant Restrictions if the senders tenant is not in the permitted list. OWA uses the Exchange service to verify, bypassing the proxy restriction.
solution: Add the senders tenant to the Restrict-Access-To-Tenants permitted list, or instruct users to use OWA for opening OME-protected emails from restricted tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Azure AD Tenant Restrictions V1

symptom: MSA consumer sign-in blocked with MSPPError=-2147197875 sad face error when trying to access consumer apps like Outlook.com or Xbox.com behind corporate proxy
rootCause: Proxy injects sec-restrict-tenant-access-policy: restrict-msa header on traffic to login.live.com, blocking all consumer MSA authentication. This is the TRv1 MSA extension feature working as designed.
solution: Verify the sec-restrict-tenant-access-policy header is present in login.live.com requests using Fiddler. If customer wants to allow MSA, remove the restrict-msa header from proxy config. No granularity control available - it blocks all consumer apps. If migrating to TRv2 MSA policy, remove the TRv1 MSA header first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Azure AD Tenant Restrictions V1

symptom: Token renewal fails with AADSTS5000211 error: A tenant restrictions policy added to this request by a device or network administrator does not allow access to the resource tenant when TRv2 is enabled via GPO/Intune
rootCause: TRv2 policy on the device injects sec-Restrict-Tenant-Access-Policy header with tenantID:policyID. ESTS enforces the cross-tenant access policy (XTAP) and blocks access if the resource tenant is not allowed.
solution: Check if the tenant admin needs to allow the resource tenant in the XTAP policy. If the blocked user belongs to the home tenant itself, verify the policy configuration. Use Kusto query against scenario table filtering by aadStsErr containing AADSTS5000211 to identify impacted resources and tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Azure AD Tenant Restrictions V2

symptom: cloudidsvc (Microsoft Cloud Identity Service) fails to sync O365 endpoint list with Event 1013 error when TRv2 policy is enabled on Windows device
rootCause: cloudidsvc is hardened by Windows Service Hardening (WSH) firewall rules to only allow outbound traffic on TCP 443. If the outbound proxy listens on a different port, cloudidsvc cannot reach endpoints.office.com to sync the O365 endpoint list.
solution: Add a firewall rule: New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:SYSTEM/CurrentControlSet/Services/SharedAccess/Parameters/FirewallPolicy/RestrictedServices/Configurable/System/ -Name $(New-Guid) -PropertyType String -Value V2.31|Action=Allow|Dir=Out|Protocol=6|Rport=XXXX|... Replace XXXX with proxy port. Restart cloudidsvc after.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Windows TRV2 (Tenant Restrictions V2) Overview and Support Guidance

symptom: User stuck at access blocked prompt on random apps (OneNote, Outlook) after TRv2 policy change, unable to access any resources even after policy is disabled
rootCause: Stale TRv2 policy data (tenantID and policyID) remains cached in Windows registry keys under Group Policy Objects or TenantRestrictions Payload, causing apps to continue injecting outdated TRv2 headers.
solution: Open Registry Editor (admin required). Remove tenantID/policyID from: 1) HKCU/Software/Microsoft/Windows/CurrentVersion/Group Policy Objects, 2) HKLM/SOFTWARE/Policies/Microsoft/Windows/TenantRestrictions/Payload. For random app errors, search registry for the policy ID in HKEY_USERS/.../Office/16.0/Common/Identity/ServiceAuthInfoCache.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Azure AD Tenant Restrictions V2

symptom: Sign-in logs show PII removed - Tenant Restrictions as userDisplayName and userId 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 from unknown external tenants in the organization Entra ID tenant
rootCause: Expected behavior when Tenant Restrictions is configured on corporate network. Restrict-Access-Context header signals Entra ID to forward masked copies of external tenant sign-in logs. PII is removed because user belongs to an external tenant.
solution: Verify TR via ASC > Sign-ins > Troubleshoot > Expert View > PerRequestLogs. Check SpecialFlow=TenantRestrictions and RestrictedTenantProxySetter. Review TRv1 policy in Diagnostics Logs to see permitted tenant list. Explain to customer this is by-design TR logging behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/TSG - Troubleshoot Tenant Restrictions Sign-ins

symptom: HTTP 500 or too many redirects when accessing Outlook with Universal Tenant Restrictions (NaaS/ZTNA) enabled - Outlook traffic goes through NaaS but Entra auth traffic does not
rootCause: Entra authentication traffic (login.microsoftonline.com) is not routed through NaaS, so the access token lacks xms_trpid TRv2 claim. When workload traffic (with TRv2 header) hits EXO/SPO, resource provider sees header but no claim, triggering claims challenge redirect loop.
solution: Enable TRv2 signaling on the Entra auth path (login.microsoftonline.com) in NaaS/ZTNA traffic profile. Verify token contains xms_trpid claim by decoding at jwt.ms. Both auth and workload traffic must have consistent TRv2 signaling.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Universal Tenant Restrictions

symptom: TRv2 enforcement via Universal Tenant Restrictions (NaaS) is not blocking access to foreign tenants as expected - users can still sign in to restricted tenant resources
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: 1) TRv2 flag propagation delay (~30 min) after enabling, 2) TRv2 flag not enabled in NaaS configuration, 3) Traffic not acquired by NaaS client, 4) TRv2 header not stamped in datapath.
solution: 1) Wait up to 30 minutes for propagation. 2) Verify TRv2 flag is enabled in NaaS UI. 3) Check client acquisition profile includes target FQDNs. 4) Use Kusto RoxyHttpOperationEvent to verify TRv2Enabled=true and Result=success. 5) Perform local TRv2 validation with Fiddler.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Universal Tenant Restrictions

symptom: User cannot access allowed partner tenant SPO/resources with Universal TR (NaaS) even though the partner is allow-listed in XTAP policy - using external identity
rootCause: Confusion between outbound access policy and TRv2: if user uses home tenant identity, this is outbound access policy not TRv2. TRv2 only applies when using external identity. Token infiltration may also occur if PID in token does not match PID in header.
solution: 1) Clarify identity type: home identity = outbound access policy, external identity = TRv2. 2) Check access token xms_trpid claim. 3) Use Kusto RoxyHttpOperationEvent to verify TRv2Enabled and Result. 4) Check app_server_status (401 = workload block). 5) If Result not success, escalate to NaaS Datapath or eSTS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Universal Tenant Restrictions

symptom: End-user blocked with missing TRv2 claim error - token has no xms_trpid claim but network/device is injecting TRv2 header on requests
rootCause: login.microsoftonline.com traffic is not being stamped with TRv2 header (not acquired by NaaS or traffic profile incomplete). Without header on auth requests, ESTS does not add xms_trpid claim. Resource provider sees TRv2 header but no claim in token, rejects with 401.
solution: 1) Confirm Traffic Profile includes login.microsoftonline.com. 2) Check Kusto for login.microsoftonline.com TRv2 data. 3) Test locally with Fiddler TRv2 header. 4) If claim present locally but not via NaaS: escalate to NaaS Datapath. 5) If claim missing even with stamped auth traffic: escalate to eSTS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Universal Tenant Restrictions

symptom: TRv2 enforcement not blocking access to Exchange Online (EXO) - user can still access restricted tenant EXO resources with Universal Tenant Restrictions enabled
rootCause: Known limitation: QUIC protocol and/or IPv6 traffic bypasses TRv2 enforcement. NaaS may not acquire traffic on these protocols. Also some EU client FQDNs may not be onboarded yet.
solution: 1) Block QUIC protocol on network/device. 2) Block IPv6 protocol. 3) Verify target FQDNs are in NaaS acquisition profile. 4) Check if EU-specific FQDNs are onboarded.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Tenant Restrictions/Universal Tenant Restrictions

symptom: User accessing App Proxy application is blocked by CAE with cae_checks_reject_ip error - IP enforcement blocking access after CAE rollout to App Proxy
rootCause: CAE extended to App Proxy performs real-time IP checks. When location-based CA policy with Block access grant is configured, CAE rejects token immediately if IP changes or is outside allowed range.
solution: 1) Check AadTokenValidationOperationEvent in idsharedweu/AADAP: verify TokenType=CaeToken and CaeValidationEnabled=true. 2) Check CaeEvents for cae_checks_reject_ip. 3) Non-strict: check downgraded token (CapolidsLatebind). 4) Auth loop: escalate to eSTS. 5) Disable per-app: PATCH Graph /applications/{id}/onPremisesPublishing with isContinuousAccessEvaluationEnabled=false.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/CaE Support for App Proxy

symptom: Authentication loop (unlimited re-authentication prompts) when accessing App Proxy application with CAE strict enforcement and location-based Conditional Access policy
rootCause: With strict enforcement, when CAE check fails the user does not receive a downgraded token. Transaction keeps failing with same CapolidsLatebind policy IDs, causing endless re-auth loop between App Proxy and ESTS.
solution: 1) Identify loop: check sign-in logs for unlimited re-auths and TransactionSummaries for CaeCookieUnauthorized. 2) Verify strict enforcement is intentional in CA policy > Session > Customize CAE. 3) Ensure user IP is in excluded locations list. 4) To disable CAE: CA policy targeting All resources, Session > Customize CAE > Disable. 5) If loop persists: escalate to eSTS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/CaE Support for App Proxy

symptom: CORS error in browser after splitting single-server application into two separate Entra Application Proxy apps, resources now on different domains
rootCause: Previously resources were on the same origin (single server, single App Proxy app). After publishing as two separate apps, cross-origin requests trigger CORS violations since browser enforces same-origin policy across different App Proxy external URLs
solution: Consolidate back to a single App Proxy app publishing (one domain = no CORS). Alternatively, use wildcard publishing to keep resources under same domain. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/manage-apps/application-proxy-understand-cors-issues
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS issues

symptom: CORS error when App Proxy app calls another App Proxy app with Entra ID pre-authentication - OPTIONS preflight request gets HTTP 302 redirect or HTTP 403 because it has no credentials/cookies
rootCause: Browser OPTIONS preflight requests do not include credentials (cookies). App Proxy with Entra ID pre-authentication redirects unauthenticated requests to login.microsoftonline.com. The preflight fails because ESTS does not support CORS OPTIONS requests
solution: Set the API app to passthrough pre-authentication to avoid 302 redirect on OPTIONS calls. If pre-auth is required, use the Complex App Support feature (private preview CORS feature is deprecated). Ensure CORS headers reference external URL, not internal URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS issues

symptom: JavaScript/HTML requests hang or fail to load when accessing App Proxy app with pre-authentication - client code cannot handle redirects to login.microsoftonline.com, no CORS error displayed
rootCause: JS/HTML code making requests to App Proxy app does not have AzureAppProxyAccessCookie for the target app. App Proxy issues 302 redirect to login.microsoftonline.com but JS code cannot follow authentication redirects
solution: Pre-authenticate to the target app before the JS call by embedding a fake image/link or using wildcard publishing to set the authentication cookie. Note: this workaround does NOT work if user authenticates via Primary Refresh Token (Entra Joined/Hybrid Joined/Registered devices)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS issues

symptom: CORS error occurs after approximately 50 minutes of using App Proxy application - browser shows CORS errors on periodic refresh/AJAX calls, Fiddler shows OPTIONS request to login.microsoftonline.com
rootCause: AzureAppProxyAccessCookie expires after ~50 minutes. On next request, App Proxy issues 302 redirect to login.microsoftonline.com. If the request is a complex JS call with custom headers, browser sends OPTIONS preflight to login.microsoftonline.com which is not allowed (ESTS does not support CORS)
solution: Follow the article on Entra Application Proxy CORS exception after the authentication cookie expires. Consider implementing silent token refresh or extending session lifetime. Common with long-running apps like Outlook Web Access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS issues

symptom: CORS error when a non-App-Proxy web app (caller) makes requests to an App Proxy published app (callee) with Entra ID pre-authentication enabled
rootCause: The callee app is published with Entra ID pre-authentication. When the non-App-Proxy caller invokes the callee, App Proxy redirects to login.microsoftonline.com, triggering CORS exception because the caller origin is not allowed by ESTS
solution: Change the callee App Proxy app to use passthrough pre-authentication type instead of Entra ID pre-authentication. This eliminates the redirect to login.microsoftonline.com that triggers CORS
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CORS issues

symptom: Forbidden - Authorization failed error accessing App Proxy app with Entra pre-authentication (IsStateSessionEnabled=true), browser shows 'Make sure to assign the user with access to this application'
rootCause: CSRF protection (IsStateSessionEnabled) validates PreauthSessionCookie against State parameter in the authorization code callback. Fails when browser cannot present PreauthSessionCookie (cookies disabled, app does not handle cookies properly, or cookie lost during redirects)
solution: Check if PreauthSessionCookie is present in the request with authorization code. If cookies are being blocked, fix browser cookie settings. If app cannot handle cookies, disable the feature by setting IsStateSessionEnabled=false via MS Graph OnpremisesPublishing API. Note: this reduces CSRF protection
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CSRF protection for State Parameter during pre-authentication

symptom: Forbidden - Authorization failed error accessing App Proxy app, SessionId in PreauthSessionCookie does not match SessionId in State parameter of the authorization code URL
rootCause: User or application constructs a custom URL with a State parameter containing a SessionId different from the one in the PreauthSessionCookie. This triggers CSRF validation failure. May indicate real CSRF attack or misconfigured app composing auth URLs manually
solution: Investigate if this is a legitimate CSRF attack. If the application manually constructs authentication URLs with custom state parameters, fix it to use the state issued by App Proxy. If caused by browser extension or network device modifying URLs, address those. Feature enabled by default for apps created after 6/16/2022
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - CSRF protection for State Parameter during pre-authentication

symptom: Creating or updating Microsoft Entra Application Proxy app fails with error 'This External Url is already in use by another application' (appProxyErrorExternalUrlMSODSDuplicate)
rootCause: The exact same external URL is already configured in another Application Proxy app, Enterprise App Reply URL, Identifier URI (SAML SSO), or ServicePrincipalNames
solution: Use ASC Tenant Explorer > Application Proxy to locate the app with the duplicate external URL. Also query MS Graph: GET /applications?$filter=identifierUris/any(uri:uri eq 'URL') and GET /servicePrincipals?$filter=servicePrincipalNames/any(x: startsWith(x, 'URL')). Check with and without trailing slash. If updating an existing app, check if the SAML Identifier or App Registration Reply URL matches the new external URL and remove the conflict.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Issues with URLs while configuring an app

symptom: Creating Microsoft Entra Application Proxy app with custom domain fails with error 'External Url entered is already in use for an application on another tenant' (appProxyErrorExternalUrlAnotherTenantDuplicate)
rootCause: The custom domain URL was previously used in another tenant and the Application Proxy app was not deleted correctly, leaving an orphaned configuration in the App Proxy database
solution: Customer must delete the orphaned app following the documented steps for incomplete App Proxy app deletion. If customer cannot self-remediate, engage the product group via Teams channel with the old tenant ID and external URL to remove the orphaned configuration from the App Proxy database.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Issues with URLs while configuring an app

symptom: Creating Microsoft Entra Application Proxy app fails with error 'Internal url entered is already being used by another application' (appProxyErrorInternalUrlDuplicate / InternalUrl_Duplicate)
rootCause: The exact same internal URL is already configured in another Application Proxy app or a Hybrid Agent (Exchange) application
solution: Use ASC Tenant Explorer > Application Proxy to locate the app with the duplicate internal URL. Customer can run the PowerShell script 'Get all Application Proxy apps and list extended information' to find it. If the Hybrid Agent configuration causes the issue, involve Exchange support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Issues with URLs while configuring an app

symptom: Unable to update Microsoft Entra Application Proxy settings - 'Application operation failed' when changing external URL, caused by Reply URL conflict, HMA webmail URL in Exchange Online SPN, or multitenant app registration
rootCause: Multiple possible causes: (1) Reply URLs in Service Principal already contain the new external URL; (2) Webmail URL configured for Hybrid Modern Authentication is added to Office 365 Exchange Online SPN; (3) App Registration is set to Multitenant instead of Single tenant
solution: For Reply URL conflict: check Service Principal replyUrls via ASC or App Registrations > Authentication > Redirect URIs, remove the conflicting URL. For HMA: remove the URL from HMA config or use https://XXXXX/owa/ as internal URL workaround. For multitenant: change app to Single tenant. Collect Fiddler trace if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Issues with URLs while configuring an app

symptom: Configuring Microsoft Entra Application Proxy fails with 'Alternate URL entered is already used by another application' when updating alternate URL or external URL with SAML SSO configured
rootCause: Multiple causes: (1) Another app uses the same alternate URL; (2) Invalid characters in app name or URL; (3) SAML SSO default Reply URL matches current external URL preventing URL change (bug: Unable to remove redirectUri used as defaultRedirectUri); (4) HMA configuration conflict; (5) New fallback domain was configured
solution: For SAML SSO conflict: in SAML configuration, modify the default Reply URL (append 'temp/' path), save, update App Proxy external URL, then restore correct default Reply URL. For other causes: locate and remove conflicting URL from the other app or fix invalid characters.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Issues with URLs while configuring an app

symptom: Kerberos Constrained Delegation (KCD) fails for Microsoft Entra Application Proxy published application with KDC_ERR_C_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN error visible in network trace (Netmon KerberosV5 filter)
rootCause: The S4U2Self request requires access to the TokenGroupsGlobalAndUniversal user-constructed attribute, which requires specific AD permissions. The Proxy connector computer account lacks these permissions.
solution: Add the Application Proxy connector computer account as a member of the Active Directory Built-in Group: Windows Authorization Access Group. This grants the necessary permissions for S4U2Self requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Kerberos Constrained Delegation failure_KDC ERR C PRINCIPAL UNKNOWN

symptom: Application Proxy configuration panel in enterprise app shows 'Unable to complete due to service connection error' or panel appears empty with no error message
rootCause: Application Service Principal contains 'WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp' tag but was never properly configured for Application Proxy, causing portal to fail locating non-existent proxy configuration
solution: Remove the tag via PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph, Get-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId $objectId, filter out 'WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp' from Tags, Update-MgServicePrincipal with new tags. Alternatively delete and re-create the application correctly. Verify in ASC that no App Proxy config exists for the AppId before removing the tag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Unable to complete due to service connection error

symptom: Published application via Entra Application Proxy loads but page is missing elements, images, styles or has rendering errors when accessed through external URL
rootCause: Page contains embedded links to internal URLs (e.g. images, scripts at paths like https://WebApp/images) that are outside the published application path, making them inaccessible externally through the proxy
solution: Three options: 1) Use Custom Domains so internal/external URLs match (https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/app-proxy/how-to-configure-custom-domain), 2) Publish the root URL of the application so all sub-paths are accessible, 3) Publish each embedded internal URL as separate App Proxy app and enable URL Translation in body. Note: URLs inside JavaScript won't be translated by option 3 - use custom domains or manually update those URLs. Collect Fiddler trace on external client to identify which internal URLs are being requested.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Web Page is not rendered Correctly

symptom: Customer needs to restrict access to Entra Application Proxy published application based on client IP address, but backend server IP filtering blocks connector IP instead of original client IP
rootCause: Application Proxy connector forwards requests using its own IP address, not the original client IP. The client's public IP is only available in the X-Forwarded-For HTTP header added by the App Proxy Cloud service.
solution: Implement IP-based filtering using X-Forwarded-For header: (1) Place a firewall/WAF in front of App Proxy endpoint (e.g. Azure Front Door), (2) Place a firewall/proxy between connector and backend to filter on X-Forwarded-For, or (3) Configure backend server (e.g. IIS Dynamic IP Restrictions) to act on X-Forwarded-For header. Note: App Proxy support is not responsible for implementing these solutions; engage appropriate support team if first-party products are involved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Restricting access based on IP address

symptom: Conditional Access location-based policy does not effectively restrict IP-based access to Application Proxy published application - users can still access the app from blocked locations after initial authentication
rootCause: Conditional Access Policy only blocks authentication, not application access. Once the client obtains the Application Proxy access cookie (which is not bound to IP address), it can continue accessing the app from any IP address regardless of CA location-based policy.
solution: Do not rely solely on CA location-based policy for IP restriction with App Proxy. Instead implement network-level IP filtering using X-Forwarded-For header: configure IIS Dynamic IP Restrictions, place firewall between connector and backend, or put WAF in front of App Proxy endpoint. CA location policy can only be used with Entra ID pre-authentication and only controls the authentication step.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - Restricting access based on IP address

symptom: AADSTS50011 error 'The reply URL specified in the request does not match the reply URLs configured for the application' when accessing SAML-based application published via Entra Application Proxy
rootCause: The External URL of the Application Proxy app is not configured as a Reply URL in the SAML SSO settings. When the published app's internal Assertion Consumer Service URL differs from the App Proxy External URL, both must be registered as Reply URLs in Entra SAML configuration.
solution: Add the App Proxy External URL as a Reply URL: Entra Portal > Enterprise Applications > app > Single sign-on > Basic SAML Configuration > Edit. Ensure both the app's ACS URL and App Proxy External URL are listed as Reply URLs. For SP-initiated sign-on, verify the SAML AuthnRequest AssertionConsumerServiceURL matches a configured Reply URL. Use ASC Sign-ins Diagnostics (Expert View > Diagnostic Logs, search 'Reply url specified in the request') to identify the mismatched URL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - SAML AADSTS50011: The reply URL specified in the request

symptom: User accessing application published via Microsoft Entra Application Proxy with # (hash/fragment identifier) in the URL gets redirected to a truncated URL after successful authentication, losing the fragment part of the URL
rootCause: Browsers truncate the URL at the # character (fragment identifier) per RFC 1738 and do not preserve the URL fragment across HTTP redirects. When the first unauthenticated request contains #, the Entra ID login endpoint redirects back to the truncated URL without the fragment
solution: Ensure the first access URL does not include the # character. Workarounds: (1) Set up a server-side redirect from a clean URL to the hash-containing URL so the first request has no #, (2) Remove # from URL if not needed, (3) Configure passthrough pre-authentication (not recommended). For SharePoint with Minimal Download Strategy (which uses #), disable that feature when published via App Proxy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy and the Hash Sign in the Request URL

symptom: Starting February 8 2020, empty fragments (#) are appended to every HTTP redirect from login.microsoftonline.com login endpoint, potentially breaking applications that depend on absence of fragments in OAuth/OIDC authorization code flow or implicit flow responses
rootCause: Microsoft intentionally appends an empty fragment to the reply URL in HTTP redirects from the login endpoint to prevent a class of redirect attacks by ensuring the browser wipes out any existing fragment in the authentication request. Affects both v1.0 and v2.0 endpoints using response_type=query
solution: This is by-design behavior (breaking change). Applications must not depend on the absence of empty fragments in redirect responses. If an application breaks, update the application code to handle empty fragments in the redirect URL
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy and the Hash Sign in the Request URL

symptom: User accessing app published via Microsoft Entra Application Proxy with Integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) SSO gets Forbidden error: This corporate app cannot be accessed, Authorization failed. Connector Admin Event logs show EventId 13022 with HTTP 401 from backend server
rootCause: Backend web server responds with HTTP 401 because it does not accept Kerberos authentication via KCD. The www-Authenticate headers show NTLM only (no Negotiate/Kerberos). The App Proxy connector obtained a Kerberos ticket but backend rejected it. HTTP 401 is converted to HTTP 403 by App Proxy to prevent browser auth popup since the connector (not the browser) authenticates to the backend
solution: Verify backend Kerberos config via Kusto - check www-Auth headers in CopyBackendResponseHeaders trace. If www-Auth lacks Negotiate/Kerberos: (1) Configure backend app for Kerberos auth, (2) For IIS: enable Windows Auth with Negotiate, disable Extended Protection, set useAppPoolCredentials=True if service account, (3) For SPNEGO/Linux: enable UseSpnegoAuthentication on connector, (4) Try On-premises SAM account name as logon identity, (5) Ensure single service account per published app. After changes: IISRESET + flush Kerberos cache on connectors
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy- Backend server responds with 401 Unauthorized_Status Code - Forbidden

symptom: Users accessing application published via Microsoft Entra App Proxy receive Bad Request Invalid URL (HTTP Error 400. The request URL is invalid), commonly seen with Jira and SAP published applications
rootCause: The request URL exceeds the default 260-character path length limit in HTTP.sys on the App Proxy Service Instance
solution: Collect Fiddler trace to confirm the error is related to URL path length exceeding the limit. Submit an ICM to the Microsoft Entra App Proxy team to move the tenant to a Service Instance that supports paths over 260 characters. Note: DNS change takes 10-15 minutes to replicate, during which some users may fail to connect
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy-Bad Request. Invalid URL

symptom: Need to identify if Enterprise Application uses Application Proxy or troubleshoot App Proxy tag association; verify or modify the WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp tag on a Service Principal
rootCause: App Proxy association is controlled by the WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp tag in the Service Principal tags property stored in MSODS. App Proxy configuration itself is stored in a separate SQL backend.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph PowerShell to check/modify the tag: (1) Connect-MgGraph -Scopes Application.ReadWrite.All, (2) Get-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId {objectId} to read tags, (3) Add WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp to tags array and Update-MgServicePrincipal to save. Verify in ASC under Applications (Service Principal) section.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy - WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryOnPremApp tag

symptom: Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector installation fails with Time out has expired and the operation has not been completed error message
rootCause: .NET Framework version older than 4.7.1 installed on the connector server. Starting from connector version 1.5.3437.0, .NET 4.7.1 or greater (4.x line) is required for successful installation.
solution: Install .NET Framework 4.7.1 or later on the connector server. This is commonly needed on older OS like Windows Server 2012 R2 and 2016 where .NET 4.7.1 may not be installed by default.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector cannot be installed

symptom: App Proxy Connector registration fails - no registration record found in Kusto RegistrationRegisterOperationEvent, or registration returns non-Success resultType
rootCause: Network issue preventing connector from reaching registration endpoint (https://{tenantId}.registration.msappproxy.net), authentication failure, or account used is a guest user / lacks Global Admin or Application Admin role.
solution: Verify: (1) Account is not guest and has Global/App Admin role, (2) Check sign-in logs for app ID 55747057-9b5d-4bd4-b387-abf52a8bd489, (3) Network: TCP 80/443 open, no SSL inspection, proxy configured, (4) Query Kusto: RegistrationRegisterOperationEvent where subscriptionId == tenantId and feature == ApplicationProxy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector cannot be installed

symptom: App Proxy Connector installation or registration fails with SSL/TLS certificate validation errors or cannot establish secure connection to registration endpoint
rootCause: Missing DigiCert root CA certificates in the local computer Root Certification Authorities store, or incorrect TLS settings on the connector server.
solution: Install required root CAs in certlm.msc: (1) DigiCert Global Root G2 (thumbprint: df3c24f9bfd666761b268073fe06d1cc8d4f82a4), (2) DigiCert Global Root CA (thumbprint: a8985d3a65e5e5c4b2d7d66d40c6dd2fb19c5436). Also verify TLS settings per Microsoft TLS enforcement reference.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy Connector cannot be installed

symptom: SAML SSO through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy fails with multiple login prompts or authentication loop; user sees repeated re-authentication requests
rootCause: Reply URL in SAML configuration does not match or is not a sub-path under the Application Proxy External URL. The handoff between pre-authentication flow and SAML SSO flow breaks when URLs are misaligned.
solution: Set SAML Reply URL root to match or be a path under the Application Proxy External URL. If the backend app expects the Reply URL to be the internal URL, install the My Apps Secure Sign-In Extension which performs automatic client-side redirect to the App Proxy Service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy SAML Single-sign On

symptom: Office client (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) fails to load or access document published via Microsoft Entra Application Proxy; certain Office features like Excel data source connections do not work through App Proxy
rootCause: MS-OFBA (Office Forms Based Authentication) protocol is used for pre-authentication through App Proxy but not all Office client components support MS-OFBA. For instance, getting data from different data sources in Excel does not support it.
solution: Verify the specific Office client feature supports MS-OFBA protocol. For unsupported components (e.g., Excel data source connections), clarify with Office Support Team whether the feature supports MS-OFBA. Consider alternative access methods or direct backend access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra Application Proxy and MS-OFBA - Microsoft Office

symptom: Application Proxy KCD (Kerberos Constrained Delegation) single sign-on fails. Data collector script shows connector server computer object has incorrect useraccountcontrol attribute value (not 16781312) or SPN mismatch in msds-allowedtodelegateto.
rootCause: Connector AD computer account delegation is not configured correctly - 'Trust this computer for delegation to specified services only' with 'Use any authentication protocol' is not selected, or the SPN for delegation does not match the SPN configured in the Application Proxy app IWA SSO settings.
solution: Open AD Users and Computers (dsa.msc) > locate connector computer object > Properties > Delegation tab > select 'Trust this computer for delegation to specified services only' and 'Use any authentication protocol' > add the SPN. Verify msds-allowedtodelegateto contains the SPN matching the App Proxy IWA SSO configuration. useraccountcontrol must be 16781312.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Data Collector Script ComputerName-AD-Attributes.txt

symptom: After moving an Application Proxy connector to a different connector group via portal/PowerShell/Graph API, the connector continues handling requests for applications assigned to the old connector group for up to 10 minutes.
rootCause: Connector retrieves its group configuration from the cloud bootstrap endpoint. After moving, the configuration is saved in cloud immediately, but the connector only refreshes its local configuration on service restart or every 10 minutes.
solution: Restart the Microsoft Entra private network connector service on the server to immediately apply the new connector group assignment, or wait at least 10 minutes before expecting the connector to handle traffic for the new group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Demystifying Moving a Connector to Another Connector Group

symptom: Application Proxy pre-authentication fails with AADSTS700020 when FIC (Federated Identity Credential) auth is enabled. The application uses a reserved URI as the application identifier URI. Client secret-based auth works for the same app.
rootCause: When a reserved URI is used as the app identifier URI, FIC token acquisition fails because the reserved URI conflicts with the FIC credential validation. FIC requires a non-reserved identifier URI.
solution: Change the application identifier URI to a non-reserved URI, or fall back to client secret-based authentication until the app identifier URI is updated. Verify the fix by checking TransactionSummaries for GrantFlow-FIC in ExtraResultData.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Federated Identity Credential (FIC) Auth

symptom: Application Proxy pre-authentication fails with AADSTS70052 when FIC (Federated Identity Credential) auth is enabled and the application is configured as multi-tenant.
rootCause: FIC-based authentication in Application Proxy does not support multi-tenant applications. The FIC token acquisition step fails for multi-tenant app configurations.
solution: Ensure the application is configured as single-tenant, or use client secret-based authentication for multi-tenant Application Proxy applications.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Federated Identity Credential (FIC) Auth

symptom: Application Proxy pre-authentication suddenly fails after the Appproxy-FIC Federated Identity Credential was manually edited or deleted from the application Certificates and Secrets in Entra portal.
rootCause: The Appproxy-FIC credential is automatically added by the Application Proxy control plane and used in the OAuth grant flow to redeem auth codes. Editing or deleting it breaks the data path authentication. FIC does not expire and is valid for the app lifetime.
solution: Delete the corrupted FIC credential if present, then disable and re-enable the Application Proxy application to trigger an app update. The control plane automatically recreates the Appproxy-FIC credential during update of the OnPremisesPublishing property.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Federated Identity Credential (FIC) Auth

symptom: KDC_ERR_S_PRINCIPAL_UNKNOWN error in Kerberos network trace when using Integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) / Kerberos Constrained Delegation (KCD) SSO through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy. The Domain Controller cannot find a Service Account with a matching SPN.
rootCause: The Service Account for the published web application does not have the correct HTTP Service Principal Name (SPN) registered in Active Directory. When the App Proxy connector requests a Kerberos service ticket via KCD, the KDC cannot locate the target SPN.
solution: Register the correct HTTP SPN on the Service Account using the setspn command: setspn -s http/webapp.domain.com serviceAccountName. Verify registration with setspn -l serviceAccountName. The SPN must match the Internal Application SPN configured in the App Proxy enterprise application SSO settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Network trace analysis for Integrated Windows Auth_KCD

symptom: Browser shows ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED error when accessing an application published through Microsoft Entra Application Proxy with the Translate URLs in Application Body option enabled.
rootCause: The backend web application uses Brotli (br) compression. The Application Proxy body link translation feature only supports deflate and gzip compression types. When Brotli-compressed content is processed, the translation service cannot properly decode/re-encode the response body.
solution: Change the backend web server compression to use deflate or gzip instead of Brotli. Alternatively, disable Translate URLs in Application Body and use an alternative link translation method: (1) custom domain matching internal URL, (2) MyApps browser extension, (3) Intune managed browser configuration, or (4) modify the web application to use relative URLs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Link Translation in the Response Body

symptom: Kerberos SSO via Microsoft Entra Application Proxy fails after applying July 2019 Windows security updates in cross-forest scenarios where unconstrained delegation was previously configured
rootCause: July 2019 security update (KB4490425) blocks TGT delegation for unconstrained delegation across forest, external, and quarantined trusts. Active Directory domain controllers intentionally reject tickets with unconstrained delegation over these trust types.
solution: Reconfigure from Kerberos Unconstrained Delegation to Resource Based Kerberos Constrained Delegation. Use Set-ADUser or Set-ADComputer with -PrincipalsAllowedToDelegateToAccount pointing to the connector server computer object. Requirements: 2-way forest trust, at least one Windows Server 2012+ DC in each domain, proper name suffix routing on trust.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Resource Based Kerberos Delegation

symptom: WebSocket connection through App Proxy fails with BadGateway error: The Connection header value upgrade, keep-alive is invalid
rootCause: Backend server HTTP 101 Switching Protocols response includes additional values in the Connection header (e.g. upgrade, keep-alive) instead of exactly Connection: Upgrade. App Proxy connector requires the header-value pair to be exactly Connection: Upgrade.
solution: Configure backend web server to return exactly Connection: Upgrade in the HTTP 101 response header. Remove any additional values like keep-alive from the Connection header.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Troubleshooting WebSocket issues

symptom: WebSocket connection through App Proxy fails with BadGateway: Could not establish trust relationship for the SSL/TLS secure channel, remote certificate is invalid
rootCause: SSL certificate of backend application is not trusted by the connector server. Root CA or Intermediate CA certificates missing from connector local computer certificate store. Note: Validate Backend SSL Certificate toggle does NOT apply to WebSocket connections.
solution: Install Root CA and Intermediate CA certificates in connector server Trusted Root Certification Authorities and Intermediate Certification Authorities stores (certlm.msc). Ensure SSL certificate covers the hostname used in internal URL including Subject or SAN.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Troubleshooting WebSocket issues

symptom: WebSocket connections through App Proxy fail or experience issues when connector server has outbound proxy configured with bypassonlocal=False
rootCause: Connector proxy configuration in MicrosoftEntraPrivateNetworkConnectorService.exe.config has bypassonlocal=False, causing WebSocket traffic between connector and backend to be routed through outbound proxy unintentionally.
solution: In MicrosoftEntraPrivateNetworkConnectorService.exe.config, set bypassonlocal=True or add backend server domain to bypasslist. Restart connector service. Alternatively configure WinHTTP proxy bypass list via netsh winhttp set proxy with bypass-list.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Troubleshooting WebSocket issues

symptom: WebSocket-based application features fail through App Proxy: session cookies lost, SSO does not work, NTLM/Kerberos auth fails, Windows Admin Center features (EventLogs/PowerShell/RDP) do not work, Origin header not forwarded
rootCause: App Proxy does not implement full WebSocket protocol stack. Cookies discarded during connection opening, SSO not applied, initial request only supports cookie auth, HTTP/2 not supported, Sec-WebSocket-Extensions and Origin headers not forwarded.
solution: Set customer expectations per documented limitations. Use cookie-based auth for WebSocket. WAC features not supported through App Proxy. Ensure client uses HTTP/1.1. If backend requires Origin header, configure it to accept connections without Origin validation for App Proxy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Troubleshooting WebSocket issues

symptom: App Proxy connector appears inactive, cannot be installed, or returns InternalServerError-ConnectorError:Unauthorized / GatewayTimeout. Network device between connector and Azure endpoints performs TLS inspection (SSL inspection/termination).
rootCause: TLS inspection between the connector server and Azure endpoints (*.msappproxy.net, *.servicebus.com) is not supported. TLS inspection breaks SSL client certificate authentication used to protect the connector-to-Azure connection.
solution: Identify TLS inspection by capturing network trace (etl->pcapng via etl2pcapng), filter for TLS handshakes to *.msappproxy.net/*.servicebus.com, check Server Hello Certificate issuer - non-Microsoft issuer indicates TLS inspection. Resolution: configure network device to allow uninspected access to URLs listed in https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/global-secure-access/how-to-configure-connectors#allow-access-to-urls. Note: this method does not work on Windows Server 2022 with TLS 1.3.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Identifying TLS inspection

symptom: Kerberos authentication fails when accessing application published via App Proxy running on third-party web server (Linux/Unix). Browser shows Kerberos error page. Backend uses Negotiate/SPNEGO for Kerberos authentication.
rootCause: SPNEGO (Simple and Protected GSSAPI Negotiation Mechanism) is not enabled by default on the App Proxy Connector. Third-party web servers (Linux/Unix) require SPNEGO for Kerberos authentication.
solution: Enable SPNEGO on the connector by setting registry key: REG ADD HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft AAD App Proxy Connector /v UseSpnegoAuthentication /t REG_DWORD /d 1. Then restart connector service: net stop WAPCSvc && net start WAPCSvc. Must be done on each connector server.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Kerberos Constrained Delegation Failure_Third Pary Web Server_SPNEGO

symptom: BadGateway error when using WIA/KCD SSO through App Proxy on Windows Server 2019 or newer. Backend returns HTTP 500 with X-Error-HRESULT: 80072F00 (ERROR_INTERNET_FORCE_RETRY). May also occur with passthrough pre-authentication.
rootCause: Windows Server 2019+ changed the default value of WINHTTP_OPTION_ENABLE_HTTP_PROTOCOL from 0 to 1, enabling HTTP/2.0 by default. The connector relies on the original default value and HTTP/2.0 breaks KCD/WIA scenarios.
solution: Set registry key on connector server: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\WinHttp\EnableDefaultHttp2 (DWORD) = 0, then reboot the server.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: App Proxy Kusto traces show error: Bytes to be written to the stream exceed the Content-Length bytes size specified, with CommunicationFailureOrigin Unknown. Backend server Content-Length header does not match actual content size.
rootCause: BranchCache service running on the connector server causes the backend server to advertise a Content-Length of X bytes but send more bytes, which App Proxy validates and rejects.
solution: Check BranchCache status: netsh branchcache show status detail=ALL. If running, disable on all connector servers: netsh branchcache set service disabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: SAML SSO App Proxy application stops working with AADSTS50011: The reply URL specified in the request does not match the reply URLs configured for the application. App worked previously for extended period.
rootCause: During CWAP secret key rotation (standard maintenance process), MEAP cloud service rewrites the reply URL and overwrites customer-configured reply URLs. Can be verified in audit log.
solution: Re-add the missing reply URL to the app registration. Verify the issue by checking audit logs for reply URL changes during CWAP key rotation. Known bug, PG working on fix (no ETA).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Set-Cookie secure flag is stripped when response passes through App Proxy. Backend sends Set-Cookie with HttpOnly; secure ; SameSite=None but client receives cookie without secure flag.
rootCause: Bug in connector triggered when there is a space between secure and the closing semicolon (secure ; instead of secure;).
solution: Install connector version 1.5.3437.0 or later. Alternatively, the app developer can remove the space in the backend application.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: WebSocket connection through App Proxy fails with BadGateway error: The Connection header value upgrade, keep-alive is invalid. Error visible in Fiddler trace or connector trace.
rootCause: .NET 4.x Framework WebSocket library requires the HTTP 101 response to contain exactly Connection: Upgrade. If backend sends Connection: upgrade, keep-alive, the WebSocket establishment fails on the connector.
solution: Configure the WebSocket server to send Connection: Upgrade only (not upgrade, keep-alive). If not possible, put a proxy between connector and WebSocket server to correct the header value. Cannot be fixed on connector side.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Gateway Timeout (HTTP 504) when request has too many headers. Connector trace shows IndexOutOfRangeException in WapCloudWrapper.ProcessRequest. Error: Too many headers (N) in request while limit is 60.
rootCause: App Proxy connector has a hard-coded header count limit (was 41 in v1.5.1975.0, raised to 60 in v1.5.2864.0). Requests exceeding this limit cause IndexOutOfRangeException.
solution: Install connector version 1.5.2864.0+ which raises limit to 60. If still exceeding, work with customer to reduce headers or contact PG via AVA for further limit increase.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Kerberos with SPNEGO authentication fails after upgrading connector to rebranded Microsoft Entra private network connector. Registry key UseSpnegoAuthentication was already configured under the old path.
rootCause: The rebranded connector reads registry from HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft Entra private network connector but the UseSpnegoAuthentication key was set under the old path HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft AAD App Proxy Connector.
solution: Also create the registry key under the old path: REG ADD HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft AAD App Proxy Connector /v UseSpnegoAuthentication /t REG_DWORD /d 1, then restart: net stop WAPCSvc && net start WAPCSvc. Issue under investigation by PG.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: After deleting App Proxy application through App Registration, the app is still accessible or the assigned Connector Group cannot be deleted. Configuration visible in ASC but app not found by Application Id.
rootCause: Deleting MEAP app via App Registration only removes config in MSODS, not from the MEAP database. Orphaned config entries remain until a background task runs every 72 hours to clean them up.
solution: Wait 72 hours for automatic cleanup. If urgent: create a new app with the exact same internal and external URLs, then delete via Enterprise Applications. Always delete App Proxy apps through Enterprise Applications (not App Registration) to avoid this issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Silent connector registration using PowerShell credential object fails with User authentication failed and AADSTS9001023: The grant type is not supported over the /common or /consumers endpoints. Connector version 1.5.2864.0.
rootCause: Connector version 1.5.2864.0 sends credential-based registration request to /common endpoint, which does not support this grant type.
solution: Install connector version 1.5.3437.0 or later. Workarounds: (1) register using offline token, or (2) install older version 1.5.1975.0 first, register, then upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Link Translation in Body enabled for multiple applications sharing the same internal hostname but different paths. Translation only matches on hostname, causing incorrect URL rewriting across apps with different external URLs.
rootCause: Link Translation in Body feature uses only the host part of the internal URL for body translation, not the complete internal URL path. This is by design (under investigation by PG).
solution: Use the same external URL hostname for all applications sharing the same internal hostname. Example: app1 at https://app-contoso.msappproxy.net/app1/ and app2 at https://app-contoso.msappproxy.net/app2/ (same external hostname, different paths).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: App Proxy connector bootstrap succeeds but proxy fails with WebSocketException Could not create SSL/TLS secure channel. Backend shows CertificateValidationException ErrorCode ClientCertificateNotFound with 2-minute timeout. Occurs with TLS 1.2.
rootCause: TLS renegotiation is disabled on the connector server via registry keys DisableRenegoOnClient/DisableRenegoOnServer=1 at HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL. App Proxy requires TLS renegotiation to obtain client certificate after initial handshake.
solution: Check registry at HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL for DisableRenegoOnClient and DisableRenegoOnServer keys. Remove or set to 0. Also check outbound proxy. Also verify no TLS/SSL inspection is occurring (check backend Kusto query).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: MS Application Proxy returns unexpected behavior when Content-Type header includes charset=UTF-8 (Unicode). Backend application uses UTF-8 encoded content type.
