rootCause: MS Application Proxy does not currently support Content-Type headers that include charset=UTF-8. Engineering investigation required.
solution: Avoid using charset=UTF-8 in Content-Type header. Consider using Microsoft Entra Private Access as a supported alternative for scenarios requiring UTF-8 headers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Known Issues

symptom: Uploading custom domain SSL certificate (.pfx) in Azure AD Application Proxy fails with 'Invalid certificate, possible wrong password' error in the portal
rootCause: Certificate password contains special characters, or the certificate was created with Key Storage Provider (CNG) using ECDH/ECDSA algorithms instead of RSA, or the PFX file does not include the full certificate chain
solution: Ensure password has no special characters. Recreate certificate using RSA algorithm if it uses CNG with ECDH/ECDSA. Include complete certificate chain (root + intermediate certs) in PFX. Use certutil to dump and verify PFX contents before upload.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Troubleshooting custom domain SSL certificate issues (Azure AD Application Proxy)

symptom: Custom domain SSL certificate uploaded successfully in Azure AD Application Proxy but not applied when accessing published app - browser shows default *.msappproxy.net certificate or SSL error
rootCause: CNAME DNS record not configured correctly (not pointing to AppDnsRecordName shown in ASC), or certificate created with Microsoft Strong Cryptographic Provider which is incompatible
solution: Verify CNAME record via nslookup: external URL must resolve to AppDnsRecordName (e.g. app-contoso.msappproxy.net). Check certutil dump for cert provider. Use Kusto: filter Traces by certificate thumbprint and upload timestamp to trace backend errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Troubleshooting custom domain SSL certificate issues (Azure AD Application Proxy)

symptom: Application Proxy connector logs event 12011 (Connector failed to download client request) and/or event 12012 (Failure Code: ProtocolError) in Connector Admin event log. Users may see ServiceUnavailable error. Event 12012 with HTTP 400 often indicates client request already timed out on cloud side.
rootCause: Network connectivity issue between connector server and Application Proxy cloud service payload endpoint (*.connector.msappproxy.net:443). Connector reaches back to cloud service to download POST request body; fails due to firewall, proxy, DNS, or latency. In rare cases issue is on cloud service side.
solution: 1) Verify latest connector version. 2) Collect logs with Data Collector script (-ServiceTraceOn for intermittent). 3) Correlate Connector Admin event log with debug trace via TransactionId. 4) Use Kusto Traces table to trace transaction. 5) Analyze _HTTP-network.etl for connectivity issues. Customer-side network = customer resolves; cloud-side = engage PG via ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy - Troubleshooting the events 12011 and 12012

symptom: Application Proxy shows InternalServerError when CWAP_AuthSecret client secret is deleted or expired. Kusto shows AADSTS7000215 Invalid client secret during token request with 401 Unauthorized.
rootCause: CWAP_AuthSecret on app registration was deleted or failed to auto-renew. Required for Entra ID pre-authentication. Auto-renewal fails if publicClient is true. Now replaced by FIC.
solution: (1) Delete and re-create the App Proxy application. (2) Remove all client secrets, move app to different connector group, wait 10s, assign back. (3) PG can re-create secret. Ensure publicClient is false/null. Refer to FIC Auth docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Microsoft Entra application proxy and CWAP_AuthSecret

symptom: Slow performance/high latency accessing apps via Application Proxy. Public endpoint deployed in region far from users/connectors. Typical for Middle East/Africa customers where config is in US.
rootCause: Application Proxy config pinned to distant region. Tenant pinned to both DCs in region. Regions: US(EUS/CUS), Europe(NEUR/WEUR), Asia(EJPN/SEAS), Australia(EAUS/SEAUS), India(INW/INS, not in Geo-Connector).
solution: Use Geo-Connector feature (India not supported). For multi-region users use Azure Front Door or Traffic Manager. Check region via Kusto: TransactionSummaries | summarize count() by DataCenter. Verify latency at azurespeed.com.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/Moving The AADAP Configuration To Another Region

symptom: After TLS hardening (migration to TLS 1.2-only scale unit) for Application Proxy endpoints, clients using TLS 1.0/1.1 cannot access apps. Custom domain apps may point to old scale unit if CNAME not updated.
rootCause: App Proxy endpoint moved to TLS 1.2-only scale unit via DNS change. TLS 1.0/1.1 clients cannot negotiate. Custom domain apps with stale CNAME still resolve to old endpoint.
solution: 1) Ensure clients support TLS 1.2 before move. 2) Customer confirms, provides 3+ PST appointments for PG. 3) DNS change ~15 min. 4) Verify via SSLLabs. 5) Update CNAME for custom domains. 6) Rollback possible. 7) Ping hostname to verify *.msappproxy.net.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra application proxy/TLS hardening for Azure AD Application Proxy public endpoints

symptom: MSOnline PowerShell cmdlets fail with 403 error and message 'MSOnline PowerShell is disallowed' during scheduled temporary outage tests (scream tests) between January-March 2025
rootCause: Microsoft performs planned temporary outage tests (3-8 hours each) as preparation for MSOnline retirement starting April 2025. During these windows, all MSOnline cmdlets are blocked with 403 responses. S500 and sovereign cloud customers had separate test schedules starting February 2025.
solution: Wait for the outage to end (typically 3-8 hours). For high-severity business impact, file an ICM to AAD Distributed Directory Services / Programmability Infra team and email aadgraphandlegacypsr@microsoft.com. Long-term: migrate all scripts to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK or Microsoft Entra PowerShell before April 2025 retirement date.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/PowerShell modules

symptom: PowerShell module import fails with error 'Running script is disabled on this system' when importing AzureAD, MSOnline, or Microsoft.Graph modules
rootCause: PowerShell execution policy is set to Restricted or AllSigned, preventing module scripts from loading
solution: Run 'Set-ExecutionPolicy -ExecutionPolicy Unrestricted' (or RemoteSigned) to allow module import. If using PowerShell ISE and install fails, use a regular PowerShell console to install the module first, then open ISE and connect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/PowerShell modules

symptom: MSOnline PowerShell module versions before 1.1.166.0 (pre-2017) experience disruptions, failures, or authentication errors after June 30, 2024
rootCause: Legacy MSOnline versions below 1.1.166.0 use deprecated RPS (Remote PowerShell) authentication with certificates that expired and cannot be renewed. These old versions cannot be maintained by Microsoft.
solution: Upgrade to the latest MSOnline version 1.1.183.81 from PowerShell Gallery (Install-Module MSOnline -Force) as interim fix. Check installed version with Get-InstalledModule MSOnline. Plan full migration to Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK before MSOnline retirement in April 2025.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/PowerShell modules

symptom: Get-MgInformationProtectionBitlockerRecoveryKey returns BitLocker recovery key entries but the actual recovery key value is null/empty. The Key property is missing from the output despite the command returning other fields (Id, CreatedDateTime, DeviceId, VolumeType).
rootCause: The BitLocker recovery key value (Key property) is not included in the default response. Microsoft Graph requires the key property to be explicitly requested via the -Property parameter. Without it, only metadata fields are returned.
solution: Use -Property key when calling Get-MgInformationProtectionBitlockerRecoveryKey to explicitly request the recovery key value: Get-MgInformationProtectionBitlockerRecoveryKey -BitlockerRecoveryKeyId $_.Id -Property key. Then access the key via .Key property of the returned object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Managing BitLockerKeys

symptom: Cannot peer VNets across subscriptions associated with different Azure AD tenants via Azure Portal - Portal does not support this cross-tenant scenario
rootCause: Azure Portal does not support VNet peering when virtual networks are in subscriptions associated with different Azure Active Directory tenants. Only same-tenant peering is available through the Portal UI.
solution: Use PowerShell, CLI, or ARM Templates instead of Portal. PowerShell approach: 1) Login-AzureRmAccount with an account having access to both subscriptions 2) Get VNet references from both subscriptions using Set-AzureRmContext + Get-AzureRmVirtualNetwork 3) Add-AzureRmVirtualNetworkPeering from both VNets to each other 4) Validate peering status. Note: AzureRM cmdlets are deprecated - use Az module equivalents (Connect-AzAccount, Get-AzVirtualNetwork, Add-AzVirtualNetworkPeering).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/Azure/Azure Virtual Network/How to peer VNets across subscriptions in different Azure AD tenants

symptom: Users on non-persistent VDI machines encounter unexpected MFA prompt when opening first application after sign-in, or error code 1000504 (invalid_grant: Request contains mismatched device ids with non-PRT scope) appears in Azure sign-in logs and Windows Event Viewer (AAD/Operational). Occurs when refresh tokens are roamed between HAADJ/AADJ VDI machines.
rootCause: Microsoft enforces blocking of roamed refresh token caches across non-persistent VDI machines. Device-bound refresh tokens from machine A are rejected on machine B due to mismatched device IDs. Machine B PRT is used silently to acquire new tokens, but if local login session lacks MFA claim, user must re-complete MFA for the first app. Only impacts device-bound RTs on HAADJ/AADJ machines; non-joined devices and PRTs are not affected.
solution: 1) Recommended: Enable Microsoft Entra auth for RDP (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-desktop/configure-single-sign-on#enable-microsoft-entra-authentication-for-rdp) and enforce MFA at device sign-in to avoid additional prompts. 2) Legacy MFA: use NGC logon (face, smartcard) so PRT includes MFA claim. 3) Temporary exemption: open ICM to Identity AuthN Client / Cloud Identity AuthN MSAL Objective C, title "Request for KDFv1 exception", Sev 2.5, include Tenant ID and customer acknowledgment email. 4) Root cause code: /Root Cause - CID O365 Auth and Access/Sign-in to Office Apps/Token Roaming blocked in VDI. 5) Feature not yet in Fairfax/Gallatin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Authentication and Access/Blocking token roaming in VDI machines

symptom: Error "New password credentials must be generated using service actions" or "Update to existing credential with KeyId GUID is not allowed" when adding a signing certificate with password to an application or servicePrincipal via Microsoft Graph API
rootCause: When adding a signing cert with a password, three requirements must be met: (1) customKeyIdentifier must be the same for all 3 credentials (verify and sign keyCredentials, and the passwordCredential), (2) the keyId must be the same for the sign keyCredential and passwordCredential, (3) existing passwordCredentials for signing certs must not be re-uploaded. Violating any of these causes the error.
solution: Ensure customKeyIdentifier (SHA256 hash of cert thumbprint) is identical across the verify keyCredential, sign keyCredential, and passwordCredential. Use the same GUID for keyId on both the sign keyCredential and passwordCredential. When re-submitting existing credentials, only include non-signing passwordCredentials (those with a hint property). Use the comprehensive script in the wiki page that automates these requirements.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Managing Certs and secrets

symptom: Error "Property keyCredentials is invalid" when updating keyCredentials on a servicePrincipal object via Microsoft Graph API (Update-MgServicePrincipal)
rootCause: App instance property lock is enabled on the servicePrincipal, preventing modification of keyCredentials and passwordCredentials properties.
solution: Disable app instance property lock on the service principal before updating credentials. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/howto-configure-app-instance-property-locks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Managing Certs and secrets

symptom: After updating keyCredentials or passwordCredentials on an application or servicePrincipal via Microsoft Graph API, existing certificates or client secrets are unexpectedly removed/missing
rootCause: Microsoft Graph API replaces the entire keyCredentials/passwordCredentials array on update. If the update payload does not include existing credentials, they are permanently removed.
solution: Before updating, always retrieve the current keyCredentials and passwordCredentials arrays (Get-MgApplication or Get-MgServicePrincipal). Include all existing credentials you want to keep in the update payload alongside the new credential. For keyCredentials, set Key=null on existing entries when re-submitting. Example: to remove Cert B while keeping Cert A, submit the update with only Cert A in the keyCredentials array.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Managing Certs and secrets

symptom: Error when adding client secret to a servicePrincipal via Add-MgServicePrincipalPassword - secret creation is blocked even though the servicePrincipal exists and permissions are correct
rootCause: Either (1) the corresponding app registration has property lock enabled, preventing credential changes on the servicePrincipal, or (2) an application management policy is configured that restricts client secret creation.
solution: Check and disable property lock on the app registration if enabled. Also check if an application management policy is blocking the operation. For property lock: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/howto-configure-app-instance-property-locks
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Managing Certs and secrets

symptom: Error "ObjectSettingsTemplate 62375ab9-6b52-47ed-826b-58e47e0e304b is not supported for DirectoryObjectClass Group" when updating Microsoft 365 group settings via Microsoft Graph PowerShell
rootCause: The Group.Unified template GUID (62375ab9-...) is for directory-level settings only. It must be applied to the DirectorySettings object, not directly to a group or other directory object.
solution: For v1.0: use Update-MgGroupSetting with the Group.Unified template at directory level. For Beta: use New-MgBetaDirectorySetting / Update-MgBetaDirectorySetting. For group-specific guest settings, use the Group.Unified.Guest template (GUID 08d542b9-071f-4e16-94b0-74abb372e3d9) with New-MgGroupSetting/New-MgBetaGroupSetting targeting the specific group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/PowerShell Scenarios/Scripts/Microsoft Graph Update Directory Settings

symptom: Azure AD provisioning job enters quarantine with DuplicateAppRoles error: 'This Azure Active Directory service principal has app roles with duplicate attribute values'. Role import fails during sync cycle.
rootCause: A role in the SaaS application has the same displayName as an existing role in the Azure AD service principal but a different unique ID. AAD Sync Fabric cannot auto-resolve such ID mismatches, causing a duplicate attribute conflict on import.
solution: 1) Identify the duplicate role via ASC using AppId in the correct tenant. 2) GET the service principal appRoles via Graph API: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/serviceprincipals/{objectId}. 3) PATCH to disable the conflicting role (set isEnabled=false). 4) PATCH again to remove the disabled role definition entirely from appRoles array. Alternatively, remove via Entra portal > App registrations > App roles.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/Pages with syntax highlighting errors/Azure AD Sync Fabric Role Import - DuplicateAppRole Quarantine

symptom: Azure AD provisioning fails to export/import new roles from SaaS apps (AWS, Salesforce, etc.) to Azure AD service principal. Role creation errors during sync cycle.
rootCause: Role names imported from SaaS app contain invalid characters or exceed 120-character length limit. Azure AD Graph API restricts allowed characters to: ! # $ % & ' ( ) * + , - . / : ; < = > ? @ [ ] ^ + _ ` { | } ~ and ranges 0-9, A-Z, a-z. Space and other characters are not allowed. Names must not begin with *.
solution: Fix role names in the source SaaS application to comply with Azure AD Graph API character and length restrictions. Ensure role displayName does not exceed 120 chars and uses only allowed characters. Verify using GlobalIfxAuditEvent Kusto query with reportableIdentifier containing the role display name to trace the import/export flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/Pages with syntax highlighting errors/Azure AD Sync Fabric Role Imports

symptom: Outlook add-ins using legacy Exchange tokens (makeEwsRequestAsync, getUserIdentityTokenAsync, getCallbackTokenAsync) stop working after Exchange Online blocks legacy tokens by default. Identity team cannot determine impact from sign-in logs.
rootCause: Exchange Online deprecated legacy Exchange user identity tokens and callback tokens starting October 2024. Add-ins relying on these legacy APIs cannot authenticate once the block takes effect. Exchange 2019 and other on-premises versions are not affected.
solution: Add-in developers must migrate to Nested App Authentication (NAA) using MSAL.js (https://aka.ms/NAAdocs). Admins can control legacy token issuance via Exchange Authentication Policies. Cases reaching M365 Identity are typically misroutes - redirect to the team owning the add-in (Outlook/Office/EXO). Developer questions go to GitHub (OfficeDev/office-js) or Microsoft Q&A. After NAA GA, App Registration questions go to Azure App Experience Team (SAP: Azure > Microsoft Entra App Integration and Development > App Registrations).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Authentication and Access/Nested App Auth and Outlook legacy tokens deprecation

symptom: Users on non-persistent VDI machines encounter error 1000504 'Request contains mismatched device ids (with non-PRT scope)' when device-bound refresh tokens are reused across different VDI machines. Event logged in AAD Operational event viewer and Entra sign-in logs.
rootCause: Since May 2025, Microsoft enforces that device-bound refresh tokens cannot roam between VDI machines. When a device-bound RT issued on one machine is presented from a different machine, the request is blocked. This enforcement targets token reuse scenarios that could bypass Conditional Access or MFA requirements.
solution: Ensure VDI environments do not roam authentication folders (%LOCALAPPDATA%\Packages\Microsoft.AAD.BrokerPlugin_cw5n1h2txyewy, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\TokenBroker, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\OneAuth, %LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\IdentityCache) or registry keys (HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IdentityCRL, HKCU\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\TokenBroker). If MFA is not required, the client silently recovers using the new machine's PRT. If MFA is required, users complete one MFA challenge per session. Set BlockAADWorkplaceJoin=1 (DWORD) at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WorkplaceJoin to prevent manual account additions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Authentication and Access/SSOFirstParty

symptom: Admin cannot see Azure subscription or resource group options when setting up Copilot Pay-As-You-Go billing in M365 Admin Center. Error displayed indicates no subscription available or insufficient permissions.
rootCause: The administrator is not assigned the Owner or Contributor role for the Azure subscription and resource group. Both roles are required to configure Copilot PAYG billing.
solution: Verify the admin has Owner or Contributor role on the target Azure subscription and resource group via Azure/Entra Admin Centers. If subscription does not exist in tenant, create one first. For subscription issues, collaborate with Azure Subscription Management team (SAP: Azure/Subscription management). For resource group issues, collaborate with Azure Resource Management team (SAP: Azure/Azure Resource Manager (ARM)/Resource Management/Resource Group management).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Pay As You Go

symptom: Admin cannot change the Azure subscription or resource group after Copilot Pay-As-You-Go billing has been configured and is active.
rootCause: By design, the subscription and resource group cannot be updated while Pay-As-You-Go is active. The configuration is locked after initial setup.
solution: Turn off Copilot Pay-As-You-Go billing first (Settings > Copilot > Pay-as-you-go > Turn off pay-as-you-go billing), confirm the action, then re-run the setup wizard to select a new subscription or resource group. Note: users will lose access to their agents while PAYG is off.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Pay As You Go

symptom: Copilot usage report in M365 Admin Center shows zeros (0) for user activity in Past 7 days and/or Past 30 days columns, even though the user has a Copilot license.
rootCause: By design: if the users last activity date is beyond the selected report period range, the usage count displays as zero. This is expected behavior, not a bug.
solution: Check the Last activity date (UTC) column for the affected user. If it falls outside the report period, the zeros are expected. Adjust the report period to include the users last activity date to see non-zero values. Reference ICM: 704544860.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Usage Reports

symptom: Licensed Microsoft 365 Copilot users using Personal Content Mode (PCM) are misclassified in usage reporting. Licensed Copilot Chat usage appears in the unlicensed (nonlicensed) usage report instead of the licensed report.
rootCause: When the Copilot Chat graph grounding service plan is toggled off (which activates PCM capability), the usage reporting logic incorrectly classifies the user as unlicensed because it relies on that specific service plan to attribute licensed Copilot Chat usage.
solution: Microsoft-side fix is being deployed (targeted end of Feb 2026). The fix updates reporting logic to treat users as licensed if they have the M365 Copilot SKU with any service plan enabled, rather than requiring the Copilot Chat graph grounding plan specifically. Reference ICM: 51000000788611.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Usage Reports

symptom: Objects from newly added domain not syncing in Entra Connect Sync. Searching the Connector Space shows no users from the new domain. Run profile is missing steps for the new domain partition.
rootCause: When a new domain was added through the Entra Connect Sync wizard, no OUs or objects were selected, so the run profile did not include import/sync/export steps for the new domain partition.
solution: 1) Suspend sync: Set-ADSyncScheduler -SyncCycleEnabled $false. 2) In ADDS Connector > Configure Run Profile, add New Step for the new domain partition for each run profile type: Full Import (Stage Only, batch=50, page=500, timeout=120), Delta Import (Stage Only), Full Synchronization (batch=0), Delta Synchronization, Export (batch=30), Specific Object Import (batch=50), Specific Object Export (batch=30). Do NOT create new run profiles - only add steps in existing ones. 3) Re-enable sync and run initial: Set-ADSyncScheduler -SyncCycleEnabled $True; Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType initial.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Directory Sync/Domain is configured to sync but is missing run profiles or run steps

symptom: DeletingCloudOnlyObjectNotAllowed error in Entra Connect Sync Service wizard after an object was moved to a non-synced OU and then restored in Entra ID.
rootCause: Object was moved to a non-synchronized OU in on-premises AD. The first sync cycle deleted it from Entra ID. Admin restored the object in Entra ID before the second sync cycle could confirm and finalize the deletion, creating an inconsistent state where Entra Connect treats the restored object as cloud-only.
solution: Clear the OnPremisesImmutableId attribute from the restored object. PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes User.ReadWrite.All; Invoke-MgGraphRequest -Method PATCH -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/Users/user@domain.com' -Body @{onPremisesImmutableId = $null}. Or Graph Explorer: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/user@domain.com with body {"onPremisesImmutableId": null}. Always back up affected accounts (Exchange, SharePoint, Teams, OneDrive) before proceeding. For bulk operations, use CSV-based PowerShell script to iterate users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Directory Sync/Troubleshoot DeletingCloudOnlyObjectNotAllowed error

symptom: Newly added managed domain or federated-to-managed converted domain does not inherit the Password Expiration Policy (PasswordValidityPeriodInDays) configured in M365 Admin Center Org Settings. Passwords on the new domain remain set to never expire.
rootCause: When a new domain is created or a federated domain is converted to managed, it does not automatically inherit the PasswordValidityPeriodInDays value from the Org Settings configuration. The policy is only applied to domains that existed when the setting was last toggled.
solution: Option 1: Disable and re-enable the Password Expiration Policy in M365 Admin Center to re-apply the setting to all managed domains. Option 2: Set the value for the specific domain using PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Domain.ReadWrite.All'; Update-MgDomain -id 'domainname.com' -PasswordValidityPeriodInDays 90.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Directory Sync/Password Expiration Policy

symptom: Password-synced users (via Entra Connect password hash sync) have passwords set to Never Expire despite organization-level password expiration policy being configured in M365 Admin Center.
rootCause: By default, when password hash synchronization is enabled, cloud account passwords are set to Never Expire. The cloud password expiration policy is not enforced for synced users unless CloudPasswordPolicyForPasswordSyncedUsersEnabled feature is explicitly enabled on the tenant.
solution: Enable the CloudPasswordPolicyForPasswordSyncedUsersEnabled feature for the tenant. Verify the setting in ASC > AADConnect Synchronization > CloudPasswordPolicyForPasswordSyncedUsersEnabled. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/how-to-connect-password-hash-synchronization#password-expiration-policy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Directory Sync/Password Expiration Policy

symptom: Agent 365 Agent Details page in M365 Admin Center fails to load with a blade error when viewing a specific agent.
rootCause: The agent does not have an owner assigned. Ownerless agents trigger a load failure on the Agent Details blade in the MAC Agents portal.
solution: Set an owner for the agent from the Entra Agent ID interface in the Entra Admin Center. Navigate to the agent entry and assign an owner, then retry loading the Agent Details page in MAC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Agent 365/M365 Admin Center Agents Portal

symptom: Agent 365 agents are not showing in the M365 Admin Center (MAC) All Agents registry despite being created in Copilot Studio or third-party platforms.
rootCause: The agent does not have an Agent ID assigned. The Agent ID is an optional field. While Copilot Studio and Foundry auto-assign Agent IDs, third-party agents may not have one, making them invisible in MAC.
solution: Ensure the agent has an Agent ID assigned. For third-party agents, the developer/publisher must assign an Agent ID during agent creation or update. Copilot Studio and Foundry agents should have Agent IDs automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Agent 365/Managing Microsoft Agent 365 Agents in the MAC

symptom: Agent Publishing Error in M365 Admin Center when attempting to activate or publish an Agent 365 agent.
rootCause: One or more prerequisites are missing: insufficient Agent 365 licenses available in the tenant, the agent does not have an owner assigned, or the agent does not have a sponsor assigned.
solution: Verify and resolve all three prerequisites: (1) Confirm sufficient Agent 365 licenses are available in the tenant, (2) Confirm the agent has an owner assigned, (3) Confirm the agent has a sponsor assigned. Then retry the publishing/activation operation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Agent 365/Managing Microsoft Agent 365 Agents in the MAC

symptom: Navigating to Baseline Security Mode (BSM) in M365 Admin Center (Settings > Org Settings > Security and Privacy > Baseline Security Mode) automatically creates two Entra Conditional Access policies without admin taking any explicit action. Audit logs show the admin who opened BSM as the actor.
rootCause: Known product bug: the BSM page automatically triggers creation of two CA policies (Block legacy authentication, Phishing-resistant MFA for admins) when an admin first navigates to the BSM page. This is not the intended behavior - policies should only be created upon explicit admin action.
solution: The CA policies are created in a Disabled state and do not impact tenant security. Inform the customer that Microsoft has identified the bug and a fix is being deployed. The policies can safely remain in disabled state. If customer wants to remove them, they can delete the CA policies from Entra Admin Center > Conditional Access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Baseline Security Mode

symptom: After admin restricts Copilot Agent organization-wide sharing (set to No Users or Specific users/groups in MAC > Copilot > Settings > Data access > Agents), agents previously shared with the entire organization remain accessible to all users who already have the sharing link.
rootCause: By design, the sharing control is forward-looking only - it prevents new org-wide sharing links from being created but does not revoke access for users who already have an existing link. The agent becomes non-compliant and cannot be updated until sharing is brought into compliance.
solution: To fully revoke access for an already-shared agent, the admin must block or delete the agent. The agent author will see a warning banner and cannot save changes until the sharing setting is compliant. Inform the customer that the sharing restriction does not retroactively revoke existing links.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Agent Organization-wide Sharing Controls

symptom: Copilot Agent sharing in M365 Admin Center only allows selection of AAD-backed groups with security enabled. Other group types (distribution lists, Microsoft 365 groups without security, mail-enabled groups) cannot be used for agent sharing scope configuration.
rootCause: By design, only AAD-backed groups with security enabled are supported for Copilot Agent organization-wide sharing controls. This is a platform limitation of the Copilot Agent sharing feature.
solution: If the customer needs to scope agent sharing to a specific set of users, they must use an AAD security group (or a Microsoft 365 group with security enabled). Convert or create an appropriate security-enabled group containing the target users, then configure the sharing control to use that group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Copilot/Copilot Agent Organization-wide Sharing Controls

symptom: Unexpected or excessive DNS queries to CNAME msoid.{domain.com} observed during Microsoft 365 sign-in. DNS provider may charge for high query volume.
rootCause: Microsoft 365 client applications query msoid.{domain} for all tenants as part of Home Realm Discovery (HRD) during authentication. This lookup identifies whether a user belongs to a Gallatin (Mooncake/21Vianet) environment because Entra ID does not sync data from Gallatin. The process checks msoid CNAME records and downloads fpUrl.xml to determine the tenant cloud environment.
solution: 1) Switch to a DNS provider that does not charge per query volume. 2) Create an MSOID CNAME entry for the customer domains and set the highest TTL supported by the DNS provider to reduce query frequency. The tenant will be deemed Global per step 4 of HRD logic. Microsoft is working on removing these MSOID calls in the future.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/DNS Queries to CNAME "MSOID.domain.com"

symptom: Customer unable to add or edit a custom domain in Microsoft 365 tenant. Receives error code 715-456280 with message 'Our systems are detecting a problem and we are unable to complete this action at this time.'
rootCause: Microsoft implemented a domain impersonation blocking feature in the M365 Admin Center. When adding a new domain, an impersonation check runs against the proposed domain name. If detected as potential impersonation or phishing, the addition is blocked with error 715-456280.
solution: 1) Do NOT provide workarounds to enable domain addition. 2) Collect required info: event ID, tenant ID, domains, company info. 3) Create DfM collaboration assigned to CFAR FROG queue 'Ops PE OneVet MPN T3' via manual queue override. Title: 'M365 - Message Code 715-456280'. SLA: 72 business hours. 4) Forward CFAR FROG response to customer exactly as provided. Never include customer on FROG emails.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain Impersonation Block

symptom: Domain verification fails in Microsoft Entra ID despite correct DNS records, successful DNS propagation, confirmed legitimate domain ownership, and domain not registered to another tenant. Consistent across retries and environments.
rootCause: Microsoft Entra ID applies fraud-prevention safeguards to domain combinations with elevated abuse risk: government-style prefixes (gov, mil) and country-code TLDs (ccTLDs) with history of high resale activity or impersonation. Certain prefix+TLD combinations automatically trigger rejection logic.
solution: 1) Confirm by checking TLDList.txt (strictly confidential, never share with customers) - if domain is listed and not commented out, blocked by platform fraud safeguards. 2) Communicate with customer using standard PG engagement template. 3) Submit ICM using template '[ID][M365][MAC] - Manage Users, Groups, and Domains' with business impact details. 4) Wait for Product Group response and guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain Verification Blocks Caused by Platform Fraud Safeguards

symptom: Customer gets error when trying to request grace period (postponement) for mandatory MFA enforcement on M365 Admin Center via https://aka.ms/manageazuremfa
rootCause: Customer is not a Global Administrator or has not enabled elevated access before attempting to postpone MFA enforcement
solution: Confirm customer is a Global Administrator with elevated access (https://aka.ms/enableelevatedaccess). If confirmed and still failing, open IcM using template https://aka.ms/postponemfaicm with Session ID (Ctrl+Alt+D in Azure portal) and HAR file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/M365 Admin Center Mandatory MFA

symptom: Customer successfully requested MFA grace period but is still prompted for MFA when signing into M365 Admin Center. ASC shows MFA is explicitly enforced by the client application Microsoft Office 365 Portal and SourcesOfMfaRequirement shows Request
rootCause: Grace period may not have propagated yet (up to 24 hours), or the MFA prompt is from mandatory MFA initiative rather than another policy source
solution: 1) Verify customer has requested grace period. 2) Use ASC to confirm MFA source - check for MFA is explicitly enforced by the client application Microsoft Office 365 Portal via request parameters and SourcesOfMfaRequirement=Request. 3) If confirmed both conditions, escalate via IcM using ASC template [ID] [M365] [MAC] - Manage Users, Groups, and Domains with Correlation ID, Timestamp, portal URL, and HAR file. 4) If MFA source is CA policy or security defaults, it is not related to mandatory MFA enforcement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/M365 Admin Center Mandatory MFA

symptom: Customer receiving unexpected mandatory MFA prompt when signing into M365 Admin Center (admin.microsoft.com, admin.cloud.microsoft, portal.office.com/adminportal). Sign-in logs show App requires multifactor authentication and sign-in diagnostic shows MFA is explicitly enforced by the client application Browser
rootCause: Tenant has been targeted for M365 Admin Center MFA enforcement rollout as part of Secure Future Initiative (SFI). Currently rolling out to Enterprise subscription tenants. App ID 00000006-0000-0ff1-ce00-000000000000 enforces MFA.
solution: 1) Customer can request grace period at https://aka.ms/manageazuremfa (requires GA with elevated access), extends until Sept 30, 2025. Allow 24 hours to take effect. 2) For 40+ tenants (S500/CSS/CSD/Azure ACE customers), use IcM template https://aka.ms/postponemfaicm with CSV of tenant IDs for bulk enablement. 3) Set SAP: Microsoft 365/Authentication and Access/Setup and Use Multifactor Authentication/Unexpected MFA prompt. 4) Set Internal Title: [Portal Enforcement]. 5) Set Root Cause: Root Cause - CID O365 Auth and Access/MFA/Mandatory MFA for M365 Admin Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/M365 Admin Center Mandatory MFA

symptom: Identifiable user, group, and site details are not visible in Microsoft 365 usage reports in M365 Admin Center
rootCause: Default privacy settings in Microsoft 365 conceal user, group, and site details in all reports
solution: Navigate to M365 Admin Center > Settings > Org Settings > Services > Reports. Uncheck Display concealed user, group, and site names in all reports and click Save. Alternatively use Microsoft Graph API adminReportSettings resource type to update tenant configuration programmatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Manage and Audit Report Settings in Microsoft 365 Admin Center and Graph API

symptom: Cannot identify who enabled or disabled report privacy settings (concealed user/group/site names) in Microsoft 365 Admin Center, no native audit log available in M365 admin center
rootCause: Microsoft 365 Admin Center does not provide native audit logs for report settings changes
solution: Use Microsoft Purview Audit solution: 1) Ensure audit log search is enabled (on by default for enterprise orgs). 2) Login to Microsoft Purview (purview.microsoft.com) with admin permissions. 3) Search for operation UpdatedUsageReportsSettings (friendly name: Updated usage report privacy settings). Note: the operation name in public documentation may be incorrect - use UpdatedUsageReportsSettings instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Manage and Audit Report Settings in Microsoft 365 Admin Center and Graph API

symptom: Customer encounters error code 715-123280 during M365 tenant sign-up or initial sign-in. Tenant is blocked by additional fraud protection mechanisms. Error message indicates the system cannot complete the action.
rootCause: Tenant flagged by Microsoft CFAR fraud protection mechanisms. The Fraud Response Operations Group (FROG) handles investigation for suspicious tenant blocks identified during tenant creation.
solution: Transfer case to CFAR FROG team via manual queue override to queue "Ops PE OneVet MPN T3". Set internal title to "M365 - Message Code 715-123280". Collect required info: date/time of error, event ID, admin status, account sharing info, email/UPN, tenant ID, company name, customer name/country. Do NOT share FROG queue or communicate FROG details to customer. FROG follows 72 business hour SLA. If customer re-escalates, treat as net new and note prior escalation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Suspicious Tenant Block 715-123280

symptom: Exchange mail flow issues caused by wrong DNS record configuration - DNS records on registrar do not match values shown in M365 admin portal for Exchange/SfBO
rootCause: Exchange DNS records (CNAME, autodiscover, MX) configured on customer DNS server differ from required values shown in M365 Admin Center > Settings > Domains > ManageDNS
solution: Compare DNS records on registrar with values shown on M365 admin portal. For TXT/domain verification records, handle in M365 Identity scope. For Exchange CNAME/autodiscover/DKIM record issues, route to Exchange Online team for correct values. DKIM issues are Exchange Online responsibility; if customer wants self-managed DKIM, uncheck DKIM on portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain setup and verification DNS records

symptom: Residual/stale nameserver records visible on M365 admin portal domain management page even after removal from DNS registrar
rootCause: Domain connection option in M365 admin portal still set to 'Set up my online services for me' causing portal to cache/display old NS records
solution: Click Manage DNS on the domain page, choose 'manage your own DNS records'. Portal will show required TXT/MX values. Copy these values to DNS registrar and update. After DNS propagation, residual NS records should disappear from M365 admin center.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain setup and verification DNS records

symptom: Error on M365 domain DNS management page stating NS records not detected, even though customer believes NS records are configured at domain registrar
rootCause: Either NS records (ns1-ns4.bdm.microsoftonline.com) not properly published at domain registrar, format issue at registrar side, or M365 detection delay
solution: 1) Verify NS records at registrar match ns1-ns4.bdm.microsoftonline.com format. 2) Use nslookup to confirm NS records resolve from internet. 3) If NS records resolve but M365 still shows error, go to Setup > Domains > Check health. 4) Wait 24 hours for propagation. 5) If still failing, escalate via ICM using ASC template: [ID] [M365] [MAC] - Manage Users, Groups, and Domains.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain setup and verification DNS records

symptom: Domain not showing as 'Managed at Microsoft 365' after updating NS records at registrar - error indicates NS records detected but domain still not managed
rootCause: Wrong domain connection option selected in M365 admin portal - user selected 'Add your own DNS records' instead of 'Set up my online services for me'
solution: Navigate to M365 admin center > Settings > Domains > select domain > Manage DNS, and change the connection option to 'Set up my online services for me'. M365 will then detect the published NS records and manage the domain.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Domains and DNS/Domain setup and verification DNS records

symptom: User receives error 715-467680 when attempting to add or edit tenant values (organization info, display name, etc.) in M365 Admin Portal or Entra portal
rootCause: CFAR Real Time Detection Platform (RTFP) blocked the tenant update to prevent phishing/fraud - the update matched patterns flagged by Microsoft fraud prevention system
solution: Collect required info (event ID, admin status, UPN, tenant ID, original and desired values, customer name/country). Do NOT provide workarounds. Escalate to CFAR FROG via email to sentesc@microsoft.com with subject 'M365 - Message Code 715-467680' or DFM queue transfer to Ops PE OneVet MPN T3. SLA: 72 business hours. If customer re-escalates after block maintained, follow re-escalation process (max 3 escalations).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Handling Tenant Management Blocks 715-467680

symptom: Idle session timeout not working in M365 web apps - users not being signed out after configured inactivity period, no sign-out prompts shown
rootCause: Third-party cookies are disabled in the browser, which prevents the idle session timeout mechanism from functioning. Idle session timeout requires third-party cookies to track user activity.
solution: Verify third-party cookies are enabled in the browser. For Microsoft Edge, set tracking prevention to Balanced (default). For other browsers, enable third-party cookies. Also verify: 1) User has Security admin/Application admin/Cloud Application admin/Global admin role. 2) Feature is enabled in M365 admin center > Org Settings > Security & privacy > Idle session timeout. 3) Check for AbtActivity cookie in browser DevTools > Application > Local Storage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Idle Session Timeout

symptom: Idle session timeout not working in a specific browser while working in others - AbtActivity cookie missing from browser Local Storage for the affected browser
rootCause: Browser-specific settings (tracking prevention, privacy extensions, or cookie blocking) preventing the AbtActivity cookie from being set, which is required for idle session timeout tracking
solution: Check browser DevTools > Application > Local Storage for AbtActivity cookie. If missing: 1) Verify cookies enabled in that browser. 2) Collect Fiddler trace or F12 network logs. 3) Look for SendChannelRoundTripNotification requests - if absent, it is a client-side issue. 4) After idle timeout triggers, look for request to identity.osi.office.net with action=SHOULD_PROMPT. 5) If identity.osi.office.net request not found, escalate to idle session PG with Fiddler from Assist 365. ICM template: [ID] [M365] [MAC] - Organization Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Idle Session Timeout

symptom: Admin unable to add or remove custom tile from M365 Admin Center portal. Error displayed when trying to save custom tile configuration.
rootCause: Custom tile image does not meet requirements: not 60x60 pixels, not publicly accessible without authentication, or URL does not start with https.
solution: 1. Ensure image size is 60x60 pixels and is accessible without authentication. 2. Make sure the URL starts with https. 3. Try different Global Admin accounts. 4. Test in different browsers and InPrivate/Incognito mode. 5. If issue persists, escalate via Assist to Microsoft Admin Portal - Organizational Settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Tiles in app launcher

symptom: Admin has removed a custom app from M365 Admin Center, but the tile is still visible to users in the app launcher.
rootCause: Changes to custom tiles can take up to 24 hours to replicate across all portals. Issue may be portal-specific (office.com vs app launcher use different backend services).
solution: 1. Test in InPrivate/Incognito and multiple browsers. 2. Check behavior across portals: myapps.microsoft.com, portal.office.com, outlook.office.com. 3. Wait up to 24 hours for replication. 4. If issue only on *.office.com, escalate to Office Shell. 5. If on App Launcher, escalate to TAOS Platform Services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Tiles in app launcher

symptom: Admin added a custom app in M365 Admin Center but the tile is missing or not showing on the app launcher.
rootCause: Custom tile changes can take up to 24 hours to propagate. The issue may be browser cache related or portal-specific.
solution: 1. Test in InPrivate/Incognito and multiple browsers. 2. Check behavior across portals: myapps.microsoft.com, portal.office.com, outlook.office.com. 3. Allow up to 24 hours for replication. 4. Collect HAR/Fiddler trace, PSR/video recording, page source. Confirm valid EXO license on end user. 5. If only on *.office.com, escalate to Office Shell. If on App Launcher, escalate to TAOS Platform Services.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Tiles in app launcher

symptom: Error message every time admin uploads a logo URL in M365 Admin Center custom theme settings.
rootCause: The logo URL is not publicly addressable, requires authentication, or does not use HTTPS protocol.
solution: Ensure the logo is a publicly addressable URL using HTTPS that allows anonymous access without authentication. Upload logos to Azure Blob Storage or the O365 CDN with SharePoint Online. Default theme logo must be less than 10KB. Supported formats: JPG, PNG, GIF, SVG. SVG resized to 24px vertically; others scaled to 200x48 pixels. Use a narrower image to avoid responsive header hiding the logo.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Themes M365 Admin Center

symptom: Warning message stating 'Does not meet minimum color contrast ratio of 4.5:1' when customizing theme colors in M365 Admin Center.
rootCause: The selected text/icon color and background color combination does not meet the recommended 4.5:1 contrast ratio for accessibility compliance.
solution: This is a recommendation only, not a requirement. The admin can override the warning and still save the theme. For better accessibility, choose colors with a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 between text, icon or button color and background color.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Themes M365 Admin Center

symptom: Admin cannot see or access group themes in M365 Admin Center. Theme customization options are not visible.
rootCause: Only Global Administrators can customize company themes. Global Readers have read-only access. Group themes require Microsoft 365 Groups (not security or distribution groups).
solution: Verify the user has the Global Administrator role. Global Readers can view but not modify themes. Group themes must be mapped to Microsoft 365 Groups. Maximum 5 themes (1 default + 4 group). Users assigned to multiple group themes see the default theme. To delete the default theme, delete all group themes first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Custom Themes M365 Admin Center

symptom: Customer unable to view or configure Release Preferences options in M365 Admin Center (Settings > Org Settings > Organization profile > Release preferences).
rootCause: User does not have Global Administrator role assigned, or browser session issue preventing the page from loading correctly.
solution: 1. Reproduce in InPrivate/Incognito window. 2. Verify the user has Global Administrator role. 3. If issue persists, collect HAR trace and Steps Recorder (PSR). 4. Escalate via ICM using template [ID] [M365] [MAC] - Organization Settings. Note: changes take up to 24 hours to take effect. Opting out of Targeted Release may cause users to lose access to features not yet in Standard release.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organization Profile/Release Preferences

symptom: Users not receiving organizational messages in Office apps (Teams, Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint) despite admin having created and sent the messages.
rootCause: Privacy settings are not enabled with Microsoft account on the user Office apps, preventing organizational messages from being pulled by the Office client in the background.
solution: On user Office app: File > Account > Account Privacy > Manage Settings and ensure privacy settings enabled. For new Outlook: View > View Settings > General > Privacy and data > Privacy Settings. Verify admin has Global Administrator or Organizational message writer role. Feature is in preview. For escalation use ICM template [ID] [M365] [MAC] - Customer Insights and Analysis, then move to Iris/IDEAs Commercial Personalization.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Organizational Messages in the M365 admin center/Adoption score Organizational Messages

symptom: Profile card property changes (show/hide optional properties like Division, Role, Employee number) not reflected after admin configuration change in M365 Admin Center > People Settings
rootCause: Profile card property visibility changes have an inherent propagation delay of up to 24 hours across Microsoft 365 services
solution: Wait up to 24 hours for changes to propagate. Note: hiding a property only removes it from profile cards - it does not delete underlying data from M365 profiles or other M365 experiences. To permanently delete data, remove from source system.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/People Settings: Profile Cards

symptom: No usage report data available in M365 Admin Center for EU institution tenants. Reports show no data from December 2025 onward.
rootCause: EDPS (European Data Protection Supervisor) compliance requirements caused Microsoft to disable required data flows for EU institution tenants (ref: MC1177096)
solution: 1) Verify tenant is impacted: check M365 Pulse ODL Portal (https://m365pulse.microsoft.com/ODL/ODLPortal/AllNodeTargets/Setting/EXO/ModelA/EDPSTenantIdList) for the tenantID. 2) No workaround available. Engineering target restoration date: June 30, 2026. Customer can view MC1177096 in Message Center for updates (requires Global Admin or Message Center Privacy Reader role).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Reporting/EDPS impact on Usage Reports

symptom: M365 Admin Center usage reports show concealed/anonymous user names instead of identifiable user names. User details appear as generic identifiers rather than real names in all reports.
rootCause: Default privacy setting hides user-level identifiable information in all reports since September 1, 2021. This is a deliberate Microsoft privacy protection measure.
solution: Global admin can unhide: 1) Go to Settings > Org Settings > Services > Reports, 2) Uncheck 'Display concealed user, group, and site names in all reports', 3) Save changes. Alternatively, use Graph API: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/admin/reportSettings (available since June 2022).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Admin Portal/Reporting/Microsoft 365 Reporting

symptom: EDU tenant users (teachers/students) cannot see or use Office Add-ins. Admin unaware that Office Store is disabled by default for EDU tenants.
rootCause: Office Store defaults to Off for EDU tenants. Prior to February 2024, this was the standard default. Even after Microsoft began rolling out On-by-default, older tenants or tenants with specific configurations may still have it disabled.
solution: Navigate to M365 Admin Center > Settings > Org Settings > Services > User owned apps and services. Selectively enable for Faculty and Staff, Adult Students, or Non-Adult Students. Verify user has correct SKU (Faculty/Staff or Student) assigned via ASC. SAP: Microsoft 365/User and Domain Management.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Identity Wiki Drafts/Set Office Store Default to On for EDU Faculty and Adult Students

symptom: Some EDU users can access Office Add-ins while others cannot, despite Office Store being enabled in M365 Admin Center.
rootCause: User Age Group attribute not correctly set on user object in Entra ID. Users with Student SKU and Age Group set to null/Minor/Non-Adult are disabled by default. Users with both Faculty and Student SKU are disabled if Age Group is not set to Adult.
solution: Set the correct Age Group attribute on user objects in Entra ID Portal (user profile > Properties). For Student SKU users, set Age Group to 'Adult' to enable as Adult Student. Note: changes to Age Group can take up to 7 days to propagate. Verify SKU assignment and Age Group in ASC.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/M365 Identity Wiki Drafts/Set Office Store Default to On for EDU Faculty and Adult Students

symptom: AADSTS5000228 error - Access to tenant is denied when signing in to any M365/Entra portal application (other than signup.microsoft.com). Error message: Access to {tenant} tenant is denied. Please contact your administrator.
rootCause: Customer has not completed the tenant sign-up process by adding a billing method. The tenant remains in TenantStage = Draft state, which only allows sign-in to Microsoft Commerce signup app (signup.microsoft.com or signup.azure.com).
solution: 1) Navigate to https://signup.microsoft.com/get-started/signup?products=35dffc92-9eb4-4d5c-82c2-2582b37bb9c4 in incognito browser. 2) Sign in with the original admin account (username@tenantname.onmicrosoft.com). 3) Complete the sign-up process by adding a payment/billing method. 4) Once complete, TenantStage changes from Draft to Live and all apps become accessible. If customer needs further assistance, route to M365 Commerce (SAP: Microsoft 365/Subscription and Billing for Business/Purchase, Sign-up, MAC Portal errors/Help or issues while making a purchase).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/M365 Identity/Sign-up Process for M365 and AzureCustomers
Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Entra Connect Sync - Breaking Change - Deprecation of Legacy AWS 1P App
symptom: Entra Connect Sync versions older than 2.5.3.0 will fail to authenticate with Microsoft Entra after September 30, 2026. Synchronization stops working because the legacy Administrative Web Service (AWS) 1P App is deprecated.
rootCause: The original AWS application shares an Application ID with AAD Graph. As part of the Secure Future Initiative, Microsoft released a new AWS first party application (Microsoft Entra AD Synchronization Service, AppID: 6bf85cfa-ac8a-4be5-b5de-425a0d0dc016) and will deprecate the legacy one on September 30, 2026.
solution: Upgrade Entra Connect Sync to version 2.5.3.0 or later. Ensure prerequisites are met: .NET Framework 4.7.2 installed, TLS 1.2 enabled. Optionally enable Auto-Upgrade feature to receive automatic updates. Public docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/how-to-upgrade-previous-version

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'HybridIdentityServiceNoActiveAgents': unable to find an active agent for the domain being synced. Error message states Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent service may not be running.
rootCause: HIS (Hybrid Identity Service) sent a signaling message to the connector but Service Bus returned error because connector is not connected to the SB endpoint. Causes include: agent machines stopped/no internet, connector crashing due to unhandled exception, or agent cannot establish Service Bus connection (firewall/proxy/TLS).
solution: 1. Verify agent machines are running and meet network prerequisites per public docs. 2. Use KustoWebUX (idsharedwus cluster, AADHIS table) to check BootstrapRootOperationEvent for agent connectivity: query 'BootstrapRootOperationEvent | where env_time > ago(5d) | summarize count() by bin(env_time, 1h) | render scatterchart'.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'AzureActiveDirectoryAuthenticationFailed' with ADAL error codes: parsing-wstrust-response-failed, Unspecified-Authentication-Failure, or service-not-available. Job cannot process requests.
rootCause: Azure Auth library exceptions or errors returned with unknown error codes. These quarantine errors are thrown when ADAL error code is not already handled by Cloud Sync.
solution: Execute Repair-AADCloudSyncToolsAccount cmdlet as described in public docs (Repair the cloud sync service account). If repair cmdlet is not effective, raise an AVA Teams post.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'AzureActiveDirectoryInvalidCredentials' with errors: AADSTS50034 (account does not exist), AADSTS50126 (invalid username/password), AADSTS50057 (account disabled), or unknown_user_type.
rootCause: Credentials-related errors where proper ADAL error code is returned by the auth service. Service account used by Cloud Sync has invalid, deleted, or disabled credentials.
solution: Execute Repair-AADCloudSyncToolsAccount cmdlet. If not effective, raise AVA Teams post. Note: all authentication issues planned to be resolved when Cloud Sync moves to Service Principal Authentication (no ETA).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'HybridSynchronizationActiveDirectoryLdapBindOperationFailed': LDAP bind operation failed when establishing connection with domain controller. Additional errors: LDAP server unavailable, timeout, unknown auth error, local error.
rootCause: Azure AD Connect Provisioning agent cannot establish LDAP bind connection to the on-premises domain controller due to network issues, server unavailability, or authentication problems.
solution: Verify domain controller accessibility and LDAP connectivity from agent machine. Check firewall rules and network path. Collaborate with Directory Services team if needed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'HybridSynchronizationActiveDirectoryLdapOperationFailed' during LDAP communication. Various errors: InsufficientAccessRights (replication access denied), Referral, UnwillingToPerform (database error), Unavailable, NoSuchObject, timeout, or Busy.
rootCause: LDAP operation failures during communication with on-premises AD. Specific causes depend on the error detail: insufficient permissions for replication, cross-domain referral issues, DC overload, or objects not found.
solution: Review the specific Additional Error Details to identify the exact issue. For InsufficientAccessRights: check replication permissions. For Referral: verify cross-domain trust configuration. For Busy/Unavailable: check DC health. Collaborate with Directory Services Team for LDAP troubleshooting.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine error 'HybridSynchronizationUnableToFindDomainController': unable to reach domain controller in domain. Error: domain controller not found.
rootCause: Azure AD Connect Provisioning agent cannot locate a domain controller for the target domain, typically due to DNS resolution failure or network connectivity issues.
solution: 1. Run nltest commands to verify DNS/DC resolution: 'nltest /DNSgetDC:domain', 'nltest /DSgetDC:domain', 'nltest /dclist:domain'. 2. Execute verbose agent log export (10 min duration) with network trace (netsh trace). 3. Flush DNS and purge Kerberos tickets during capture. 4. Collaborate with Directory Services team if logs indicate DC location failure.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Cloud Sync Common Quarantine Solutions
symptom: Cloud Sync quarantine with 'QuotaExceededException': maximum message size quota for incoming messages (65536) exceeded during service bootstrap request. Agent fails to bootstrap.
rootCause: HIS has a limit on the number of agent groups an agent can be added to. Cloud Sync uses one agent group per domain, but stale agent groups (not assigned to a domain) still count toward the limit, causing the bootstrap message to exceed size quota.
solution: 1. Check agent group count via Graph Explorer: GET onpremisesPublishingProfiles/provisioning/agents/?$expand=agentGroups. 2. Remove stale/unused agent groups. 3. Split domains across different agents as workaround. 4. HIS engineering raised limit to 1MB (PR 8887837). If further increase needed, open ICM to HIS (Hybrid Identity Services / Data path) team.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Incorrect NetbiosName value synchronized
symptom: After migrating from Entra Connect to Cloud Sync, users experience Explorer constantly blinking/flashing/crashing in a loop, or unable to login to client computers. Issue occurs in disjoint namespace environments where AD domain name differs from NetBiosName.
rootCause: Default Cloud Sync mapping for NetBiosName used domain name prefix instead of actual AD NetBiosName. In disjoint namespaces (e.g., domain 'WINGTIPTOYS.LOCAL' with NetBiosName 'WINGTIP'), Cloud Sync synced 'WINGTIPTOYS' instead of correct 'WINGTIP'. This caused identity cache mismatch on client devices (SID lookup with wrong authority name creates duplicate/conflicting cache entries).
solution: Fix rolled out Feb 2025 for new configurations. Older affected configs require case-by-case mitigation when customer reports the issue. Update the Cloud Sync NetBiosName attribute mapping to read the correct value from Active Directory rather than deriving from domain name prefix.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/EntraID to AD/Exchange Hybrid Writeback using Cloud Sync
symptom: Exchange hybrid writeback option is disabled/greyed out in the Cloud Sync configuration Basics flyout page when attempting to enable the feature
rootCause: The on-premises Active Directory does not have the Exchange schema installed. Cloud Sync checks for Exchange schema presence and disables the option if not found.
solution: Install the Exchange schema on the on-premises Active Directory. After Exchange schema is present, the Exchange hybrid writeback option will become available. Minimum agent version required is 1.1.1107.0.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/EntraID to AD/Group Provisioning to AD using Cloud Sync
symptom: Domain not found in the new Cloud Sync group provisioning configuration dropdown, or the dropdown is disabled when trying to create a new configuration
rootCause: Domain is already configured, the on-premises provisioning agent is not installed, or the agent is not set up with the Cloud Sync extension enabled.
solution: Ensure domain is not already configured, agent is installed with Cloud Sync extension enabled, and agent service is running with active status. Verify agent build version is 1.1.1370.0 or later.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/EntraID to AD/Group Provisioning to AD using Cloud Sync
symptom: Cloud Sync group provisioning 'Review and enable' button is disabled and configuration cannot be enabled
rootCause: Scope filters or target container (parentDistinguishedName) attribute mapping is not configured, or no groups are selected when using Selected security groups scope.
solution: Configure the target container (parentDistinguishedName) attribute mapping. If using Selected security groups scope, ensure at least one group is selected via app role assignments.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/EntraID to AD/Group Provisioning to AD using Cloud Sync
symptom: Cloud security groups and/or membership are not being provisioned to on-premises AD. No errors appear in provisioning logs or configuration status.
rootCause: Cloud configuration is disabled, sync job has not run yet after changes, group is not a cloud-created security group, group is not entitled (not in Group scope), attribute scope filter excludes it, or on-premises user onPremisesObjectIdentifier attribute is not initialized.
solution: Enable cloud configuration, wait for sync cycle (20 min interval, ~10 min best case). Verify group is cloud-created security group and in scope. Ensure users are synced using Cloud Sync or AAD Connect 2.2.8.0+ (required to sync AD:user:objectGUID to AAD:user:onPremisesObjectIdentifier).

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/EntraID to AD/Group Provisioning to AD using Cloud Sync
symptom: Cloud Sync group provisioning fails with HybridSynchronizationInvalidResponse error containing Unsupported modification types: Add in the error details
rootCause: The provisioning agent build version does not support provisioning to AD (group provisioning to AD feature).
solution: Upgrade the provisioning agent to build version 1.1.1366.0 or later that supports provisioning to AD.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/General/FAQ for Cloud Sync
symptom: Cloud Sync Run Profile is quarantined with error AzureDirectoryServiceObjectDeletionThresholdMet (Action = Delete)
rootCause: The tenant has (or had) AAD Connect ADSync which sets an AccidentalDeletionPrevention policy in the tenant DirSyncConfiguration. Cloud Sync uses the same DirSync Webservices API which always honors this Deletion Threshold.
solution: Disable AccidentalDeletionPrevention or increase the Deletion Threshold using Disable-ADSyncExportDeletionThreshold cmdlet or MS Graph API (Update onPremisesDirectorySynchronization). Graph requires permission scopes: OnPremDirectorySynchronization.Read.All, OnPremDirectorySynchronization.ReadWrite.All.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/General/FAQ for Cloud Sync
symptom: Large number of Warning Event ID 402 in Application event log from Cloud Sync agent with description DirectoryUtility::FindPreferredDC() null credentials and partition-name unavailable
rootCause: The Cloud Sync agent calls DirectoryUtility::FindPreferredDC() with null credentials. This is a benign behavior that does not affect Cloud Sync provisioning functionality.
solution: These Event ID 402 warning events can be safely ignored. No action is required as Cloud Sync provisioning agent functionality is not affected.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/General/FAQ for Cloud Sync
symptom: Cloud Sync job quarantined with error HybridSynchronizationInvalidReferralContext. Cross-domain manager or member references are not resolved.
rootCause: By default, the Microsoft Entra provisioning agent does not chase referrals in AD. Cross-domain manager/member references fail because the agent cannot follow LDAP referrals across domains.
solution: Apply registry key on all agent servers: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure AD Connect Agents\Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent, add string value ReferralChasingOptions=96 (ReferralChasingOptions.All for subtree and base-level referrals). Restart Microsoft Entra Connect Provisioning Service on all agents. Note: enabling referral chasing will slow down the sync job.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Object Sync/Attributes not appearing when loading the source schema
symptom: Global administrator cannot see additional attributes in the Cloud Sync attribute mapping dropdown when going into Edit attribute mappings. Expected attributes do not appear in the source schema drop-down list.
rootCause: The user has additional roles (e.g., Hybrid Identity Administrator) assigned alongside Global Administrator. This triggers a regression where only the default schema is discovered instead of the full directory schema. Related ICM: 480473044.
solution: Remove the additional roles from the user (keep only Global Administrator). After making attribute mapping changes, the roles can be re-added if needed. Check ICM 480473044 and PR 10349674 to verify if the code fix has been deployed.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Duplicate accounts showing up in AAD Portal
symptom: Duplicate user accounts appear in Azure AD portal - one licensed (original) and one unlicensed (new/duplicate). Error in O365 Exchange: 'Failed to sync the ArchiveGuid of mailbox because one cloud archive exists'. Occurs in Account-Resource forest topology with Entra Connect.
rootCause: Linked mailboxes were converted to regular (remote) mailboxes on-premises, which cleared the MsExchMasterAccountSID attribute in the resource forest. Since Entra Connect sync rules use MsExchMasterAccountSID to join objects from two forests into one MS Online object, clearing this attribute broke the join rule, causing two separate MS Online objects to be created instead of one merged object.
solution: 1) Convert affected on-premises remote mailboxes back to linked mailboxes: Set-User -Identity <email> -LinkedCredential $cred -LinkedDomainController <DC FQDN> -LinkedMasterAccount <account>. 2) Verify MsExchMasterAccountSID is populated with ObjectSID from the account forest. 3) Update msExchRecipientDisplayType, msExchRemoteRecipientType, and msExchRecipientTypeDetails to match unaffected users. 4) Move resource forest AD object to non-syncing OU, run Delta sync (removes duplicate). 5) Move back to syncing OU, run Delta sync again (objects rejoin into one). 6) Verify: single merged object, no validation errors.

symptom: Cloud Sync password writeback fails - agent connectivity issues or provisioning errors. Prerequisites check shows TLS 1.2 not enabled on the server, or TLS 1.3 enabled on non-Windows Server 2022 without .NET 4.8
rootCause: Cloud Sync agent requires TLS 1.2 enabled. TLS 1.3 only works on Windows Server 2022 with .NET 4.8+. If TLS 1.2 is disabled or TLS 1.3 is misconfigured, agent cannot establish secure connection
solution: Enable TLS 1.2 using the PowerShell script from Microsoft docs (reference-connect-tls-enforcement). For TLS 1.3, ensure Windows Server 2022 and .NET 4.8+. Also verify agent build 1.1.977.0 or greater is installed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Passsword Management/Password Writeback/Password Writeback and SSPR with Cloud Sync

symptom: Password writeback via Cloud Sync fails with ADUserNotFound error. SSPR cannot find the target user in on-premises AD
rootCause: An OU in scope was renamed in Active Directory. The OnPremisesDistinguishedName property of child objects does not get updated with the new DN. Cloud Sync SSPR uses the on-premises DN to find the user in AD, so the old DN fails to match
solution: Update the user OnPremisesDistinguishedName to reflect the new OU path, or move and re-sync the affected users. Verify the DN used by SSPR matches the current AD structure after OU rename
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Passsword Management/Password Writeback/Password Writeback and SSPR with Cloud Sync

symptom: Password reset via Cloud Sync fails with event ID 33001. Verbose logs show System.Runtime.InteropServices.COMException (0x8007200A): The specified directory service attribute or value does not exist
rootCause: Cloud Sync password writeback uses DirectoryServices.AccountManagement.UserPrincipal.FindByIdentity method which requires read permissions on the built-in Users container at root domain level. If the gMSA account lacks these permissions, the method fails with COMException 0x8007200A
solution: 1) Check gMSA permissions using Set-AADCloudSyncPermissions cmdlet. 2) Verify gMSA has at least read permissions on the built-in Users container at the Root Domain level. Grant permissions if missing
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Passsword Management/Password Writeback/Password Writeback and SSPR with Cloud Sync

symptom: Password writeback via Cloud Sync fails with System.UnauthorizedAccessException: Access is denied (HRESULT: 0x80070005 E_ACCESSDENIED) at SDSUtils.SetPassword. Agent trace shows password writeback operation ResetPassword completed but throws access denied exception
rootCause: A policy restricting remote calls to SAM (Security Account Manager) is blocking the gMSA account from performing password reset operations on the target user, even though gMSA permissions appear correctly configured
solution: Review and modify the Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM policy to allow the gMSA account. Follow the detailed steps in the ADO wiki article on Troubleshooting Password Writeback (SSPR_0029 - servers not allowed to make remote calls to SAM)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Passsword Management/Password Writeback/Password Writeback and SSPR with Cloud Sync

symptom: Cloud Sync password writeback fails with System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException: A certificate chain could not be built to a trusted root authority. Agent cannot verify signing certificate signing.passwordreset.microsoftonline.com. CAPI2 event 41 shows revocation server offline
rootCause: Cloud Sync gMSA service account cannot validate CRL/OCSP endpoints for the password reset signing certificate. WinINet (used for HTTP proxy) is not supported for service accounts including gMSA, so even with proxy configured, gMSA cannot reach CRL URLs like oneocsp.microsoft.com, crl.microsoft.com, ocsp.digicert.com
solution: Allow Cloud Sync provisioning agent direct access (bypassing proxy) to: crl3.digicert.com, crl4.digicert.com, ocsp.digicert.com, crl.microsoft.com, oneocsp.microsoft.com, ocsp.msocsp.com (port 80/HTTP), and ctldl.windowsupdate.com, www.microsoft.com/pkiops (port 443/HTTPS)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Passsword Management/Password Writeback/Password Writeback and SSPR with Cloud Sync

symptom: Cloud Sync provisioning job goes into quarantine with HybridIdentityServiceNoActiveAgents and GatewayTimeout. Agent bootstrap fails with The maximum message size quota for incoming messages (65536) has been exceeded
