rootCause: Agent bootstrap response from Azure exceeds the default 65536 byte message size limit due to redundant Service Bus endpoints in the control channel response. Agent cannot process the oversized response and enters repeated bootstrap retry loop
solution: Update agent to latest version - PG has fixed this by removing redundant Service Bus endpoints from the control channel response. Ensure agent build is current
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Connectivity Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync test connection fails with GatewayTimeout and NoActiveConnector. Agent verbose trace shows The remote name could not be resolved for Service Bus endpoint (his-sb-hisgeneral-*.servicebus.windows.net)
rootCause: DNS resolution failure on the Cloud Sync agent server - unable to resolve Azure Service Bus hostnames required for agent connectivity
solution: Update DNS settings on the agent server to ensure it can resolve Azure Service Bus endpoints (*.servicebus.windows.net). Verify firewall/proxy allows DNS resolution for Azure endpoints
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Connectivity Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync Provisioning Agent MSI installation fails with error during package installation. Service AADConnectProvisioningAgent cannot start. Or wizard completion fails after using Domain Admin workaround with System EventID 7043
rootCause: Local Security Policy Log on as a service does not have NT Service\ALL SERVICES listed. During installation the service logon credentials are set to NT SERVICE\AADConnectProvisioningAgent which requires this permission
solution: Open secpol.msc > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment > Log on as a service. Add NT Service\ALL SERVICES. Note: this setting may be controlled by Group Policy (GPO), in which case modify the GPO. Do not use Domain Admin credentials as a workaround
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync agent wizard fails with Failed changing Windows service credentials to gMSA. System event log shows EventID 7038 indicating incorrect password for managed service account. Service logon tab shows credentials not greyed out as expected
rootCause: The gMSA account is not properly registered as a managed account on the server. The managed account status is set to False preventing proper credential management by the system
solution: Run sc.exe qmanagedaccount aadconnectprovisioningagent to verify status. If False, run sc.exe managedaccount aadconnectprovisioningagent true to fix. Then complete the wizard again
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync agent installation fails with DirectoryServicesCOMException (0x80072030): There is no such object on the server. Error occurs during gMSA creation at wizard configuration stage
rootCause: The Managed Service Accounts container is missing from the domain. This can happen if the container was never created or was deleted. The container is required for gMSA creation
solution: Verify the Managed Service Account container exists in the domain using dsa.msc. If missing, collaborate with Windows Directory Services team to restore/create the container using ADPrep /Domainprep. Ensure AD schema is updated to Windows Server 2012 level
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync agent installation fails with DirectoryServicesCOMException (0x80072030): There is no such object on the server. Managed Service Accounts container exists in dsa.msc but agent still cannot find it via WellKnown GUID lookup
rootCause: The OtherWellKnownObjects attribute on the domain root has orphan metadata pointing to a deleted Managed Service Accounts container (contains DEL: and CN=Deleted Objects in the DN). The WellKnown GUID 1EB93889E40C45DF9F0C64D23BBB6237 lookup fails
solution: Check OtherWellKnownObjects using PowerShell: Get-ADObject (Get-ADRootDSE).DefaultNamingContext -Properties otherwellKnownObjects. If the value contains DEL: or Deleted Objects, collaborate with Windows Directory Services team to fix the orphan metadata
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync agent configuration fails with Unable to create gMSA because KDS may not be running on domain controller. Netlogon EventID 9001/9002 present in event logs
rootCause: Kerberos encryption type mismatch between the server SupportedEncryptionTypes registry setting and the gMSA account msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes attribute. Server and gMSA account do not share a common encryption type
solution: 1) Check server encryption types: reg query HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Kerberos\Parameters. 2) Check gMSA msDS-SupportedEncryptionTypes in dsa.msc. 3) Align: Set-ADServiceAccount -Identity provAgentgMSA -KerberosEncryptionType AES128,AES256. 4) Reboot agent server and reinstall
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync Provisioning Agent installation fails at final configuration with The remote server returned an error: (401) Unauthorized. Agent registration completes but domain assignment fails. Agent shows inactive in portal
rootCause: The Azure AD account used for installation either does not have the Hybrid Admin role assigned or the account is disabled in Entra ID
solution: 1) Verify the AAD account has at least Hybrid Admin role. 2) Ensure account is not disabled. 3) Uninstall and reinstall the agent. 4) Verify agent shows active in portal and test connection succeeds
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync agent installation fails with Unable to install service account provAgentgMSA$ after 6 retries when using Create gMSA option in the wizard
rootCause: A gMSA named provAgentgMSA already exists in a different AD domain (child or tree domain). Known PG bug (Bug 1527424) - agent setup fails on parent domain if gMSA already exists in child domain
solution: Use custom gMSA option instead of Create gMSA. Create a custom gMSA with a different name following Microsoft docs steps (Create gMSA account with PowerShell), then select Use custom gMSA during wizard setup
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync Provisioning Agent service fails to start during installation with Verify that you have sufficient privileges to start system services. Application event log shows .NET Runtime EventID 1026 with unhandled exception at PasswordVault.RetrieveAll()
rootCause: The Credential Manager service startup mode is set to Disabled. The provisioning agent depends on Credential Manager to retrieve password vault of the gMSA service account. When disabled PasswordVault.RetrieveAll() throws an unhandled exception crashing the service
solution: Set the Credential Manager service startup type back to Manual (default). Then re-run the Cloud Sync agent installation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Cloud Sync Provisioning Agent installation fails with DirectoryServicesCOMException (0x8007202F): A constraint violation occurred while configuring DsReplicationGetChanges permissions on domain for gMSA account
rootCause: Customer has implemented AD Administrative Tier Model. The admin account used is Tier 1 restricted from accessing Domain Controllers (Tier 0 assets). Even with domain admin rights the Tier Model prevents operations on the domain root
solution: Use a Tier 0 account to install the Cloud Sync Provisioning Agent. In environments with AD Administrative Tier Model only Tier 0 accounts have permissions to grant DsReplicationGetChanges on the domain root
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cloud Sync/Provisioning agent/Cloud Sync Installation Issues

symptom: Microsoft Entra Connect Health for Sync Services blade shows old server name instead of current name. Server was renamed before domain join but after OS installation and Entra Connect was installed afterwards
rootCause: The MachineIdentity registry key (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft Online\Reporting\MonitoringAgent\MachineIdentity) stores a client-side generated GUID created during initial registration. The machine name at registration time is cached and does not update when server name changes
solution: 1) Delete registry key: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Microsoft Online\Reporting\MonitoringAgent\MachineIdentity. 2) Re-register the Health agent and delete the old server entry from portal. This generates a new GUID with correct machine name. Note: changing server name after Entra Connect is installed is not supported
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Health/Connect Health for Sync/Old Server Name is shown on Entra Connect Health Sync Services

symptom: Group Writeback V2 export fails with cd-error code 8625 during AD export run profile. The group is not created in on-premises Active Directory. EventID 2866 logged on Domain Controller under Directory Service events.
rootCause: The Domain Controller's audit event cache reached maximum limit (default 17000). Large group membership changes (e.g., deleting membership of a large group where 'member' attribute is audited) cause excessive audit events that exceed the cache.
solution: On all Domain Controllers: 1) Check/create registry HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\services\NTDS\Parameters\Maximum Audit Queue Size (DWORD 32-bit, default 17000). 2) Increase the value as needed. 3) Restart Domain Controllers. 4) Run 2 sync cycles on AADConnect to confirm successful writeback provisioning. Consider collaborating with Directory Services team for large environments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Group Writeback/Troubleshooting Group Writeback V2 export fails with cd-error - error code 8625

symptom: Users with expired passwords are not prompted to change their password even after EnforceCloudPasswordPolicyForPasswordSyncedUsers is enabled. User continues to access resources without being forced to update expired password.
rootCause: Password expiration is only evaluated during interactive password-based sign-ins. If the user signed in via session cookie (Credential Type: Session Token), password expiration is not evaluated and user is not prompted to change their expired password.
solution: 1) Check ASC Auth Troubleshooter Expert View > Credentials tab for Credential Type. If 'Session Token', it is not interactive auth. 2) Admin should revoke all refresh tokens: Revoke-MgUserSignInSession. 3) Force interactive sign-in via incognito browser to portal.azure.com with username/password. 4) Verify PasswordPolicies is Blank/None and LastPasswordChangeTimestamp exceeds domain PasswordValidityPeriodDays (default 90).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Hash Synchronization (PHS)/Password Hash Sync Features

symptom: User cannot login to Entra ID. Sign-in error code 50173: 'The provided grant has expired due to it being revoked, a fresh auth token is needed.' LastPasswordChangeDateTime shows a date in the future in Entra portal.
rootCause: The LastPasswordChangeDateTime attribute was set to a future time (customer error) and synchronized to Entra ID, causing all tokens issued before that future date to be considered invalid.
solution: If PHS is enabled: reset/change user password on-premises, the value updates after next delta sync cycle. If PHS is disabled: 1) Reset password via MgGraph PowerShell (Update-MgUser with passwordProfile), 2) If domain is federated, temporarily switch UPN to @tenantName.onmicrosoft.com before reset, then switch back. 3) Then change/reset password on-premises. Note: Does not work if user has admin roles or is Owner/Member of Role Assigned groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Hash Synchronization (PHS)/TSG LastPasswordChangeTimestamp Value in Future

symptom: Synced AD administrator (member of AD Protected Group) gets SSPR_0029 error when trying to reset forgotten password via SSPR from cloud. Application Event 33004/6329 with hr=80230626 'management agent credentials were denied access'.
rootCause: User is member of on-premises AD Protected Group (adminCount=1). SDProp background task overwrites AD permissions with AdminSDHolder settings every 60 minutes, removing Password Writeback MSOL account permission to reset password on protected accounts.
solution: Protected group admins cannot use Forgot Password flow to reset password via SSPR+Password Writeback. They can only use Change Password (where current password is known). To enable reset: manually grant MSOL account Reset Password permission on user AD object (will be overwritten by SDProp unless AdminSDHolder is also modified). Check adminCount attribute on user object to confirm protected status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: SSPR_0029 error for non-protected synced user. Event 6329 shows hr=80230626 'management agent credentials were denied access' with 'Access denied setting password: Server Error 0x5, Ldap Error 0x32'.
rootCause: The AD DS Connector account (MSOL_xxx) lacks necessary AD permissions (Reset Password, Write lockoutTime, Write pwdLastSet) on the target user OU or object in Active Directory.
solution: Grant proper AD permissions to the AD DS Connector account on the user OU: Reset Password, Write lockoutTime, Write pwdLastSet. Use the AADConnect wizard Set AD DS Connector Account Permissions option, or manually configure via ADUC Security tab. Verify which MSOL account is in use by checking Synchronization Service Manager > Connectors > AD Connector Properties.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: SSPR_0029 error with Event 6329 showing 'Failed to acquire user information: Contoso\MSOL_xxx. Error Code: ERROR_ACCESS_DENIED' in admaexport.cpp(2944).
rootCause: The 'Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM' security policy is applied on the DC or AADConnect server, blocking the MSOL account from making remote SAM calls. Even after disabling the GPO, the RestrictRemoteSam registry value persists.
solution: Option A: Keep the policy but add the AD DS Connector account (MSOL_xxx) to the allowed list. Option B: 1) Run GPupdate /force, 2) Verify policy is Not Defined via GPresult, 3) Manually delete registry: Remove-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa -Name restrictremotesam. Important: Repeat on ALL Domain Controllers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: SSPR_0029 error with Event 6329 showing hr=80005000 'unable to get error text' and Event 33001. User AD Distinguished Name contains reserved characters such as forward slash (/).
rootCause: User AD Distinguished Name contains reserved LDAP characters (forward slash, comma, plus sign, backslash, angle brackets, etc.) which the new Password Writeback implementation cannot handle properly, causing ExportPasswordSet to fail with 0x80005000.
solution: Remove the reserved character from user Distinguished Name in AD. Reserved characters: comma, plus sign, double quote, backslash, angle brackets, semicolon, LF, CR, equals sign, forward slash. Workaround (not recommended): Enable legacy implementation via registry HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\ADSync\Parameters\PerMAInstance\domainName.com with PasswordChangeAccessCheckLegacy=dword:1. Warning: Legacy mode depends on NetUserGetInfo API prone to Access Denied on DCs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: User with Azure AD Administrator role gets SSPR_009 error: 'Your organization has not turned on password reset' when trying to reset password via SSPR Forgot my password flow. Works fine for non-admin accounts.
rootCause: SSPR for Administrators (SSPR-A) is not enabled on the tenant. SSPR-A and SSPR for Users (SSPR-U) are separate configurations. Azure AD accounts with admin roles (Global Admin, Billing Admin, etc.) use SSPR-A which must be independently enabled.
solution: Enable password writeback in SSPR: 1) Sign in to Entra admin center as Global Administrator. 2) Browse to Protection > Password reset > On-premises integration. 3) Check Write back passwords to your on-premises directory. 4) Optionally check Write back passwords with Microsoft Entra Connect cloud sync if provisioning agents detected. 5) Check Allow users to unlock accounts without resetting their password to Yes. 6) Save.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: Password change/reset via SSPR fails with message: Unfortunately you cannot reset this user password because your on-premises policy does not allow it. DC Security Event 4724 shows Audit Failure for password reset attempt by MSOL account.
rootCause: The new password provided does not comply with on-premises AD password policy (complexity, minimum length, minimum age, password history, or fine-grained password policy applied to user group).
solution: Provide a compliant password. Check AD password policy via: 1) NET ACCOUNTS on DC, 2) Get-ADDefaultDomainPasswordPolicy PowerShell, 3) GPresult /H GPreport.htm. Check Fine-Grained Password Policies: Get-ADFineGrainedPasswordPolicy -Filter * and NET USER username /DOMAIN. Compare unicodePwd/pwdLastSet metadata across DCs with: repadmin /showobjmeta * DN to check for AD replication issues.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: Password writeback fails with Event 33009/6329: hr=8023062C 'The password could not be set because the server is not configured for insecure setting of passwords, or a 128 bit TLS or SSL connection is required.'
rootCause: Sign and Encrypt LDAP Traffic is disabled in one or more of the three configuration locations in AADConnect Synchronization Service Manager AD Connector properties.
solution: Enable Sign and Encrypt LDAP Traffic in all 3 locations: 1) Connectors > AD Connector > Properties > Connect to Active Directory Forest > Options. 2) Configure Directory Partitions > select partition > Options (DC connection settings). 3) Configure Directory Partitions > select partition > Set Credentials (if alternate credentials) > Options. Restart ADSync service after enabling all three.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: Admin resets user password from Office Admin Portal (Microsoft 365 admin center). User can sign in online with the new password, but the password is not written back to on-premises AD. On-prem and cloud passwords become out of sync.
rootCause: Office Admin Portal does not use SSPR / Password Writeback libraries. Password reset from Office Admin Portal only updates Azure AD password without triggering Password Writeback to on-premises AD. This is by design.
solution: Use Azure Portal instead of Office Admin Portal for password reset operations that need Password Writeback. Distinguish the portal used via ASC > Tenant Explorer > Audit Logs: Reset password (by admin) = Azure Portal; Reset user password = Office Admin Portal. Note: Admin-initiated resets from PowerShell v1/v2 or Microsoft Graph API (except Graph beta) also do not trigger writeback.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: Password reset/change fails with SSPR_0030 connectivity error. All connectivity tests from Connect Sync pass successfully. Customer previously had Cloud Sync configuration with password writeback enabled that was later deleted.
rootCause: When Cloud Sync configuration with password writeback was deleted, orphaned published resources remained in the tenant. These stale domain-specific published resources interfere with Connect Sync password writeback routing, causing connectivity failures.
solution: Use ASC Graph Explorer to query: onPremisesPublishingProfiles/provisioning/publishedResources to find orphaned published resources. Remove stale published resources from the previous Cloud Sync configuration. Also verify standard connectivity requirements: servicebus endpoints (ssprdedicatedsbprodscu/ncu.servicebus.windows.net port 443), TLS 1.2 support, and required SSL certificates (DigiCert Global Root G2 and CA).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Features/Password Writeback/Troubleshooting Password Writeback

symptom: AADConnect on Windows Server 2019 shows staging-error discovery errors during import cycle or Password Hash Sync fails. Event ID 6301 in Application log with TVP (Table Valued Parameter) errors: 'BAIL: MMS(...): rcvtvp.h(...): 0x80004005 (Unspecified error)'
rootCause: Windows Server has 'Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support' (Beta) checkbox enabled under Region settings. The AADConnect SQL database engine does not support this Unicode setting, causing table valued parameter operations to fail.
solution: Uncheck 'Use Unicode UTF-8 for worldwide language support': Control Panel > Region > Administrative tab > Change system locale > uncheck the UTF-8 beta checkbox, then restart the server. AADConnect sync cycles should resume normally after reboot.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Table Valued Parameter (TVP) errors when AADConnect is installed on Windows Server 2019

symptom: Entra Connect Sync export to Entra ID fails with 'An internal error has occurred' error containing a Tracking ID. No clear indication of which attribute is causing the failure.
rootCause: Entra Connect Sync is attempting to export an attribute value that is not supported or invalid in Entra ID. The specific invalid attribute is not surfaced in the error message directly.
solution: 1) Use ASC > AADConnect Synchronization > DirSync WebServices logs, search by the Tracking ID from the error. 2) Confirm the immutableID matches the target user. 3) Extract the internalCorrelationID. 4) Query Kusto MSODS cluster: GlobalIfxUlsEvents table filtered by env_time range, internalCorrelationId, and immutableID to identify the unsupported attribute being exported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Troubleshoot "An internal error has occurred" - Invalid attributes

symptom: Entra Connect Wizard fails on last page with 'Unable to configure directory extensions. Please consult the event log for additional information.' Event ID 904 in Application logs with error code 94 (ExtensionAttributeMismatch).
rootCause: A directory extension attribute was originally created as multi-valued in on-premises AD, synced to Entra ID, then defuncted/renamed in AD and recreated as single-valued with the same name. The Tenant Schema Extension App still has the old multi-valued definition and cannot convert it to single-valued.
solution: 1) Remove the old multi-valued extension definition from Tenant Schema Extension App: via Graph Explorer DELETE https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications/{tenantSchemaAppId}/extensionProperties/{extensionAttributeObjectID}, or via Graph PowerShell SDK: Remove-MgApplicationExtensionProperty. 2) Re-run Entra Connect Wizard to add the new single-valued attribute.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Troubleshooting Directory Extensions

symptom: Directory extension sync fails with completed-export-errors. Detailed error: 'The following extension properties cannot be synced because the corresponding application is not installed: extension_<AppID>_<extensionAttributeName>'.
rootCause: The Tenant Schema Extension App service principal was deleted. The Entra portal does not currently block deletion of this system-only app (known portal bug). Without the service principal, directory extension sync cannot function.
solution: 1) Check if SP is soft-deleted: Graph GET /directory/deletedItems/microsoft.graph.servicePrincipal?$filter=displayName eq 'Tenant Schema Extension App'. 2) If found, restore: POST /directory/deletedItems/{id}/restore. PowerShell: Get-EntraDeletedServicePrincipal + Restore-EntraDeletedDirectoryObject. 3) If hard-deleted (>30 days): recreate via POST /servicePrincipals with {appId from GET /applications}. 4) Verify by re-running sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Troubleshooting Directory Extensions

symptom: Graph API PATCH to update onPremisesExtensionAttributes on a cloud-only user (with EXO license) returns Request_BadRequest: 'Unable to update the specified properties for objects that have originated within an external service', even though the user was never synced from on-premises AD.
rootCause: The user existed during a DirSync disablement (authority transfer), which stamped _SingleAuthorityMetadata with ExchangeMastered=True on all objects including cloud-only ones. This incorrectly blocks Graph from updating onPremisesExtensionAttributes, treating them as externally-mastered.
solution: Workaround: update extension attributes from EXO side using Exchange PowerShell: Set-MailUser -Identity <user> -CustomAttribute<N> 'value'. The value will be forward-synced to Entra ID. Permanent fix pending PG work item (ADO #1908437) to remove multi-authority properties.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Troubleshooting onPremisesExtensionAttributes updates

symptom: User removed from AAD Connect sync scope (soft-deleted in Azure AD), then restored from Azure AD Recycle Bin - user appears as cloud-only (DirSyncEnabled=false) but retains shadow properties
rootCause: Restoring a soft-deleted synced user from Azure AD Recycle Bin is an unsupported loophole. It only flips DirSyncEnabled true>false but all shadow properties (ShadowProxyAddresses etc) remain, creating a disconnected/orphaned object that cannot be properly managed.
solution: Do NOT use this method to convert synced users to cloud-only. Use the supported method: Configure user Source of Authority (SOA) per Microsoft docs. If object is already in this state, it may cause issues like ProxyAddresses conflicts stuck in ShadowsProxyAddresses.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Need to reprovision a user that was deleted from on-premises AD but restored from Azure AD Recycle Bin - object is orphaned with DirSyncEnabled=false and shadow properties
rootCause: The Azure AD object is disconnected from on-premises source. Shadow properties remain from previous sync. The object needs to be hard-matched back to a new on-premises AD object using SourceAnchor/ImmutableId.
solution: 1) Ensure AADConnect uses ms-ds-ConsistencyGuid as SourceAnchor. 2) Create similar user in on-prem AD (same UPN/Mail/ProxyAddresses). 3) Get ImmutableId: Get-MgUser -Property OnPremisesImmutableId. 4) Copy ImmutableId to on-prem AD: Set-ADSyncToolsMsDsConsistencyGuid -Identity <UPN> -Value <ImmutableId>. 5) Run delta sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Stale on-premises attributes remain on user objects after disabling DirSync on the tenant
rootCause: Behavior changed over time: pre-Aug 2023 and post-Jan 2025, DirSync disablement no longer automatically clears on-premises attribute values. Microsoft introduced a new Graph API for self-service management of on-premises attributes.
solution: Use the Microsoft Graph API for self-service cleanup of stale on-premises attributes. Follow: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/tshoot-clear-on-premises-attributes. No Microsoft support intervention required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Need to clean up previously synced password hashes from Azure AD after disabling Password Hash Sync (PHS)
rootCause: PHS stores password hashes in Azure AD including the last 4 password history entries. Simply disabling PHS does not remove the already-synced hashes.
solution: 1) Disable Password Writeback in AAD Connect wizard (Optional features). 2) Disable Password Writeback for Cloud Sync from Azure Portal > Entra ID > Password Reset > On-premises integration. 3) Use Update-MgUserPassword to set random passwords on all affected users - run 5 times per user to flush the 4-hash history.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Azure AD Connect installation fails with error 0x80231310 when using Azure AD Groups for role assignments to assign Hybrid Identity Administrator role
rootCause: Azure AD Connect does not support role assignment via Azure AD Groups (isAssignableToRole groups). Only direct role assignment to the account is supported. Groups-based role causes Password Writeback feature enablement to fail.
solution: Directly assign Hybrid Identity Administrator or Global Administrator role to the Azure AD account used for AAD Connect installation. Do not use Azure AD Groups for role assignments with AAD Connect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Password Hash Synchronization (PHS) not working or taking very long after switching Azure AD Connect from staging to active mode. Users report password changes not reflected in Azure AD.
rootCause: When AAD Connect is in staging mode, PHS is disabled. Switching to active mode forces the PHS agent to catch up on ALL password updates since the last active time. Large environments can take hours or days. If no full sync was completed before staging, FromUsn=0 forces full PHS sync.
solution: Wait for PHS catch-up to complete. Monitor via PHS verbose logs for FromUsn values. Expect Application event IDs 657 (Password Change Result) and 668 (Refetching change). Event 6948 context not initialized is normal until full PHS sync completes. New password updates may be delayed during catch-up.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Password Writeback (SSPR) fails with error 407 Proxy Authentication Required - unable to reach ssprdedicatedsbprodncu.servicebus.windows.net on ports 80/443
rootCause: Web proxy on the network requires user authentication for internet access. While logged-on users can authenticate seamlessly (domain-authenticated), the ADSync service cannot interact with proxy authentication prompts.
solution: Configure the web proxy to allow the AAD Connect server to access the internet without authentication. The ADSync service account cannot handle proxy auth challenges. Ensure SSPR Service Bus endpoints are accessible without proxy auth.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Synced group/user/device/contact object is orphaned in Azure AD (no longer exists in on-premises AD) but portal does not allow deleting synced objects
rootCause: Azure portal prevents deletion of objects marked as directory-synced, even when the on-premises source object no longer exists.
solution: Use PowerShell cmdlets: Remove-MgGroup, Remove-MgUser, Remove-MgDevice, or Remove-MgContact. Alternatively use Graph API: DELETE https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/directoryObjects/{objectId} via Graph Explorer or Invoke-MgGraphRequest.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Cannot restore user from Entra Recycle Bin due to ProxyAddresses or UserPrincipalName conflict with another active object. Error: Conflicts detected while trying to restore the user
rootCause: The deleted user ProxyAddresses or UPN is already in use by another active object in the directory, blocking restoration.
solution: Use Restore-MgDirectoryDeletedItem with optional parameters: -AutoReconcileProxyConflict $true (removes conflicting proxy addresses from active user) and -NewUserPrincipalName (sets new UPN). Example: Restore-MgDirectoryDeletedItem -DirectoryObjectId <id> -BodyParameter @{autoReconcileProxyConflict=$true; newUserPrincipalName='new@domain.com'}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: AAD Connect Refresh Directory Schema triggers unexpected Full Import/Full Sync cycle
rootCause: Schema refresh generates new object class and attribute inclusion lists from sync rule templates. If the new lists differ from existing ones (new class or attribute in AD Schema referenced in templates), Full Import/Sync is triggered automatically.
solution: 1) Before schema refresh, uncheck Start the synchronization process in wizard. 2) After refresh, run Get-ADSyncSchedulerConnectorOverride to check if full import/sync is pending. 3) If pending, defer and schedule full sync during maintenance: Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Initial.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: EmployeeLeaveDateTime attribute value is always empty/null when querying user via Microsoft Graph, despite the attribute being synced from on-premises AD
rootCause: Microsoft Graph requires a specific scope User-LifeCycleInfo.ReadWrite.All to read EmployeeLeaveDateTime. Without this scope, the attribute value is not returned.
solution: Connect to Microsoft Graph with the required scope: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes @('User.ReadWrite.All', 'User-LifeCycleInfo.ReadWrite.All'). Then query the user normally.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Azure AD Connect Wizard fails with Access Denied on Get-MsolUserRole command
rootCause: The tenant has blockMsolPowerShell enabled, or MSOL PowerShell module is retired and no longer functional. Older AAD Connect versions rely on MSOL cmdlets.
solution: Update to the latest version of Entra Connect. MSOL is retired. See Breaking Change on Entra Connect Sync in version history documentation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Group export fails in Azure AD Connect with error dn-attributes-failure
rootCause: One or more members referenced in the group membership attribute have errors or conflicts in the Azure AD Connector Space, preventing the group from being exported.
solution: 1) Open Sync Service Manager. 2) Open the object with Export Error dn-attributes-failure on AAD Connector. 3) Check membership attribute New Value column. 4) Copy each new DN. 5) Search that DN in AAD Connector Space for errors/conflicts. 6) Resolve each member error. 7) Repeat. 8) Next delta sync should resolve.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: LastPasswordChangeTimestamp in Azure AD does not match on-premises AD ShadowLastPasswordChangeTimestamp after changing user password on-premises
rootCause: AAD:LastPasswordChangeTimestamp is only updated when the actual password is changed in Azure AD - either directly or via PHS sync. No direct sync of ShadowLastPasswordChangeTimestamp to AAD:LPCT.
solution: Enable PHS or fix any issues affecting PHS synchronization. AAD:LastPasswordChangeTimestamp will only update when PHS successfully syncs the password hash from on-premises AD to Azure AD.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: GroupWriteback V2: after setting a group to DoNotWriteBack, the group is not deleted from on-premises AD even though it shows ReasonFiltered=WriteBackOutOfScope
rootCause: By design, AAD Connect does not automatically delete groups from on-premises AD when they are disabled for writeback. A custom sync rule is required.
solution: Create a custom sync rule to delete groups when disabled for writeback or soft deleted: https://learn.microsoft.com/entra/identity/hybrid/connect/how-to-connect-modify-group-writeback#delete-groups-when-theyre-disabled-for-writeback-or-soft-deleted
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Customer rebuilt on-premises AD and all Object IDs changed - synchronization fails, objects do not match
rootCause: Hard match requires BOTH ImmutableID (Source Anchor) AND On-premises Object GUID to match. When AD is rebuilt, both values change. Security design prevents compromised on-prem admins from hijacking cloud identities.
solution: 1) Clear the onPremisesObjectIdentifier on the cloud user first. 2) Set correct ImmutableId on the new on-premises AD object. 3) Hard match is intentionally gated - requires cloud admin approval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Azure AD Connect synchronization is throttled during object provisioning - customer wants to increase write limits for large-scale sync
rootCause: Write limits during object provisioning are by design and uniformly enforced. High-volume burst writes can cause directory contention, data corruption risk, and downstream impact. Microsoft Support cannot increase write limits.
solution: 1) Distribute writes over time - introduce synced objects in phases. 2) Reduce high-cost writes: minimize on-premises group memberships, favor cloud-native groups. 3) Use Group SOA with Group Writeback via Cloud Sync. No exception process for increasing limits.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Manually upgrading ODBC/OLE DB drivers on Entra Connect server causes sync service failure
rootCause: Manually upgrading ODBC Driver for SQL Server 17 or OLE DB Driver for SQL Server 18 bundled with Entra Connect is not supported. Must maintain the same major version.
solution: Maintain same major version of ODBC/OLE DB drivers. Only minor version upgrades permitted. Keep original drivers for rollback. Wait for Entra Connect team to integrate new drivers in future releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Synced string attribute values truncated when exceeding maximum character length
rootCause: String attributes have a 448 character max in Azure AD Connect sync. Directory Extension attributes have a 250 character limit. Values exceeding these are truncated.
solution: For string attributes that may exceed 448 characters, add Left() function in attribute flow: attributeName <- Left([attributeName],448). Directory Extensions auto-truncate at 250 characters.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Cannot remove or modify alias@tenant.onmicrosoft.com ProxyAddress from a synced user
rootCause: The alias@tenant.onmicrosoft.com address is required by EXO for all licensed users. Since the object is synced from on-premises AD, cloud-managed addresses cannot be directly modified.
solution: Workaround: 1) Pause AADConnect sync. 2) Soft-delete user (Remove-EntraUser). 3) Create/rename another account with target UPN, assign EXO license. 4) Restore original user: Restore-EntraDeletedDirectoryObject -Id <id> -AutoReconcileProxyConflict. 5) Delete temp account. 6) Resume sync.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: DirSync should NOT be disabled on tenant for troubleshooting - causes extended downtime
rootCause: Disabling DirSync transfers SOA for ALL objects and can take 72+ hours. It is a very disruptive operation causing extended downtime for Azure AD synchronization.
solution: Only valid scenarios: 1) Switching SourceAnchor attribute during AADConnect reinstall. 2) Decommissioning AADConnect for all objects to manage from cloud. For individual object fixes, raise an IcM instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/General/FAQ for ADSync

symptom: Set IsExchangeCloudManaged to True and edited Exchange attribute in EXO but change did not take effect
rootCause: SOA transfer for Exchange attributes to Exchange Online takes up to 24 hours to complete. Individual attribute updates after SOA transfer can also take up to 24 hours.
solution: Wait 24 hours. Then check Azure Audit logs for the change (Actor: Microsoft Substrate Service). If change not in logs after 24 hours, escalate to EXOManageability team. Changes flow: EXO -> Entra AD -> forward sync back to EXO.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object Level SOA/Moving SOA to EXO

symptom: Mailbox migrated (offboarded) to on-premises while IsExchangeCloudManaged is True - broken synchronization between on-premises AD and Entra
rootCause: When IsExchangeCloudManaged is True, Connect Sync, Entra, and EXO interpret Exchange attribute SOA as in the cloud. Migrating to on-prem without resetting breaks sync.
solution: 1) Move mailbox back to cloud. 2) Set-Mailbox -Identity <user> -IsExchangeCloudManaged $false. 3) Manually update on-prem AD with Exchange attribute changes made during cloud SOA. 4) Perform directory sync. 5) Then migrate to on-prem again.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object Level SOA/Moving SOA to EXO

symptom: Errors when managing users in Exchange Online after enabling IsExchangeCloudManaged - old Entra Connect version
rootCause: IsExchangeCloudManaged requires Entra Connect version 2.5.76.0 or higher. Older versions do not understand the new SOA model.
solution: Upgrade Entra Connect to version 2.5.76.0 or higher. Needed even though Exchange attributes do not write back, because Entra Connect must understand the new SOA flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object Level SOA/Moving SOA to EXO

symptom: Entra Connect export fails with error type/code 114, no UPN shown, a new unique GUID, and detail message: This synchronization operation, Delete, is not valid. Contact Technical Support. The object has DirSyncEnabled set to False.
rootCause: Entra Connect is trying to delete a restored user or object that was moved to an un-synced OU or Lost+Found. The object was previously soft-deleted and restored from Entra ID Recycle Bin or had ImmutableID cleared, resulting in DirSyncEnabled=False.
solution: 1) Move the cloud account to Azure Recycle Bin via Delete User. 2) Run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta. 3) Confirm deletion successful. 4) Restore user from Recycle Bin. 5) Run another delta sync to confirm error is resolved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Resolving Error 114

symptom: Export to Azure AD fails with ExceededAllowedLength error for Directory Extension attributes (e.g., extension_*_userCertificate). Error message: the attribute in the local Directory exceeds the maximum allowed length. Re-running Entra Connect Wizard to remove the extension does not resolve pending export errors.
rootCause: Directory Extensions feature is limited to 250 characters per extended attribute. Large attributes like UserCertificate or thumbnailPhoto easily exceed this limit. Removing the extension via Wizard does not clear pending exports because the sync engine loses control over the excluded attribute and abandoned values persist.
solution: 1) Re-add problematic attributes in Directory Extensions wizard page. 2) Clone the Inbound sync rule 'In from AD - User DirectoryExtension' with lower precedence (e.g., 50). 3) Set FlowType=Expression and Source=AuthoritativeNull to null out values. 4) Run full sync cycle. 5) Confirm null export. 6) Delete cloned rule, re-enable original. 7) Remove problematic attributes from Directory Extensions in wizard. 8) Run another full sync cycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Resolving ExceededAllowedLength errors

symptom: Export to Entra ID fails with ReferenceUpdateFailure error: The reference attribute [RegisteredOwner] could not be updated in Azure Active Directory. Error occurs on computer objects created by Intune Connector for Autopilot Hybrid Azure AD Join, synchronized by Entra Connect.
rootCause: Intune Connector server does not have proper delegation to create computer objects in the target OU. Without delegation, ms-ds-CreatorSID is populated with the connector server ObjectSID. Since Hybrid Azure AD joined devices are organization-owned (no individual registered owner), Entra ID rejects the RegisteredOwner reference update.
solution: Set up proper OU delegation for the Intune Connector server to create computer objects. Previously created computer objects with invalid ms-ds-CreatorSID cannot be repaired; they must be re-created by the Autopilot/Intune Connector process after delegation is correctly configured.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Resolving ReferenceUpdateFailure errors

symptom: ProxyAddresses confirmed in on-premises AD and visible in ShadowProxyAddresses in Azure AD, but the address does not appear in the final Azure AD ProxyAddresses attribute after sync.
rootCause: ProxyCalc sanitizes (removes) SMTP ProxyAddresses with non-verified domain suffixes when the user has Exchange service plans (excluding MyAnalytics/Bookings/PremiumEncryption), is an Exchange Shared Resource recipient type (CloudMsExchRecipientDisplayType = 0/2/7/8/10/15/16/17/18/1073741824/1073741840), or has MSExchRemoteRecipientType set. Alternatively, the address may contain invalid characters.
solution: Add and verify the domain suffix as a verified domain on the tenant. Check if user has Exchange service plans triggering sanitization via ASC Graph: GET /users/{upn}?$select=assignedPlans. For invalid characters, review ProxyAddress format. Use a test account in lab to isolate which service plan triggers sanitization by assigning one plan at a time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/ProxyCalc Overview

symptom: User on-premises UPN has a custom domain suffix but after synchronizing to Azure AD the UPN suffix is replaced with the tenant initial domain (.onmicrosoft.com).
rootCause: ProxyCalc checks if the UPN suffix from ShadowUserPrincipalName is a verified domain on the tenant. If the suffix is not verified, ProxyCalc replaces it with the tenant initial domain. If ShadowUserPrincipalName is absent, ProxyCalc falls back to primary SMTP from ShadowProxyAddresses, then MailNickname + initial domain (with 4 random digits if UPN already exists).
solution: Add and verify the custom domain suffix as a verified domain on the Azure AD tenant. After adding the verified domain, a tenant-wide background task will re-run ProxyCalc for all objects to recalculate UPN values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/ProxyCalc Overview

symptom: After removing a synchronized user from Entra Connect sync scope and restoring from Entra ID Recycle Bin, the object shows DirSyncEnabled=False and appears as Cloud Only in portal, but on-premises Exchange properties (MSExchRecipientDisplayType, MSExchRemoteRecipientType, Shadow attributes) are retained. Admin cannot fully manage the object as a true cloud-only user.
rootCause: Removing an object from sync scope and recovering from Recycle Bin does not transfer Source of Authority (SoA) to the cloud. The object retains all synchronized Shadow attributes because it can be reconnected. A disconnected synced object has DirSyncEnabled=False but onPremisesLastSyncDateTime still has a value (unlike a true cloud-only object where both are null). The only supported method to transfer SoA is disabling DirSync on the entire tenant.
solution: Never disable DirSync as a troubleshooting step (only for permanent migration). Identify disconnected objects by checking onPremisesLastSyncDateTime (not null = disconnected synced object). For hard-matching to a new on-prem object, use ADSyncTools Set-ADSyncToolsMsDsConsistencyGuid to write Entra ID ImmutableId into ms-DS-ConsistencyGuid attribute. Do not manually restore soft-deleted synced objects to convert SoA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/Source Of Authority in hybrid environments

symptom: Entra Connect export fails with ProxyAddress conflict error. Connect Health shows Duplicate Attribute Error but may not display Existing Object properties (especially for MEPF conflicts). Subsequent syncs show DirSyncProvisioningError as duplicate attribute resiliency quarantines the conflicting values.
rootCause: An existing object in Entra ID (user, group, contact, or Mail-Enabled Public Folder/MEPF) holds the same ProxyAddress as the incoming synced object. MEPF objects are particularly problematic because they cannot be queried via standard Graph APIs and can only be found using ADSyncTools via DirSync Webservice (AdminWebService).
solution: Identify conflicting object: for users/groups/contacts use Get-MgUser/Get-MgGroup/Get-MgContact with proxyAddresses filter. For MEPF, use Get-ADSyncToolsAadObject -SyncObjectType PublicFolder. Remove conflicts: users via Remove-MgUser, contacts via Remove-MgBetaContact (Graph v1.0 cannot delete OrgContact), groups via Remove-MgGroup, MEPF via Remove-ADSyncToolsAadPublicFolders -SourceAnchor. For bulk MEPF removal, use Export-ADSyncToolsAadPublicFolders to CSV then Remove-ADSyncToolsAadPublicFolders -InputCsvFilename.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/TSG Resolving conflicts with orphaned (MEPF) objects in Entra ID

symptom: MailNickname in Azure AD does not update after changing the primary SMTP address or other source properties of a synced group or user in on-premises AD.
rootCause: ProxyCalc only generates MailNickname from fallback properties (ShadowProxyAddresses primary SMTP prefix, UPN prefix, or CN) during initial object provisioning when no explicit MailNickname is provided from on-premises. Once set, changing these fallback source properties does not trigger MailNickname recalculation.
solution: Explicitly set the MailNickname (Alias) attribute in on-premises AD to the desired value and sync. ProxyCalc uses the explicit on-premises MailNickname value directly without recalculation. For groups, the same logic applies: set MailNickname explicitly rather than relying on derived values.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/ProxyCalc Overview

symptom: Azure AD Connect staging server wizard fails with 'An error occurred retrieving the Active Directory schema' and 'GetPrincipalBySamAccountName: Principal MSOL_xxx was not found'. Error occurs at ConfigureSyncEngineStage when granting group writeback permissions to AD connector account.
rootCause: Group Writeback feature requires Exchange schema in AD forest. When Exchange schema is not detected (log: 'No forest has exchange schema present'), the wizard cannot resolve the MSOL connector account SamAccountName, causing NoMatchingPrincipalException during group writeback permission grant.
solution: Uncheck the Group Writeback option in Azure AD Connect wizard configuration. Complete the wizard without group writeback enabled. Group Writeback requires Exchange Server 2016 CU15+ and Exchange hybrid prerequisites.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/An error occurred retrieving the Active Directory schema. Azure AD connect - Staging Server

symptom: Delta Synchronization fails with 'Stopped-error-limit' after swing migration from Entra Connect V1.x to V2.x using existing database. Stack trace shows 'Attribute employeeHireDate not found in schema with GUID: 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' during Provisioning step.
rootCause: The remote database was deployed using schema data from V1.x endpoints which does not include the employeeHireDate attribute (introduced in V2.0.3.0). The V2.x sync engine cannot resolve this attribute from the old schema, causing sync-generic-failure.
solution: Uninstall Entra Connect on target server and delete all related folders. Create a brand-new DB instance on SQL server with proper permissions. Perform manual installation instead of importing JSON config from old instance. Run full sync in staging mode, verify Pending Export container for no accidental deletions, then enable sync cycle and disable staging mode.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Entra connect Error : Sync-generic-failure  Synchronization step : Provisioning

symptom: Entra Connect upgrade fails due to error upgrading OLE DB SQL drivers. Trace log in C:\ProgramData\AADConnect shows installer unable to get or parse OLE DB SQL driver update parameters.
rootCause: The Entra Connect installer fails to automatically update the OLE DB SQL drivers during upgrade for unknown reason, unable to parse required parameters for the msoledbsql driver update.
solution: 1) Locate the Entra Connect installer MSI. 2) Extract files: msiexec /a <path>\AzureADConnect.msi /qb TARGETDIR=<path>\AzureADConnectSetupFiles. 3) Navigate to extracted folder and locate msoledbsql.msi. 4) Manually install msoledbsql.msi. 5) Retry Entra Connect upgrade.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Error OLE DB SQL drivers

symptom: Error 'Failed to Disable staging mode' when activating a new staging Entra Connect server. Log shows InvalidOperationException at SetSSPRAndPHSStateForStagingMode with error code 80004005 in server.cpp(10880).
rootCause: Password Writeback feature conflicts with the process of disabling staging mode. SetSSPRAndPHSStateForStagingMode fails with InvalidOperationException when both Password Writeback is enabled and staging mode is being disabled simultaneously.
solution: 1) Cancel the Entra Connect wizard. 2) Re-launch wizard and uncheck Password Write back feature, complete wizard. 3) Re-launch wizard again and deselect 'Enable staging mode'. 4) Complete wizard - it should succeed without error. Password Writeback can be re-enabled after staging mode is successfully disabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Failed to disable Staging mode

symptom: ADSync service fails to start due to corrupted Model database in SQL LocalDB. Event Viewer shows EventID 528 (WaitForMultipleObjects), EventIDs 2005/6226 (OLEDB login timeout 0x80004005). SQL error.log shows Error 9003: 'The log scan number passed to log scan in database model is not valid'. Affects AADConnect 2.x with SQL 2019 LocalDB.
rootCause: SQL LocalDB backup logic bug creates inconsistent Model database bootpage. After backup, dbi_status is set to FULL Recovery Mode (0x40010000) and dbi_dbbackupLSN points to a log file, but actual recovery mode (governed by Master DB) is SIMPLE. When logs are truncated and LocalDB restarts, it detects backup LSN earlier than earliest log file and refuses to start.
solution: Mitigation: Navigate to ADSync2019 instance folder, rename model.mdf/modellog.ldf to old_*, copy fresh files from C:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server\150\LocalDB\Binn\Templates, start ADSync service. Permanent fix: Upgrade to Entra Connect 2.1.1.0+, or manually run ALTER DATABASE [model] SET RECOVERY FULL then SET RECOVERY SIMPLE to reset dbi_status to 0x40010008.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Resolving Model database corruption in SQLLocalDB

symptom: ADSync service (miiserver.exe) crashes during import run profile with stopped-server error. Event Viewer EventID 1000/1001 shows miiserver.exe crash with MSVCR120.dll faulting module, exit code -1073741819 (0xC0000005 access violation). EventIDs 6332 (MMS_SERVER_FAILED_TO_CONVERT_REFERENCES) and 6951 follow the crash.
rootCause: Dead links (corrupted object references) in the ADSync database cause miiserver.exe to crash with access violation (0xC0000005) when attempting to convert references during import synchronization. The dead links point to non-existent objects in the connector space.
solution: 1) Backup ADSync database and stop Sync Scheduler. 2) Open admin CMD, cd to C:\Program Files\Microsoft Azure AD Sync\Bin. 3) Run storechk.exe to check database for dead links. 4) Confirm dead link entries in output log. 5) Run storechk.exe with -repair flag to fix corrupted references. 6) Run check again without -repair to verify all issues resolved.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/TSG - ADSync service crashes and Cycle is interrupted with Stopped-Server

symptom: Synchronized user's MobilePhone or AlternateMobilePhones attributes modified from Azure portal or PowerShell are not overridden back to on-premises values even after running delta/full DirSync
rootCause: Cloud updates to MobilePhone/AlternateMobilePhones set Azure AD as Source of Authority for these attributes via DirSyncOverrides, bypassing DirSync. The BypassDirSyncOverrides tenant feature is not enabled.
solution: Enable BypassDirSyncOverrides on the tenant: Get-MgDirectoryOnPremiseSynchronization, then Update-MgDirectoryOnPremiseSynchronization with Features @{BypassDirSyncOverridesEnabled=$true}. Then make a delta change in user properties (e.g. add a proxy address) to trigger re-sync. Verify with Get-ADSyncToolsDirSyncOverridesUser. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-bypassdirsyncoverrides
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/What is BypassDirSyncOverrides feature of an Azure AD tenant

symptom: New Entra Connect staging server shows pending export updates for msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute on devices after initial synchronization, while the existing active server does not show these pending updates
rootCause: November 2020 encoding change in Azure AD affected LastLogonTimestamp value in stkDeviceKey byte array (from DateTime.MinValue to 64-bit signed integer). Active server with no full import since Nov 2020 has old encoding; new staging server imports fresh data with new encoding, causing mismatch.
solution: Run a full import and full synchronization cycle on the active Entra Connect server. This allows the active server to import the updated encoding and correctly update msDS-KeyCredentialLink in on-premises AD. After this, subsequent sync cycles on the staging server will no longer show pending exports.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Object sync/msDS KeyCredentialLink updates pending export in AD CS

symptom: Azure AD Connect installation interrupted with error: An error occurred while executing the Get-MsolUserRole command. Access Denied. You do not have permissions to call this cmdlet
rootCause: Customer has set blockMsolPowerShell authorization policy to True in Azure AD, which denies access to all MSOL PowerShell commands including Get-MsolUserRole used by the AADConnect wizard.
solution: Set blockMsolPowerShell to False via Graph API: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/policies/authorizationPolicy with body {blockMsolPowerShell:false}. Verify with GET authorizationPolicy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: Azure AD Connect installation fails with Unable to install the Synchronization Service. The ADSync service failed to start with an unexpected error for NT SERVICE\ADSync
rootCause: Windows Event Viewer Application log permissions have been customized (typically via Domain GPO) restricting NT AUTHORITY\SERVICE from WriteData access. The ADSync service account cannot write event logs during installation, causing bootstrap failure.
solution: Check CustomSD registry value at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Eventlog\Application. Compare with default permissions using wevtutil gl Application. Ensure NT AUTHORITY\SERVICE has ListDirectory+WriteData access. If GPO-enforced, exclude AADConnect server from GPO and delete CustomSD to restore defaults. Re-run wizard.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: Azure AD Connect installation/upgrade fails with Service ADSync was not found on computer error, even though previous AADConnect was uninstalled
rootCause: Windows Installer database has inconsistent or stale product code for Azure AD Sync Engine from a previous installation. The wizard detects old product code and attempts upgrade instead of clean install, but ADSync service registry entries are gone.
solution: Check trace log in %ProgramData%\AADConnect for GetInstalledPackagesByUpgradeCode to identify stale product code GUID. Uninstall KB3139923 if present, install KB3072630. Use MSIEXEC /x {productcode} /qn /norestart to remove stale code. If still failing, use Microsoft Program Install and Uninstall troubleshooter. Re-run wizard.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: Azure AD Connect Wizard fails during Configure AAD Sync with error: hexadecimal value 0x##, is an invalid character when configuring Domain/OU filtering
rootCause: An Active Directory OU name contains a non-printable ASCII control character (0x00-0x1F). The AADConnect wizard cannot parse the OU distinguished name containing these invalid characters.
solution: Open trace log from %ProgramData%\AADConnect, search for Inclusion List AFTER configuration. Paste content into Notepad++ or HEX editor to identify the control character. In AD Users and Computers, check if OU canonicalName shows in HEX in Attribute Editor. Remove all control characters from OU name and parent containers. Re-run wizard.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: Entra Connect Wizard first-time installation fails with ArgumentOutOfRangeException during final configuration stage, trace log shows failed-authentication and Invalid Credentials error code 0x31
rootCause: AD DS service records in DNS are misconfigured or missing, causing the wizard to fail LDAP authentication (error 0x31) against the domain controller when trying to retrieve the AD DS schema.
solution: Follow AD DNS troubleshooting guide to fix AD DS SRV records in DNS. Verify _ldap._tcp, _kerberos._tcp etc. are correctly registered. Re-run Entra Connect wizard after DNS is fixed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: Microsoft Entra Connect Wizard Domain/OU filtering page does not display all OUs - OUs beyond approximately 1000 are missing from the selection list with no error message
rootCause: Active Directory LDAP MaxPageSize default is 1000. The Entra Connect Wizard does not retrieve additional LDAP result pages, so any OUs beyond the first 1000 in a single container are truncated.
solution: Option 1 (recommended): Reorganize OU structure to keep child OUs per container below 1000. Option 2: Temporarily increase LDAP MaxPageSize on domain controllers using ntdsutil (Set MaxPageSize to 2000, Commit Changes). Re-run wizard to see all OUs. Revert MaxPageSize when done if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting AADConnect Wizard Issues

symptom: MIM ADLDS (AD LDS) connector fails to detect changes, discover schema, read objects, or modify objects in ADAM application partitions due to insufficient permissions
rootCause: The service account used by the ADLDS management agent lacks the required minimum permissions on the ADAM partition. Different operations require different permission sets: Replicate Directory Changes for change detection, Generic Read for schema discovery and reading, Generic Write for modifications.
solution: Grant minimum required permissions: (1) Detect changes: Replicate Directory Changes on partition head (not inherited). (2) Discover schema: Generic Read on configuration container, or List Contents + Read Property + List Object. (3) Read objects: Generic Read, or List Children + Read Property. (4) Create/modify/delete: Generic Write, or Delete Child + Write Property. All permissions except Replicate Directory Changes can be inherited from partition head.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Microsoft Identity Manager/MIM 2016 Overview/Connectors/ADLDS

symptom: MIM ADLDS connector cannot connect to AD LDS (ADAM) instance - connection timeout or refused
rootCause: LDAP TCP port 389 (default) is blocked by firewall or misconfigured. The port may need to be changed if AD LDS is running on a non-standard port.
solution: Ensure TCP port 389 is open between MIM server and AD LDS instance. If AD LDS uses a non-standard port, change it in Management Agent Designer. SSL can also be used and does not affect the port number.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Microsoft Identity Manager/MIM 2016 Overview/Connectors/ADLDS

symptom: Customer reports GALSync issues when running in Azure or Microsoft 365 environment - sync failures, unexpected behavior, or errors with cloud-based Exchange
rootCause: GALSync is on-premises only and only supports on-prem Exchange Server. Any integration with Azure or Microsoft 365 is a custom unsupported solution that the customer implemented.
solution: Set correct expectations: Azure/M365 GALSync is out of Microsoft support boundary. For simple issues, try to help but clarify limitations. For complex scenarios, recommend the customer engage a skilled Partner or Microsoft Consulting Services (MCS). Always involve CSAM/IM in the conversation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Microsoft Identity Manager/MIM 2016 Overview/Connectors/GALSync for Exchange Server

symptom: GALSync deployment fails or behaves unexpectedly when using multiple Management Agents (MAs) together in a single GALSync deployment
rootCause: The customer has modified the GALSync DLL for provisioning or is using sync rules in the portal alongside GALSync. This mixed configuration is not the shipped GALSync product and is not recommended by Microsoft.
solution: Clarify that using other MAs in a single GALSync deployment is not the shipped GALSync and is not recommended. Microsoft support may not be able to identify the cause or solve the issue. Customer should engage the consultant or team who created the custom solution. Involve CSAM/IM in the conversation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Microsoft Identity Manager/MIM 2016 Overview/Connectors/GALSync for Exchange Server

symptom: Entra Connect Sync upgrade to version 2.5.190.0 fails and rolls back. Trace log shows 'System.InvalidOperationException: There was an issue obtaining cloud sync intervals' caused by System.IO.FileLoadException for 'System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, Version=6.0.0.1'. Get-ADSyncScheduler also fails with the same error.
rootCause: Assembly version mismatch during upgrade. The machine.config runtime binding does not redirect System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource to the correct version (8.0.0.0), causing a manifest definition mismatch (HRESULT: 0x80131040).
solution: Add an assembly binding redirect in C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework64\v4.0.30319\config\machine.config. Replace the empty <runtime /> tag with a <runtime> block containing <assemblyBinding> that redirects System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource from oldVersion='0.0.0.0-8.0.0.0' to newVersion='8.0.0.0'. Restart the ADSync service after the change.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/TSG Connect Sync Upgrade Fails issue obtaining cloud sync intervals

symptom: After server reboot, ADSync service starts but all sync runs fail. Application event log shows Event ID 6231 ('encryption keys could not be loaded from Windows Credential Vault'), Event ID 6206/6227 ('encryption keys could not be found'), and Event ID 6601 ('encrypted configuration was encoded in context of a different ADSync service account'). Often correlated with Windows updates requiring reboot.
rootCause: The connector account password was changed (AD connector or Azure AD connector), causing ADSync encryption keys to become inaccessible because they were encrypted under a different service account context.
solution: 1) Re-enter the AD connector account password: Open Synchronization Service Manager > Connectors tab > right-click AD connector > Properties > 'Connect to Active Directory Forest' > enter correct password. 2) Reinitialize the AAD connector account: Run PowerShell commands '$credential = Get-Credential' then 'Add-ADSyncAADServiceAccount -AADCredential $credential' (enter Global Admin credentials when prompted). 3) Start a sync cycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting AADConnect unable to access service encryption keys

symptom: After server reboot, ADSync service starts but all sync runs fail with Event ID 6231 ('encryption keys could not be loaded from Windows Credential Vault'), Event ID 6206/6227, and Event ID 6601. The ADSync service runs under a Group Managed Service Account (GMSA). Connector account passwords have NOT been changed.
rootCause: ADSync runs under a GMSA and DPAPI (Data Protection API) cannot decode connector saved credentials after reboot because the DPAPI master key is not available from a Domain Controller. A known cause is ProtectionPolicy being enabled in the DPAPI registry key, which prevents a backup of the master key from being created on a Domain Controller.
solution: Collaborate with the Windows support team to run and investigate a DPAPI trace to determine where the failure occurs. Check if ProtectionPolicy is enabled in the DPAPI registry key. If enabled, this prevents the DPAPI master key backup to the DC, causing decryption failures after GMSA password rotation (default every 30 days). Disable ProtectionPolicy or ensure DC connectivity for DPAPI key retrieval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting AADConnect unable to access service encryption keys

symptom: Entra Connect installation wizard throws 'ArgumentOutOfRangeException: Index was out of range' error. Trace log reveals 'failed-authentication' with error code 0x31 (Invalid Credentials) during schema retrieval against the AD connector.
rootCause: Missing or outdated DNS SRV records for Active Directory DC Locator (e.g., _kerberos, _ldap, _gc records in _sites, _tcp, _udp, DomainDnsZones, ForestDnsZones zones). Old/lingering DNS records may point to decommissioned hosts, or a 3rd-party DNS service is not AD-integrated and missing required service records.
solution: Check AD-integrated DNS zones on the DC: verify SOA, NS, A records for the domain, confirm _sites/_tcp/_udp/DomainDnsZones/ForestDnsZones sub-zones have correct _gc/_kerberos/_ldap SRV records. Remove outdated records pointing to decommissioned DCs. Fix DNS registration: run 'ipconfig /registerdns' and 'net stop netlogon && net start netlogon' on the DC. Clear DNS cache and reboot both DC and Entra Connect server.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting AD DNS Issues

symptom: Synchronization Service Manager shows 'Invalid Credentials/Failed-authentication' and Application Event ID 6311 intermittently. Issue recurs randomly hours after re-entering correct AD DS connector credentials. Sync works briefly after re-entering credentials but fails again.
rootCause: Outdated DNS SRV records pointing to a non-existent or decommissioned Domain Controller. Particularly common with 3rd-party DNS services not integrated with Active Directory, where DNS records are not automatically updated when DCs are decommissioned.
solution: Identify and remove outdated DNS records pointing to hosts no longer running AD DS services. For 3rd-party DNS: manually verify all SRV records match current DC infrastructure. After cleanup, clear DNS cache on the DNS server and reboot both the DC and Entra Connect server. Consider migrating to AD-integrated DNS for automatic record maintenance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting AD DNS Issues

symptom: Get-ADSyncScheduler fails with 'AADSTS700027: The certificate with identifier used to sign the client assertion is not registered on application' after rotating the Modern Authentication (ABA) certificate on one of multiple Connect Sync servers. The error references ConnectSyncProvisioning app.
rootCause: Both Connect Sync servers share the same cloud connector account. Rotating the ABA certificate on one server invalidates the certificate registration for the other server because they share the same application/service principal.
solution: 1) On the working server: roll back to Legacy Authentication using Roll back to a legacy service account by using PowerShell. Confirm with Get-ADSyncEntraConnectorCredential that account shows Sync_SERVERNAME format. 2) On the broken server: rotate the certificate, then also roll back to Legacy Auth. 3) Re-enroll both servers in ABA via wizard separately - each should create its own ConnectSyncProvisioning_SERVERNAME app. Validate each server has its own ConnectSyncProvisioning app and Get-ADSyncScheduler runs without errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting Connect Sync Modern Authentication ABA

symptom: Get-ADSyncScheduler fails with 'AADSTS700027: The certificate with identifier used to sign the client assertion is not registered on application' on one of multiple Connect Sync servers. Both servers were set up by cloning a Windows Server image.
rootCause: The cloned Connect Sync server has the same SyncMachineIdentifier GUID as the original (HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure AD Connect\SyncMachineIdentifier). Both servers share the same ConnectSyncProvisioning application identity, leading to certificate conflicts when ABA is configured.
solution: 1) Roll back both servers to Legacy Auth. 2) Delete the shared ConnectSyncProvisioning ABA app from Entra ID. 3) Reconfigure ABA on active server via wizard. 4) On staging server: Stop-Service ADSync, generate new SyncMachineIdentifier with [Guid]::NewGuid().ToString('N'), set via Set-ItemProperty at HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure AD Connect, Start-Service ADSync. 5) Configure ABA on staging server via wizard. Verify each server has its own ConnectSyncProvisioning app.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Troubleshooting Connect Sync Modern Authentication ABA

symptom: Azure AD Sync fails to start with AADSTS50079 error when running Start-ADSyncSyncCycle, error message says MFA enrollment required to access resource 00000002-0000-0000-c000-000000000000
rootCause: MFA is enforced on the Sync connector account (Sync_<ComputerName>@tenant.onmicrosoft.com) via Conditional Access policy or per-user MFA enforcement
solution: Check sign-in logs in ASC by Correlation ID to verify MFA is blocking the Sync connector account. Disable MFA enforcement on the Sync connector account (Sync_*@*.onmicrosoft.com) or exclude it from CA policies requiring MFA. Then re-run Start-ADSyncSyncCycle.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Connect Sync/Wizard and ADSync service/Troubleshooting guides/Unable to start Azure AD sync -AADSTS50079

symptom: EntityTypeNotSupported warning in cross-tenant sync provisioning logs when groups are in sync scope
rootCause: Cross-tenant group sync is not enabled in the sync configuration. The Provision Microsoft Entra ID Groups object mapping is disabled.
solution: If customer wants to sync groups: enable group sync by setting Provision Microsoft Entra ID Groups to enabled in sync config Mappings and enabling Allow group synchronization in target tenant inbound access settings. If not intending to sync groups, the warning can be safely ignored.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cross Tenant Group Synchronization

symptom: Cross-tenant sync test connection fails with message You appear to have entered invalid credentials but no error code is shown
rootCause: The Application Service Principal has been deleted by the customer, or cross-tenant access policies (inbound/outbound/auto-redemption) are misconfigured or missing
solution: Verify target tenant ID is correct (not the source tenant). Check auto-redemption outbound policy in source tenant and inbound policy in target tenant. Verify cross-tenant sync policy is enabled in target. If Service Principal is missing, delete the configuration and create a new one.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cross tenant synchronization/Troubleshooting/TSG Cross-tenant sync (Azure2Azure scenarios)

symptom: Cross-tenant sync skips provisioning a user to target tenant, user is not synchronized despite being in scope
rootCause: User has an AlternativeSecurityIdentifier (AltSecId) present on their object in the source tenant, indicating the user is external/B2B. Cross-tenant sync only provisions internal users. Common AltSecId types: type 1 (MSA linked), type 5 (converted B2B via AAD Connect), type 6 (SMS sign-in enabled).
solution: Check AltSecId in ASC. For type 6 (SMS sign-in): disable SMS sign-in via Graph API POST disableSmsSignIn or PowerShell Disable-MgUserAuthenticationPhoneMethodSmsSignIn. For type 1 (MSA): user must change MSA email alias. For type 5 (converted B2B): remove the AltSecId. Verify user is assigned to the application and account is enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/Cross tenant synchronization/Troubleshooting/TSG Cross-tenant sync (Azure2Azure scenarios)
